title: TSMC’s Culture Clash at Arizona Fab
summary: TSMC is facing cultural challenges at its new Arizona fab, particularly concerning work hours and management style, which differ significantly from practices in Taiwan. American employees are reportedly unaccustomed to the long hours and numerous meetings, prompting calls for changes towards an eight-hour workday to compete for talent. Although TSMC acknowledges some cultural shifts within the company, the deep-rooted Taiwanese work ethic, which often includes extended hours and continuous equipment monitoring, is being transplanted to the Arizona facility, creating a need for American engineers to adapt.

title: Living With a Dell Latitude 9420 2-in-1
summary: Michael J. Miller reviews the Dell Latitude 9420 2-in-1, a premium enterprise convertible laptop designed for demanding customers. He details its features, performance, battery life, and Dell Optimizer software, noting its solid build and strong performance despite a few minor reservations. The article concludes that it is one of the best 2-in-1 laptops he has seen.

title: CES 2022: Intel Achieves Major Milestones Across Automotive, PCs and Graphics
summary: During CES 2022, Intel announced significant advancements across its automotive, PC, and graphics divisions. Mobileye unveiled the EyeQ Ultra, an advanced system-on-chip for autonomous vehicles, and expanded partnerships with major automakers. Intel also launched its 12th Gen Intel Core H-series and new P-series mobile processors, setting new performance standards for laptops, and began shipping its Intel Arc graphics products to OEMs.

title: Top Automakers Double-Down on Mobileye
summary: Intel subsidiary Mobileye announced new strategic collaborations with Volkswagen Group, Ford, and Zeekr, showcasing its advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS) and autonomous vehicle (AV) technologies. Volkswagen Group will integrate Mobileye's mapping tech for enhanced ADAS, Ford will use its Road Experience Management (REM) for future BlueCruise versions, and Zeekr plans to launch an L4 self-driving vehicle powered by Mobileye Drive by 2024. These partnerships highlight Mobileye's leadership in scalable ADAS-to-AV solutions and signal significant advancements in the automotive industry.

title: CORSAIR ONE i300 Compact PC Announced, powered by Intel Alder Lake, DDR5, RTX 3080 with a starting price of $3999.99 US
summary: CORSAIR has announced their new ONE i300 compact form-factor desktop PC series, starting at $3999.99. These power-packed systems feature 12th Gen Intel Core i9-12900K CPUs, NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080/3080 Ti GPUs, and CORSAIR's VENGEANCE DDR5 memory up to 64GB. Designed for gamers and creatives, the ONE i300 offers a 20% performance improvement over previous generations within a small 12-liter frame, stabilized by a patented convection-assisted liquid cooling system.

title: Mobileye’s Self-Driving Secret? 200PB of Data
summary: Mobileye utilizes a massive 200 petabyte dataset of driving information, combined with advanced computer vision and natural language models, to accelerate the development of autonomous vehicles. This vast amount of data, gathered over 25 years, helps self-driving systems prepare for rare and unexpected scenarios, overcoming the "long tail" problem inherent in autonomous driving. The company's ability to efficiently process and learn from this data, supported by significant cloud infrastructure, differentiates its AV solutions and aims to achieve a high mean time between failure rate.

title: HP OMEN reveals products for 2022, including the unique cooling device, the OMEN Cryo Chamber
summary: HP OMEN has unveiled its 2022 gaming lineup, featuring the OMEN 45L Desktop with the innovative OMEN Cryo Chamber CPU cooling solution, which significantly lowers temperatures. The lineup also includes the OMEN 25L Desktop, the compact Victus by HP 15L Desktop, and the OMEN 27u 4K Gaming Monitor, designed for next-gen consoles. These new products offer advanced performance, cooling, and customization options for a diverse gaming audience.

title: Micron Technology searches for site for $40B factory. Is Boise among the contenders?
summary: Micron Technology is reportedly scouting locations for a new $40 billion manufacturing plant, with sites in Arizona, California, Texas, and North Carolina under consideration, though Boise is apparently not on the list. This search is part of Micron's larger plan to invest over $150 billion in chip production over 10 years to address a global chip shortage and meet growing demand driven by AI, 5G, and autonomous vehicles. The company is evaluating locations based on factors like site availability, talent pool, utility services, sustainability, and regulatory environment, with a focus on domestic production potentially spurred by the CHIPS Act.

title: CES 2022: HP shows off Elite Dragonfly G3, Dragonfly Chromebook and lots more new hardware
summary: At CES 2022, HP unveiled a comprehensive portfolio targeting the hybrid-work market, including the Elite Dragonfly G3, Dragonfly Chromebook, and several new monitors, laptops, and desktop PCs. The new Elite Dragonfly G3 is a premium ultraportable laptop with a 13.5-inch screen and 12th-gen Intel Core processors, while the Dragonfly Chromebook offers a Chrome OS alternative with unique features like a haptic trackpad. HP also introduced various conferencing and webcam monitors, updated EliteBook and ProBook business laptops, and new desktop PCs and curved displays for creators.

title: Intel Names David Zinsner Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer
summary: Intel Corporation announced the appointment of David Zinsner as its new Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, effective January 17, 2022. Zinsner brings over 20 years of experience in semiconductors and manufacturing, having previously served as CFO at Micron Technology, Inc. He will report to CEO Pat Gelsinger and oversee Intel's global finance organization, succeeding George Davis, who will retire in May 2022.

title: Intel Gains After Recruiting Micron’s David Zinsner as Its Next CFO
summary: Intel Corp. stock rallied after announcing the appointment of David Zinsner, previously CFO of Micron Technology Inc., as its new Chief Financial Officer. Zinsner will replace the retiring George Davis and will report to CEO Pat Gelsinger, tasked with overseeing the company’s finances as it aims to regain its prominence in the semiconductor industry. He is set to begin his new role on January 17th.

title: Corsair One i300 gaming PC review
summary: The Corsair One i300 is a compact, high-end gaming PC featuring Intel's Core i9 12900K, an Nvidia GeForce RTX 3080 Ti, and 64GB of DDR5-4800 RAM, priced at $4,999. It delivers excellent 4K gaming performance and operates quietly due to its unique liquid cooling system, though the CPU can run hot under heavy load. The article praises its aesthetic and hardware-packed design, acknowledging its high cost but highlighting its cutting-edge components and quiet operation.

title: Hawley Demands Answers After Intel Apologizes to CCP, Scrubs References to Chinese Slave Labor
summary: Senator Josh Hawley has sent a letter to Intel CEO Patrick Gelsinger demanding answers regarding the company's apparent failure to condemn slave labor and its accommodation of the Chinese Communist Party (CCP). This action follows Intel's decision to remove all references to Xinjiang from its annual supplier letter and apologize to the CCP for adhering to U.S. law concerning forced labor. Hawley questions Intel's commitment to U.S. law and human rights, especially concerning the use of forced labor in the Xinjiang region.

title: Aptiv acquires Wind River for $4.3 billion
summary: Aptiv PLC, an auto parts manufacturer, announced its acquisition of Wind River from TPG Capital for $4.3 billion in cash. Wind River, known for its edge-to-cloud software platform, will continue to operate as a standalone business unit within Aptiv's Advanced Safety & User Experience segment. This acquisition aims to accelerate a software-defined future for the automotive industry by integrating Wind River Studio with Aptiv's Smart Vehicle Architecture.

title: BigPanda Raises $190 Million in Funding at $1.2 Billion Valuation
summary: BigPanda, a leader in AIOps Event Correlation and Automation, announced a new funding round of $190 million, valuing the company at $1.2 billion. The funding, led by Advent International and Insight Partners, will be used to innovate AIOps capabilities, scale go-to-market activities, and potentially make strategic acquisitions. The company has seen significant growth, with 2021 sales increasing by 155% year-over-year, reflecting the growing demand for AI-powered IT operations solutions.

title: What will be included in 5G Advanced Release 18?
summary: The upcoming 5G Advanced Release 18 will focus on end-to-end system enhancements and expanding support for new device types and use cases. Qualcomm's Juan Montojo detailed improvements such as MIMO radio arrays, mobile Integrated Access/Backhaul, AI/ML-driven network design, enhanced mobility, and optimizing power consumption. The release also aims to develop a scalable, unified air interface to support a diverse range of devices, from high-performance XR headsets to various IoT sensors, paving the way for the future 6G vision.

title: How Intel relies on inclusion in retention and hiring—for big results
summary: Intel has been a leader in diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) for over a decade, achieving full representation of women and underrepresented minorities in its U.S. workforce and global gender pay equity ahead of schedule. Dawn Jones, Intel's chief diversity and inclusion officer, discussed the company's continuous commitment to fostering an inclusive culture, especially amid the Great Resignation, and its efforts to track DEI progress through data and partnerships like the Alliance for Global Inclusion. Intel focuses on retention, progression, and recruitment, utilizing data-driven decision-making and a hybrid work model to support its diverse workforce globally.

title: Intel reportedly picking Ohio for factory that also had a chance at landing in Central Texas
summary: Intel is reportedly planning to build a new semiconductor plant in Ohio, bypassing Central Texas, which was also considered for the factory. This move aligns with CEO Pat Gelsinger's goal to establish Intel's role in a national semiconductor strategy, with plans for significant investment in new fabrication facilities. An official announcement is anticipated around January 21st.

title: Initial Sound Open Firmware Support For AMD Hardware Comes With Linux 5.17
summary: The Linux 5.17 kernel updates include initial Sound Open Firmware (SOF) support for AMD hardware, specifically the Renoir Audio Co-Processor. This marks a significant step for AMD, bringing its audio hardware into the open-source SOF ecosystem previously championed by Intel. The update also adds HDA support for new audio codecs and other ASoC improvements.

title: Chip giant Nvidia expands R&D operations in Israel
summary: Nvidia is expanding its R&D operations in Israel by establishing a new design and engineering group focused on next-generation CPUs for AI, robotics, and autonomous vehicles. The company, which acquired Israel's Mellanox Technologies in 2020, plans to hire additional engineers in Israel, further solidifying the country's role as Nvidia's largest R&D hub outside the US. This expansion intensifies competition for tech talent in Israel, where both Nvidia and Intel have significant R&D presence, and Google is also entering the chip-making sector.

title: American Airlines Names Gregory D. Smith to its Board of Directors
summary: American Airlines (NASDAQ: AAL) has announced the election of Gregory D. Smith to its board of directors. Smith, who retired from The Boeing Company in July 2021 after a 30-year career, will serve on the board’s Audit Committee and Corporate Governance, Public Responsibility and Safety Committee. His extensive experience in finance, operations, and strategy in the aviation and aerospace industry is expected to be a significant asset to American Airlines.

title: Vodafone switches on first 5G OpenRAN site
summary: Vodafone has launched the UK's first 5G OpenRAN site in Bath, marking the initial step towards its commitment to deploy 2,500 5G and 4G OpenRAN sites by 2027. This initiative supports the government's strategy to diversify the telecoms supply chain and accelerate OpenRAN development. The technology, which separates software from hardware, is expected to foster innovation, improve energy efficiency, and speed up network deployment.

title: How to Invest in the Top 100 U.S. ESG Companies
summary: The article discusses the 2022 list of top 100 U.S. ESG companies compiled by Just Capital, highlighting major movements like Alphabet's rise and Meta's fall. It explains the methodology behind the rankings, which prioritize issues important to Americans, and introduces the Goldman Sachs JUST U.S. Large Cap Equity ETF (JUST) as a way to invest in companies demonstrating "just business behavior."

title: Intel CEO urges Europe, U.S. to push ahead with chip manufacturing plans
summary: Intel's CEO is advocating for Europe and the U.S. to advance their chip manufacturing plans. This push comes amidst global supply chain concerns and tariffs affecting various industries, including the automotive sector. The emphasis is on strengthening domestic manufacturing capabilities for critical components like semiconductors.

title: Intel places ASML order to step up TSMC competition
summary: Intel has ordered ASML's new TWINSCAN EXE:5200 extreme ultraviolet (EUV) system, marking a move to compete with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) in advancing to 2-nanometer process technology. This purchase aims to accelerate Intel's production with the new technology by 2025. While Intel is the first to order, a supply chain source suggests TSMC is also likely to acquire the system and lead in commercializing 2-nanometer production.

title: Intel to build $20B Ohio chip facility amid global shortage
summary: Intel announced its plan to invest $20 billion in building a new chip manufacturing facility in Ohio to help address the global chip shortage and boost domestic production. The project, involving two factories, is expected to create thousands of jobs and establish a new technology hub. This significant investment is also intended to support U.S. national security by increasing the domestic supply of crucial microprocessors.

title: Governor DeWine Announces Monumental Investment by Intel to Bring Their Most Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Plants to Ohio
summary: Ohio Governor Mike DeWine announced a monumental investment by Intel Corporation to build two state-of-the-art semiconductor manufacturing facilities in Licking County, Ohio. This $20 billion project is the largest single private sector investment in Ohio's history, expected to create over 20,000 jobs and add $2.8 billion to Ohio’s annual gross state product. The initiative aims to rebalance global chipmaking capacity, boost U.S. semiconductor manufacturing leadership, and strengthen the domestic supply chain for critical technologies.

title: Intel's $20 bln Ohio factory could become world's largest chip plant
summary: Intel announced plans to invest an initial $20 billion in a new chip manufacturing complex in Ohio, which could expand to $100 billion with eight fabrication plants, potentially making it the world's largest. This move is part of CEO Pat Gelsinger's strategy to restore Intel's chipmaking dominance and reduce U.S. reliance on Asian manufacturing amidst a global semiconductor shortage. The company is actively seeking government subsidies and expects the shortage to last into 2023.

title: Intel Vs. Micron Technology: Which Semiconductor Stock Is The Better Buy?
summary: This article compares Intel (INTC) and Micron Technology (MU), two major semiconductor stocks, to determine which is the better investment. It notes that Intel is larger and more diversified than Micron, and currently offers lower price-to-earnings and price-to-free cash flow ratios. The author, Bill Zettler, has a beneficial long position in Intel shares.

title: Land around Foxconn was finalist for Intel's $20 billion semiconductor production facility
summary: Land near the Foxconn Technology Group site in Mount Pleasant, Wisconsin, was a finalist for Intel Corporation's $20 billion semiconductor production facility. Despite strong competition from Wisconsin officials, Intel ultimately chose a 1,000-acre site in Licking County, Ohio. Wisconsin officials remain optimistic, stating that the qualities that attracted Intel will continue to draw other investors to the I-94 corridor.

title: Is chip giant Nvidia going to scrap its $40BN bid for Arm?
summary: Nvidia is reportedly preparing to abandon its $40 billion acquisition of Arm Ltd. due to fierce regulatory backlash and little progress in securing approvals. Regulators and customers, including the U.S. FTC, have expressed concerns that the deal would make Nvidia too powerful. SoftBank, Arm's current owner, is stepping up preparations for an Arm initial public offering as an alternative.

title: Intel Corporation v Commission (Case T-286/09 RENV): General Court Quashes Intel's €1.06 Billion Fine
summary: The General Court has partially annulled the European Commission's €1.06 billion fine on Intel for abusing its dominant position through exclusivity rebates. The Court ruled that the Commission failed to establish to the requisite legal standard that the rebates were capable of having anticompetitive effects, rejecting the previous per se illegality stance. This decision involved a detailed review of the Commission's AEC test and its application of relevant criteria.

title: Intel scores major win as court scraps $1.2 bln EU antitrust fine
summary: Intel has successfully overturned a 1.06-billion-euro ($1.2 billion) EU antitrust fine that was imposed 12 years ago for allegedly stifling rival Advanced Micro Devices. Europe's second-highest court annulled the fine, criticizing the European Commission's analysis as incomplete. This ruling is a significant setback for EU antitrust regulators and could impact similar cases involving other major tech companies.

title: Intel stock hits lowest price in more than a year, and there is another shoe to drop
summary: Intel's stock dropped over 6% to its lowest point in more than a year after analysts warned of potential troubles beyond falling margins: an impending oversupply of PCs. This oversupply is expected to severely impact Intel's largest business segment. The stock's performance made it the worst performer on the Dow Jones Industrial Average Thursday.

title: Ohio lured Intel's chip plant with $2B incentive package
summary: Ohio successfully attracted Intel's new $20 billion chip manufacturing plant with a lucrative $2 billion incentive package, deemed the largest economic development deal in the state's history. This investment aims to alleviate the global chip shortage, create 3,000 factory jobs with an average salary of $135,000, and establish a new technology hub in the Midwest. The state anticipates that this facility will attract additional industries and support further regional growth.

title: Chip shortage to last six months, one year or 18 months-- depending on who’s talking
summary: The article discusses the varying predictions for how long the global chip shortage will last, with estimates ranging from six months to 18 months or longer. It highlights views from IDC, Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger, and Texas Instruments, alongside ongoing efforts by the U.S. Congress and Commerce Secretary to address the issue through funding and incentives for domestic chip production. The consensus indicates that while some improvements might be seen, the shortage is expected to persist through 2022 and potentially into 2023 for various sectors.

title: TSMC: Can anything stop Taiwan's chipmaking giant?
summary: TSMC, the world's largest contract chip manufacturer, reported bumper financial results in late 2021, with revenue of $15.7bn and profits of $6bn. The company plans to invest $40-$44bn into capital expenditure to maintain its market dominance, dwarfing its rivals. Despite its strong position, TSMC faces challenges from competitors like Intel and Samsung, as well as geopolitical risks and the complexities of global expansion.

title: Chip giants are ramping up spending by the billions as semiconductor demand booms
summary: Major semiconductor companies like TSMC and Intel are investing billions into new fabrication plants and research to meet the booming global demand for chips, despite the ongoing chip shortage. TSMC plans to spend $100 billion over three years, while Intel announced a $20 billion investment in Arizona. Analysts expect a "choppy" recovery from the shortage in the short term, but demand is projected to continue increasing, benefiting suppliers like ASML and Applied Materials.

title: Intel W680 Motherboard Retail Listings Point To Imminent Alder Lake Xeon Launch
summary: Retail listings for Supermicro W680 motherboards in Europe suggest an imminent launch of Intel's Alder Lake Xeon processors designed for server and workstation use. These new motherboards support the LGA 1700 socket, are intended for upcoming Xeon chips with up to 16 cores, and are expected to include PCIe 5.0 and DDR5 memory support. The listings precede official announcements, indicating that Intel is preparing to expand its Alder Lake architecture to the server/workstation segment soon.

title: Intel Foundry Services Launches Ecosystem Alliance to Accelerate Customer Innovation
summary: Intel Foundry Services (IFS) has launched Accelerator, a comprehensive ecosystem alliance designed to help foundry customers accelerate their silicon product development. This initiative brings together leading companies in electronic design automation (EDA), intellectual property (IP), and design services to provide a full spectrum of solutions optimized for Intel's manufacturing platform. The alliance aims to foster innovation and address the increasing demand for semiconductors by making Intel's technology platforms accessible to partners for accelerated product development and time to market.

title: SoftBank’s sale of Arm to Nvidia collapses, Arm to IPO
summary: The proposed sale of Arm Ltd by SoftBank Group Corp to U.S. chipmaker Nvidia Corp has collapsed due to regulatory hurdles and scrutiny from various international bodies. Instead of the sale, which was valued at up to $80 billion, Arm will now plan for an initial public offering (IPO). The deal faced opposition from U.S., British, EU, and Chinese regulators over concerns about competition, pricing, and innovation in vital chip markets.

title: Global chip shortage: Toyota profits fall as production hit
summary: Toyota reported a 21% drop in profits for the last quarter of 2021 due to the global chip shortage severely impacting its production. The company has reduced its annual production target and apologized for delivery delays, with analysts predicting the chip shortage will continue to affect the automotive sector. Despite these challenges, Toyota aims to manage the shortage better than competitors and is also working to catch up in the growing electric vehicle market.

title: Design Challenges Increasing For Mixed-Die Packages
summary: The semiconductor industry faces growing design challenges for mixed-die packages as it moves beyond Moore's Law towards heterogeneous integration. While combining dies from different foundries offers benefits like cost reduction and optimal IP use, it also introduces complexities in supply chain relationships, liability, and the need for standardized interconnect schemes. The article highlights that business and organizational hurdles may be more significant than technological ones in advancing chiplet adoption, requiring closer collaboration across the ecosystem.

title: Intel posts first job openings for its $20 billion New Albany chip factory
summary: Intel has begun posting job openings for its $20 billion chip factory project in New Albany, Ohio, including various engineering and project management roles. These initial hires will help build the plant, which is expected to employ 3,000 workers with an average wage of $135,000, in addition to 7,000 construction jobs. The postings are spread across multiple platforms to attract a wide range of candidates.

title: ETF of the month: VanEck Semiconductor UCITS ETF (SMGB)
summary: The VanEck Semiconductor UCITS ETF (SMGB) has been named ETF of the month, highlighting its impressive performance in 2021 by returning 44.4% and gathering $859m in assets since its December 2020 launch. The ETF has benefited from increased demand for semiconductor chips driven by global digitalization, the reopening of economies, and emerging technologies like electric vehicles and 5G. Despite some recent market concerns from hedge fund selling, the semiconductor industry is projected to see continued revenue growth in 2022.

title: Intel Introduces New Mining Chip
summary: Intel has developed a new energy-efficient crypto mining chip designed to tackle the high energy consumption associated with cryptocurrency mining. The chip, which will be detailed at an upcoming conference, aims to offer significantly better performance per watt than mainstream GPUs for SHA-256 based mining. Jack Dorsey's Block (SQ) is among the first buyers, a move that could benefit companies focused on digital transformation and sustainability in the crypto space.

title: Intel teams up with Benteler and Beep to develop self-driving shuttles
summary: Intel, in collaboration with auto parts supplier Benteler and transportation startup Beep, is developing fully autonomous Level 4 shuttles for deployment in the U.S. These electric vehicles will utilize Intel's Mobileye Drive system and the new EyeQ Ultra chip for their self-driving capabilities. Benteler will handle the design and assembly, while Beep will leverage its experience in operating autonomous shuttle services to aid in development and eventual commercialization, with a target launch on public roads by 2024.

title: Intel appoints interim general counsel
summary: Intel has appointed Susie Giordano, corporate vice president and corporate secretary, as its interim general counsel, effective immediately. This appointment follows the announced retirement of current general counsel Steven Rodgers, who plans to retire on May 31 after more than 22 years with the company. Giordano previously oversaw Intel's corporate legal group and provided legal support to Intel Capital.

title: Intel to Buy Israel’s Tower Semiconductor for $5.4 Billion
summary: Intel Corp. has agreed to acquire Israel’s Tower Semiconductor Ltd. for $5.4 billion as part of CEO Pat Gelsinger’s strategy to expand into the outsourced chip-manufacturing business. This acquisition aims to enhance Intel's expertise and customer base in the chip-foundry market, directly competing with industry leaders like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. The deal is expected to close in about 12 months and is a key part of Intel's broader investment cycle to modernize its factories and rebuild manufacturing capabilities.

title: ETFs to Gain on Intel's Buyout of Tower Semiconductor
summary: Intel's $5.4 billion acquisition of Tower Semiconductor is expected to boost several technology ETFs. These ETFs, including FTXL, SOXX, SOXQ, TDIV, and VLUE, have significant allocations to Intel and are positioned to benefit from the deal's impact on Intel's manufacturing capacity and market position. The acquisition aims to enhance Intel's foundry services and address the soaring demand for semiconductors.

title: Intel to buy Israel's Tower Semiconductor for $5.4bn
summary: Intel announced its acquisition of Israeli chipmaker Tower Semiconductor for $5.4 billion. This deal strengthens Intel's position in specialized chip production, particularly for analog chips used in various applications like automotive and medical sensors. The acquisition aims to capitalize on the increasing global demand for semiconductors.

title: Inside Pat Gelsinger’s audacious $43.5B plan to revive Intel
summary: This article details Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger's plans to revitalize the company, which has recently faced significant challenges from competitors like Apple and AMD, and struggled with manufacturing delays. Gelsinger, who rejoined Intel after a period away, aims to tackle both technological and cultural issues to restore Intel's preeminence in the chip industry. His experience at Intel during its heyday positions him to address these intricate problems, including a new business model centered on investing $43.5 billion to regain its manufacturing edge.

title: Intel Names Christoph Schell Executive Vice President and Chief Commercial Officer
summary: Intel Corporation announced the appointment of Christoph Schell as executive vice president and chief commercial officer, effective March 14, 2022. Schell, formerly of HP Inc. and Philips, will lead the Sales, Marketing and Communications Group, succeeding Michelle Johnston Holthaus. He will report directly to CEO Pat Gelsinger and focus on driving customer and partner success and expanding Intel’s business opportunities worldwide.

title: Intel delays 2023 server chip, says it needs to boost spending to catch competitors
summary: Intel announced it would need to significantly increase spending over the next two years to fund its turnaround and transformation into a semiconductor manufacturer. The company also confirmed that the release of its forthcoming server chip, codenamed Granite Rapids, has been pushed from 2023 to 2024. This delay and investment strategy led to a drop in Intel's stock as investors questioned the impact on profit margins and the capital-intensive nature of its new foundry business.

title: Timing: What Enterprises can learn from the Sports industry
summary: This article explores how the precision timing technologies used in the sports industry, such as RFID and high-speed cameras, can offer valuable lessons for enterprises. It highlights the evolution of timing technology in sports, from early photo-finishes to modern-day microsecond accuracy, emphasizing how these advancements build credibility and ensure fairness. The article then suggests practical applications of these technologies for businesses in various sectors, including toll collection, manufacturing, and event management, to improve operational speed, efficiency, and data collection.

title: AMD CPU Shipments in Japan Sink to 25% of Total, Intel Achieves 74%
summary: AMD's CPU market share in Japan fell to 25% in January, a three-year low, while Intel captured 74% of total sales. This decline follows a period in 2020 where AMD reached market share highs of 68-70%, buoyed by its Ryzen 5 series and Intel's 10nm production issues. The recent shift is attributed to Intel's recovery from manufacturing woes and the successful launch of its Alder Lake processors, which restored performance parity with AMD.

title: Like building the world's tallest tower every two years
summary: This article delves into the critical role of Intel Israel's Core Group in developing the most essential parts of Intel's processors: the cores. It explains the evolution of processor cores from single-core to multi-core designs, highlighting the challenges and innovations, including the creation of hybrid architectures with Performance and Efficiency Cores in the Alder Lake processor. The piece emphasizes the immense complexity and continuous innovation required in core development, likening it to building and constantly improving the world's tallest tower.

title: An Interview with Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger
summary: This article features an interview with Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger, discussing his IDM 2.0 strategy, the acquisition of Tower Semiconductor, and Intel's role in the semiconductor industry. Gelsinger explains why Intel is committed to manufacturing its own chips despite the success of fabless competitors, emphasizing the geopolitical importance of a Western technology provider and the benefits of integrating foundry operations. He also addresses concerns about potential overcapacity in the industry and the future of x86 architecture.

title: Behind Intel’s HPC Chip that Will Pierce the Exascale Barrier
summary: Intel has unveiled new details about its Ponte Vecchio processor, which will power the Aurora supercomputer, aiming to achieve exascale performance. The chip utilizes advanced 2D and 3D chiplet integration technologies, combining over 100 billion transistors across 47 pieces of silicon using various manufacturing processes, including TSMC's N5 and Intel 7. This heterogeneous integration and sophisticated thermal management are crucial for achieving the targeted 2 exaflops for the Aurora supercomputer.

title: ‘Bullish engulfing’ chart patterns abound after stock market’s big bounce
summary: Following Russia's invasion of Ukraine, the stock market saw a significant rebound, resulting in numerous "bullish engulfing" chart patterns. This pattern, a well-known candlestick formation, indicates a potential shift in momentum towards bulls and suggests that the recent downturn for many stocks and possibly the broader market may be over. The article highlights that such patterns suggest a reversal of preceding bearish trends.

title: Semiconductor sales to Russia banned, but that shouldn’t hurt Intel, AMD and other chip makers
summary: Semiconductor companies are banning sales to Russia in response to its invasion of Ukraine. Despite a broader market sell-off and sanctions against Russia, an analyst believes these bans will have little financial impact on major chip makers like Intel and AMD because Russia's semiconductor market is likely too small to significantly affect their revenue.

title: Intel Boosting Chipmaking With $5.4B Tower Buy
summary: Intel Corp. is set to acquire Tower Semiconductor Ltd. for $5.4 billion to expand its foundry business and meet chip demand. The acquisition aims to leverage Tower's expertise in manufacturing analog chips for various industries and scale Intel's foundry services globally. The deal, pending regulatory approval, is expected to enhance Intel's capacity in the growing foundry market.

title: AMD, Arm, Google, Intel, TSMC and others join Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) group
summary: Leading semiconductor companies including AMD, Arm, Google, Intel, Meta, Microsoft, Qualcomm, Samsung, and TSMC have formed the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) industry consortium. This group aims to establish an open chiplet ecosystem by standardizing die-to-die interconnects, with the UCIe 1.0 specification already ratified. This initiative leverages existing PCI Express and Compute Express Link standards to enable easier mixing and matching of chiplet components from multiple vendors for System-on-Chip (SoC) construction.

title: Leaders in Semiconductors, Packaging, IP Suppliers, Foundries, and Cloud Service Providers Join Forces to Standardize Chiplet Ecosystem
summary: A consortium of leading technology companies, including ASE, AMD, Arm, Google Cloud, Intel, Meta, Microsoft, Qualcomm, Samsung, and TSMC, has formed to standardize the chiplet ecosystem. They have ratified the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) 1.0 specification, an open industry standard for die-to-die interconnects, aiming to enable multi-vendor compatibility for System-on-Chip construction. The consortium is open for membership and plans to develop future generations of the UCIe technology.

title: Intel vPro platform for 12th Gen Core offers enhanced performance and security
summary: Intel has announced its latest Intel vPro platform, powered by 12th Gen Intel Core processors, which promises enhanced performance, security, manageability, and stability for businesses. The new vPro offerings include Intel vPro Enterprise for Windows, Intel vPro Essentials for small and medium-sized businesses, Intel vPro Enterprise for Chrome OS, and Intel vPro, An Evo Design. Key features include a hybrid architecture with Efficient-cores and Performance-cores, and advanced security measures like hardware-based ransomware detection and anomalous behavior detection.

title: Amid Ukraine fallout, crisis-hardened chipmakers move to adapt
summary: Semiconductor manufacturers are adapting to potential disruptions from the Russia-Ukraine conflict by stockpiling materials, diversifying suppliers, and leveraging previous lessons from geopolitical events like Russia's annexation of Crimea. While Russia itself is a small market for chips, the war's broader impact on electronics consumption and supply chain stability for key materials like neon gas is a concern, though the industry has shown resilience. The conflict highlights the need for strategic reshoring and decoupling from hostile states to build greater industry robustness against future crises.

title: Consortium formed to standardize chiplet ecosystem
summary: A consortium comprising major tech companies like Intel, AMD, Arm, Google Cloud, Microsoft, Qualcomm, Samsung, and TSMC has been formed to standardize the die-to-die interconnect and foster an open chiplet ecosystem. They have ratified the UCIe 1.0 specification, which provides a ubiquitous interconnect standard for package-level integration, enabling easy mixing and matching of chiplet components from multiple vendors for System-on-Chip construction. The consortium aims to address customer demand for customizable package-level integration and reduce development time and cost through open standards.

title: Will semiconductor plants really drain Arizona? That theory is overblown
summary: This article addresses concerns that new semiconductor plants in Arizona, like those from TSMC and Intel, will deplete the state's water resources amidst ongoing drought. The author argues that these concerns are "overblown" due to significant water recycling efforts by the companies. The article highlights that these plants, while large water users, employ advanced water reclamation technology and their economic benefits, including high-wage jobs and substantial tax revenue, make them a worthwhile investment for Arizona.

title: Intel’s Mobileye unit files for IPO at reported $50B+ valuation
summary: Intel's autonomous driving unit, Mobileye, has filed to go public with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, with reports suggesting a valuation of over $50 billion. Intel plans to retain a majority stake after the IPO, which is expected by mid-2022. This move reflects significant growth for Mobileye since its $15.3 billion acquisition by Intel in 2017, with its revenue, chip shipments, and headcount approximately tripling.

title: Maricopa Community Colleges and Intel to Launch New Semiconductor Manufacturing Workforce Development Initiative
summary: Maricopa Community Colleges and Intel are launching a new Semiconductor Manufacturing Quick Start program at Mesa Community College to address the growing employment needs of the semiconductor industry. This accelerated two-week program aims to train a diverse workforce, including women, veterans, and underrepresented minorities, for technician roles. Successful participants will receive certification, college credit, a tuition stipend, and interview opportunities with Intel for full-time positions.

title: Intel's $20 billion investment in Ohio promises thousands of jobs, but can the state and company deliver?
summary: Intel's $20 billion investment in Ohio, set to create 3,000 permanent jobs and 7,000 construction jobs, faces scrutiny regarding its long-term success. While the state offers over $2 billion in incentives, experts question Intel's ability to maintain its competitive edge and Ohio's capacity to sustain the promised economic benefits, drawing comparisons to past manufacturing ventures in the state. Intel plans to address this by investing in talent development and implementing a new strategy to regain leadership in chip manufacturing.

title: Industry Leaders Join Forces to Standardize Chiplet Ecosystem
summary: A consortium of industry leaders, including AMD, Arm, Google Cloud, Intel, Meta, Microsoft, Qualcomm, Samsung, TSMC, and ASE, has formed to standardize die-to-die interconnects and foster an open chiplet ecosystem. They have ratified the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) 1.0 specification, an open industry standard for package-level connectivity. This initiative aims to reduce development time and cost, promoting customizable and interoperable chiplet designs for future silicon innovations.

title: Oregon, passed over for Intel fabs, could get share of $10 billion chip research hub
summary: Oregon, despite missing out on recent Intel factory investments, is being considered for a portion of a $10 billion federal semiconductor research allocation. Both Intel and U.S. Sen. Ron Wyden believe the state, which hosts Intel's most advanced research, deserves a share of this funding. This initiative aims to bolster semiconductor research within the United States.

title: Why Nvidia sees a future in software and services: Recurring revenue
summary: Nvidia is shifting its business model to focus on software and services to generate recurring revenue, moving beyond its traditional hardware sales. CFO Colette Kress highlighted opportunities in autonomous vehicles, where Nvidia's software will be monetized over the life of the car, and the metaverse, envisioning annual revenues of $1,000 per creative user and per digital twin bot. This strategic pivot aims to create more predictable income streams, leveraging existing hardware with new software offerings and expanding into new markets.

title: What Intel's New Albany plans mean for a Dayton-area manufacturer
summary: A Dayton-area manufacturer specializing in silicon crystals and processing equipment, with plants in Springfield and western Ohio, is owned by one of Intel's suppliers that has already committed to expanding in the state. This article explores the implications of Intel's New Albany plans for this manufacturer.

title: Micron CEO says chip shortage improving, but could last into 2023
summary: Micron Technology CEO Sanjay Mehrotra stated that the semiconductor shortage, which has affected numerous industries, is improving but could extend into 2023. He highlighted the automotive sector as particularly impacted, with modern vehicles becoming "data centers on wheels" that require significant memory and storage. Mehrotra emphasized the need for a partnership between the U.S. government and the private sector to establish the U.S. as a leader in the chip industry, noting that Micron plans to invest over $150 billion globally in memory manufacturing, research, and development.

title: Top 10 Foundries Post Record 4Q21 Performance for 10th Consecutive Quarter at US$29.55B, Says TrendForce
summary: The top 10 foundries achieved a record-breaking US$29.55 billion in 4Q21 revenue, marking their 10th consecutive quarter of growth driven by limited production capacity and rising average selling prices. TSMC and Samsung continued to dominate, with Nexchip making a significant entry into the top 10 by surpassing DB Hitek. TrendForce anticipates continued growth in 1Q22, primarily fueled by ASP appreciation.

title: Intel commits $36 billion to making chips in Europe
summary: Intel announced plans to invest over 33 billion euros ($36 billion) to boost chipmaking in the European Union, aiming to reduce the bloc's reliance on external semiconductor sources. This includes building two new factories in Magdeburg, Germany, creating an R&D and design hub in France, and investing in other European countries like Ireland, Italy, Poland, and Spain. The investment is part of Intel's broader commitment to spend up to 80 billion euros in Europe over the next decade, aligning with the EU's new European Chips Act to strengthen its semiconductor sector.

title: Oregon’s largest private employer, Intel, announces plans to expand in Europe
summary: Intel, Oregon's largest private employer, has announced significant expansion plans in Europe, intending to invest nearly $19 billion in two new semiconductor factories in Germany and an additional $13 billion to expand its Irish facility. This European expansion is part of CEO Pat Gelsinger's strategy to aggressively increase manufacturing capacity globally, diversify the chip industry away from its concentration in Asia, and produce chips for both Intel and other companies. The company is seeking substantial government support for these projects, similar to its U.S. expansion plans for which it is lobbying for the CHIPS Act.

title: Extending Copper Interconnects To 2nm
summary: As transistor scaling reaches 3nm and beyond, novel strategies are being explored to extend copper interconnects to the 2nm node and reconfigure power delivery. These strategies include adopting buried power rails (BPRs) and backside power distribution (BPD), alongside optimizing copper damascene interconnects with low-resistance via processes, alternative liners, and fully aligned vias. Challenges such as edge-placement errors, electromigration, and the potential replacement of copper with ruthenium or molybdenum for certain levels are being addressed through innovative material and integration schemes to meet future performance, power, area, and cost goals.

title: Dow jumps 417 as stocks rally, oil hits $102
summary: U.S. stocks rallied following the Federal Reserve's first interest rate hike since 2018, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average rising over 400 points and crude prices rebounding above $100 per barrel. Key stock movers included Dollar General after boosting its dividend and Intel with a major investment announcement in Ohio, while economic data showed a mixed picture for housing and stable jobless claims.

title: Intel announces $50 million investment in Ohio higher education
summary: Intel is investing $50 million in Ohio higher education institutions, complemented by an additional $50 million from the U.S. National Science Foundation, to develop a skilled workforce for its new semiconductor factories in Ohio. This $100 million initiative over the next decade aims to establish robust semiconductor manufacturing education partnerships, creating new curriculums, training programs, and research opportunities. The investment is part of Intel's larger $20 billion plan to build two chip factories in Ohio, expected to generate thousands of high-tech and construction jobs.

title: Is It Finally Time to Start Buying Intel Stock?
summary: Intel, a pioneer in micro-processing, has seen its stock underperform competitors for years, but an ambitious makeover and new leadership may signal an attractive entry point for long-term investors. CEO Pat Gelsinger's plans for a massive $20 billion semiconductor plant in Ohio and other investments aim to restore Intel's market leadership. Despite investor skepticism reflected in its depressed stock price, Morningstar analysts see an appealing opportunity with a 27% discount to its fair value estimate and a 3.11% dividend yield.

title: גונן ברקן עוזב את GM כדי לעמוד בראש פיתוח המכ"ם של ארבה
summary: גונן ברקן, ראש צוות פיתוח המכ"ם של ג'נרל מוטורס (GM), מונה לסמנכ"ל המו"פ של חברת ארבה רובוטיקס. בתפקידו החדש, ברקן יוביל את האסטרטגיה הטכנולוגית ופיתוח מוצרי המכ"ם של ארבה המיועדים לשוק הרכב, כולל שבבי מכ"ם 4D המייתרים, לטענת החברה, את הצורך בחיישני LiDAR. ארבה, שפיתחה שבבי מכ"ם מבוססי FMCW, החלה במכירות קטנות ב-2020 ונמצאת בשלבי הערכה מתקדמים עם יצרני רכב וחברות לפיתוח רכבים אוטונומיים.

title: NVIDIA Introduces Grace CPU Superchip
summary: NVIDIA has unveiled its Grace CPU Superchip, an Arm Neoverse-based discrete data center CPU engineered for AI infrastructure and high-performance computing. This new chip boasts 144 high-performance cores and 1 terabyte/second memory, offering double the performance and energy-efficiency compared to current leading server chips. It is designed to work seamlessly with NVIDIA's existing software stacks and is expected to be available in the first half of 2023.

title: ASML is the only company making the $200 million machines needed to print every advanced microchip. Here's an inside look
summary: ASML is the sole manufacturer of extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography machines, which are essential for producing advanced microchips. These $200 million machines are critical for every advanced processor chip used today, leading to ASML's monopoly in a vital part of the semiconductor industry. The company's technology is in high demand, contributing to its significant market value and its ongoing efforts to enable the production of more chips despite global supply chain challenges.

title: Nvidia CEO says interested in exploring chip manufacturing with Intel
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang expressed interest in exploring Intel's chip manufacturing services. Intel, which aims to expand its foundry business, confirmed ongoing discussions with Nvidia. This potential collaboration could be significant for Intel's efforts to attract external chip design companies to its factories.

title: Possible Nvidia, Intel GPU Alliance Could Frustrate Rivals AMD, TSMC
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is in talks with Intel to use its foundries for GPU production, a move that could significantly impact competitors AMD and TSMC. This potential alliance would diversify Nvidia's supply base and challenge TSMC's dominance in the foundry market, while also putting pressure on AMD's supercomputing ambitions. Both Intel and Nvidia are interested in the collaboration, with Intel aiming to become the top GPU foundry by 2025.

title: Nvidia considers teaming up with Intel on chip manufacturing
summary: Nvidia is in discussions with Intel about potentially using Intel as a foundry for manufacturing its chips. This consideration comes amid a global semiconductor chip shortage and Intel's efforts to become a major U.S. foundry supplier, despite the challenges of aligning technologies and business models between the companies. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang expressed openness to the idea, noting existing close collaboration between the firms.

title: The Top 5: Biggest Automotive Semiconductor Manufacturers
summary: The automotive semiconductor market, valued at $43.6 billion in 2021, is projected to exceed $115 billion by 2030, driven by the growth of electric vehicles and autonomous technologies. This article identifies the top five automotive semiconductor manufacturers, excluding TSMC which dominates the foundry market, and details their market share, financial performance, and strategic investments. The leading companies are Infineon Technologies AG, NXP Semiconductors N.V., Renesas Electronics Corp., Texas Instruments, Inc., and STMicroelectronics N.V.

title: MRAM Market Size to Grow by USD 952.71 Million |Evolving Opportunities with Honeywell International Inc. and Fujitsu Ltd. | Technavio
summary: The MRAM market is projected to grow by USD 952.71 million between 2019 and 2024, with an accelerating CAGR of 39% during the forecast period. This growth is expected to be driven by evolving opportunities with key players like Honeywell International Inc. and Fujitsu Ltd. North America is anticipated to contribute 32% of the market's growth, with STT MRAM holding the largest market share due to its low power consumption and fast read/write times.

title: Gladstone Commercial Corporation Executes 15-Year Lease Renewal at Office Property in New Albany, Ohio
summary: Gladstone Commercial Corporation announced a 15-year lease renewal with CVG Management Corp. for its 50,499 square foot office building in New Albany, Ohio. This renewal extends CVG's occupancy until March 31, 2037, and is expected to increase Gladstone Commercial's AFFO and FFO. The property is strategically located near Intel's upcoming $20+ billion chip manufacturing campus.

title: Intel Gave CEO Pay Package Worth Nearly $180 Million Amid Turnaround Effort
summary: Intel's CEO, Pat Gelsinger, received a compensation package worth nearly $180 million for 2021, much of which is tied to the company's stock performance. This substantial pay aims to incentivize a significant turnaround at the chip giant. The package placed him as the second-highest-paid CEO among S&P 500 companies for the year.

title: Intel CEO earned 1,711 times more than average company worker in 2021
summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger earned $178.6 million in 2021, which is 1,711 times more than the average Intel employee's salary and 698% higher than his predecessor's pay. Most of Gelsinger's compensation was in stock awards, including one-time new-hire equity. This disclosure comes as Intel asks shareholders to approve executive compensation at their upcoming annual meeting, while executive pay across the U.S. continues to rise.

title: Intel to Acquire Granulate
summary: Intel Corporation announced an agreement to acquire Granulate Cloud Solutions Ltd., an Israel-based developer of real-time continuous optimization software. This acquisition aims to help cloud and data center customers enhance compute workload performance and decrease infrastructure and cloud costs. The transaction is expected to close in Q2 2022, with Granulate's 120 employees joining Intel's Datacenter and AI business unit.

title: Internet of Things Market Size [2022-2029] Worth USD 2465.26 Billion | Exhibiting a CAGR of 26.4%
summary: The global Internet of Things (IoT) market is projected to reach USD 2,465.26 billion by 2029, growing at a CAGR of 26.4% from USD 384.70 billion in 2021. This growth is driven by the increasing penetration of smart cities, deployment of vehicle solutions, and strong demand from the healthcare sector. Key players like Amazon Web Services, Intel, and Microsoft are focusing on product rollouts and technological advancements.

title: Corsair One i300 review
summary: The Corsair One i300 is a compact and stylish small form factor gaming PC that offers extremely high-end performance with components like the Intel Core i9-12900K and Nvidia GeForce RTX 3080 Ti. While it excels in performance for 4K gaming and also serves as a powerful workstation for content creators, its major drawbacks are its exorbitant price tag ($4,999 / £4,399) and limited upgradeability due to its small chassis. The review concludes that while it's a top-tier machine for those who can afford it and don't plan on tinkering, it's overkill and too expensive for most casual gamers.

title: New IBM Z mainframe blunts quantum cyber attacks
summary: IBM has introduced its latest Z mainframe system, featuring a new Telum processor with a built-in AI accelerator. This system is designed to enhance AI inferencing capabilities for financial transactions, enabling real-time fraud analysis. Additionally, it incorporates advanced security measures, including quantum-safe cryptography, to defend against cyberattacks from quantum computers.

title: Technology ETF
summary: VanEck offers a suite of Technology ETFs that allow investors to gain exposure to disruptive and transformative megatrends such as semiconductors, spatial technology, hydrogen, esports, and cryptocurrency. These ETFs provide diversified exposure, potential for future winners through rules-based selection, regular rebalancing, and lower fees compared to actively managed funds. The article also details various risks associated with investing in Technology ETFs, including industry concentration, market risk, and liquidity risks.

title: Intel Becomes Latest Western Tech Firm to Suspend Business in Russia
summary: US chipmaker Intel Corp has announced the suspension of its business operations in Russia, joining other Western companies exiting the country following the invasion of Ukraine. This move follows Intel's earlier decision to halt shipments to Russia and Belarus, as the company states it is implementing business continuity measures and condemns Russia's actions. Other major tech firms like IBM have also suspended shipments, as Ukraine urged US cloud-computing and software companies to cut ties with Russia.

title: Intel, Micron, and Analog Devices Join MITRE Engenuity’s Semiconductor Alliance
summary: Intel, Micron, and Analog Devices have partnered with MITRE Engenuity to form a Semiconductor Alliance. This initiative aims to accelerate semiconductor research, development, and prototyping in the U.S. to strengthen the domestic industry, foster advanced manufacturing, and protect intellectual property amidst global competition. The Alliance seeks a "whole-of-nation approach" to ensure American innovation leads to American growth in the semiconductor sector.

title: Leveraging Intel Technology as a Force for Good
summary: Intel's RISE Technology Initiative supported 63 projects in the past year, investing $9.5 million to leverage Intel technology, employee talent, and partnerships for global good. These projects address accessibility, economic recovery, education, health and life sciences, social equity and human rights, and sustainability and climate. The initiative demonstrates how technology can be a force for positive change across diverse communities worldwide.

title: Kathleen Gallagher: Could Foxconn be stalling development to dodge statute of limitations on fraud?
summary: The article discusses whether Foxconn is deliberately stalling development of its Wisconsin project to avoid a potential fraud lawsuit and its upcoming $30 million annual tax bill. The author highlights Foxconn's failure to initiate meaningful work, its apparent resistance to development opportunities by others, and the significant financial burden this situation poses for local taxpayers and governments.

title: Which is the bigger company: AMD, Nvidia, or Intel?
summary: This article analyzes Intel, AMD, and Nvidia to determine which is the "bigger" company, looking beyond market capitalization to factors like employee count, revenue, product diversity, and market share. While Nvidia leads in market cap, sustained analysis reveals Intel to be the largest overall due to its revenue and employee numbers, despite AMD and Nvidia's significant recent growth. The article also touches on the unique advantage Intel holds with its chip manufacturing capabilities.

title: DeFiChain Adds New dTokens Corresponding to Disney, Intel, MicroStrategy, and iShares MSCI China ETF
summary: DeFiChain has announced the addition of four new decentralized tokens, or dTokens, following a community vote. These new dTokens correspond to Walt Disney Co ($dDIS), iShares MSCI China ETF ($dMCHI), MicroStrategy Incorporated ($dMSTR), and Intel Corporation ($dINTC), allowing users to gain price exposure to these traditional assets without ownership. DeFiChain, a blockchain built on the Bitcoin network, aims to provide decentralized financial applications and services, expanding its dToken universe to offer an alternative to traditional financial brokers.

title: Chip Startup Ampere, Founded By Ex-Intel Exec, Files IPO
summary: Ampere, a chip startup founded by former Intel president Renée James, confidentially filed for an initial public offering (IPO). The company, which already has significant backing from Oracle and uses ARM designs, aims to grab more data center market share from Intel and AMD. Industry experts anticipate continued growth in specialized chip designs for data centers, particularly ARM processors.

title: The end of one-chip wonders: Why Nvidia, Intel and AMD’s valuations have experienced massive upheaval
summary: This article discusses the dramatic shift in market capitalization within the semiconductor industry over the past five years. Once dominant Intel has been surpassed by Nvidia, which is now valued as much as Intel and AMD combined, reflecting fundamental changes in how technology is used and specialized solutions. This upheaval highlights a significant transformation in the industry's landscape.

title: The end of one-chip wonders: Why Nvidia, Intel and AMD’s valuations have experienced massive upheaval
summary: The article discusses the dramatic shift in valuations among semiconductor companies, specifically Nvidia, Intel, and AMD. Five years ago, Intel's market capitalization surpassed Nvidia and AMD combined, but now Nvidia alone is worth more than the other two combined, reflecting significant changes in the semiconductor industry. This upheaval is attributed to evolving technology consumption patterns and companies specializing in new areas.

title: Intel Commits to Net-Zero Greenhouse Gas Emissions in its Global Operations by 2040
summary: Intel Corporation has announced its commitment to achieve net-zero greenhouse gas emissions in its global operations by 2040. This plan includes interim goals for 2030, such as using 100% renewable electricity, investing $300 million in energy conservation, and building new factories to LEED standards. Additionally, Intel aims to reduce value chain emissions by partnering with suppliers and customers, and enhancing the energy efficiency of its products.

title: Intel development prompts St. Albans Township, Alexandria to explore merger
summary: Following New Albany's annexation of land for Intel's new facility, officials in St. Albans Township and the Village of Alexandria are considering a merger to protect their area from further annexation. St. Albans Trustee Bruce Lane and Alexandria Mayor Jim Jasper are spearheading the effort, but face opposition from other officials and residents concerned about tax increases and loss of funding. A similar merger occurred in 1997 in Licking County to prevent annexation by larger cities.

title: PAX East 2022 to Include Newegg Gaming PCs and Giant Robot Costume
summary: Newegg will be exhibiting at PAX East 2022 in the Intel booth #18007, showcasing ABS gaming PCs and top brand laptops powered by 12th Gen Intel Core processors. A highlight of their presence will be "Core Bot," an 8-foot, 8-inch fully lit robot costume, along with PAX East-exclusive offers on gaming systems. Attendees can also see a high-end PC built by ENIAC and a beverage-mixing PC called The Hydrator.

title: The Chips That Rebooted the Mac
summary: Apple successfully revitalized its Mac computer line by designing its own M1 chips, replacing Intel processors. This strategic move, led by Johny Srouji, improved Mac performance, energy efficiency, and profitability, and helped Apple navigate supply-chain disruptions. The in-house chip design capability also positions Apple for future products like cars and extended-reality headsets.

title: HP Spectre x360 14 Vs. Dell XPS 13: 2-in-1 Laptops Face Off
summary: This article compares the HP Spectre x360 14 and Dell XPS 13, two prominent 2-in-1 laptops. It evaluates them across several categories including dimensions, display, ports, memory, and performance specifications. The HP Spectre x360 14 is ultimately declared the winner due to its superior display, specs, and features, despite its higher price point.

title: Boeing, Boston Fed, Intel and Lucid join the 2022 STEM Reentry Task Force – All Together
summary: The Society of Women Engineers (SWE) and iRelaunch announced that Boeing, the Federal Reserve Bank of Boston, Intel, and Lucid Motors have joined the 2022 STEM Reentry Task Force. This initiative aims to help employers diversify their mid- to senior-level workforce by hiring professionals, predominantly women, who have taken career breaks. The Task Force seeks to achieve this by expanding career reentry programs for these returning professionals.

title: Synopsys Introduces Industry's Highest Performance Neural Processor IP
summary: Synopsys has launched its DesignWare ARC NPX6 and NPX6FS NPU IP, along with the MetaWare MX Development Toolkit, to meet the increasing performance demands of AI systems-on-chip. This new NPU IP delivers the industry's highest performance, offering up to 3,500 TOPS for AI applications and support for complex neural network models, while also addressing functional safety requirements for automotive use. The comprehensive software environment aims to accelerate application development and ensure compliance with standards like ISO 26262.

title: Intel CEO Patrick Gelsinger will deliver spring 2022 commencement address
summary: Intel CEO Patrick Gelsinger will deliver the commencement address at The Ohio State University's spring 2022 ceremony on May 8, where over 12,000 degrees and certificates are expected to be issued. Gelsinger, a visionary engineer and leader at Intel, has committed to investing in Ohio's chip manufacturing and educational institutions. The ceremony will also feature remarks from other distinguished individuals including UMBC President Dr. Freeman A. Hrabowski III, Sen. Robert J. Portman, and statistician Dr. Grace Wahba, all of whom will receive honorary degrees.

title: Better Buy: Intel vs. Micron Technology
summary: This article compares Intel and Micron Technology, two semiconductor stocks, evaluating which is a better buy. While Intel is working on a turnaround with an aggressive product roadmap and expected revenue growth by 2026, Micron is already achieving strong revenue and earnings growth due to high demand for memory chips. The author concludes that Micron is the better choice for investors seeking a value stock, given its current financial performance and attractive valuation.

title: Latest Oracle Exadata servers ascend to the cloud
summary: Oracle has announced the availability of its latest Exadata Cloud Infrastructure X9M appliance in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, marking the rollout of its newest generation of Exadata processors. This new service, based on AMD microprocessors, offers significant improvements in performance, throughput, and capacity without increased costs, allowing organizations to consolidate various database workloads. Oracle claims superior performance compared to AWS RDS and Azure SQL, with the service designed for scalable deployment both on-premises and across Oracle's expanding global cloud footprint.

title: Arrow Electronics Launches NTU-Arrow Invent Lab to Support Tech Startups in Singapore
summary: Arrow Electronics has launched the NTU-Arrow Invent Lab in collaboration with Nanyang Technological University, Singapore, to support local tech startups. The lab provides free consultation, access to world-class engineering and supply chain capabilities, and state-of-the-art equipment to accelerate deep-tech energy innovation. This initiative aims to reduce product development time and costs for startups while fostering Singapore's innovation ecosystem through knowledge sharing and expert guidance.

title: Self-driving Cars, Rare Earth Metals, ESG Activism: New ETFs On Syfe
summary: Syfe has introduced three new ETFs to its platform: IDRV (iShares Self-Driving EV and Tech ETF), REMX (VanEck Rare Earth/Strategic Metals ETF), and VOTE (Engine No. 1 Transform 500 ETF). These ETFs offer investors exposure to rapidly growing sectors such as electric vehicles and autonomous driving, critical rare earth metals, and shareholder activism focused on ESG initiatives. The article details the holdings, market outlook, and expense ratios for each of these new investment options.

title: Chipmaker Texas Instruments expects revenue hit as China lockdowns dent demand
summary: Texas Instruments (TXN.O) forecast second-quarter revenue below Wall Street estimates due to reduced demand caused by COVID-19 lockdowns in China, sending its shares down 7% in extended trading. The company noted that the impact was primarily due to disruptions at its customers' factories. Despite this, Texas Instruments reported a 14% revenue increase to $4.91 billion for the first quarter, exceeding analyst expectations.

title: Ford Motor loses $3.1 billion due to chip shortage and Rivian
summary: Ford Motor reported a first-quarter loss of $3.1 billion, a significant drop from the $3.2 billion profit in the same period last year. This loss is attributed to the ongoing semiconductor chip shortage, which disproportionately affected the availability of pickups and SUVs in North America, and a substantial decrease in the value of its investment in electric-vehicle startup Rivian. Despite the losses, Ford's CFO noted a strong demand for new products and a pretax profit of $2.3 billion, although supply chain issues continue to constrain production.

title: Amazon, Intel shares fall on results, forecasts
summary: Shares of Amazon (AMZN) and Intel (INTC) both experienced significant declines in premarket trading after reporting disappointing quarterly results and issuing forecasts below Wall Street expectations. Amazon posted its first quarterly loss since 2015, largely due to a write-down of its investment in Rivian Automotive and increased costs from inflation, while Intel's forecast was impacted by weak PC demand and supply-chain uncertainties. Both companies anticipate further challenges in the coming quarter.

title: Intel CEO now expects chip shortage to last into 2024
summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger now anticipates the global semiconductor shortage to extend into 2024, a revision from earlier estimates of 2023. This revised timeline is primarily attributed to a scarcity of manufacturing equipment needed to expand capacity. The announcement follows Intel's weaker-than-expected guidance for its fiscal second quarter, leading to a more than 6% drop in its shares.

title: Chip consortium ISMC plans $3-billion semiconductor plant in Karnataka
summary: The chip consortium ISMC plans to invest $3 billion in setting up a semiconductor manufacturing plant in Karnataka, India. ISMC is a joint venture between Abu Dhabi-based Next Orbit Ventures and Israel's Tower Semiconductor, which is currently being acquired by Intel Corp. Officials confirmed the agreement on Sunday, May 1, 2022.

title: Chip foundry group ISMC plans $3b semiconductor plant in India
summary: ISMC Digital, an international semiconductor consortium, plans to invest $3 billion in a new manufacturing plant in Karnataka, India, focusing on 65nm analog semiconductors. This venture, involving Next Orbit Ventures and Tower Semiconductor (which Intel plans to acquire), seeks to leverage India's $10 billion chip subsidy program and aims to create 1,500 direct and 10,000 indirect jobs. The initiative is part of India's broader strategy to become an electronics manufacturing hub, including developing RISC-V based chips and boosting semiconductor R&D.

title: ISMC inks MoU to set up $3 billion chip plant in Karnataka
summary: International semiconductor consortium ISMC Analog Fab has signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the Karnataka government to invest $3 billion (Rs 22,900 crore) in a 65nm analog semiconductor fabrication plant. The project, a joint venture between Abu Dhabi-based NextOrbit Ventures and Tower Semiconductor, is expected to create over 1,500 direct and 10,000 indirect employment opportunities. ISMC is looking for 150 acres in Mysuru's Kochanahalli industrial area for the plant, which could be built in four years once government incentives are approved under the India Semiconductor Mission.

title: Israel's Tower will have a 10-15% stake in venture: Next Orbit's Ajay Jalan
summary: Ajay Jalan, ISMC Director and Next Orbit Founder and Managing Partner, states that Israel's Tower Semiconductor will hold a 10-15% equity stake in their joint venture to establish a $3 billion chip-making plant in Mysuru. He elaborates on the funding and technology roadmap for the three-phase project, which also includes plans for a digital chip plant and a memory fab plant. Jalan also discusses the reasons for choosing Mysuru for the facility and the market's potential growth.

title: AMD Confirms Zen 4 Dragon Range, Phoenix APUs for 2023
summary: AMD has released an updated roadmap confirming Zen 4 Dragon Range and Phoenix mobile APUs for 2023. Dragon Range is designed for enthusiast gaming laptops, while Phoenix targets thin and light gaming devices, both supporting PCIe 5.0 and either DDR5 or LPDDR5 memory. The company also teased Ryzen 7000 (Raphael) desktop processors, expected in late 2022, which will bring Zen 4 to the mainstream market on TSMC's 5nm process node and the new AM5 platform with DDR5-only support.

title: Intel to add hundreds of jobs in Atlanta starting at six-figure salaries
summary: Intel plans to create over 100 high-paying jobs in Atlanta, with average salaries exceeding $100,000, for a new software innovation division. Midtown is a strong contender for the location of this new outpost. This move signifies Intel's expansion into the Atlanta tech scene.

title: Intel Corporation pledges to emit zero emissions by 2040
summary: Intel Corporation has announced a commitment to achieve net-zero greenhouse gas emissions across its global operations by 2040. The company plans to reduce its carbon footprint through various initiatives, including using 100% renewable electricity, investing in energy conservation, and developing more energy-efficient products and platforms. Intel will also collaborate with customers and engage in cross-industry research for greener technologies.

title: HP debuts 2 new Chromebooks for business in the Elite c600 series with AMD and Intel on board
summary: HP has introduced two new Chromebooks, the Elite c640 G3 and Elite c645 G2, aimed at business users but also available to consumers. These devices emphasize security and productivity for hybrid work environments, with the c645 G2 featuring AMD Ryzen 5000 C-Series processors and the c640 G3 utilizing Intel's 12th-gen processors, along with enhanced features like improved screens, advanced security options, and AI capabilities for audio and visual. Both models are designed to offer a robust and affordable option within HP's high-end Chromebook portfolio, starting in the $500 range.

title: Intel Nabs Former AMD Exec Matt Poirier To Lead M&A Quest
summary: Intel has appointed former AMD executive Matt Poirier as senior vice president of corporate development, effective May 30, to lead its mergers and acquisitions strategy. Poirier, who previously focused on M&A at AMD and Xilinx, will report directly to CEO Pat Gelsinger. This move comes as Intel actively pursues acquisitions, including recent deals for Tower Semiconductor and Siru Innovations, and highlights the growing importance of M&A for Intel's expansion in various technology sectors.

title: China Orders Government, State Firms to Dump Foreign PCs
summary: China has mandated that central government agencies and state-backed corporations replace foreign-branded personal computers with domestic alternatives within two years. This move aims to eliminate key overseas technology from sensitive government operations and is expected to replace at least 50 million PCs. Staff were instructed to swap foreign PCs for local ones running domestically developed operating software after the May holiday.

title: Bausch + Lomb valued at $6.5 billion after stock ticks up in NYSE debut
summary: Bausch + Lomb made its U.S. market debut with a valuation of $6.48 billion, as its shares opened nearly 3% above an offer price that was below its targeted range. The modest reception comes amidst a challenging year for U.S. capital markets, influenced by the Ukraine conflict and a hawkish Federal Reserve outlook. The eye-care company raised $630 million for its parent company, Bausch Health Companies Inc, but none of the proceeds went to Bausch + Lomb itself.

title: Lockheed CEO Says Supply Chain Must ‘Crank Up’ to Build Javelins
summary: Lockheed Martin is working to nearly double its annual production of Javelin missiles to 4,000, a process that will require a significant increase in its supply chain's capacity and could take up to two years. CEO Jim Taiclet emphasized the importance of the Bipartisan Innovation Act to boost domestic microprocessor manufacturing, as each Javelin missile requires approximately 250 microprocessors. This production ramp-up is critical as the U.S. and its allies are depleting stockpiles due to the high demand for Javelins in Ukraine, where they have been highly effective against Russian armor.

title: Vietnam 'darling' of global electronics giants
summary: Vietnam has emerged as a leading hub for electronics manufacturing in Southeast Asia, attracting significant investments from global giants like Samsung, LG, Intel, and Apple suppliers such as Foxconn, Pegatron, Goertek, and Luxshare. The country's electronics exports have become a key earner, with major manufacturers expanding operations and establishing R&D centers, contributing to substantial economic growth and technological advancement.

title: Intel Expands Atlanta Presence Across Software Development Team
summary: Intel is significantly expanding its software development team in Atlanta, planning to add over 100 new local jobs with six-figure salaries as part of a larger goal to create 500 jobs by 2024. This move reinforces Atlanta's growing reputation as a tech hub, alongside other major companies like Cash App, Nike, and Microsoft also establishing or expanding their presence in the city. The new roles will focus on areas like AI, cloud, and security, utilizing Atlanta's diverse technology community and talent base.

title: Intel launches new AI chips, challenging Nvidia's market
summary: Intel Corp launched new AI chips, Gaudi2 and Greco, to challenge Nvidia's dominance in the AI chip market. The company is focusing on developing its open-standard software platform to differentiate itself and aims to capture a significant share of the rapidly growing AI chip market, estimated to reach $50 billion in the next five years.

title: Intel Vision 2022: AI Gaudi2 Chip, Accenture Collaboration And More Products Unveiled
summary: Intel announced a range of new products and collaborations at its Intel Vision 2022 event, including the AI Gaudi2 chip, 4th generation Xeon Scalable processors, and a collaboration with Accenture on Project Apollo. The announcements also highlighted new IPU and GPU solutions, and the expansion of its 12th Gen Core HX processors for hybrid work laptops, emphasizing performance and flexibility for professional users. These introductions aim to accelerate digital transformations and enhance AI accessibility and infrastructure compute capabilities.

title: Intel Introduces Project Amber for Cloud-to-Edge and On-Premises Trust Assurance
summary: Intel announced Project Amber, a new service designed to provide remote verification of trustworthiness in cloud, edge, and on-premises environments, addressing the growing need for enhanced security and confidential computing. The company also detailed its efforts in securing AI deployments through partnerships and outlined a strategy for quantum-resistant cryptography by 2030, beginning with its 3rd Generation Intel Xeon Scalable platform. These initiatives aim to build trust and security for current and future technology landscapes.

title: Intel engineer, running for Congress, mounts union drive at Oregon chip factory
summary: An Intel engineer and congressional candidate, Matt West, is leading a unionization effort for hundreds of his colleagues at Intel's Hillsboro semiconductor factories in Oregon. The drive aims to address issues like long hours, pay disparities, and job security, particularly for engineers working on temporary visas.
