This initiative comes amidst a broader resurgence of organized labor in the US and during Intel's push for increased domestic chip manufacturing.

title: Corporate Responsibility Report Letter from Intel's CEO
summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger outlines the company's progress and future commitments in its Corporate Responsibility Report. Key achievements include investments in conservation, accelerating diversity and inclusion efforts, evolving the Pandemic Response Technology Initiative into the Intel RISE Technology Initiative, and launching the AI Incubator Network. Looking ahead, Intel aims for net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2040 and a $100 million investment in semiconductor manufacturing education and research.

title: Licking County officials impressed with Intel's efforts to be a good neighbor in Arizona
summary: Licking County officials visited Intel's Arizona facilities and were impressed by the company's efforts to be a responsible and collaborative neighbor. The visit alleviated some concerns about Intel's upcoming $20 billion facility in Licking County, Ohio. Local representatives noted Intel's positive community engagement in Arizona, including addressing resident concerns and partnering with local entities.

title: Micron Teases 232-Layer NAND For Next-Gen Storage, Lays Out Roadmap To 500 Layers
summary: Micron announced its development of 232-layer NAND flash memory, aiming for production ramp-up by the end of the year. This "world's most advanced" NAND solution, utilizing double-stacked 3D memory cells and CMOS under array technology, is expected to enhance density, power efficiency, and bandwidth for next-generation SSDs. Micron also revealed a roadmap to eventually reach 500-layer NAND and beyond, with a focus on extending its quad-level cell leadership.

title: Intel shareholders reject executive pay
summary: Intel shareholders have rejected the company's executive compensation package in an advisory vote. While not immediately impacting executive pay, the decision signals investor scrutiny on CEO Pat Gelsinger's turnaround plan and follows a trend of shareholders voting against executive compensation, given Gelsinger's substantial 2021 pay package and Intel's share performance. Intel stated that it takes stockholder feedback seriously and is working to address concerns regarding compensation transparency and linking pay to performance.

title: Intel shareholders reject compensation packages for top executives
summary: Intel Corp shareholders have rejected compensation packages for top executives, including CEO Pat Gelsinger's potential $178.6 million payout. Although the vote is non-binding, Intel stated it takes investor feedback seriously and plans to address their concerns, acknowledging "clearly more work to do" despite linking pay to performance. This comes as Gelsinger's 2021 earnings were over 1,700 times that of a typical Intel employee, and the company recently forecasted lower-than-expected revenue and profit for Q2.

title: Oracle really does owe HPE $3b after Supreme Court snub
summary: The US Supreme Court has rejected Oracle's appeal to overturn a $3 billion damages payout to HPE, solidifying the IT giant's obligation. This decade-long legal battle stemmed from Oracle's refusal to support HP's Itanium-based systems, which HP argued violated a contractual agreement between the two companies. Oracle had claimed it was unfairly roped into long-term support despite Intel's waning commitment to Itanium, and also argued that part of the damages award violated its First Amendment rights.

title: バレンス、自動運転向けカメラの高速データ転送を実現するMIPI A-PHY対応製品「VA7000」
summary: イスラエルの半導体メーカーValens Semiconductorは、自動運転やADAS用のカメラ向けにMIPI A-PHY準拠チップセット「VA7000ファミリ」のサンプル出荷を開始しました。この新製品は、MIPI CSI-2では対応していなかった自動車グレードの機能安全を実現し、高速、低遅延、高信頼性でのデータ転送を可能にします。Valensはすでにメルセデス・ベンツに製品供給実績があり、Mobileyeやソニー、デンソーなどからも歓迎のコメントを得ています。

title: A union of Intel employees?
summary: Intel engineer Matt West is publicly advocating for a union for lithography engineers at Intel's chip factories, citing grueling hours and exhaustion as primary motivators. While west aims to join CODE (Campaign to Organize Digital Employees) and seeks Intel's neutrality, current efforts face challenges as coworkers fear retaliation and are hesitant to publicly support the union drive.

title: Intel, AMD Ban Sees Russia Turn To Chinese Chips
summary: Following the ban on chip shipments from Intel and AMD due to the Ukraine invasion, Russian motherboard manufacturers are now using Chinese microprocessors. Russian firm Dannie introduced a new motherboard featuring Zhaoxin's KaiXian KX-6640MA processor, which utilizes the x86 microarchitecture. This shift highlights Russia's limited options and its reliance on Chinese technology, despite the Chinese chips often being based on older manufacturing processes compared to the latest from Intel and AMD.

title: HP Powers Today’s Hustle to Create Without Limitation
summary: HP has launched new Spectre and Envy laptops designed for today's hybrid world and the growing creator economy. These devices emphasize seamless collaboration and powerful performance, featuring high-resolution cameras, AI-based audio/visual enhancements, and intelligent power management. The lineup aims to offer diverse options for creators, from enhanced displays to cross-device compatibility via HP Palette, all while incorporating sustainable materials.

title: Panel addresses annexation, growth and public safety at Johnstown Intel forum
summary: A Johnstown community forum addressed resident concerns about Intel's $20 billion computer chip facility. Officials discussed how the development would impact annexation, traffic, public safety, and local services like the fire department. The panel emphasized providing accurate information and managing growth responsibly, projecting thousands of new jobs and a significant economic impact for the region.

title: TSMC to begin R&D for 1.4nm chip technology
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) is reportedly intensifying its research and development efforts for sub-2 nanometer chip manufacturing processes, specifically targeting 1.4nm technology. The company plans to reassign its 3nm R&D team to this new endeavor starting in June. Commercial products utilizing TSMC's 1.4nm process could emerge by 2028, following the company's historical three-year cadence for new node introductions.

title: Pinterest, Inc. Class A Common Stock (NYSE:PINS) Stock Quote
summary: This article provides a stock quote and financial details for Pinterest, Inc. Class A Common Stock (NYSE: PINS). It includes current price, volume, trading ranges, and a list of recent news headlines related to Pinterest and the broader tech market. The article also features a FAQ section answering common questions about Pinterest's public trading status and stock information.

title: Texas Instruments Inc. stock rises Wednesday, outperforms market
summary: Texas Instruments Inc. (TXN) stock increased by 1.28% to $170.01 on Wednesday, outperforming the broader market indexes like the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average. The company's stock closed $32.25 below its 52-week high, while competitors like NVIDIA Corp. and Qualcomm Inc. also saw gains. Trading volume for Texas Instruments was 4.7 million, which was below its 50-day average.

title: Qualcomm maintains multiple sourcing strategy
summary: Qualcomm Inc. has stated it will maintain a multi-sourcing strategy for its chips, including advanced chips from two major suppliers, to ensure a stable supply amidst the pandemic and a slowing global economy. The company anticipates continued strong growth in 5G penetration and expects Wi-Fi 7 technology adoption to rapidly increase, with end products equipped with these chips by the end of this year.

title: Next-Generation Distributed Static Timing Analysis On The Cloud
summary: The article discusses the increasing challenge of chip complexity on Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tools, especially Static Timing Analysis (STA). It highlights that parallelism, particularly distributed STA in the cloud, is the only scalable solution to accelerate analysis and sign-off processes. The Synopsys PrimeTime HyperGrid technology is presented as a solution that provides accurate, distributed STA on the cloud, achieving up to 10x speedup and memory reduction through automatic partitioning.

title: Intel selects Ohio-based team to lead early excavation work for new chip factories
summary: Intel has chosen four construction companies, led by Gilbane Building Company, to begin early excavation work for its two new chip factories in Licking County, Ohio. This $20 billion project, the largest private-sector investment in Ohio's history, is expected to create 7,000 construction jobs, with production slated to begin in late 2025. The selected team includes Ohio-based firms, emphasizing local economic growth.

title: Purdue launches nation’s first comprehensive Semiconductor Degrees Program
summary: Purdue University has launched the nation's first comprehensive Semiconductor Degrees Program (SDP) to address the critical need for 50,000 trained semiconductor engineers in the U.S. within the next five years. This innovative program offers a multidisciplinary curriculum across various credentials, including master's degrees, certificates, and minors, with flexible online and residential options. The initiative is strongly supported by industry leaders and government efforts like the CHIPS Act, aiming to reassert American leadership in the semiconductor industry.

title: Intel's local site manager committed to being good neighbor, job creator in Licking County
summary: Jim Evers, Intel's site manager for the new computer chip manufacturing facility in Licking County, acknowledges community concerns about the company's arrival but pledges to be a good neighbor and mitigate negative impacts. Intel plans to create 3,000 jobs directly, along with thousands more in construction and indirectly, with production slated to begin in 2025. Evers highlights Intel's track record in Arizona and its commitment to community involvement and employee development to address workforce challenges.

title: JPMorgan CEO Dimon calls for raising minimum wage, more education to address racial gaps
summary: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon advocates for raising the minimum wage, expanding education, and making mortgages more affordable to address racial gaps that cost the Columbus economy $10 billion annually. He also discusses the current economic outlook, noting a strong economy despite "storm clouds" like inflation, and emphasizes the importance of local initiatives and returning semiconductor production to the U.S.

title: Adas/Connected Car - June 2022 v1
summary: The "ADAS/Connected Car - June 2022" special report details advancements in automotive technology, focusing on advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS) and the critical need for high-speed data transmission within vehicles. It addresses the challenges of integrating high-resolution cameras and radars, demanding greater bandwidth and lower latency in in-vehicle networks, and highlights the role of edge inference computers and radar technology in enhancing vehicle safety and performance. The report emphasizes the importance of testing solutions, processing capabilities, and continuous innovation in shaping the future of connected vehicles.

title: Wolfspeed Appoints Owen DeLeon as VP of Sales for the Americas and Promotes Rick Madormo to SVP of Global Sales and Marketing
summary: Wolfspeed, Inc. has announced two key leadership changes in its sales department. Rick Madormo has been promoted to Senior Vice President of Sales and Marketing, succeeding Thomas Wessel, who is retiring. Concurrently, Owen DeLeon has been appointed as the new Vice President of Sales for the Americas.

title: Fabless startup Rebellions raises $50 mn in Series A
summary: South Korean fabless startup Rebellions Inc. has successfully raised $50 million in its Series A funding round, bringing its total accumulated investment to $80 million and tripling its valuation to $350 million. The company, founded by ex-quant developer Park Sung-hyun, specializes in AI accelerators, with its "Ion" chip demonstrating 30% faster processing and lower power consumption than Intel's Habana Goya. Rebellions plans to use the new funds for production expansion, establishing a US office, and recruiting talent for global growth.

title: 6 High-Quality, Undervalued Semiconductor Stocks
summary: Semiconductor stocks have seen significant declines in 2022, creating a strong buying opportunity for long-term investors. Morningstar analysts highlight several high-quality, undervalued semiconductor companies with strong competitive advantages, benefiting from attractive valuations and a long-term demand surge for chips across various industries. The article discusses when the chip shortage might end and the positive long-term outlook for the sector despite potential short-term slowdowns.

title: ASML engineer who fled charges of stealing chip tech is living comfortably in China as CEO of XTAL Inc.
summary: A former ASML engineer, Zongchang Yu, accused of stealing intellectual property, is now serving as CEO of XTAL Inc. in China. Yu founded two companies, XTAL Inc. and DongFang JingYuan Electron, both facing IP theft allegations from ASML. Despite lawsuits and a massive uncollectable fine against XTAL, Yu fled to China and reportedly runs DongFang with government support, benefiting from patents that include the contested OPC software.

title: OpenLight launches as fabless silicon photonics outfit
summary: OpenLight, a new company formed by Synopsys and Juniper Networks, has launched with the goal of accelerating silicon photonics development. The company specializes in integrated on-chip lasers for high-performance photonic integrated circuits, using Tower Semiconductor's PH18DA fabrication process. OpenLight aims to overcome the challenge of integrating laser sources directly onto silicon photonics wafers, reducing costs and improving power efficiency for various applications like data communications, AI, and optical computing.

title: Intel Demonstrates Commitment to Security at RSA Conference 2022
summary: At RSA Conference 2022, Intel reinforced its commitment to security, showcasing innovations and partnerships aimed at addressing complex cyber challenges like hybrid workforces and connected devices. The company highlighted new solutions such as the Intel® NetSec Accelerator Reference Design, the expansion of Project Circuit Breaker, and advancements in hardware-based security with the Intel vPro® platform and confidential computing initiatives like Project Amber. Intel emphasized its holistic approach to security, spanning products, research, and ecosystem collaboration.

title: TSMC Chairman Mark Liu on the revolution in semiconductors
summary: TSMC Chairman Mark Liu discusses how 5G, A.I., and high-performance computing (HPC) are ushering in a "golden era" for the semiconductor industry. He highlights how continuous innovation, exemplified by the pure-play foundry model pioneered by TSMC, has driven down costs and enabled widespread technological adoption. Liu projects significant growth for the industry, emphasizing the increasing value and omnipresence of semiconductors in modern society and foreseeing their critical role in future applications like VR/AR and personalized medicine.

title: Intel Names April Miller Boise Executive Vice President and Chief Legal Officer
summary: Intel Corporation announced the appointment of April Miller Boise as executive vice president and chief legal officer, effective July 6. Miller Boise will lead Intel’s global legal, trade, and government affairs team, succeeding Steven Rodgers. She brings over 25 years of experience and was previously executive vice president and chief legal officer at Eaton Corp.

title: Intel’s Problems Haven’t Gone Away
summary: Intel's stock and business are facing difficulties, with the company reporting a weakening macroeconomic environment. This has led to further delays for one of its key products. The persistent challenges indicate a tough period for the tech giant.

title: Lilt: delivering multilingual experiences driven by AI
summary: Lilt, founded in 2015, is a global experience platform that leverages AI to provide human-powered, technology-assisted translations, helping organizations build and deliver multilingual experiences. Their technology significantly improves translator speeds and reduces localization costs, making them a trusted partner for companies like Intel, UiPath, Canva, and Juniper Networks. Lilt offers products like Lilt Translate, Lilt Connect, and Lilt Manage to streamline translation workflows and content exchanges, demonstrating a strong commitment to AI-driven multilingual solutions.

title: 'Intel jobs will raise standard of living for everybody,' Lt. Gov. Husted tells conference
summary: Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted stated that Intel Corporation's new facility in Jersey Township, expected to bring 3,000 direct jobs and thousands more indirectly, will significantly raise the standard of living for all Ohioans. He emphasized the need for the state's workforce and communities to be prepared for the influx of jobs, highlighting challenges in talent availability and the importance of local planning. Husted also underscored the project's national security importance, noting the decline in U.S.-made computer chips.

title: Micron aims to hire 2,000 in Taiwan
summary: Micron Technology Inc. plans to hire about 2,000 additional employees in Taiwan over the next two to three years, expanding its workforce by 20% to become the largest foreign employer in Taiwan. This hiring drive supports the company's goal to accelerate the deployment of advanced DRAM manufacturing technologies, including 1-beta nanometer node DRAM production by the end of this year and 1-gamma nanometer node DRAM in 2024 through its new Taichung fab, A3, which will utilize extreme-ultraviolet (EUV) tools. Micron aims to leverage Taiwan's extensive EUV ecosystem and focus on growing markets like automotive electronics, industrial devices, and data centers despite short-term industry weaknesses.

title: AMD Reveals Roadmap for Zen 4-based laptop CPU, Zen 5, RDNA 3, and RDNA 4
summary: AMD has unveiled its roadmap for upcoming processors and graphics architectures, including the Zen 4-based Ryzen 7000 series, Zen 5, and RDNA 3, with RDNA 4 slated for 2024. The Zen 4 architecture promises significant performance and efficiency gains, competing with Intel's offerings, while Zen 5 will be a ground-up redesign with AI and machine learning enhancements. The RDNA 3 graphics architecture will be chipset-based and on a 5nm architecture, aiming for 50 percent better performance per watt than its predecessor.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. stock underperforms Monday when compared to competitors
summary: Shares of Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) fell 8.26% on Monday, closing at $86.99, amidst a general market downturn with the S&P 500 and Dow Jones also dropping. The stock recorded its seventh consecutive day of losses and underperformed against competitors such as NVIDIA Corp., Intel Corp., and Texas Instruments Inc. Trading volume for AMD was also below its 50-day average.

title: Intel picks next legal chief
summary: Intel has appointed April Miller Boise as its new executive vice president and chief legal officer, effective July 6. She will lead the company's global legal, trade, and government affairs team, reporting directly to CEO Pat Gelsinger. Miller Boise joins Intel from Eaton and succeeds Steven Rodgers, who retired earlier this year.

title: Amid domestic semiconductor wave, Texas Instruments begins $30B build
summary: Texas Instruments has broken ground on an estimated $30 billion semiconductor manufacturing facility in Sherman, Texas, which is the largest private sector investment in Texas history. Production is slated to begin in 2025, creating up to 3,000 direct jobs and contributing to a nationwide effort to increase domestic chip manufacturing. This project aligns with a broader industry trend of expanding semiconductor fabrication in the U.S., driven by rising demand and supply chain challenges.

title: Evans Foundation helps launch public-private planning effort for Intel development
summary: The Evans Foundation initiated a public-private planning effort in Licking County, Ohio, to prepare for Intel's $20 billion development. This initiative brings together business leaders and elected officials to ensure planned growth aligns with community values and enhances quality of life, leveraging expertise from national planning firms. Multiple local organizations are financially supporting the effort, which will include public forums and data analysis to shape the county's future in anticipation of the economic and social changes brought by Intel.

title: Homebuilder D.R. Horton targets Intel with proposed 302-home subdivision in Heath
summary: D.R. Horton has proposed a 302-home subdivision called South Fork Ridge in Heath, Ohio, targeting employees of the new Intel Corporation computer chip development. The development, which would require annexation into Heath for utilities, aims to address the housing demand created by Intel's arrival, despite the homes being priced higher than what the average Heath resident can currently afford. Heath Mayor Mark Johns, initially hesitant about a larger proposal, supports the revised plan, recognizing the need for diverse housing options in the area.

title: With Funding Stalled, Chip Makers Warn Congress the U.S. Is Lagging
summary: Chip makers are urging Congress to pass the CHIPS Act, which would provide over $52 billion to build semiconductor factories in the U.S. They warn that further delays could lead them to move manufacturing overseas, putting the U.S. at a disadvantage against other nations and exacerbating the global chip shortage. The Biden administration views this legislation as critical for American jobs and addressing supply chain issues.

title: 7 Stocks to buy that will make you rich by 2030
summary: This article identifies seven stocks across various sectors that could lead to significant wealth by 2030, assuming current revenue growth rates continue. The selected companies—Applied Materials, Coinbase Global, Intel, Altria Group, Novartis, Pfizer, and StoneCo—are analyzed for their market positions, growth prospects, and potential challenges. The author emphasizes long-term investing over short-term speculation.

title: Artificial Intelligence at Intel – Three Current Applications
summary: This article explores three current applications of Artificial Intelligence at Intel: building deep learning applications, identifying sales and marketing opportunities, and optimizing inventory. Intel uses its oneDNN software to streamline deep learning, integrates AI and NLP for market segmentation, and applies basic AI algorithms for spare parts inventory optimization. These applications highlight Intel's strategic investment in AI to enhance its operations and business growth.

title: These software companies are unsung winners in the semiconductor industry
summary: This article highlights software companies like Synopsys Inc. (SNPS) and Cadence Design Systems Inc. (CDNS) as critical but often overlooked players in the semiconductor industry. These companies provide Electronic Design Automation (EDA) software, which is essential for chip design engineers. Despite the focus on chip giants, these software firms play a crucial role in enabling the broader semiconductor sector, especially amidst ongoing chip shortages.

title: New Albany City Council approves 30-year, 100% tax abatement for Intel plant
summary: The New Albany City Council has approved a 30-year, 100% tax abatement for Intel's planned semiconductor complex, a move expected to bring significant economic benefits to central Ohio. This project, representing the largest private-sector investment in Ohio's history, is projected to create over 20,000 jobs. Intel is investing $20 billion to build two facilities, with construction anticipated to begin this year and operations by late 2025.

title: cnvrg.io, an Intel Company, Wins AI Breakthrough Award for MLOps Platform of the Year
summary: cnvrg.io, an Intel company, has won the AI Breakthrough Award for "MLOps Platform of the Year," marking its second consecutive win from the organization. The company was recognized for its two recent developer-friendly AI innovations: cnvrg.io AI Blueprints and cnvrg.io Metacloud, which enable seamless management, building, and deployment of AI on any infrastructure. This award highlights cnvrg.io's cutting-edge technology and its commitment to streamlining the machine learning lifecycle for enterprises.

title: Intel wins latest race in chip uses for the cloud, says KeyBanc Capital Markets
summary: KeyBanc Capital Markets research indicates that Intel Corp. is leading the growth in cloud technology, specifically in server processors for "cloud instances." Their latest monthly data tracking highlights Intel's victory in this area, focusing on newer processor architectures like Ice Lake.

title: Intel wins latest race in chip uses for the cloud, says KeyBanc Capital Markets
summary: KeyBanc Capital Markets' new research indicates that Intel Corp. is currently leading the growth in cloud technology, specifically in server processors used for "cloud instances." The research, released on Thursday, highlights Intel's performance with newer processor architectures such as Ice Lake, positioning the chip giant as the winner in the latest race for chip uses in the cloud.

title: DDR5: How Faster Memory Speeds Shape The Future
summary: This article discusses the advancements and challenges brought by DDR5 memory, which doubles the data rate of its predecessor, DDR4. It highlights how DDR5's increased speeds and power efficiency enable emerging technologies like IoT and V2X autonomous vehicles. The article also touches upon the future of DDR6, anticipating further speed increases and new signaling innovations to overcome physical limitations.

title: Intel Labs Announces Integrated Photonics Research Advancement
summary: Intel Labs has announced a significant advancement in integrated photonics research with the demonstration of an eight-wavelength distributed feedback (DFB) laser array fully integrated on a silicon wafer. This breakthrough, which features excellent output power and wavelength spacing uniformity, is crucial for increasing communication bandwidth in data centers and across networks. The innovation paves the way for high-volume manufacturing of next-generation co-packaged optics and optical compute interconnects for AI and ML workloads, leveraging Intel's existing 300-millimeter silicon photonics manufacturing process.

title: Intel vPro enables hybrid work with security, performance, manageability, and stability
summary: This article highlights how the Intel vPro platform supports hybrid work environments by providing essential security, performance, manageability, and stability for commercial PCs. Kate Porter, Sr. Director, Segmentation & Scale at Intel, explains that the platform has 15 years of validation and industry partnerships, making it well-suited for the evolving demands of remote and office work. She emphasizes how Intel vPro offers employees freedom and autonomy while maintaining productivity, especially as workplace transformations continue to accelerate.

title: Chip manufacturers press for federal funding as bill flails in Congress
summary: Semiconductor manufacturing giants Intel and GlobalFoundries are urging Congress to pass the $52 billion CHIPS Act, warning that delays in funding could jeopardize their domestic expansion plans. Intel has already postponed a groundbreaking for its Ohio facility, while GlobalFoundries states its New York plant timeline depends on government investment. The legislation faces hurdles in reconciling differing House and Senate versions, but proponents emphasize its importance for U.S. semiconductor leadership and supply chain resilience.

title: Building a Silicon Bulwark: How the United States and Taiwan Can Retain Joint Leadership of the Global Semiconductor Industry
summary: This article examines how the United States and Taiwan can jointly maintain their leadership in the global semiconductor industry against challenges, particularly from China's talent poaching. It proposes three key areas for cooperation: strengthening mutual legal assistance, harmonizing export control approaches, and pooling investments in semiconductor R&D and fabrication. The goal is to fortify Taiwan's "silicon shield" into a broader transnational bulwark.

title: Ohio families mourn the homes that will be supplanted by Intel’s new factories
summary: Intel's plans to build a $20 billion semiconductor operation in Johnstown, Ohio, are displacing over 50 homeowners, including 85-year-old Tressie Corsi, who has lived on 7 acres for 50 years. Although homeowners received offers above market rates and assistance with relocation, many are mourning the emotional loss of their "forever homes" and the disruption to their lives, despite acknowledging the economic benefits of the project for Ohio. The development highlights the tension between economic growth initiatives and the personal impact on local communities.

title: Chip shortage drives up vehicle prices, cuts sales
summary: The global semiconductor shortage severely impacted U.S. new vehicle sales in the second quarter of 2022, leading to a 21% drop compared to the previous year. Despite high gas prices, inflation, and rising interest rates, demand outstripped supply, pushing average new vehicle prices to a record high of nearly $45,000. Automakers like GM and Toyota experienced significant sales declines, and while some expect gradual improvement, the shortage is anticipated to continue frustrating buyers and limiting inventory for the foreseeable future.

title: Direxion SOXL ETF: How To Trade This Leveraged Play On Semis (NYSEARCA:SOXL)
summary: This article discusses the Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3X Shares ETF (SOXL), a 3X leveraged ETF on the semiconductor index. Despite SOXL being down 85% this year, the author advises against buying it immediately. The recommendation is to wait for signs of a bottom in the broader semiconductor index (SOXX) before initiating a position in SOXL.

title: Intel's Arizona operations offer clues about what chip maker will do in Ohio
summary: Intel's operations in Chandler, Arizona, provide a blueprint for what to expect from the company's new $20 billion project in New Albany, Ohio. The Arizona site, Intel's currently largest with 12,000 workers and six factories, showcases the economic impact, technological sophistication, and environmental considerations of a major semiconductor manufacturing hub. Ohio's New Albany site is envisioned to eventually surpass the Arizona operations in size and investment, becoming one of the largest semiconductor sites globally.

title: Mobileye Launches EyeQ Kit: New SDK for Advanced Safety and Driver-Assistance Systems
summary: Mobileye, an Intel company, has launched the EyeQ Kit, its first software development kit for the EyeQ system-on-chip. This SDK enables automakers to integrate Mobileye's core vision, mapping, and driving policy technologies while also deploying their own customized code and human-machine interface tools. The EyeQ Kit aims to reduce development costs, accelerate time to market, and provide flexibility for vehicle manufacturers in developing advanced safety and driver-assistance systems.

title: Intel starts construction at New Albany semiconductor campus
summary: Intel Corp. has begun construction on its $20 billion semiconductor manufacturing campus in New Albany, Ohio. Site work for the complex is currently underway. This development marks a significant investment by Intel in the region.

title: This Texas town is making itself into a semiconductor epicenter
summary: Sherman, Texas, is transforming into a major semiconductor manufacturing hub, with significant investments from Texas Instruments and GlobalWafers totaling over $35 billion. This growth is driven by local leadership, existing infrastructure, and a pro-business tradition, positioning the city to play a critical role in the U.S. semiconductor supply chain. The expansion is expected to bring substantial economic benefits and population growth, though it also presents challenges related to rising housing costs and infrastructure demands.

title: Micron Announces Availability of DDR5 DRAM For Next-Gen Intel & AMD Server and Workstation Platforms
summary: Micron has announced the commercial and industrial availability of its DDR5 DRAM specifically designed for next-generation Intel and AMD server and workstation platforms. This new DDR5 memory is expected to dramatically increase system performance by up to 85% over DDR4, catering to demanding AI, HPC, and data-intensive applications. Micron has been a key player in the development of DDR5 specifications and offers support through its Technology Enablement Program.

title: Intel champions path of sustainability in enterprise computing
summary: Intel is actively pursuing sustainability in enterprise computing, aiming for net-zero emissions by 2040 after already reducing its carbon footprint by 75%. Jen Huffstetler, Intel's chief product sustainability officer, emphasizes that efficient "green compute" through virtual technologies is crucial for maximizing existing data center resources and that sustainability is a shared responsibility across the industry. This effort extends to integrating acquisitions into Intel's sustainability goals.

title: Vodafone, Wind River, Intel, Keysight Technologies and Radisys test ‘green’ Open RAN network
summary: Vodafone, in collaboration with Wind River, Intel, Keysight Technologies, and Radisys, successfully tested a "green" Open RAN network, demonstrating energy efficiency during an industry event. This initiative aims to make future Open RANs more sustainable, with tests showing a 9% to 12% reduction in power consumption under varying traffic loads. The companies plan to integrate these energy-saving techniques into Vodafone's goal of having 30% of its EU mobile sites on Open RAN by 2030, emphasizing cross-industry collaboration for environmental sustainability.

title: Intel’s Dave Guzzi On The Big Channel Bet, Emerging Technologies And GPU Strategy
summary: Dave Guzzi, General Manager for Systems and Components Partners at Intel, discusses the company's significant commitment to its channel partners, driven by a massive manufacturing investment aimed at shifting chip production away from Asia. He emphasizes the importance of providing partners with secure supply chains and resources like Partner University to help them develop solutions with Intel's emerging technologies, including AI. Guzzi also highlights Intel's ambition to compete in the GPU market, offering partners more choice and leveraging Intel's extensive software development expertise.

title: Will TSMC and Intel's plans to diversify semiconductor supply chains stall?
summary: The semiconductor industry faces potential setbacks in its efforts to diversify supply chains outside Asia, with financial and political pressures threatening to scale back multi-billion dollar factory plans by companies like TSMC and Intel. A forecasted downturn in chip demand, signaled by Micron Technology, combined with delays in government funding like the US CHIPS Act, could lead to a rethinking of these significant investments. While diversification remains a long-term goal, the immediate economic climate and political wrangling over subsidies may slow the shift away from Asia's manufacturing dominance, especially for older node chips.

title: Wyden: Congress must act on grants for domestic semiconductor makers
summary: U.S. Sen. Ron Wyden is advocating for swift congressional action to provide billions in grants to boost domestic semiconductor manufacturing and research. He emphasizes the importance of these grants for lowering costs for U.S. consumers, creating high-wage jobs, and addressing national security concerns related to chip production dependency. Executives from Intel, nLIGHT, and Microchip Technology Inc. joined Wyden, highlighting the critical need for immediate federal investment to prevent the U.S. from falling behind global competitors and to address current chip shortages.

title: STMicro, GlobalFoundries plan new $5.7 billion French chip factory
summary: STMicroelectronics and GlobalFoundries announced plans to build a new semiconductor factory in France, a project valued at over 5.7 billion euros, with significant government funding. This initiative aims to boost Europe's chip production capacity and reduce reliance on Asian suppliers, addressing ongoing chip shortages that have impacted industries like automotive. The facility is targeted for full capacity by 2026, producing 18-nanometer wafers.

title: EXCLUSIVE BC Partners explores $4 bln sale of IT firm Presidio -sources
summary: Private equity firm BC Partners Ltd is reportedly exploring the sale of Presidio Inc., a U.S. information technology provider, for over $4 billion, including debt. Presidio, which generated $3.1 billion in revenue in 2021, specializes in secured cloud-based technology for a wide range of organizations and has benefited from increased demand for IT services during the COVID-19 pandemic. This potential sale follows a history of private equity ownership, with BC Partners having taken the company private in 2019 for $2.1 billion.

title: Intel hits water conservation milestone
summary: Intel has achieved a significant milestone in its water conservation efforts, now restoring more freshwater than it consumes in the United States, Costa Rica, and India. The chipmaker announced it is "net positive" for water used in manufacturing in these countries, largely due to on-site water treatment facilities and partnerships with environmental nonprofits for water restoration projects. Although Intel has not yet reached its global 2030 net-positive goal, this progress highlights its commitment to sustainable manufacturing practices amidst plans for further expansion.

title: Samsung Announces 3nm Process Node, the First with Gate-All-Around FETs
summary: Samsung has announced initial semiconductor production using its 3nm process node, which incorporates Gate-All-Around (GAA) FETs, trademarked as “MBCFET.” This technology marks a significant advancement beyond FinFETs, offering substantial improvements in power consumption, performance, and area reduction. Competitors like Intel and TSMC are also developing their own GAAFET processes, indicating a major shift in transistor architecture to continue Moore's Law.

title: An Atlanta developer has big plans for former DEC property in Hudson
summary: Portman Industrial, an Atlanta-based real estate developer, plans to purchase Intel's 148-acre property in Hudson, Massachusetts, and replace the existing buildings with a massive distribution center, potentially exceeding 1 million square feet. The site, originally built for Digital Equipment Corp. (DEC) in 1979, was a key player in the "Massachusetts Miracle" of the 1980s and was later acquired by Intel. The redevelopment requires numerous permits and studies due to its scale and impact on the area.

title: Intel To Hike Chip Prices As Semiconductor Giant Deals With Inflation
summary: Intel has announced price increases across its product lines, including chipsets and microprocessors, by as much as 20 percent, effective in Q4 2022. The company attributes these hikes to inflationary pressures, rising material costs, and increased production expenses. Other companies in the semiconductor industry, including TSMC and various material suppliers, are also implementing price increases, suggesting a broader trend of rising costs in the tech sector.

title: Taiwan edge AI company Gorilla Technology IPO surges 123% on first day of Nasdaq trading
summary: Taiwanese edge AI company Gorilla Technology Group saw its IPO surge 123% on its first day of Nasdaq trading, closing at US$33.43 under the ticker "GRRR." The company plans to use the proceeds from its business combination with Global SPAC Partners for working capital and international expansion, aiming to form a "Smart City Diverse Industry Alliance" to expand global markets for edge AI analytics solutions. Gorilla Technology has developed its edge AI technology over 20 years, holding 31 patents, and provides comprehensive video-centric and content management solutions across various sectors with key partnerships including Softbank, Intel, and Dell.

title: Santa Paula's hillside 'SP' monogram is fading. Here's what we know
summary: The iconic "SP" monogram etched on South Mountain above Santa Paula is fading due to overgrown shrubs and a lack of maintenance since the landowner's death. This landmark, nearly a century old and located on private property, has prompted calls for restoration efforts. City officials and community members are seeking permission from the current property representatives to organize new cleanup and potential solar lighting initiatives.

title: IQE files IP lawsuit against Tower Semiconductor
summary: IQE, a producer of compound semiconductor wafers, has filed a lawsuit against Tower Semiconductor, alleging the Israeli chipmaker misappropriated its intellectual property related to porous silicon technology. IQE claims Tower unlawfully obtained patents based on IQE's trade secrets, which are crucial for 5G telecoms and advanced sensing applications. Tower Semiconductor, currently being acquired by Intel, strongly denies the allegations, stating it did not misappropriate any trade secrets and is the rightful owner of its patents.

title: Chip parts supplier IQE files lawsuit against Tower Semiconductor
summary: Chip components supplier IQE has filed a lawsuit against Israeli chipmaker Tower Semiconductor, accusing it of misappropriating its intellectual property. The complaint was filed in the U.S. Federal Court in California. Tower Semiconductor, currently being acquired by Intel Corp, has not yet commented on the lawsuit.

title: Chip designers warm to U.S. bill despite big benefits to Intel
summary: Major U.S. chip firms are largely supporting a legislative package designed to boost the U.S. computer chip industry, despite earlier concerns that the subsidies disproportionately benefit manufacturers like Intel Corp. The legislation includes $52 billion in subsidies and an investment tax credit, primarily aiding companies that both design and manufacture chips. While chip designers like AMD and Qualcomm initially worried about not receiving direct benefits, they have reportedly agreed not to oppose the bill.

title: GlobalFoundries CEO: New York chip factory likely delayed if U.S. subsidy bill fails
summary: GlobalFoundries' CEO, Tom Caulfield, stated that a planned semiconductor factory in Malta, New York, will likely face delays if a U.S. bill providing $52 billion in subsidies and tax credits for the chip industry does not pass. He highlighted that while the company could eventually build the plant, government co-investment is crucial for swifter development. This situation contrasts with GlobalFoundries' recent decision to proceed quickly with a new $5.7 billion factory in France due to rapid government subsidies there.

title: TSMC: Life’s A Beach, And Then You Make Dies
summary: TSMC reported strong Q2 2022 results with $18.16 billion in revenue and a 44.4% profit margin despite a slowdown in PC and smartphone sales, driven by high-performance computing (HPC) demand. The company is advancing its 3nm and 2nm process nodes, with 3nm on track for volume production in H2 2022 and 2nm for risk production in 2024. The article highlights TSMC's industry leadership compared to Intel's struggles and discusses the implications of the CHIPS Act for domestic chip manufacturing.

title: Intel Spends Record Sum on Lobbying Amid Global Chip Shortage
summary: Intel (INTC) spent a record $1.75 million on federal lobbying in the last three months, a 65% increase year-over-year, as the semiconductor industry sought billions in government grants and subsidies. This surge in lobbying occurred amidst a global chip shortage. The company's spending surpassed its previous high set in the first quarter of the same year.

title: 7 Best Nanotech Stocks to Buy Now
summary: This article identifies seven top nanotech stocks for investment, highlighting the growing prevalence and potential of nanotechnology across various sectors like medicine and electronics. It details companies such as Applied Materials, Intel, IBM, and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., providing insights into their nanotechnology contributions, market performance, and reasons for investment. The piece emphasizes the significant growth projections for the advanced materials market, making nanotech stocks an exciting opportunity despite inherent risks.

title: Intel to produce Taiwanese company MediaTek's chips
summary: Intel announced a significant deal to produce chips for MediaTek, one of the world's largest chip design firms, under its foundry business. This move marks a major step for Intel's relatively new foundry service, aimed at building chips designed by other companies, a sector dominated by TSMC. The chips for smart devices will utilize Intel's 16-nanometer process technology, with initial products expected within 18-24 months.

title: Intel Signs Up Taiwan-Based MediaTek In Win For Foundry Plans
summary: Intel has secured a significant deal with Taiwanese chip designer MediaTek to build new chips through Intel Foundry Services (IFS), marking a major win for Intel's growing foundry business. This partnership aims to provide MediaTek with a more resilient supply chain by utilizing IFS capacity in the U.S. and Europe. Despite the positive outlook from Intel, some industry figures express concern that focusing on manufacturing for other companies could exacerbate existing supply chain issues.

title: Micron Ships World’s First 232-Layer NAND, Extends Technology Leadership
summary: Micron Technology has announced it has begun volume production of the world's first 232-layer NAND, marking a significant advancement in storage solutions. This new NAND technology offers industry-leading areal density, unprecedented performance with the fastest I/O speed, and improved energy efficiency, making it suitable for data-intensive applications from client devices to cloud services. The innovation is expected to drive breakthroughs in diverse markets, including data centers, mobile devices, PCs, and artificial intelligence applications.

title: MediaTek forms strategic partnership to make chips using Intel’s technology
summary: MediaTek Inc. has partnered with Intel Corp. to manufacture chips using Intel’s matured process technology, Intel 16 node, aiming to diversify its foundry sources beyond TSMC. This collaboration will enhance MediaTek's supply for mature process nodes used in devices like TVs and routers, while its partnership with TSMC for advanced nodes remains strong. Intel's Foundry Services will support MediaTek's smart edge devices, leveraging Intel's expanding manufacturing capacity in the US and Europe.

title: Opinion: Which Intel CEO is to blame for the current woes? Or is it actually AMD’s CEO?
summary: This article discusses the blame for Intel's recent disastrous second-quarter results, particularly the significant revenue miss in its data-center business. It explores whether the fault lies with current CEO Pat Gelsinger, his predecessor, or the success of rival AMD CEO Lisa Su. The article highlights that Intel's results were so poor that analysts questioned why the company did not pre-announce them.

title: U.S. Congress passage of subsidies prompts chip makers to move on projects
summary: Major semiconductor manufacturers are accelerating plans for new chip factories and expansions in the U.S. following the passage of the "Chips and Science Act." This legislation authorizes approximately $52 billion in government subsidies and a $24 billion investment tax credit for domestic semiconductor production and research. Companies like SkyWater Technology, GlobalFoundries, Intel, and NXP Semiconductors have indicated they will proceed with or expand their U.S. investments, with some projects previously stalled awaiting this funding.

title: AMD passes Intel in market cap
summary: AMD's market capitalization surpassed Intel's for the second time, reaching $153 billion compared to Intel's $148 billion. This shift, while largely symbolic, highlights increased competitiveness in the PC and server chip markets and suggests investors may favor asset-light chipmakers. Intel's market cap decline followed disappointing earnings and a reduced full-year forecast, which the company attributed to execution issues and a slowing PC market.

title: The Take: $52 Billion for Chip Plants… Better Than Nothing
summary: The US House of Representatives passed a $280 billion bill, the Chips and Science Act of 2022, primarily aimed at boosting US semiconductor manufacturing with $52.7 billion in subsidies for new plants. While the legislation is a step towards increasing domestic chip production, SAP global vice president Mike Lackey suggests that this funding might only support about 10 new fabrication facilities, which take years to build. The bill's effectiveness is further questioned by its lack of provisions for crucial capital equipment and the broader supply chain, amidst rapidly growing global demand for semiconductors.

title: The 5 Leading Semiconductor Manufacturers Today
summary: This article identifies the five leading semiconductor manufacturers globally, noting the significant revenue figures for Samsung Electronics, Intel, Taiwan Semiconductor (TSMC), SK Hynix, and Micron Technology in 2021. It details the extensive expansion plans and multi-billion-dollar investments each company is undertaking to address the persistent global chip shortage and geopolitical tensions. The piece also highlights the role of U.S. government initiatives like the CHIPS Act in incentivizing domestic semiconductor manufacturing.

title: Judge hits pause in Intel patent case, says VLSI must detail investors
summary: A Delaware federal judge has placed VLSI Technology LLC's patent case against Intel Corp on hold, citing the plaintiff's failure to provide sufficient information about its investors. U.S. District Judge Colm Connolly stated that a court declaration from VLSI CEO Michael Stolarski contained vague details and improperly redacted information about several entities with a stake in the company. This decision is part of ongoing patent disputes between the two companies, which have previously resulted in significant jury verdicts.

title: UCIe™ (Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express™) Consortium Announces Incorporation and New Board Members; Open for Membership
summary: The UCIe™ (Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express™) Consortium has officially incorporated and announced new board members, including Alibaba and NVIDIA, joining founding members like ASE, AMD, Intel, and Samsung. The consortium aims to advance the UCIe specification to create a universal and interoperable chiplet ecosystem, with the UCIe 1.0 specification already available for download. They are actively seeking new members to contribute to the development of future specifications.

title: Pelosi’s Taiwan trip puts the world's biggest chipmaker back in the spotlight of U.S.-China rivalry
summary: Nancy Pelosi's visit to Taiwan brought renewed attention to the island's critical role in the global chip supply chain, specifically highlighting Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC), a key manufacturer for tech giants like Apple and Nvidia. The visit underscored the strategic importance of semiconductors to U.S. national security and its rivalry with China. TSMC's dominance in the foundry market and the ongoing U.S. efforts to boost domestic chip manufacturing and secure supply chains were central themes, with concerns about China's potential actions towards Taiwan posing a significant risk to the global chip market.

title: China’s top memory chip maker could be targeted by US equipment export ban
summary: China’s top memory chip maker, YMTC, is facing increased pressure as the US is reportedly considering a ban on exporting chipmaking equipment to Chinese factories producing advanced NAND chips. This potential move is part of Washington's escalating efforts to curb the advancement of China’s semiconductor industry, following calls from US senators to add YMTC to a trade blacklist. The restrictions could also affect other Chinese chip makers like SMIC by targeting equipment for advanced chip production.

title: Taiwan dominates the world’s supply of computer chips – no wonder the US is worried
summary: Taiwan holds a dominant position in the global semiconductor market, particularly through TSMC, which controls 53% of the foundry market. This dominance has made Taiwan crucial to US geopolitical and national security interests, especially as semiconductors are vital for advanced technology and military applications. The US is actively working to reduce its reliance on foreign chip manufacturing, encouraging companies like TSMC to establish operations in the US and implementing policies like the Chips and Science Act to boost domestic production.

title: Intel Corporation provides interactive demonstrations at Hartford Fair
summary: Intel Corporation showcased its technology at the Hartford Fair with interactive demonstrations for fairgoers, including children's activities and a mini-experience of a fabrication plant. This event highlights Intel's presence in Ohio, where it is building a $20 billion computer chip manufacturing operation expected to start production in 2025 and create thousands of jobs.

title: Infineon, AMD Stock Prices Show Diverging Fortunes in Chip Industry
summary: The semiconductor industry is experiencing a divergence in fortunes, with chip manufacturers serving data centers, automotive, and industrial sectors maintaining strong demand, while those in consumer electronics face slowing sales and excess inventory. This trend is evident in the stock performance of companies like Infineon, which saw gains due to automotive demand, and AMD, which fell due to a PC market slump. The article highlights that analysts are drawing a "line in the sand" between these two segments of the chip market, indicating a shift from past blanket statements about the industry.

title: GlobalFoundries, Applied Materials and Ford to Co-Host CEO Summit to Secure U.S. Chip Supply Chain
summary: GlobalFoundries, Applied Materials, and Ford are co-hosting a CEO summit in Washington, D.C. to discuss securing the U.S. chip supply chain following the passage of the CHIPS and Science Act. The summit aims to foster public-private partnerships to accelerate semiconductor manufacturing, support automotive electrification, and strengthen U.S. economy and national security. Business leaders and senior government officials will explore how new investments can advance technology leadership and create jobs in the semiconductor industry.

title: Chuck Schumer’s Family Is Getting Nice Work on Wall Street
summary: The article highlights how Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer's family members and former staffers are being hired by major corporations like Blackstone, Amazon, Intel, and Google to lobby on legislation before the Senate. This raises concerns about potential conflicts of interest, especially regarding Schumer's role in advancing or delaying legislation that impacts these companies. Specifically, it details Blackstone's hiring of Schumer's son-in-law around the time a tax loophole benefiting private equity was being debated and Schumer's delay in bringing antitrust legislation against tech monopolies to a vote, given his daughters' employment by these tech giants.

title: Biden signs bill to boost U.S. semiconductor chip production, compete with China
summary: President Biden signed a landmark bill providing $52.7 billion in subsidies for U.S. semiconductor production and research, aiming to strengthen domestic manufacturing and enhance competitiveness with China. The legislation, which includes a 25% investment tax credit for chip plants, seeks to address persistent chip shortages affecting various industries and boost U.S. scientific research. Despite some criticism regarding aid to profitable companies, the White House highlighted new investments spurred by the bill from companies like Qualcomm and Micron.

title: Chipmakers tumble on fears of worst downturn in 10 years
summary: Semiconductor stocks experienced a significant downturn following Micron Technology Inc.'s warning of slowing demand, raising concerns about the industry heading into a prolonged slump. Analysts believe the sector is entering its worst downturn in a decade, affecting every company and end market. Despite recent shortages and investments, the rapid evaporation of demand has taken experts by surprise, with some companies like Micron already cutting future spending.

title: AMD Stock Is a Bargain
summary: Morningstar considers AMD stock a bargain with a fair value estimate of $130 per share, giving it a 4-star rating and a narrow economic moat. The company is praised for its impressive growth, market share gains against Intel, and strong execution in new chip designs, especially in the data center and PC markets. However, high uncertainty remains due to the cyclical semiconductor industry and AMD's smaller scale compared to Intel and Nvidia.

title: Intel Elects Lip-Bu Tan to Its Board of Directors
summary: Intel Corporation announced the election of Lip-Bu Tan to its board of directors, effective September 1, 2022. Tan, executive chairman of Cadence Design Systems Inc., brings extensive experience in semiconductors, software, and venture capital, and will join Intel's M&A Committee. This appointment is expected to provide valuable perspective to Intel's ongoing transformation.

title: Thematic Times: Semiconductors Power Thematic Indexes
summary: The article discusses how semiconductor stocks are crucial to many thematic indexes, particularly after the CHIPS and Science Act. It highlights direct and indirect exposure of various thematic indexes to semiconductors and the long-term benefits of the CHIPS Act for the industry and end users. The growth of the semiconductor market is primarily driven by the automotive sector, data storage, and wireless communications.

title: Desktop CPU Sales Lowest in 30 Years, AMD Gains Market Share Anyway (Updated)
summary: Desktop CPU shipments hit a nearly 30-year low in Q2 2022, marking the largest year-over-year decline in Mercury Research's records. Despite the overall market slump, AMD significantly increased its market share and revenue in desktop, mobile, and server segments, setting new records for x86 processor share and mobile unit share. Intel, in contrast, reported a rare loss and delayed key products, indicating a challenging period for the long-standing leader.

title: Palladium: U.S. Foreign Dependence
summary: The article highlights the United States' significant reliance on foreign sources for palladium, with 37% of its 2021 domestic consumption met by imports. A substantial portion (30%) of these imports originated from Russia, leading to price increases and supply concerns due to the war in Ukraine. The article notes that Russia and South Africa dominate global palladium mine production, emphasizing its critical role in automotive catalytic converters and semiconductor manufacturing.

title: Top Reliable SSD Storage Brands in Indian Market
summary: This article identifies top reliable SSD storage brands in the Indian market, highlighting "Intel", "Acer", "Samsung", "Western Digital", and "Crucial". It details the unique features, target users, and technological advancements of each brand's SSD offerings. The article underscores how SSDs, with their flash-based memory, provide faster and more reliable data storage compared to traditional hard disks.

title: Ohio senators impressed with Intel after Israel trip
summary: Ohio State Senator Jay Hottinger expressed greater appreciation for Intel and Israel after visiting Intel facilities in Israel with an Ohio Senate delegation. Hottinger noted that Intel, which has been in Israel since 1974, has grown significantly and is a major part of Israel's tech economy. He also mentioned that 30-40 Intel suppliers are expected to come to Ohio, reinforcing the company's commitment to the state's economic development with a $20 billion chip manufacturing operation.

title: Intel hands over nearly 5,000 patents in deal with IP management outfit
summary: Intel has transferred a portfolio of nearly 5,000 patents to Tahoe Research Limited, a new company within IPValue Management Group, which will license them to third parties. This deal aims to maximize revenue from Intel's older, non-core patents by leveraging IPValue's expertise in intellectual property management and licensing. This move follows Intel's past sales of its smartphone modem business to Apple and NAND flash business to SK hynix, indicating a strategy to streamline its IP portfolio.

title: Intel Elects Lip-Bu Tan To Its Board
summary: Lip-Bu Tan, executive chairman of Cadence Design Systems and founding managing partner at Celesta Capital and Walden Catalyst Ventures, has been elected to Intel's board of directors, effective September 1, 2022. Tan brings extensive experience from the semiconductor industry, venture capital, and public company boards, and will join Intel's M&A Committee. This appointment is part of Intel's ongoing transformation under CEO Pat Gelsinger.

title: US ban on chip tech could hurt China
summary: The US ban on exporting advanced electronic design automation (EDA) software for gate-all-around field-effect transistors (GAAFET) could significantly impede China's semiconductor industry. While the immediate impact on China's less advanced chipmakers might be minimal, the ban will make it difficult for them to design next-generation 3-nanometer chips. This move aims to prevent China from accessing technology critical for advanced military equipment.

title: A message to prospectors of the semiconductor gold rush
summary: Governments worldwide are heavily investing in semiconductor manufacturing, recognizing their critical role in future technologies like AI, IoT, and 5G/6G, and their impact on national security and economic growth. While India is making efforts with a significant budgetary allocation, the article argues for a more strategic approach, focusing on building a comprehensive semiconductor ecosystem starting with simpler devices, rather than just pouring money into the industry, to avoid past mistakes observed in China and the USSR. The author emphasizes the long-term commitment needed for India to become a semiconductor superpower.

title: Report: Qualcomm plans to return to the server chip market
summary: Qualcomm Inc. is reportedly planning to re-enter the server chip market four years after exiting it, seeking customers for a new server processor. This new processor is based on technology acquired from Nuvia Inc. for $1.4 billion and is being evaluated by Amazon Web Services Inc. The move aligns with Qualcomm's strategy to expand beyond mobile and boost revenue, potentially increasing competition for Intel Corp. and Advanced Micro Devices Inc.

title: Intel shares new details about upcoming Meteor Lake processor series
summary: Intel has revealed new technical details about its upcoming Meteor Lake processor series, which will utilize chiplet technology for desktop and laptop CPUs. These processors will feature five chiplets, with the primary CPU module manufactured by Intel and other vital components like the GPU and I/O module produced by TSMC. This chiplet architecture allows for greater flexibility in design, improved manufacturing efficiency, and better performance through 3D stacking with Intel's Foveros technology.

title: Ohio searching for 7,000 construction workers to build Intel chip plants
summary: Ohio faces a significant challenge in finding 7,000 construction workers for Intel's new $20 billion semiconductor manufacturing operation, a project that is among the state's largest ever. This shortage is exacerbated by an already thriving construction market and a national deficit of skilled tradespeople. State officials and Intel are addressing this through educational funding, training programs, and the high wages expected for these jobs, acknowledging that the project's scale will likely attract new workers and potentially shift some from residential construction, which could impact housing availability.

title: Intel and Brookfield set to invest $30 billion into chip factories in Arizona
summary: Intel and Brookfield Asset Management will jointly invest up to $30 billion in Intel's advanced chip factories in Arizona. This initiative follows the recent signing of the CHIPS and Science Act, which provides substantial subsidies for U.S. semiconductor production. Brookfield will hold a 49% stake in the expansion project for $15 billion, with Intel retaining majority ownership and operational control.

title: Intel Scores $30 Billion Deal With Brookfield to Fund Chip Factories
summary: Intel has secured a $30 billion deal with Brookfield Asset Management to help fund the construction of new chip factories. This strategic partnership aims to support Intel's ambitious plans for expanding its chip manufacturing capabilities through financial engineering. The deal will grant Brookfield majority ownership of the two new factories.

title: 4 million Uncrustables a day and counting
summary: J.M. Smucker Co.'s Uncrustables brand is on track to become a $1 billion brand, reaching $500 million in sales a year ahead of projections, with the company producing 4 million Uncrustables daily. To meet soaring demand, Smucker is expanding existing plants and building a new one. The article also touches on other Smucker brands experiencing growth despite price increases, as well as unrelated news about companies committed to zero waste and stabilizing rent costs in major U.S. metro areas.

title: Blakes counsels ECN Capital in $271-million sale of subsidiary to US private equity firm
summary: Blakes is advising ECN Capital on its $271 million sale of Kessler Group to US private equity firm Stone Point Capital. This article also highlights other significant deals, including Brookfield Infrastructure and Intel's $39 billion co-investment in Arizona chip factories, SNDL and Valens' $138-million cannabis merger, TFI International's $678-million truckload business sale to Heartland Express, and Limestone Technologies' acquisition by Lafayette Instrument.

title: Newegg Joins Intel Gamer Days with Deals on CPUs, Gaming PCs and Laptops
summary: Newegg is participating in Intel Gamer Days, offering promotional sales on Intel processors, gaming PCs, and laptops from August 25 to September 4. The event includes livestreams, exclusive deals, and a sweepstakes to win a custom PC valued over $3,400, powered by a 12th Gen Intel Core i9-12900K processor. Customers purchasing eligible products will also receive complimentary game codes for Arcadegeddon or Call of Duty: Modern Warfare II.

title: Intel spokeswoman tells Licking County commissioners access road open, site work continues
summary: An Intel spokeswoman provided an update to Licking County Commissioners on the company's site preparation, stating that a new western access road called Haul Road has been completed to redirect construction traffic. She also mentioned ongoing early site preparation, future construction phases, and Intel's commitment to community engagement and workforce development, especially concerning STEM education and preparing a local talent pipeline for production beginning in 2025. The commissioners praised Intel's proactive community involvement.

title: As Intel Shares Slide, CEO Gelsinger Doubles Down
summary: Despite a significant drop in Intel's stock value during 2022, CEO Pat Gelsinger has demonstrated confidence in the company by making his largest open-market purchase since taking the helm in February 2021. This move signals a strong belief in Intel's future amidst its financial challenges.

title: AMD Launches Ryzen 7000 Series Desktop Processors with “Zen 4” Architecture: the Fastest Core in Gaming¹
summary: AMD has officially launched its Ryzen 7000 Series Desktop processors, featuring the new "Zen 4" architecture. These processors, built on a 5nm process, offer significant performance improvements for gaming and content creation, including up to 16 cores and 32 threads. Alongside the new CPUs, AMD introduced the Socket AM5 platform, which supports DDR5 memory and PCIe 5.0, and unveiled AMD EXPO™ technology for advanced DDR5 memory overclocking.

title: Details Emerge On Nvidia’s “Grace” Arm CPU
summary: Nvidia recently revealed new details about its "Grace" Arm server CPU at the Hot Chips 34 conference, confirming it will use Armv9 specification cores and SVE2 vector math units. The article explores the potential design, core count, and the decision to use LPDDR5 memory, highlighting Grace's integration into Grace-Grace or Grace-Hopper superchip complexes and its performance estimates for memory-intensive AI workloads like GPT-3.

title: How to Get Windows 11 on HP Computers
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide on how to upgrade HP computers to Windows 11. It covers the benefits of upgrading, the essential system requirements, and step-by-step instructions for installation via Windows Update or the Windows 11 Installation Assistant. Additionally, it offers advice on preparing the computer for installation, backing up files, and troubleshooting common issues after the upgrade.

title: Arm sues Qualcomm, wants it to “destroy” Nuvia’s pre-acquisition CPU designs
summary: Arm is suing Qualcomm over its $1.4 billion acquisition of Nuvia, claiming the purchase caused Nuvia to breach its Arm licenses. Arm alleges that Qualcomm and Nuvia continued to develop Arm-based technology under the terminated licenses without consent, demanding that they stop using and destroy the relevant Nuvia technology. Qualcomm denies these claims, asserting it has broad licensing rights covering its custom-designed CPUs.

title: Arm sues Qualcomm, aiming to unwind Qualcomm's $1.4 bln Nuvia purchase
summary: Arm Ltd. has sued Qualcomm Inc. and its recently acquired chip design firm Nuvia Inc. for breach of license agreements and trademark infringement, seeking to unwind Qualcomm's $1.4 billion Nuvia purchase. Arm is requesting an injunction that would compel Qualcomm to destroy designs developed under Nuvia’s license agreements with Arm, alleging its approval was needed before the transfer to Qualcomm. This lawsuit signifies a major rift between two key technology partners, as Qualcomm had acquired Nuvia to boost its custom computing core designs and challenge competitors in the PC market.

title: STMicroelectronics Introduces Stellar P6 Automotive MCU for EV Platform System Integration
summary: STMicroelectronics has introduced its new Stellar P6 automotive microcontrollers, designed for next-generation electric vehicles. These MCUs support electrified drivetrains and domain-oriented, over-the-air updateable systems, integrating the new CAN-XL in-car communication standard. The Stellar P6 aims to facilitate the transition to software-defined vehicles, handling growing data flows and ensuring secure, real-time performance and energy management.

title: Investors are Scooping up Residential Properties Near Central Ohio’s Future Intel Site
summary: Real estate demand is booming near Intel's future $20 billion development in Central Ohio, three years before its operational date. Investors, including those from the West Coast, are actively buying residential properties to rent out, anticipating future scarcity and higher values. The rapid growth highlights challenges such as limited available housing, high land costs, and the need for significant infrastructure development to support new homes and attracted businesses.

title: Intel and Brookfield partner up to finance new chipmaking facilities
summary: Intel and Brookfield have partnered to finance new chipmaking facilities, with Brookfield helping to fund new Intel facilities in Chandler, Ariz. This collaboration aims to support the expansion of Intel's manufacturing capabilities.

title: Spot.io founders raise $28M for secret cyber startup two years after $450M exit
summary: The founders of Spot.io, which was sold to NetApp for $450 million two years ago, have launched a new cybersecurity company called Upwind. The startup has already secured $28 million in Seed funding from prominent VCs like Greylock, Cyberstarts, and Leaders VC, despite currently operating in stealth mode. The founders, including former Spot CEO Amiram Shachar, are re-teaming for this new venture after their successful exit.

title: Intel says it has no current plans to start manufacturing in India
summary: Intel Corp has stated it currently has no plans to establish a manufacturing facility in India. This announcement comes after India's transport minister had previously claimed that the chipmaker intended to set up a semiconductor plant in the country. Intel's comments clarify its current position on manufacturing operations in India.

title: Intel Names Shlomit Weiss Senior Vice President and General Manager of Design Engineering
summary: Intel Corporation announced that Shlomit Weiss has been appointed Senior Vice President and General Manager of the Design Engineering Group, replacing Sunil Shenoy who will retire at year-end. Weiss, with over 30 years of experience, will lead the company's design, development, validation, and manufacturing support of IPs and SoCs, reporting directly to CEO Pat Gelsinger. This change is part of Intel's IDM 2.0 strategy to drive product leadership and engineering excellence.

title: Intel’s chief people officer on why HR leaders must master technology
summary: Christy Pambianchi, Intel's Chief People Officer, emphasizes the critical role technology plays in modern HR leadership, stating that CPOs must be experts in technological advancements to effectively manage increasingly complex workforces. She highlights how tech has revolutionized talent acquisition, onboarding, employee experience, and people analytics, enabling HR to move beyond traditional administrative tasks. Pambianchi also discusses Intel's initiatives, including "greenfield" projects in Ohio and Germany and the use of predictive analytics for workforce retention.

title: Chip Delivery Times Shrink in August
summary: Global chip delivery times shortened in August to an average of 26.8 weeks, a slight improvement signifying easing supply shortages due to slowing demand for products like phones and PCs. Despite this, certain semiconductors such as power management and microcontrollers remain hard to acquire, and the industry struggles to match supply with demand, leading to concerns about over-ordering and inventory build-up. The Philadelphia Stock Market Semiconductor Index has seen a significant downturn, reflecting investor apprehension.

title: Pentagon eyes commercial solution to supply chain problems
summary: The U.S. Department of Defense is seeking commercial software solutions to address ongoing supply chain disruptions, which have been exacerbated by the pandemic and inflation. The Defense Innovation Unit (DIU) issued a solicitation for software that can build new supply pathways and manage risk for critical components, especially important given concerns about foreign reliance for microelectronics and recent issues like the F-35's Chinese-made magnet. The Pentagon aims to use analytical techniques to integrate data from various sources, create supply chain models, estimate costs, and identify risks.

title: What to know about Ohio's Intel Silicon Heartland factory project
summary: Intel is investing over $20 billion to build two new semiconductor manufacturing factories in Ohio, dubbed the "Silicon Heartland," aiming to boost domestic chip production. The project, located on nearly 1,000 acres in Licking County, is expected to create 3,000 Intel jobs, 7,000 construction jobs, and tens of thousands of long-term jobs, with production slated to begin in 2025. Significant road improvements and an additional $100 million in educational partnerships are also part of this massive development.

title: Intel Ohio Fab Breaks Ground, Leading Chip Plant Project Wave
summary: Intel Corp. has officially broken ground on a $20-billion project to construct two semiconductor chip manufacturing plants in central Ohio. This initiative is part of a larger trend to boost domestic chip production, spurred by the U.S. government's Chips and Science Act. The mega-site has the potential for up to eight plants with a total investment estimated at $100 billion.

title: Editorial: Responsibly Harnessing the Power of AI
summary: This editorial by Lama Nachman, Intel Fellow, discusses Intel's commitment to the responsible development and deployment of artificial intelligence. It highlights how Intel leverages AI to mitigate risks, drive positive change, and details their approach through a Responsible AI Advisory Council, focus on diversity and inclusion, and stringent privacy and security measures. The article emphasizes collaboration and continuous effort to ensure ethical AI solutions across industries.

title: Supermicro Adds New 8U Universal GPU Server That Delivers Maximum Performance and Flexibility for Large Scale AI Training, NVIDIA® Omniverse, and Metaverse
summary: Supermicro has launched a new 8U Universal GPU server designed for large-scale AI training, NVIDIA Omniverse, and the Metaverse. This server features eight NVIDIA H100 Tensor Core GPUs, offering maximum performance and flexibility. Its advanced airflow design helps reduce Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) in data centers while maintaining high performance.

title: Cloud optimization co Zesty raises $75m
summary: Israeli cloud optimization company Zesty has raised $75 million in a Series B funding round co-led by B Capital and Sapphire Ventures, bringing its total funding to $116 million. The company's automated system dynamically adjusts cloud configurations to ensure stability and optimize performance rather than solely focusing on cost reduction. Zesty has doubled its annual recurring revenue since early 2022 and employs 120 people, serving over 300 customers with most revenue from North America.

title: Arm unveils updated Neoverse CPU roadmap, targeting cloud, hyperscale and HPC workloads
summary: Arm has announced an updated Neoverse CPU roadmap to provide a foundation for various workloads in cloud, hyperscale, and high-performance computing. The new Neoverse V2, codenamed Demeter, aims to deliver top per-thread performance and power efficiency, with partners like Nvidia already integrating it. Additionally, Arm detailed upcoming N-series and E-series chip designs set for a 2023 launch, catering to different compute needs prioritizing thread count or data plane processing.

title: To Infinity and Beyond!
