summary: This article explores the futuristic concept of space-based data centers, following the current trend of underwater data centers. It discusses the potential advantages of such an endeavor, including cost reduction, natural cooling, and improved disk drive performance in zero-gravity environments. However, it also highlights the significant challenges, primarily the need for completely maintenance-free systems and the risks associated with the harsh space environment.

title: Intel Will Replace Celeron, Pentium Chips In Laptops Next Year
summary: Intel announced it will retire its Celeron and Pentium brands for laptops in 2023, replacing them with a new "Intel Processor" brand for its low-tier CPUs. This change aims to simplify the product purchasing experience for consumers and sharpen Intel's focus on flagship brands like Core, Evo, and vPro. The company assured that this is primarily a branding change and will not alter its current product offerings or roadmap.

title: Data, Data everywhere, but where to store it all
summary: The world is generating an unprecedented amount of data, leading to concerns about running out of storage space. Current trends show data creation outpacing storage capacity, with Zettabytes of information being generated daily. The article explores technological advancements in hard drive capacities, such as HAMR and MAMR, and radical concepts like quantum computing and graphene storage, while also highlighting the practical challenges of implementing these solutions and the long-term environmental impact of data storage.

title: The making of TSMC and its ecosystem
summary: TSMC has solidified its dominant position in the global wafer foundry market due to its advanced technology and strategic ecosystem development, securing orders from major IC designers worldwide. Despite global economic headwinds, TSMC projects significant revenue growth and continues massive expansion projects, fostering extensive collaborations with its supply chain partners through initiatives like the Open Innovation Platform. The company faces competition in advanced process nodes but maintains a leading edge through strong client relationships and continuous investment in technological advancements.

title: Supermicro Delivers Second-Generation NVIDIA® OVX™ Computing System for 3D Collaboration, Metaverse, and Digital Twin Simulation, Powered by the New NVIDIA L40 GPU
summary: Supermicro announced the release of its second-generation NVIDIA OVX computing system, powered by the new NVIDIA L40 GPU, designed for 3D collaboration, metaverse, and digital twin simulation. These new servers aim to provide the necessary graphics, AI, and network performance for building and operating immersive 3D virtual worlds at data center scale. The systems will support up to eight NVIDIA L40 GPUs, ConnectX-7 SmartNICs, and current/future CPU processors from Intel and AMD.

title: Supermicro Delivers Second-Generation NVIDIA® OVX™ Computing System for 3D Collaboration, Metaverse, and Digital Twin Simulation, Powered by the New NVIDIA L40 GPU
summary: Supermicro has launched its second-generation NVIDIA OVX computing systems, powered by the new NVIDIA L40 GPU, designed to accelerate 3D collaboration, metaverse applications, and digital twin simulations. These servers are optimized for NVIDIA Omniverse Enterprise applications, offering advanced graphics, AI, and networking performance. The systems support up to eight NVIDIA L40 GPUs, ConnectX-7 SmartNICs, high-performance CPUs, and NVMe storage.

title: L3Harris resorts to cannibalizing parts amid chip shortage
summary: Defense firm L3Harris Technologies has resorted to buying back and cannibalizing its own old radios to extract field-programmable gate arrays (chips) to meet customer demand due to persistent semiconductor shortages. Other defense industry executives, including Raytheon's CEO, also expect the chip shortage to continue into mid-2023 or beyond, forcing companies to find creative solutions and highlighting the defense sector's reliance on the broader commercial chip market. The situation underscores the urgency of efforts by the U.S. and Europe to boost domestic chip manufacturing capacity and reduce reliance on overseas production, as seen with the CHIPS and Science Act.

title: Intel promotes three Israelis to executive technological positions
summary: Intel has promoted three Israeli engineers—Fadi Aboud, Jack Doweck, and Marcelo Yuffe—to the position of Intel Fellow, which is equivalent to a Vice President. This marks a significant recognition of their contributions to processor design and development, with Fadi Aboud being the first Arab engineer in Intel Israel's history to achieve this technological capacity. The appointments highlight Intel's trust and appreciation for its Israeli human capital, who have been instrumental in various computing revolutions.

title: Uncover Big Opportunities in Smaller Companies
summary: With small- and mid-cap stock valuations at 20-year lows relative to large caps, investors may find a compelling opportunity in SMID-cap companies. Historically, SMID caps have demonstrated outperformance over large caps, and the current valuation discount, coupled with a larger universe of under-covered stocks, presents a potential for long-term growth. A focused strategy, rather than broad-market exposure, is recommended to identify suitable companies in this segment.

title: How Arm conquered the chip market without making a single chip, with CEO Rene Haas
summary: In this interview, Arm CEO Rene Haas discusses how Arm has become a central player in the tech ecosystem by designing chip instruction sets without manufacturing chips themselves. He explains Arm's licensing and royalty business model, its broad customer base including giants like Apple and Qualcomm, and how it is diversifying into cloud, automotive, and IoT markets. Haas also addresses the company's decision to go public, its role in navigating the chip shortage, and its competitive stance against x86 and RISC-V architectures.

title: Intel Unveils New Chips and Software as It Chases a Comeback (INTC)
summary: Intel Corp. introduced new personal-computer processors, graphics semiconductors, and software as part of its strategy to regain its position in the chip industry. The new lineup includes Core desktop processors for high-performance users and a graphics chip named Ponte Vecchio for data centers, aimed at challenging Nvidia. These announcements were made at Intel's Innovate event, showcasing their efforts towards a comeback.

title: Lazard Asset Management: 3 undervalued global equities
summary: Warryn Robertson from Lazard Asset Management identifies three undervalued global equities: eBay, Intel, and International Game Technology (IGT). He explains that eBay and Intel are attractively priced after significant declines, having refocused their strategies and invested heavily in R&D, respectively. IGT is highlighted for its strong, underappreciated earnings growth and low valuation compared to its peers.

title: Intel Accelerates Developer Innovation with Open, Software-First Approach
summary: Intel announced its continued commitment to an open, software-first approach to accelerate developer innovation, showcasing new tools and services at Intel Innovation 2022. The company introduced new offerings in AI, security, and quantum computing, emphasized the importance of the open oneAPI ecosystem, and detailed partnerships with companies like Red Hat and Leidos to foster widespread innovation and address industry challenges.

title: Intel Innovation 2022 Unveils New CPUs, Expanded Dev Cloud
summary: Intel Innovation 2022 introduced the new 13th generation Intel Core processors, the Intel Geti computer vision platform, and an expanded Intel Developer Cloud. These advancements highlight Intel's progress in enterprise-focused technology, offering improved performance, AI model creation capabilities, and early access to future hardware for developers. The event reinforced Intel's commitment to innovation in computing.

title: SIA Applauds Appointment of Industry Leaders to Advise Commerce Department on CHIPS R&D Implementation
summary: The Semiconductor Industry Association (SIA) has praised the U.S. Commerce Department's selection of an Industrial Advisory Committee (IAC). This committee, comprised of 24 industry leaders, will guide the Secretary of Commerce on semiconductor research and development related to the CHIPS for America Act. SIA emphasizes the importance of collaboration between government, industry, and academia to effectively implement the act's R&D investments and boost U.S. semiconductor innovation.

title: Intel Files IPO Plans for Its Mobileye Autonomous Driving Unit
summary: Intel has filed paperwork with the SEC for an initial public offering (IPO) of its Mobileye Global unit. Mobileye specializes in developing chips and software for autonomous driving systems. This move indicates Intel's strategy to spin off and potentially raise capital for its autonomous driving business.

title: Japan to give Micron Technology up to $320 mln to boost chip output in Hiroshima
summary: Japan announced it will provide Micron Technology with a subsidy of up to 46.5 billion yen ($320 million) to help build advanced memory chips at its Hiroshima plant. This financial aid highlights the increasing collaboration between the U.S. and Japan in chip manufacturing, amidst technological competition with China. The move follows similar support for Western Digital Corp and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co, as Japan seeks to bolster its domestic chip production capabilities.

title: Meet the Harvard Law School graduate helping China cut its reliance on US AI chips
summary: Michael Zhang Wen, a Harvard Law School graduate and chairman of Biren Technology, is leading China's efforts to reduce its dependence on US artificial intelligence (AI) chips. His Shanghai-based start-up recently released the BR100, a 7-nanometre GPU, which some believe could position Biren to become "China's Nvidia" amidst escalating US export restrictions on advanced chips. Zhang faces pressure to deliver, as his company has raised a substantial US$655 million from major venture capital funds.

title: SoftBank’s Son expected to meet Samsung's Lee for Arm M&A discussion
summary: SoftBank Group CEO Masayoshi Son is anticipated to meet with Samsung Electronics de facto leader Jay Y. Lee to discuss a potential sale or strategic alliance for British chip designer Arm. This meeting follows a collapsed deal to sell Arm to Nvidia due to regulatory hurdles and a broader industry consensus that a single company acquisition might be difficult. Various scenarios are being considered, including a consortium of chipmakers or a partial acquisition by Samsung, though some industry watchers express skepticism about significant outcomes given Arm's high valuation and SoftBank's financial state.

title: Top 12 Data Center Companies in the USA
summary: This article lists and discusses the top 12 data center companies in the USA, highlighting their revenue, number of hedge fund holders, and recent performance. The data center industry is experiencing significant growth due to increased digitalization, cloud adoption, remote work, and advancements in AI and machine learning, with the global market projected to grow by 21.98% CAGR from 2021 to 2026. Companies like Digital Realty Trust, Iron Mountain, Equinix, American Tower Corporation, Intel, Advanced Micro Devices, and Alphabet are examined for their contributions and challenges within this expanding sector.

title: Intel’s Journey From 100 Billion To 1 Trillion Transistors Was Painted At The IntelON 2022 Event
summary: During the IntelON 2022 event, CEO Pat Gelsinger outlined Intel's ambitious goal to increase transistor count from 100 billion to 1 trillion by the end of the decade, reinforcing Moore's Law. The company also announced significant advancements, including new data center and gaming GPUs (Flex Series, Ponte Vecchio, Arc A770), and the 13th Gen Core Processors (Raptor Lake). Intel's focus remains on an end-to-end foundry approach, expansion of the UCIe consortium for open chiplet ecosystems, and strengthening its offerings in both consumer and enterprise markets.

title: Micron picks Syracuse suburb for huge computer chip plant that would bring up to 9,000 jobs
summary: Micron Technology plans to invest up to $100 billion to build a massive computer chip manufacturing complex in Clay, a Syracuse suburb, which is slated to be the largest single private investment in New York history. This project is expected to create 9,000 direct jobs and an additional 40,000 supply-chain and construction jobs over the next 20 years, significantly boosting the region's economy and establishing Central New York as a major semiconductor manufacturing hub. The decision comes after an intense effort by state, local, and federal officials, backed by over $6 billion in state and local incentives and the federal Chips and Science Act.

title: Intel hits major milestone as it moves toward mass production of quantum computer chips
summary: Intel's research organizations, Intel Labs and Components Research, have announced significant progress towards the large-scale production of quantum computing processors. They've achieved the highest reported yield and uniformity rates for silicon spin qubit devices, a crucial step for fabricating quantum chips using existing transistor manufacturing processes. This development positions Intel as a key player in the quantum computing race, leveraging its ability to produce fragile qubits on standard silicon wafers with high efficiency.

title: Intel Hits Key Milestone in Quantum Chip Production Research
summary: Intel Labs and Components Research organizations have achieved the industry's highest reported yield and uniformity to date of silicon spin qubit devices, developed at Intel's transistor R&D facility. This milestone uses Intel’s second-generation silicon spin test chip and demonstrates success across an entire 300-millimeter silicon wafer with a 95% yield rate, marking a crucial step toward scaling quantum chips for commercialization. The research shows fabricating quantum chips on Intel’s established transistor process nodes is a sound strategy for the future of quantum computing.

title: Biden hails IBM's $20 billion New York manufacturing deal
summary: President Joe Biden celebrated IBM's announcement of a $20 billion investment in New York's Hudson Valley for semiconductor development, AI, and quantum computing. This investment follows the Chips and Science bill, which provides $52 billion in subsidies for chip manufacturing, and is part of the administration's effort to boost U.S. manufacturing and job creation in critical sectors. Biden emphasized the importance of government funding to ensure national security and revitalize manufacturing, citing similar investments from Intel and Micron.

title: Micron would change Syracuse area for decades to come. Are we up to the challenge?
summary: Micron Technology's plan to build a $100 billion semiconductor fabrication campus near Syracuse promises thousands of high-paying tech jobs and significant regional growth. However, a major challenge lies in developing a skilled workforce to fill these specialized positions, requiring a concerted effort from educational institutions. The expansion also necessitates planning for infrastructure, housing, and social services to support the anticipated population influx and economic changes in the area.

title: 10 Best Laptops For Data Science And Data Analysis
summary: This article identifies the key specifications required for laptops used in data science and data analysis, such as ample RAM, fast processors, SSD storage, and CUDA-capable graphics cards. It then lists and describes 10 HP laptop models, including the HP ENVY 17t, HP Pavilion, and HP ZBook 15 G5, highlighting their suitability for various data-intensive tasks and user budgets. The guide emphasizes that while cloud solutions are common, local machine power remains crucial for data professionals.

title: Can Any Emerging Memory Technology Topple DRAM and NAND Flash?
summary: This article explores the challenges emerging memory technologies face in unseating DRAM and NAND Flash, prompted by Intel's exit from the Optane business. It delves into the history and current state of ferroelectric RAM (FeRAM), resistive RAM (RRAM), phase-change memory (PCM), and magnetic RAM (MRAM), discussing their breakthroughs and limitations. The author questions whether any of these contenders can achieve the "Holy Grail" of storage-class memory.

title: Intel plans to cut thousands of jobs hit by PC slowdown - Bloomberg News
summary: Intel Corp is reportedly planning a significant reduction in its workforce, potentially numbering in the thousands, due to a downturn in the personal computer market. This move comes after the company cut its annual sales and profit forecasts in July, stemming from missed second-quarter estimates and reduced consumer spending on PCs. Some divisions, such as sales and marketing, could see cuts affecting about 20% of staff, with an announcement expected as early as this month.

title: Intel CEO Pushes to Further Separate Chip-Design, Production Arms
summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger is planning to further separate the company's chip-design and chip-making factory operations. This new structure is intended to allow Intel's factories to function as contract chip-making operations, serving both Intel engineers and external chip companies equally, as part of a broader effort to revamp the company and increase returns.

title: Intel Plans to Lay Off Thousands of Employees As the Chipmaker Looks to Trim Costs
summary: Intel Corp. is preparing for significant cost-cutting layoffs, potentially affecting thousands of employees, in response to a struggling personal-computer market and declining demand for its processors. The reductions are expected to be announced around the time of its third-quarter earnings report on October 27, with some divisions facing cuts of up to 20% of staff. This move reflects Intel's efforts to trim expenses, improve profit margins, and regain its competitive edge amidst a broader tech spending downturn.

title: Microsoft debuts new Surface devices with chip and design upgrades
summary: Microsoft unveiled three new Surface devices: the Surface Pro 9, Surface Studio 2+, and Surface Pro 5 Laptop, all featuring updated processors and design enhancements. The Surface Pro 9, a two-in-one device, offers both Intel Alder Lake and Arm-based SQ3 chip options. The Surface Studio 2+ workstation was updated with an Intel Core i7-11370H chip and Nvidia RTX 3060 graphics, while the Surface Pro 5 Laptop is available in two screen sizes with Intel Alder Lake processors.

title: Heterogeneous Integration Co-Design Won’t Be Easy
summary: Heterogeneous integration, which involves combining smaller chiplets into a system-in-package (SiP), is becoming crucial for advanced silicon chip design, reducing dependence on Moore's Law. This approach requires seamless co-design and collaboration across engineering teams, utilizing advanced EDA tools for electrical, thermal, and mechanical analysis. Successful implementation hinges on optimizing chip design, process, and packaging together, with the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) standard facilitating this complex integration.

title: Cadence, Google Cloud team on system, semiconductor design ...
summary: Cadence has partnered with Google Cloud to accelerate system and semiconductor design using cloud-ready tools, resulting in up to a 10x improvement in design and verification throughput. Cadence tools, including the Cloud Passport, have been certified for Google Cloud, with Google silicon teams already utilizing them for developing Tensor SoCs and TPUs. This collaboration aims to provide engineers with enhanced scalability, access to state-of-the-art HPC infrastructure, and faster time to solution.

title: U.S. agency panel says VLSI patent in $2.2 bln Intel case should be reviewed
summary: A U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) tribunal has agreed to review the validity of one of the patents that led to a $2.18 billion trial loss for Intel Corp against VLSI Technology LLC. This decision opens a potential path for Intel to overturn part of the verdict, despite the USPTO director having previously sanctioned the company that sought the review for attempted extortion. The Patent Trial and Appeal Board (PTAB) panel found the petition strong enough to warrant review, noting a "highly likely" chance that parts of the patent are invalid based on prior art.

title: SEMI: Global 300mm Chip Fab Capacity Forecast to Reach New High in 2025
summary: Global 300mm chip fab capacity is projected to expand at a nearly 10% CAGR from 2022 to 2025, reaching 9.2 million wafers per month, driven by strong demand for automotive semiconductors and new government funding. China is expected to significantly increase its global share of 300mm front-end fab capacity, nearing Korea and surpassing Taiwan. Regional investments and incentives, including the U.S. CHIPS Act and European CHIPS Act, are also contributing to this growth, with power-related and analog capacities showing the strongest expansion rates.

title: Supermicro Extends Best of Breed Server Building Block Solutions to Include a Broad Set of OCP Technologies - Driving Customer Innovation and Time-to-Market
summary: Supermicro is expanding its server portfolio to incorporate open hardware and open-source technologies, aiming to drive innovation and reduce proprietary lock-in for customers. These solutions, including an 8U 8-GPU AI training system and OCP 3.0 compliant AIOM cards, integrate best-in-class components like Intel, AMD, and ARM CPUs and NVIDIA GPUs, alongside open technologies such as OpenBMC and Open BIOS. This initiative provides customers with high-performance, efficient, and flexible server options for various applications, from cloud and AI/ML to storage and 5G/Edge computing.

title: ASML reports strong Q3, unaffected by U.S. China sanctions
summary: ASML Holding NV posted better-than-expected third-quarter sales and profit, along with record new bookings of 8.9 billion euros. The company, a crucial equipment supplier for chip manufacturers like TSMC, Samsung, and Intel, stated that it anticipates no significant impact from U.S. sanctions on China. Despite global macroeconomic concerns and weakness in memory chips, ASML's demand remains strong, with a backlog exceeding 30 billion euros, prompting plans to expand production capacity by 2025.

title: ASML shrugs off slowdown, U.S. China sanctions, reports strong Q3
summary: ASML Holding NV reported better-than-expected third-quarter sales and profit, along with record new bookings, despite the current economic slowdown and U.S. sanctions on China. The company's CEO, Peter Wennink, noted strong long-term demand from customers and a limited impact from U.S. regulations on China because ASML is a European company with few U.S. parts in its non-EUV machines. ASML's backlog has reached 38 billion euros, and it is expanding production capacity to meet demand.

title: KIOXIA's next-gen EDSFF E1.S SSDs for hyperscale data centers released
summary: KIOXIA has unveiled its new XD7P Series SSDs, which are designed for hyperscale and general server applications using the EDSFF E1.S form factor. These second-generation E1.S SSDs come with improved performance, including up to double the sequential write and random read/write performance compared to their predecessors. While initially available in PCIe 4.0 variants, KIOXIA has PCIe 5.0 versions under development, to be released based on customer demand.

title: US weighs China tech restrictions on quantum computing and AI
summary: The Biden administration is considering new export controls to limit China's access to emerging computing technologies like quantum computing and artificial intelligence. These potential restrictions follow earlier measures aimed at curbing China's use of advanced semiconductors for military and surveillance purposes. The move risks further escalating tensions with China and forcing other nations to choose between the two economic powers.

title: China’s Biren GPUs Can’t Be Made at TSMC Due To US Regulations & Competition With NVIDIA
summary: Birentech, a Chinese chip startup, is reportedly barred from manufacturing its Biren GPUs at TSMC due to US regulations aimed at restricting China's access to advanced AI and datacenter chips. This decision is influenced by the Biren BR100's potential to outperform NVIDIA's A100 chips, which are already banned for the Chinese market, and could severely impact Birentech's manufacturing plans.

title: US-China chip war causing collateral damage in Israel
summary: The US-China chip war is impacting Israel's tech sector, forcing Israeli chipmakers and startups to navigate new US export controls designed to prevent China from gaining a military advantage through advanced semiconductors. Companies like Applied Materials and Nova Measuring Instruments are already experiencing significant revenue losses and share price drops due to these restrictions. The situation is complicated for Israeli firms, requiring them to reassess their ties with the US and potentially apply for exemptions from the Commerce Department to continue operating in China.

title: Chips and China
summary: This article examines the implications of the U.S. chip sanctions on China, contrasting the integrated model of Intel with the modular approach of TSMC to explain the complexities of semiconductor manufacturing. It analyzes the long and difficult path China faces to build a self-sufficient advanced chip industry, discussing its unique advantages and the potential geopolitical consequences, particularly for Taiwan. The author concludes by raising concerns about the immediate effects of the sanctions and the potential for mutually assured economic destruction.

title: Mobileye IPO: 5 things to know about the Intel autonomous-driving spinoff
summary: Intel's autonomous-driving spinoff Mobileye Global Inc. has returned to the public markets through an IPO, pricing shares at $21, above the targeted range. While Intel initially hoped for a valuation as high as $50 billion, Mobileye's market capitalization is now close to its original acquisition price of $15.3 billion at roughly $16.7 billion due to a struggling IPO market. Mobileye, a pioneer in automated-driving technology, is focusing on a larger market while facing increasing competition.

title: Intel To Carve-Out Mobileye On October 26
summary: Intel is proceeding with the carve-out of its Mobileye division through an IPO on October 26, 2022, with shares trading under the symbol "MBLY". The IPO is priced at $21.00 per share, above the initial range, and Intel will maintain a significant majority stake after the offering, making Mobileye a controlled company. This article provides valuation analysis for both Intel and Mobileye, reiterating a 'Hold' rating for Intel given current market conditions and strategic challenges.

title: Intel unit Mobileye prices IPO above range to raise $861 mln
summary: Mobileye Global Inc (MBLY.O), the self-driving unit of Intel Corp (INTC.O), successfully priced its IPO above its target range, raising $861 million. The IPO valued Mobileye at $16.7 billion, significantly lower than Intel's initial $50 billion hope, amid one of the worst years for U.S. IPOs on record due to market volatility. Despite the challenging market, Mobileye proceeded with the offering, with Intel retaining a majority stake.

title: Intel names compliance chief
summary: Intel Corporation has promoted Carol Tate to vice president and chief compliance officer (CCO), as announced by April Miller Boise, Intel's executive vice president and chief legal officer. Tate had been acting CCO since January and has a strong background in ethics and compliance roles at various companies. This promotion highlights Intel's focus on strengthening its ethics and compliance culture under new leadership.

title: AMD Issues Performance Advisory for Ryzen 7000 Processors
summary: AMD has released a performance advisory for its new Ryzen 7000 processors, acknowledging reports of unexpected performance variations in certain games. The company is actively investigating these issues and plans to collaborate with game developers and hardware partners for optimization. AMD also addressed concerns about Windows 11 performance, stating that their testing showed no significant differences compared to Windows 10.

title: Residents suing town of Hudson, Intel over redevelopment plans
summary: Residents of Hudson, Massachusetts, have filed a lawsuit against the town and Intel Corp. in Massachusetts Land Court. The suit challenges a proposal to redevelop Intel's former property into a 1.2 million-square-foot warehouse, arguing that such a development is not allowed by town bylaws for "by right" construction. The plaintiffs, including residents of nearby housing complexes and a children's center, contend that the proposed use is commercial and not industrial, and therefore does not meet zoning requirements.

title: Semiconductors: Three-way Contest In 2022, Plus Predictions For 2023 And Beyond
summary: The semiconductor industry saw a three-way contest in 2022, with TSMC topping the third quarter, while Intel is expected to finish a distant third for the full year. Although a chip downturn is predicted for 2023, growth is expected to return in 2024, with the industry potentially reaching $1 trillion in sales by 2032. The author emphasizes the importance of India approving silicon fab proposals to capitalize on the upcoming upcycle in the middle of the decade.

title: Micron Ships World's Most Advanced DRAM Technology With 1-Beta Node
summary: Micron Technology has announced it is shipping qualification samples of its 1β (1-beta) DRAM technology to select smartphone manufacturers and chipset partners, achieving mass production readiness for the world's most advanced DRAM technology node. This LPDDR5X mobile memory delivers top speeds of 8.5 gigabits per second, with significant gains in power efficiency (15% improvement) and bit density (over 35% improvement) at 16Gb per die capacity. The 1β node is poised to support highly responsive applications from mobile to data centers and intelligent vehicles, stemming from Micron's pioneering R&D and nanomanufacturing capabilities.

title: Glass-based semiconductor fab breaks ground
summary: Absolics Inc., a subsidiary of SK Group, has started construction on a $600 million glass-based semiconductor manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia. This new fab will produce glass substrates that allow for more compact multi-chip packaging and reduce power consumption by up to 50%. The project aligns with the U.S. CHIPS and Science Act, aiming to strengthen the domestic semiconductor supply chain and create over 400 jobs, with Absolics claiming to be the first globally to mass-produce this material.

title: Crypto firm Iris Energy close to defaulting on debts
summary: Crypto firm Iris Energy is nearing default on hardware debts for three special purpose vehicles (SPVs) as their generated cash is insufficient to meet repayment requirements. While data centers remain unaffected, the company is in discussions with its lender. This situation highlights broader challenges in the crypto industry, with other firms like Core Scientific and Compute North also facing financial distress.

title: BDA advises onsemi on sale of Niigata foundry to Mercuria, Sangyo Sosei, Fukuoka Capital
summary: BDA Partners advised onsemi on the sale of its Niigata wafer manufacturing facility in Japan to investment vehicles managed by Mercuria Holdings Co Ltd, Sangyo Sosei Advisory Inc, and Fukuoka Capital Partners Co Ltd. This transaction will create Japan's first independent specialty semiconductor foundry. The sale is expected to close upon regulatory approvals and fulfillment of closing conditions.

title: Intel Says Its On Track With “Aggressive” 2030 Foundry Goals, Aiming The 2nd Spot By The End of The Decade
summary: Intel is aggressively pursuing its 2030 foundry goals, aiming to become the world's second-largest chip manufacturer, surpassing Samsung and closing in on TSMC. The company is investing significantly in new fabrication plants globally, developing next-gen process technologies, and building capacity to attract clients and diversify the semiconductor supply chain. Intel plans to achieve this through strategic investments, global expansion, and strong competition against its rivals.

title: Silverman: Got an itch to switch from an Intel Mac to an Apple Silicon one? You’re not alone.
summary: The article discusses the growing trend of Mac users considering switching from Intel-based Macs to those with Apple Silicon (M1 or M2 chips). The author, an Intel Mac owner, highlights how new macOS features and third-party applications are increasingly becoming exclusive to Apple Silicon due to its distinct architecture and performance benefits. While most of these individual features might not compel an immediate upgrade, the cumulative effect and the direction of developer focus suggest that Intel Mac owners will likely face a strong incentive to switch in the coming year.

title: What to expect during AMD’s 4th Gen EPYC Genoa Release: Join theCUBE Nov. 10
summary: AMD is set to release its 4th Gen EPYC Genoa CPU on November 10, entering a competitive x86 processor market. The new chips are expected to feature 96 cores and 192 threads based on a 5-nanometer manufacturing process, offering higher density and power efficiency. This launch positions AMD ahead of competitor Intel, which has delayed its Xeon Scalable CPUs.

title: Tower Semiconductor: Opportunity or risk?
summary: Intel agreed to acquire Tower Semiconductor for $53 per share, but the market fears regulatory hurdles, especially from China and Japan, causing Tower's share price to drop significantly. The deal, valued at $5.4 billion at the time of agreement, needs approvals from multiple countries, including the US, China, Germany, Israel, Japan, the UK, and Italy. Analysts suggest that while regulatory risk exists, particularly with China due to US-China tensions, previous Israeli semiconductor acquisitions ultimately received Chinese approval, and Japan's hesitancy regarding M&A deals might be overcome as Intel plans to maintain operations there.

title: Top 10 Quantum Companies Ranked According To Their Number Of Quantum Patents
summary: IPRdaily has released a ranking of the top quantum companies based on their number of quantum patents, highlighting the rapid growth and international participation in the quantum computing industry. IBM leads the list with 1323 patents, followed by Google and D-wave, demonstrating the significant investment by major tech firms. The analysis also revealed that the U.S., China, Japan, and Canada are the leading countries in patent holdings.

title: Intel loses bid to revive antitrust case against patent foe Fortress
summary: A U.S. appeals court upheld the dismissal of Intel Corp's antitrust lawsuit against Fortress Investment Group LLC, finding insufficient evidence that Fortress's patent aggregation practices had anticompetitive effects. Intel had accused Fortress of stockpiling patents to threaten tech companies, but the court said Intel failed to show it paid higher royalties or was prevented from making products. This decision follows a nearly $2.2 billion patent verdict against Intel in favor of a Fortress-affiliated entity.

title: Intel debuts powerful Max Series CPUs and GPUs for high-performance computing and AI
summary: Intel has launched its new Max Series CPUs and GPUs, designed to compete in the high-performance computing (HPC) and artificial intelligence (AI) markets against rivals like AMD and Nvidia. These new chips will power the upcoming Aurora supercomputer at Argonne National Laboratory, aiming to make HPC and AI more accessible for solving complex global challenges. Intel claims its Max Series offers significant performance advantages and better power efficiency compared to competitive silicon.

title: Intel’s Quantum Computing Technology: An Overview [2024]
summary: This article provides an overview of Intel's quantum computing technology, highlighting its strategic advantages rooted in decades of experience in mass producing complex technologies. It discusses Intel's approach to addressing quantum complexity and scalability through projects like its second-generation cryogenic control chip, Horse Ridge II, and its focus on mass-producible quantum technology. The article also covers Intel's quantum hardware, its Quantum Simulator, and its Quantum Software Development Kit, concluding that Intel is pursuing a hybrid approach that integrates classical and quantum computing.

title: ASML launches 12 bln euro buyback, upgrades 2025 forecast
summary: ASML Holding NV announced a 12 billion euro share buyback program running through 2025 and significantly upgraded its revenue forecast for 2025 to 30 billion to 40 billion euros, up from a previous estimate. The company, a key supplier of chipmaking equipment, anticipates sustained growth and plans to expand production of its EUV machines. Following the announcement, ASML shares jumped 9.7%.

title: AMD launches data center CPU 'Genoa', taps Google, Microsoft as customers (Nov. 10)
summary: AMD has launched its fourth-generation EPYC processor, codenamed "Genoa," for data centers, securing major customers like Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, and Oracle. This new chip offers significant performance and energy efficiency improvements over its predecessor, translating into lower operational costs for enterprises and cloud data centers. The launch comes as rival Intel faces delays with its own data center chip, and AMD continues to gain market share, boosting its stock by about 14%.

title: Cisco to open its first EU chip design center in Barcelona
summary: Cisco Systems announced it will establish its first EU chip design center in Barcelona, Spain, aiming to bolster Europe's semiconductor ecosystem. This initiative aligns with Spain's €12 billion PERTE program and the EU's strategy to reduce dependence on chip production in China. The investment's specific amount was not disclosed, but the project is expected to consolidate Barcelona as a technological hub.

title: HPE's low-end supercomputers take aim at the AI market
summary: HPE and Dell are introducing new supercomputing and high-performance computing (HPC) systems designed to meet the growing demands of AI-driven workloads. HPE is focusing on smaller, lower-cost supercomputers with advanced cooling, while Dell is enhancing its HPC offerings with powerful GPUs, aiming to make supercomputing capabilities more accessible to a broader range of enterprise users. This convergence of supercomputing and HPC is blurring the lines between the two, as both companies compete to provide infrastructure that can efficiently handle complex AI, modeling, and simulation tasks.

title: Does the U.S. Need to Reduce Its Dependence on Taiwan for Semiconductors?
summary: The U.S. is heavily reliant on Taiwan for semiconductors, with Taiwan accounting for 65% of the global supply and TSMC alone producing 55%. While the U.S. is taking steps like the CHIPS Act to boost domestic production and reduce this dependence, experts believe Taiwan will remain a dominant force. The U.S. also seeks Taiwan's cooperation for export controls against China, highlighting a complex relationship where diversification is planned but difficult to achieve quickly.

title: AMD announces its reprogrammable Versal AI chips are ready for space-based deployments
summary: AMD's reprogrammable Versal AI Core chip has achieved Class B qualification, making it suitable for space-based deployments in commercial satellites. This radiation-resistant SoC enables significant advancements in onboard AI processing for satellites, offering greater performance and reconfigurability compared to traditional ASICs. Raytheon Co. is an early adopter, planning to use these chips for its next-generation space processors.

title: Lam Research Acquires SEMSYSCO to Advance Chip Packaging
summary: Lam Research has acquired SEMSYSCO GmbH, a global provider of wet processing semiconductor equipment, to enhance its capabilities in advanced chip packaging. This acquisition broadens Lam's offerings to include cleaning and plating for heterogeneous integration, particularly for fan-out panel-level packaging, which is crucial for high-performance computing and AI applications. The deal also brings SEMSYSCO's R&D facility in Austria, expanding Lam's global development network.

title: AMD chip to power auto supplier Aisin’s new automated car parking system
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) announced that its Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC microprocessor will power a new automated parking system developed by Japan's Aisin Corp. This system aims to make automated parking more mainstream by offering a cost-effective solution for automakers. The use of AMD's FPGA-based chip, acquired from Xilinx, will allow for post-sale software and chip updates in vehicles, with production expected to begin on 2024 models.

title: Intel hit with $949 mln U.S. verdict in VLSI computer chip patent trial
summary: A federal jury in Texas ordered Intel Corp to pay VLSI Technology LLC $948.8 million for infringing a VLSI patent related to computer chips. Intel plans to appeal the verdict, which follows a previous $2.2 billion verdict against the company in a separate patent trial. VLSI, a patent-holding company affiliated with Fortress Investment Group, argued that Intel's Cascade Lake and Skylake microprocessors violated its patent for improvements to data processing.

title: Intel FakeCatcher detects deepfakes with a 96% accuracy rate
summary: Intel has launched FakeCatcher, a deepfake detection platform that boasts a 96% accuracy rate and provides real-time results. Unlike other solutions, FakeCatcher identifies genuine human characteristics like subtle blood flow signals in video pixels to distinguish real content from AI-generated fakes. This technology aims to restore trust in media and can be used by news organizations, social media platforms, and non-profit groups to combat the spread of manipulated videos.

title: Astera Labs Accelerates Company Growth with Series-D Funding and Expanded Board of Directors
summary: Astera Labs announced significant company growth, including closing a $150M Series-D funding round led by Fidelity Management & Research, which raised its valuation to $3.15B. The company also expanded its Board of Directors with Dr. Alexis Black Bjorlin of Meta and Michael Hurlston of Synaptics, and grew its product portfolio with the Leo Memory Connectivity Platform for CXL™. These milestones, alongside global expansion with new R&D centers in Canada, position Astera Labs for continued leadership in connectivity solutions for intelligent systems.

title: Competition gets heated on Fungible’s DPU patch
summary: Fungible, a data processing unit (DPU) startup founded in 2015, is facing intense competition from major players like AMD, Intel, Kalray, Marvell, and Nvidia. Despite developing impressive DPU chips and acquiring composability software, Fungible has struggled to secure major customers or OEM deals and recently pivoted away from composable systems to focus on its storage technology. The company reportedly tried to raise more funding unsuccessfully and is now in a precarious position amidst rumors of acquisition.

title: Applied Materials to weather slowdown, forecast shows
summary: Applied Materials Inc. provided a better-than-expected sales forecast for the current quarter, indicating it can navigate an industry slowdown despite challenges like declining chip orders and US-China trade tensions. The company expects to lose up to $2.5 billion in fiscal 2023 revenue due to trade restrictions but is optimistic about outperforming the broader industry by resolving supply chain issues and leveraging continued customer spending on new technology. Its Q4 earnings and sales exceeded analyst expectations.

title: Man/Machine or Man+Machine: A future of synthesis?
summary: This article explores the growing trend of human-machine integration, from embedded microchips for payments and door access, exemplified by biohackers like Patrick Paumen, to the more advanced Internet of Bodies (IoB) devices. It details progress in brain implants, including Elon Musk's Neuralink and Synchron Inc.'s FDA-approved Stentrode, which aim to address neurological conditions and enable thought-controlled devices. The piece also touches on cybernetic piercings and the broader societal implications of this synthesis, highlighting that thousands have already embraced such technologies.

title: Exxact Corp. Works with NVIDIA to Support Omniverse Enterprise
summary: Exxact Corporation, a high-performance computing provider, is now offering integrated solutions to the media and entertainment industry to support NVIDIA Omniverse Enterprise. This collaboration provides studios with workstations powered by NVIDIA RTX GPUs and Omniverse Enterprise, enabling real-time collaborative 3D scene creation and simulation. Exxact aims to enhance productivity, communication, and creativity for M&E clients, particularly as remote production models become more prevalent.

title: Intel Foundry head steps down, as contract chip making division tries to ramp up
summary: Randhir Thakur, the head of Intel's Foundry business, has resigned to pursue other opportunities, a move that comes amidst company-wide layoffs and spending cuts. He will remain until early 2023 to assist with the leadership transition and the acquisition of Tower Semiconductor. This departure impacts Intel's efforts to grow its contract chip-making division, which faces stiff competition from established leaders like TSMC and Samsung, despite securing major customer wins.

title: Intel Elects Barbara G. Novick to Board of Directors
summary: Intel Corporation announced the election of Barbara G. Novick, co-founder and senior advisor at BlackRock Inc., to its board of directors, effective December 1, 2022. Novick will serve as an independent director and join the board’s Audit & Finance and Compensation committees, bringing extensive expertise in investment, finance, and unlocking stockholder value to assist Intel's transformation. Her background includes co-founding BlackRock in 1988 and leading its Global Government Relations and Public Policy Group.

title: Microcontrollers learn to embrace neural networks
summary: Deep learning is increasingly moving to constrained edge systems like microcontrollers due to concerns about latency, bandwidth, and privacy with cloud-based processing. Chipmakers such as NXP and Silicon Labs are integrating machine learning accelerators into their microcontrollers to improve efficiency, while Microchip Technology explores future acceleration options. The article also discusses the potential future role of Transformer architectures in microcontroller-based AI, despite their current complexity.

title: Lam Research completes acquisition of semiconductor equipment distributor
summary: Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) announced it completed the acquisition of SEMSYSCO GmbH, a semiconductor equipment provider. This acquisition enhances Lam's capabilities in advanced packaging and chiplet-based solutions, broadening its offerings with cleaning and plating capabilities for heterogeneous integration. The deal also includes a new research and development facility in Austria, expanding Lam's global development network.

title: A Rising Star in the Biden Administration Faces a $100 Billion Test
summary: Commerce Secretary Gina Raimondo is spearheading a massive industrial policy initiative, distributing nearly $100 billion to bolster the U.S. semiconductor industry and expand broadband access. This effort aims to enhance American competitiveness in future technologies like AI and secure chip supplies, while also navigating a growing economic rivalry with China. Her success in this endeavor will significantly impact the future of the U.S. economy.

title: Bechtel picked to build $20 billion Intel project in New Albany
summary: Intel has selected Bechtel Corp. to build its $20 billion semiconductor manufacturing project in New Albany, Ohio. This significant economic development for Ohio will involve constructing two factories, called fabs, with the potential for up to eight, eventually totaling a $100 billion investment. Construction on the 2.5 million square feet of space will require the equivalent of eight Eiffel Towers in steel and anticipates 5,000 to 7,000 workers on site during peak activity, despite current industry weakness in the semiconductor market.

title: Supermicro W790 E-ATX Motherboard Leaks Out: Sapphire Rapids-WS CPU Support, 16 ‘8-Channel’ DDR5 DIMMs, Lots of PCIe Gen5 Slots
summary: A Supermicro W790 E-ATX motherboard supporting Intel's Sapphire Rapids-WS Xeon Workstation CPUs has leaked, revealing key features. The X13SWA-TF motherboard includes an LGA 4677 socket, 16 DDR5 DIMM slots with 8-channel memory support, and six PCIe Gen5 x16 slots. This new platform, known as Fishhawk Falls, is anticipated for workstation and HEDT PC builds, with CPU launches expected by early 2023.

title: Company selected to build Intel's new Ohio semiconductor manufacturing facilities
summary: Intel has chosen Bechtel as the general contractor for its new $20 billion semiconductor manufacturing facilities in Licking County, Ohio. Bechtel will design and build Phase 1 of the 2.5 million square foot facility, which includes 600,000 square feet of cleanrooms. This project aims to enhance U.S. semiconductor manufacturing, create new construction jobs, and address supply chain challenges.

title: Intel details precious metals, asset recovery initiatives
summary: Intel is actively pursuing a zero-waste-to-landfill goal by implementing circular strategies for its manufacturing scrap, recovering precious metals, and redesigning products for easier recycling. The company has significantly increased its nonhazardous material recycling rate and decreased total waste generation. Intel also educates consumers on e-waste recycling through initiatives like a video game.

title: As Intel begins layoffs, no clear indication of effects in Rio Rancho
summary: Intel has begun layoffs, but a spokesperson for the company's New Mexico operations provided no specific details regarding the impact on its Rio Rancho facility. This comes after Intel's CEO and CFO announced expected workforce reductions during their third-quarter earnings call.

title: Lockheed Martin and Intel Demonstrate 5G Capabilities For Military Aircraft Use
summary: Lockheed Martin, in partnership with Intel Corp., successfully demonstrated 5G capabilities for military aircraft use, integrating military tactical radios into Lockheed Martin’s 5G.MIL Hybrid Base Station. This achievement paves the way for 2023 flight testing of crewed and uncrewed aircraft for distributed teaming as part of Project Carrera. The collaboration aims to bridge commercial 5G stacks with military datalinks to enhance resilient communication solutions for the Department of Defense.

title: Intel says it will squeeze 1 trillion transistors onto a chip package by 2030
summary: Intel's researchers have revealed innovations in chip packaging that could lead to chips with over a trillion transistors by 2030, significantly extending Moore's Law. This development would result in computer chips ten times more powerful than current advanced silicon. The company aims to address the increasing demand for computing power driven by data consumption and AI.

title: Intel and Penn Medicine Announce Results of Largest Medical Federated Learning Study
summary: Intel Labs and Penn Medicine have completed the largest medical federated learning study to date, involving 71 institutions globally. The study utilized Intel's federated learning technology and Software Guard Extensions (SGX) to improve brain tumor detection by 33% while preserving patient data privacy. This approach allows AI models to be trained on vast, decentralized datasets without sharing raw patient information, addressing long-standing data accessibility issues in medical research.

title: Wi-Fi NOW Awards: List of finalists announced! Read who made it to the final round here.
summary: Wi-Fi NOW has announced the finalists for its annual awards across various categories, including Affordable Connectivity, Best Wi-Fi IoT Product, Enterprise Wi-Fi Solution, and more. The winners will be revealed at the Wi-Fi NOW Awards Gala on Monday, December 12. This year saw nearly double the number of entrants compared to the previous year, indicating increased competition and value for the awards.

title: Taiwan Semiconductor Plans to Build Second Fab at Phoenix Site
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) has begun construction on a second semiconductor chip plant in Phoenix, investing a total of $40 billion across both facilities. The first fab is expected to be operational in 2024 with 4-nanometer technology, while the second will feature 3-nanometer technology by 2026. President Biden highlighted these projects as a significant step in bringing manufacturing back to the U.S. and strengthening the supply chain, supported by initiatives like the Chips and Science Act.

title: When will it open? How can you find jobs? 5 things to know about Phoenix's TSMC semiconductor plant
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) marked a significant milestone with a "tool-in" ceremony at its new Phoenix semiconductor fab, announcing a second factory and attracting President Joe Biden's visit. This massive $40 billion investment will create 4,500 direct TSMC jobs and tens of thousands more in construction and supporting industries, boosting metro Phoenix's economy. The fabs will produce advanced 4-nanometer and 3-nanometer chips, generating substantial annual revenue and tax contributions, while also prioritizing sustainability with advanced water reclamation and solar energy.

title: Pison Technology Appoints John Croteau as CEO
summary: Pison Technology, a pioneer in neural biosensing with AI-powered Electroneurography (ENG) for health, fitness, and smart device control, has appointed semiconductor industry veteran John Croteau as its new chief executive officer. Croteau, who joined Pison in January 2022, aims to embed biosensors in consumer smartwatches for Neural Health and Fitness apps and 3D Touchless controls. His appointment is expected to drive the company's transition to commercial markets through strategic partnerships and operational scaling.

title: Tech: Self-driving cars hype and reality check
summary: This article examines the gap between the hype and reality of self-driving cars, tracing predictions from 2015 to the current state of technology. It outlines the six levels of autonomous driving, discusses the progress and challenges faced by companies like Mobileye, Tesla, Waymo, and Cruise, and notes the significant impact of rising interest rates on funding for long-gestation projects in the self-driving vehicle industry.

title: Oregon's Most Admired Companies 2022: Intel on restoring its manufacturing edge
summary: Intel Corp. has been recognized as the 2022 Oregon's Most Admired company in the technology sector. The article highlights Intel's focus on restoring its manufacturing edge. This acknowledgment comes from the Portland Business Journal's annual "Oregon's Most Admired Companies" list.

title: A dip in Alder Lake with an HP Elitebook is spoiled by avoidable mistakes
summary: The HP Elitebook 1040 x360 g9, featuring an Intel Alder Lake CPU, offers solid performance and thoughtful additions but is marred by several design flaws and software issues. Reviewer Simon Sharwood notes its speed in benchmarks and daily tasks but criticizes the poor keyboard, inconvenient port placement, useless tablet mode, and excessive pre-installed "nagware." Despite its processing power, the laptop ultimately feels uninspired and fails to deliver a "fresh perspective" often sought in the "Desktop Tourism" series.

title: The 10 Hottest Semiconductor Startup Companies Of 2022
summary: Despite a turbulent year for the semiconductor industry, driven by macroeconomic hurdles, the market for AI, ML, high-power computing, cloud, and intelligent edge business is thriving. This article highlights 10 semiconductor startups making significant strides in 2022. These companies are attracting substantial investments and developing innovative solutions for next-generation technologies.

title: Emerging memories move to replace embedded NOR flash
summary: Emerging memories like ReRAM, MRAM, and FRAM are poised to replace embedded NOR flash, driven by its limitations below 28-nm process technology and the need for low-power, high-reliability solutions in demanding applications. Major foundries like Samsung, TSMC, and GlobalFoundries are shipping production parts with ReRAM or MRAM, while companies like Infineon, Weebit Nano, and Everspin Technologies are advancing their respective technologies for automotive, industrial, aerospace, and data center applications, addressing challenges in density, scalability, and integration.

title: $20B Intel Ohio project to utilize extensive quantities of steel and concrete
summary: The $20 billion Intel Ohio project, involving the construction of two semiconductor manufacturing facilities, will require massive amounts of steel and concrete, comparable to eight Eiffel Towers and the world's tallest skyscrapers, respectively. Bechtel Corporation will manage Phase 1 of the project, which aims to boost Intel's chip production and establish a "Silicon Heartland" in Ohio, creating numerous construction jobs and strengthening regional economic development. Intel and Bechtel emphasize safety, quality, and innovation in this endeavor to advance U.S. semiconductor manufacturing.

title: Happy Birthday Robert Noyce: The Founder of Intel Corporation
summary: This article celebrates the birthday of Robert Noyce, co-founder of Intel Corporation and Fairchild Semiconductor, known as the "Mayor of Silicon Valley." It highlights his pivotal role in co-inventing the integrated circuit and his entrepreneurial spirit, managerial style, and lasting contributions to the tech industry and Silicon Valley's work culture. The piece also shares lesser-known facts about his life, including his creativity as a child, his academic achievements, and his early career before founding Intel.

title: System in Package Market Size, Industry Share, Forecast to 2034
summary: The System in Package (SiP) market is projected to grow significantly due to increasing demand for compact, internet-based electronics, IoT devices, 5G-connected devices, and the miniaturization of electronic components. Key drivers include the adoption of SiP technology in smart wearables, smartphones, gaming processors, and graphic cards. The Asia Pacific region is expected to lead this growth, primarily fueled by the consumer electronics industry.

title: ASML CEO says U.S. chip export rules on China contradictory
summary: ASML CEO Peter Wennink has criticized U.S. chip export rules targeting China as contradictory, arguing that American chip manufacturers can sell advanced chips to Chinese customers while ASML is restricted to older equipment. Wennink highlighted that while 15% of ASML's sales are in China, U.S. chip equipment suppliers see 25-30% or more. This comes as the U.S. aims to restrict China's semiconductor development and China has sued the U.S. through the WTO over the controls.

title: Intel Asks Delaware Court to Dismiss $4 Billion VLSI Patent Suit
summary: Intel Corp. has requested the U.S. District Court for the District of Delaware to dismiss a $4.1 billion patent lawsuit brought by VLSI Technology, citing VLSI's failure to disclose its full ownership structure as required. Intel argues that VLSI's opaque ownership, controlled by investment funds managed by a SoftBank subsidiary, is characteristic of hedge fund-driven patent litigation. The case highlights ongoing disputes between Intel and VLSI, including previous multi-billion dollar judgments against Intel, and concerns surrounding disclosure requirements set by U.S. District Judge Colm Connolly.

title: U.S. sanctions Chinese firm selling video surveillance tech to Iran
summary: The U.S. Biden administration has blacklisted Tiandy Technologies, a Chinese video surveillance company, for its involvement in the repression of Uyghurs in China and for providing U.S.-made technology to Iran’s Revolutionary Guard Corps. As a result, Intel Corp. ceased doing business with Tiandy, and the Commerce Department restricted U.S. firms from exporting components to the company. This action is part of a broader effort by the U.S. to counter Chinese techno-authoritarianism and prevent human rights abuses.

title: Marvell, Nokia upgrade ReefShark SoC chipset for 5G
summary: Marvell and Nokia have deepened their partnership to upgrade Nokia's ReefShark system-on-chip portfolio, aiming to deliver an advanced radio access and transport processing platform. This collaboration leverages Marvell's 5nm Arm Neoverse N2 multi-core solution and Octeon 10 DPU, enabling high performance and low latency for new 5G services while working to minimize costs and energy consumption. The upgraded chipsets are crucial for meeting the increasing demands of wireless connectivity in 5G networks.

title: Abbott Laboratories Plans $536M Factory in Ohio
summary: Abbott Laboratories announced plans to build a new $536 million factory in Bowling Green, Ohio, to produce specialty and metabolic powder nutritional products. This expansion aims to boost the U.S. supply of essential formulas, especially after a baby formula shortage earlier in the year linked to issues at its Sturgis, Michigan plant. The facility is expected to create 450 permanent jobs and begin production by 2027, contingent on state and local incentives.

title: New Intel oneAPI 2023 Tools Maximize Value of Upcoming Intel Hardware
summary: Intel has released its oneAPI 2023 tools, designed to maximize the value of upcoming Intel hardware such as 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable processors, Intel Xeon CPU Max Series, and Intel Data Center GPUs. These tools offer enhanced performance and productivity, cross-platform compatibility through SYCL code for non-Intel GPUs, and improved support for AI and machine learning frameworks. The update aims to address the growing need for efficient multi-architecture programming in a developer landscape increasingly targeting heterogeneous systems.

title: Intel delaying German factory start, wants more subsidies, Volksstimme reports
summary: Intel Corp has reportedly delayed the start of its chip factory in Magdeburg, Germany, seeking more public subsidies due to rising energy and raw materials costs. The original budget of 17 billion euros has increased to nearly 20 billion euros, and Intel cited geopolitical challenges and decreased semiconductor demand. The company is currently in discussions with the German government to bridge the funding gap.

title: Mobileye among best Wall Street flotations of 2022
summary: Mobileye Global Inc. led Wall Street's largest flotations in 2022 by valuation, with a market cap reaching $27.1 billion after its October IPO, providing a 61% return. Acquired by Intel for $15 billion, Mobileye successfully went public at nearly $17 billion. The company's performance, alongside other larger but less valuable IPOs, stood out in a year that saw the lowest number of flotations in three decades.

title: ENVY Laptops: A Complete Review
summary: This article provides a comprehensive review of HP ENVY laptops, highlighting their blend of style and functionality. It details key features such as design, performance specifications, entertainment capabilities, connectivity options, battery life, and security features. The review also offers a closer look at specific models like the 13-inch, 15-inch, and x360 convertible HP ENVY laptops, helping consumers choose the best fit for their needs.

title: Intel executive Raja Koduri returns to chief architect role
summary: Intel announced that Raja Koduri, head of its AXG graphics card division, is returning to his previous role as chief architect. This move is part of Intel's broader organizational restructuring for its graphics divisions. Koduri will now focus on accelerating high-priority technical programs across CPU, GPU, and AI development within the company.

title: What TSMC CEO CC Wei says about the semiconductor industry (1)
summary: TSMC CEO CC Wei delivered a speech where he discussed new challenges in the semiconductor industry, emphasizing the critical need for talent and a distinct business strategy from founder Morris Chang. Wei highlighted the shift towards regional division of labor and the global scramble for self-sufficient semiconductor ecosystems, while asserting that there are no shortcuts to developing semiconductor capabilities. He stressed the importance of collaboration and warned against the risks of attempting to leapfrog technological advancements without sufficient resources.

title: Despite Billions in Federal Subsidies, Chipmakers Are Laying Off Thousands of Workers
summary: Despite significant federal subsidies from the CHIPS Act, major semiconductor manufacturers like Micron Technology, Intel, AMD, Nvidia, and Qualcomm are implementing layoffs or hiring freezes due to slowing demand in key markets. Micron, one of the largest memory chip makers, plans to cut 10% of its workforce, impacting 4,800 employees, citing a "dramatic drop in demand" for PC and smartphone chips. While these companies invest heavily in new facilities to leverage tax breaks, government incentives are not alleviating their short-term financial challenges exacerbated by reduced consumer spending.

title: Media Alert: Intel Hosts 4th Gen Xeon Scalable and Max Series Launch
summary: Intel announced a media alert for the official launch of its 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable processors, Intel Xeon CPU Max Series, and Intel Data Center GPU Max Series. The virtual event, hosted by Sandra Rivera and Lisa Spelman, will take place on January 10, 2023, showcasing the value and customer impact of these new products with a focus on AI, networking, HPC, security, and sustainability.

title: I'm Passing on Intel Because IBM and Texas Instruments Are Better
summary: The author, a dividend investor, explains why they are passing on Intel (INTC) despite its high dividend yield, favoring International Business Machines (IBM) and Texas Instruments (TXN) instead. Intel's dividend history and lack of business model adaptation are concerns, while IBM offers a stronger history of business transformation and Texas Instruments boasts greater product diversification. The author concludes that while Intel has attractive features, the risks outweigh the benefits compared to their preferred investments.

title: Intel’s New Compliance Chief Navigates Geopolitics, Supply-Chain Shift
summary: Intel's new Chief Compliance Officer, Carol Tate, is tasked with navigating complex geopolitical and economic challenges as the company shifts manufacturing capabilities from Asia to the U.S. and Europe. This move comes as Intel faces a period of political tension, an increasingly challenging economic environment, and a 20% drop in third-quarter sales. Tate's role will be crucial in ensuring business integrity during this ambitious supply-chain restructuring and cost-cutting period.

title: Micron Technology Sees Chip Recovery by the End of 2023
summary: Micron Technology Inc. stock has fallen significantly in 2022 due to weak demand for DRAM chips and an oversupply in the market. Despite a challenging fiscal Q1 2023 with missed earnings and lowered guidance, Micron expects improving customer inventories to lead to increased revenue in the fiscal second half of 2023. Analysts have revised their ratings and price targets, with some maintaining overweight or buy ratings despite the current downturn.

title: TSMC starts mass-producing three-nanometer chips
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. Ltd. (TSMC) has begun mass-producing processors using its advanced three-nanometer chip technology. This new technology offers a 15% improvement in chip performance and up to 35% less power consumption compared to its earlier five-nanometer process. TSMC, a major contract chip manufacturer for companies like Apple, AMD, and Nvidia, plans to develop multiple versions of this technology and is building new fabrication plants in Arizona.

title: Sales of computer graphics cards slump to lowest level in two decades
summary: Sales of computer graphics cards hit a 20-year low in Q3 2022 due to a global economic slowdown and the impact of the Ethereum merge on cryptocurrency mining. Major chipmakers like AMD and Nvidia experienced significant declines in discrete desktop GPU shipments, with AMD particularly hard hit. Despite the slump, new high-end GPU launches and Intel's entry into the market (grabbing a 4% share) offer potential for future sales boosts.

title: Bitcoin Miners Got Crushed by Crypto Winter. 2023 May Bring More Pain
summary: The crypto winter of 2022 severely impacted Bitcoin miners due to high energy prices, increased competition, and a bear market, leading to bankruptcies and loan defaults for highly leveraged companies. Industry experts predict 2023 will continue to be challenging, with a focus on balance sheet strengthening, cost minimization, and debt reduction. The article also highlights the need for miners to adopt better hedging strategies, optimize power consumption, and explore new technologies to improve efficiency and reduce costs.

title: U.S. Pours Money Into Chips, but Even Soaring Spending Has Limits
summary: The U.S. is experiencing a significant surge in chip manufacturing investments, with companies pledging nearly $200 billion since early 2020. This boom, driven by a tech cold war with China, aims to solidify U.S. technological leadership and prevent China from dominating advanced chip production. Major companies like Intel, Micron Technology, and TSMC are investing billions in new factories and expansions across 16 states.

title: What is TSMC?
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) is the world's largest semiconductor manufacturer, producing chips for major tech companies like Apple, Amazon, and Google. It focuses solely on manufacturing for third parties, unlike competitors, and boasts significant market dominance and advanced process technology. The article also discusses the potential impact of a Chinese invasion of Taiwan on TSMC and its global operations, as well as the company's expansion plans in the US.

title: Logitech Products Now Verified for Engineered for Intel® Evo™ Accessory Program
summary: Logitech announced that several of its mice and keyboards have received Engineered for Intel® Evo™ verification, confirming they meet Intel's strict standards for reliability, interoperability, and security. This partnership aims to provide exceptional user experiences for Intel Evo laptop users in both consumer and enterprise settings. The verification covers various Logitech MX and Signature series products for business and general consumer use.

title: CES: Intel Extends Performance Leadership with World's Fastest Mobile Processor
summary: At CES 2023, Intel launched its 13th Gen Intel Core mobile processor family, including the flagship Intel Core i9-13980HX, dubbed the world's fastest mobile processor and the first 24-core processor for a laptop. Intel also introduced new 13th Gen Intel Core desktop processors and the Intel Processor N-series for education and entry-level computing. These advancements aim to deliver superior performance, better battery life, and enhanced experiences across various computing segments, from enthusiast gaming laptops to thin-and-light devices and IoT applications.

title: AMD Unveils Suite of New Radeon GPUs to Power High-Performance, Power-Efficient Gaming Laptops
summary: AMD announced its new Radeon RX 7000 Series Graphics for Laptops at CES 2023, built on the RDNA 3 architecture for exceptional energy efficiency and performance. This includes the Radeon RX 7000M Series for high performance-per-watt and the Radeon RX 7000S Series optimized for thin-and-light laptops. These new GPUs aim to deliver high-framerate gaming at ultra settings and support advanced features like dedicated AI and raytracing hardware, AV1 encoding, and AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution technology.

title: HP at CES 2023: Leading in Hybrid and Gaming Experiences
summary: At CES 2023, HP Inc. announced a new line-up of products and solutions aimed at enhancing hybrid work and gaming experiences, focusing on sustainability. Key introductions include the HP Dragonfly Pro for freelancers, the Poly Voyager Free 60 Series earbuds, new HP E-Series monitors, and OMEN Gaming Hub with NVIDIA GeForce NOW integration. HP also emphasized its commitment to sustainability by incorporating unique recycled materials like coffee grounds and ocean-bound plastics into its new PCs and peripherals.

title: Intel's Mobileye sees $17 bln in assisted-driving product revenues by 2030
summary: Intel Corp's self-driving tech unit, Mobileye Global Inc., projects over $17 billion in revenues from its advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) products by 2030. The company, a leading player in the ADAS market, also anticipates an additional $3.5 billion in revenues through 2028 from its autonomous mobility-as-a-service products. Mobileye expects the volume of its SuperVision product-based vehicles to reach 1.2 million units by 2026, driven by strong demand in China.

title: CES 2023: AMD, Nvidia, auto applications get the hype, but analysts say this one chip maker ruled
summary: At CES 2023, while companies like Advanced Micro Devices Inc. and Nvidia Corp. received significant attention, analysts suggest that a lower-profile chip maker is better positioned post-convention. Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore noted ongoing caution regarding overall chip demand, particularly due to softness in China, but highlighted autos as a strong theme at the event.

title: Wide Moat ETF Rebalance: Intel, BlackRock, Honeywell Demoted; Dominion, Fortinet Added
summary: The VanEck Morningstar Wide Moat ETF (MOAT) invests in US companies with wide economic moats, as determined by Morningstar's methodology. The article discusses MOAT's recent rebalancing in December, which saw Intel, BlackRock, and Honeywell removed, and Dominion Energy and Fortinet added. The author believes MOAT remains an effective tool for identifying blue-chip stocks at reasonable valuations.

title: Intel 4th Gen Xeon series offers a leap in data center performance and efficiency
summary: Intel has launched its 4th Gen Intel Xeon scalable processors, including the Intel Xeon CPU Max Series and Intel Data Center GPU Max Series, featuring enhanced security and new AI capabilities. These processors promise significant improvements in data center performance and efficiency, offering workload-first acceleration and optimized software for better total cost of ownership. The new line boasts built-in accelerators, power savings, and expanded confidential computing features designed to address critical computing challenges and sustainability goals.

title: Intel Launches 4th Gen Xeon Scalable Processors, Max Series CPUs and GPUs
summary: Intel has launched its 4th Gen Xeon Scalable processors, along with the Intel Xeon CPU Max Series and Intel Data Center GPU Max Series. These new processors aim to deliver significant improvements in performance, efficiency, and capabilities for AI, cloud, network, edge, and high-performance computing, emphasizing built-in accelerators and sustainability features. The company highlights broad industry adoption and performance gains across various key workloads.

title: Inari pares loss as analysts see limited impact from Apple's plan to drop Broadcom chip
summary: Inari Amertron Bhd shares recovered some losses after analysts indicated that Apple's plan to replace Broadcom chips with in-house designs would have a limited impact on Inari's outsourced semiconductor assembly and test (OSAT) business. Analysts noted Inari's direct exposure to Apple's revenue through Broadcom is relatively small (17-18%) and that Inari's involvement in complex RF filters is unlikely to be immediately affected. Despite a maintained "market perform" rating with a slightly reduced target price, some analysts suggest the negative news is already priced in, presenting a potential buying opportunity.

title: HPE and Intel combine forces to extend the next-gen ProLiant server portfolio in a hybrid world
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) and Intel have partnered to expand HPE's ProLiant Gen11 server portfolio, which is powered by Intel's 4th Gen Xeon scalable processors. This collaboration aims to address the growing demand for hybrid computing in enterprises, including supporting edge use cases and AI workloads. The new ProLiant models offer enhanced performance, security, and scalability for diverse IT environments.

title: IDC, Gartner: 2022 PC Demand Dropped To Its ‘Lowest Level In Years’
summary: PC demand in 2022 dropped significantly to its lowest level in years, according to reports from IDC and Gartner. This decline was attributed to global recession, inflation, higher interest rates, and consumers already owning newer PCs purchased during the pandemic. The business market too saw a decline as enterprise buyers extended PC life cycles and delayed purchases, with growth not expected until 2024.

title: Taiwan ASE plant listed as 'lighthouse factory' by WEF
summary: Advanced Semiconductor Engineering Inc (ASE) has been recognized as a "lighthouse factory" by the World Economic Forum (WEF) for its Kaohsiung bumping factory. The factory was inducted into the WEF's Global Lighthouse Network due to its adoption of Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) technologies, specifically AI-enabled processes. These innovations led to a 67% increase in output and a 39% reduction in order lead time.

title: Intel Corp. stock falls Tuesday, underperforms market
summary: Intel Corp. (INTC) shares fell 1.69% on Tuesday, closing at $29.60, underperforming the broader market as the S&P 500 and Dow Jones also declined. This marked the second consecutive day of losses for the stock, leaving it $26.05 short of its 52-week high. Competitor NVIDIA Corp. (NVDA) saw gains, rising 4.75%.

title: Intel is committed to Germany chip plant, working with government -exec
summary: Intel is reaffirming its commitment to building a chip fabrication plant in Magdeburg, Germany, despite recent reports suggesting a delay. Chief Global Operations Officer Keyvan Esfarjani stated that Intel is actively working with the German government on funding details for the project. The company acknowledges increased geopolitical challenges, declining semiconductor demand, and global economic disruptions since the initial announcement.

title: Intel Chairman Omar Ishrak steps down
summary: Intel announced that Chairman Omar Ishrak has stepped down and Frank Yeary has been appointed as his replacement.
