Ishrak, who became chairman in 2020 and was instrumental in bringing CEO Pat Gelsinger back to the company, will continue to serve as an independent director on Intel's board. Yeary, a long-time Intel director, also serves on the boards of PayPal Holdings and Intel's Mobileye.

title: Intel Board of Directors Appoints Frank D. Yeary as New Independent Chair
summary: Intel Corporation has announced the appointment of Frank D. Yeary as the new independent chair of its board of directors, succeeding Dr. Omar Ishrak. Yeary, who has been on Intel's board since 2009, will lead the board during a pivotal time for the company. Dr. Ishrak will remain on the board as an independent director, continuing his service on key committees.

title: Bad Week of Earnings Pulls Q4 Growth Rate Lower
summary: The S&P 500's blended EPS growth for Q4 decreased to -4.6% due to a week of mixed earnings reports from major financial institutions and other companies. This marks the lowest growth rate in over two years, driven by factors like increased loan loss provisions and consumer credit deterioration. Despite the decline, the earnings season is picking up, with key companies like Microsoft, Tesla, IBM, and Intel expected to report, and some companies like Danaher and Weyerhaeuser signaling positive results.

title: ASML beats earnings forecasts, sees 2023 growth amid China worries
summary: ASML Holding NV exceeded fourth-quarter earnings forecasts and projects over 25% sales growth in 2023, despite potential new U.S. export restrictions affecting its sales to China. The company's order backlog reached a record 40 billion euros, indicating strong demand from major semiconductor manufacturers. CEO Peter Wennink noted that economic conditions are expected to improve towards the end of the year, with China's economy recovering, maintaining high demand for ASML's equipment.

title: Intel shares drop on weak quarterly results and expectations for another quarter of losses
summary: Intel's shares fell after the company reported weaker-than-expected fourth-quarter results and provided a soft forecast for the first quarter of 2023. The chipmaker experienced a significant decline in revenue for the fourth consecutive quarter due to a downturn in PC chip sales and broader economic headwinds. The company also announced a net loss for Q4 and anticipates further losses in Q1, leading to concerns about its financial performance in the near future.

title: Intel's horrible quarter revealed an inventory glut and underused factories
summary: Intel's Q4 earnings revealed significant declines in sales, profit, and gross margin, with the company forecasting a challenging 2023. These struggles are attributed to an inventory glut in the PC and server markets and underutilized factories leading to inefficient performance. Despite the grim outlook, Intel maintained its dividend and saw a bright spot with its self-driving subsidiary, Mobileye, reporting strong growth.

title: Licking County becomes the 'bullseye' for development one year after Intel
summary: One year after Intel announced its plans for a $20 billion computer chip manufacturing facility in Licking County, Ohio, the area has become a hub for development. This growth has led to escalating property values and changes in the rural landscape, causing some residents to relocate. While the Intel project promises significant economic benefits and job creation, local officials are also grappling with challenges like funding disparities and utility expansion conflicts.

title: Intel's 'historic collapse' erases $8 billion from market value
summary: Intel Corp saw its market value drop by about $8 billion after it issued dismal earnings projections and a surprise loss forecast for the first quarter, exacerbating fears about a slump in the personal-computer market and slowing data center growth. Analysts view this as a "historic collapse," with Intel losing market share to rivals like AMD due to superior chip technology and price-performance advantages. CEO Pat Gelsinger faces significant challenges in his efforts to restore Intel's dominance, with some analysts suggesting the company may need to cut its dividend due to high capital expenditures.

title: An open-source option is shaking up the microchip industry
summary: RISC-V, an open-standard instruction set architecture developed at UC Berkeley, is rapidly gaining traction in the microchip industry. Unlike proprietary ISAs like x86 and ARM, RISC-V offers flexibility, scalability, and extensibility, allowing companies to design custom, optimized chips for various devices, from laptops to autonomous car systems. This open approach is saving tech companies time and money, and its adoption is expected to continue growing significantly in the coming years.

title: ‘Everybody was wrong’: The $160B memory-chip sector is suffering one of its worst routs ever despite vows to escape the boom-and-bust cycle
summary: The $160 billion memory-chip sector is currently experiencing one of its most severe and unprecedented downturns, despite earlier industry assurances of more predictable earnings. This crisis, marked by chip gluts, plunging prices, and order cuts, is severely impacting major players like SK Hynix Inc. and Micron Technology Inc., destabilizing suppliers, and affecting Asian economies. The industry is responding with production cuts and investment reductions, with consolidation expected, particularly in the NAND market, as companies navigate a complex mix of economic challenges.

title: Hinge Health names Deborah Conrad as first chief marketing officer
summary: Hinge Health has appointed Deborah Conrad, formerly Intel's chief marketing officer, as its first chief marketing officer. Conrad will be responsible for leading the company's brand expansion into new and existing markets, as well as refining customer and partner experiences. Her appointment comes as Hinge Health, a digital clinic for chronic pain, experiences significant growth, including a recent launch of a physical therapy house call service and a $300 million Series D funding round in 2021.

title: Points of light for Intel Israel
summary: Intel's recent financial results were poor, and its acquisition of Tower Semiconductor faces delays due to US-China tensions. Despite these challenges, there are positive developments for Intel Israel, including the continued success of Mobileye, positive reception for the Sapphire Rapids processor, and the upcoming Lunar Lake processor and a new Kiryat Gat fab.

title: AMD CEO promises to keep taking data-center from Intel even as cloud demand pauses following 'strong' 2022
summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su has pledged to continue gaining data-center market share from Intel, despite an anticipated pause in cloud demand after a robust 2022. Following Intel's worst year since the dot-com bust, AMD's stock rose as its Q1 2023 sales forecast of over $5 billion reassured Wall Street. This positive outlook comes as AMD's data-center sales grew, even with initial light demand from cloud customers.

title: Why AMD is faring much better than Intel in the same tough economy
summary: AMD is showing a much brighter short-term outlook compared to Intel, despite both operating in a difficult chip market. While Intel faces widespread declines, AMD's data center business is experiencing growth, aided by new chip introductions and its acquisition of Xilinx. Both companies are affected by a slumping PC market, but AMD expects to gain market share in that segment and grow its data center revenue at Intel's expense this year.

title: Data Center Sales Drive AMD's Q4 Resurgence
summary: AMD reported better-than-expected Q4 results, driven by strong data center sales which helped offset declining demand for PC chips. The company forecasts first-quarter revenue of up to $5.6 billion and expects its data center business to continue growing, allowing it to gain market share against rivals like Intel despite an overall chip glut.

title: AMD revenue beats targets, Wall St relieved after Intel's grim outlook
summary: AMD announced revenue that surpassed Wall Street expectations, with its shares rising about 1.5% in after-hours trading, offering relief to investors following a grim outlook from rival Intel. Despite a challenging PC market leading to a 51% drop in client segment revenue for AMD, its Data Center segment saw a 42% increase. CEO Lisa Su expressed confidence in continued market share gains and a stronger second half of the year, contrasting with Intel's struggles in inventory correction and slumping PC business.

title: Pegatron, Intel Develop a Portable 5G Network Solution for Taiwan’s Emergency Responders
summary: Pegatron and Intel have partnered to develop a portable 5G network-in-a-box base station for emergency responders in Hsinchu, Taiwan. This solution addresses connectivity challenges in remote, mountainous areas by providing low-latency, multiparty voice and video communication with high-resolution streaming, powered by a portable generator. The system utilizes Intel hardware and software, including Xeon Scalable processors, and is designed to create a reliable network for disaster relief, with future updates planned to enhance its technology and portability.

title: Brutal Earnings From Intel Signal Changing of Guard in Chips
summary: Intel's recent devastating earnings report highlights a significant shift in the semiconductor industry, with Asian giants Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) and Samsung Electronics Co. demonstrating greater resilience amidst a market downturn. While Intel and SK Hynix faced record losses and cut spending, TSMC and Samsung maintained capital expenditure plans, showcasing their emerging dominance. This signals a changing of the guard, as Intel, once an industry leader, struggles to keep pace.

title: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger and CFO David Zinsner Buy Up Stock
summary: Intel's CEO Pat Gelsinger and CFO David Zinsner have purchased shares of the chipmaker on the open market. This move follows the company's release of disappointing guidance. This isn't the first time these executives have bought company stock under similar circumstances.

title: ParkerVision Settles with Intel in Judge Albright’s Court
summary: ParkerVision has settled its patent infringement lawsuit against Intel in Judge Alan Albright's court in Waco, Texas, just three years after filing. The article highlights Judge Albright's effective courtroom management in driving settlements by balancing risks for both parties, contrasting this with lengthy litigation processes seen in other courts. Despite the settlement, ParkerVision continues with other patent infringement cases against companies like TCL, LG, MediaTek, and RealTek in the same court.

title: Qualcomm Introduces the World's First 5G NR-Light Modem-RF System to Fuel a New Wave of 5G Devices
summary: Qualcomm Technologies has announced the Snapdragon X35 5G Modem-RF System, the world’s first 5G NR-Light modem-RF system, designed to bridge the gap between high-speed mobile broadband and low-bandwidth IoT devices. This new system aims to expand 5G to new use cases like smartwatches, XR glasses, and industrial IoT by offering smaller, more cost-efficient devices with longer battery life. Customer sampling is expected in the first half of 2023, with commercial devices launching by the first half of 2024.

title: Fortinet Continues to Invest in Custom Chips to Power Security Offerings
summary: Fortinet continues to invest in its custom ASIC chip strategy, which executives claim is a unique differentiator and a key factor in the vendor's firewall market dominance. The company designs its own Arm-based ASICs for its products to achieve better performance, efficiency, and cost savings compared to traditional CPU-based solutions. This long-term investment also provides supply chain advantages and contributes to Fortinet's leading market share in firewall appliance unit shipments.

title: AMD wins nearly a third of processor market, Arm's climb slows: analyst report
summary: AMD has significantly increased its market share in central processor units, capturing nearly a third of the x86 processor market from Intel, according to a recent analyst report from Mercury Research. This growth occurred during the worst PC chip market downturn since the 1980s. Meanwhile, Arm's rise in the PC market, primarily driven by Apple's Mac computers, slowed in the fourth quarter of 2022.

title: Intel’s New Semiconductor Plants Need Workers. Community Colleges Are Stepping Up.
summary: Intel is investing $20 billion in two new semiconductor plants in central Ohio, creating over 12,000 jobs, most of which will only require an associate degree. Community colleges in Ohio are collaborating with Intel to develop curriculum to train the necessary workforce, adapting existing manufacturing courses and adding new specialized training. This initiative highlights the crucial role community colleges play in rapidly addressing local workforce demands and is part of a broader national effort to boost advanced manufacturing.

title: Exclusive: Intel weighs boost to investment in Vietnam chip packaging plant
summary: Intel Corp is reportedly considering a significant increase, possibly around $1 billion, to its existing $1.5-billion investment in Vietnam to expand its chip testing and packaging plant. This potential move highlights Vietnam's growing importance in the global semiconductor supply chain, as companies seek to diversify away from China and Taiwan due to geopolitical risks. While other locations like Singapore and Malaysia are also being considered, an expansion in Vietnam would help Intel manage supply disruptions and align with Vietnam's aggressive push to grow its chipmaking industry.

title: GM locks up chip supply in deal with GlobalFoundries
summary: General Motors Co (GM) has signed a deal with GlobalFoundries Inc to secure semiconductor production capacity, aiming to alleviate the chip shortage that has impacted auto production for several years. GlobalFoundries will dedicate capacity at its New York plant for chips used in GM vehicles, with GM planning for its chip design suppliers to utilize this production for essential components. This move is intended to ensure a resilient supply of critical technology as GM anticipates its semiconductor requirements more than doubling in the coming years.

title: Corsair Vengeance RGB DDR5-6000 C30 Review: Optimized For Zen 4
summary: The Corsair Vengeance RGB DDR5-6000 C30 memory kit is an enthusiast-grade option optimized for AMD's Ryzen 7000 processors with EXPO profiles. It offers stable performance out-of-the-box and features a clean design with customizable RGB lighting. While it performs well, especially on Intel systems, its overclocking headroom is limited due to requiring 1.4V for its advertised speeds.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: Shares of Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) fell by 0.90% to $85.18 on Wednesday, despite a generally positive trading session for the stock market. The decline broke a two-day winning streak for AMD, which closed significantly below its 52-week high. Compared to competitors, AMD's performance was mixed, with NVIDIA Corp. also falling, while Intel Corp. and Applied Materials Inc. saw gains.

title: How ASML’s big bet on China turned into headaches over data thefts
summary: ASML, a Dutch semiconductor equipment giant, is facing mounting questions regarding data thefts in China, a market that has become its third-largest. Despite its CEO Peter Wennink defending the company's significant presence there, new revelations about intellectual property theft by a former employee could lead to tighter controls on ASML amid escalating US-China tech tensions. The company's near-monopoly on advanced lithography systems makes it a critical target for espionage, highlighting the challenges of balancing market growth with technology protection in a sensitive geopolitical landscape.

title: Meet the 16 members of the EDA Alliance underpinning TSMC dominance
summary: This article details the 16 members of the TSMC EDA Alliance, which is crucial for TSMC's continued dominance in the foundry industry. It emphasizes the importance of Electronic Design Automation (EDA) in IC design and provides profiles of each company within the alliance, including their headquarters, specialties, and market presence. The alliance helps IC design houses optimize their designs for TSMC's sophisticated manufacturing processes.

title: Ronnie Vasishta Author Page
summary: This page profiles Ronnie Vasishta, NVIDIA's Head of Telecom and a key figure in the company's strategy and products for the telecommunications sector. It highlights his extensive background in the semiconductor and networking industries, including his previous roles at Intel and eASIC Corporation. The page also features a collection of articles authored or co-authored by Vasishta, focusing on AI trends in telecom, network optimization, and the adoption of generative AI in the industry.

title: Intel slashes dividend by over 65%, to 12.5 cents
summary: Intel has announced a significant cut to its quarterly dividend, reducing it by over 65% from 36.5 cents to 12.5 cents. This decision follows weeks of broader cost-cutting measures by the company and comes after a weak financial performance in Q4 2022, which included a $664 million net loss. CEO Pat Gelsinger stated the board considers the dividend critical and intends to resume its growth over time.

title: Intel cuts dividend to lowest since 2007 to save cash
summary: Intel Corp has cut its quarterly dividend payout by 66% to $0.125 per share, the lowest level since 2007, to conserve cash. This decision comes as the company faces slowing demand for its chips amid a deteriorating macro-economic environment and aims to scale back major investments in manufacturing equipment. CEO Pat Gelsinger indicated the move was necessary to prioritize capital for its foundry business and achieve significant cost reductions by 2025.

title: Meet the $10,000 Nvidia chip powering the race for A.I.
summary: The Nvidia A100 chip, costing around $10,000, has become a critical tool in the artificial intelligence industry, powering applications like ChatGPT and Stable Diffusion. Nvidia dominates the market for GPUs used in machine learning, with the A100 serving as a "workhorse" for AI professionals and requiring substantial investment for companies to train and deploy AI models. Despite the high costs and emerging competition, Nvidia's CEO Jensen Huang asserts that their GPUs offer a much more affordable solution for developing sophisticated AI models compared to traditional CPU-based data centers.

title: Intel Corp. stock outperforms market on strong trading day
summary: Shares of Intel Corp. (INTC) increased by 0.55% to $25.61 on Thursday, outperforming the S&P 500 Index and the Dow Jones Industrial Average, which rose 0.53% and 0.33% respectively. This rise ended a four-day losing streak for Intel, although the stock remains significantly below its 52-week high. Despite the overall positive day for Intel, it underperformed some competitors like NVIDIA Corp.

title: Drone market size is estimated to grow by USD 27.78 billion between 2022 and 2027, Historic market size was valued at USD 19.72 billion in 2017- Technavio
summary: The global drone market is projected to grow by USD 27.78 billion between 2022 and 2027, accelerating at a CAGR of 13.58%. This significant growth is driven by advancements in sensor technology and the increasing availability of low-cost drones. The Asia-Pacific (APAC) region is expected to contribute 38% to this growth, with China being a primary revenue driver due to widespread drone adoption in various industries.

title: Cisco Showcases Simple and Secure Wireless Experiences to Help Businesses Connect at MWC 23
summary: Cisco is showcasing new wireless, cloud, and IoT solutions at MWC 23, including its Mobility Services platform, Meraki 5G Gateways, and Wi-Fi 6E products, to simplify and secure connectivity for businesses. The company is partnering with global service providers and integrating Webex into Mercedes-Benz vehicles, while also announcing collaborations with Intel, NEC, NTT, and Qualcomm to advance 5G and IoT adoption. Cisco aims to help businesses navigate the digital era by providing innovative 5G and Wi-Fi connectivity solutions.

title: An open standard threatens to disrupt the cloistered world of wireless networking
summary: The Open RAN standard is poised to disrupt the proprietary wireless networking market by enabling organizations to build their own 5G networks using off-the-shelf hardware and software. This open architecture promises to reduce costs, foster innovation among smaller vendors, and accelerate market entry for new applications, similar to how Unix and TCP/IP transformed data centers. While facing challenges in maturity, security, and ROI, industry attitudes are shifting, with major carriers and tech companies beginning to embrace Open RAN, especially as 6G approaches and demands increased network virtualization.

title: Intel Accelerates 5G Leadership with New Products
summary: At MWC 2023, Intel unveiled new products and solutions designed to accelerate its 5G leadership. Key announcements include the 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable processors with Intel vRAN Boost, offering significant capacity gains and power savings by integrating Layer 1 acceleration, and the Intel Infrastructure Power Manager for 5G core reference software for power efficiency. The company also demonstrated breakthrough 5G user plane function (UPF) workload performance and introduced the Intel Converged Edge Media Platform for flexible edge services, reinforcing its commitment to virtualized and cloud-native networks.

title: MWC 2023: Iceotope pairs with HPE, Intel, and nVent to launch Ku:l Extreme
summary: At MWC 2023, Iceotope, in collaboration with HPE, Intel, and nVent, unveiled Ku:l Extreme, a new scalable Open RAN liquid-cooling solution. This offering is designed to support "Far Edge" computing across the entire telco data center estate, emphasizing sustainability, energy efficiency, and serviceability. The modular design allows for deployment from single servers at base stations to enterprise-grade data centers, aiming to reduce energy consumption and maintenance costs.

title: Applied Materials to Challenge ASML's Grip With New Machines
summary: Applied Materials Inc. has introduced a new chipmaking machine, the Centura Sculpta, designed to reduce the industry's reliance on ASML Holding NV. This machine aims to make ultra-powerful semiconductors more cheaply, particularly those used for artificial intelligence tasks, by streamlining the lithography process and reducing the need for multiple patterning. While not directly challenging ASML's extreme ultraviolet lithography, Applied Materials' innovation seeks to complement existing methods and reduce production costs, energy, and water consumption for chipmakers.

title: Intel Releases Quantum Software Development Kit Version 1.0 to Grow Developer Ecosystem
summary: Intel has released version 1.0 of its Quantum Software Development Kit (SDK) to foster a developer ecosystem for quantum computing. The SDK includes a full quantum computer simulation that can interface with Intel's quantum hardware and offers a customizable C++ based development environment. This initiative aims to accelerate the development of quantum applications and solutions, with beta users already exploring diverse use cases.

title: This university is addressing the shortage of U.S. semiconductor engineers
summary: Purdue University has launched an interdisciplinary Semiconductor Degrees Program to address the shortage of U.S. semiconductor engineers, partnering with major chip companies. This initiative includes undergraduate and graduate training, associate degrees, and an advisory board of industry executives to shape the curriculum and recruit talent. The program also involves plans for a MediaTek chip design center and a SkyWater Technology manufacturing facility on Purdue's campus, reinforcing the university's commitment to supporting the domestic chip industry.

title: How Phoenix became the "semiconductor desert"
summary: Phoenix has earned the nickname "semiconductor desert" due to decades of strategic development, culminating in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.'s significant investment, including a planned $40 billion total investment for two factories. This growth, supported by existing semiconductor businesses and federal initiatives like the CHIPS and Science Act, has brought high-wage jobs and positioned Arizona as a national leader in semiconductor investment. The success despite concerns about potential cultural clashes and the company's R&D focus is due to the U.S. government's push for domestic chip production, viewing it as crucial for national security and reducing reliance on China.

title: MWC: KT (Korea) partners with Intel to become first Wi-Fi 7 operator, Qualcomm claims 175 Wi-Fi 7 design wins
summary: During Mobile World Congress, Korea Telecom (KT) announced a partnership with Intel to develop and promote Wi-Fi 7 services, aiming to be the world's first operator to deploy the standard. Simultaneously, Qualcomm revealed it has secured 175 design wins for new Wi-Fi 7-capable access points and end-user devices, showcasing its strong position in the nascent Wi-Fi 7 market. Both companies are moving forward with Wi-Fi 7 technology, with Intel expected to release chipsets later this year and Qualcomm already shipping numerous Wi-Fi products.

title: China's chip sector needs more than state money to dull impact of US restrictions
summary: China plans to invest $140 billion to bolster its domestic chip sector against US export restrictions, but industry experts suggest that financial aid alone may not be sufficient. Chinese firms struggle to innovate and move up the value chain due to a lack of exposure to advanced chipmaking facilities and a reliance on emulating existing foreign technology. Some industry insiders advocate for a strategic shift towards R&D for new technologies rather than trying to catch up with current Western chipmaking methods.

title: Why Hidden Artificial Intelligence Features Make Such an Impact in Education
summary: This article discusses how hidden Artificial Intelligence features, particularly those developed by Qualcomm Technologies, Inc., are significantly impacting education. It highlights Qualcomm's role in providing connectivity, efficient processing, and AI at the edge, emphasizing the benefits of extended battery life in devices and advanced AI functionalities like noise cancellation for educators. The piece also underlines the importance of addressing the digital divide and encourages educators to embrace AI tools to enhance learning experiences and develop higher critical thinking skills in students.

title: Smaller Oregon companies looking for semiconductor funding
summary: Oregon lawmakers are preparing to allocate $200 million to attract federal semiconductor funding by supporting local companies and universities. While much attention has been on large fabrication plants, a network of smaller firms like Inpria and HP Inc. in Corvallis also contribute significantly to chip manufacturing. State Representative Janelle Bynum emphasized the need for a diverse workforce and better return on investment in the semiconductor industry, advocating for varied educational paths from apprenticeships to advanced degrees.

title: New UH Program Introduces Students to the Vast Potential of Artificial Intelligence
summary: The University of Houston C.T. Bauer College of Business has partnered with Intel Corp. to launch a new "AI for Entrepreneurs" business certification program. This program, focusing on the role of artificial intelligence in business, aims to prepare students for the rapidly expanding field of AI, which is projected to significantly contribute to the global economy. Houston Mayor Sylvester Turner recognized the initiative by proclaiming Feb. 7, 2023, as AI Innovation and Entrepreneurship Day.

title: ASUS Intel 700, 600 Series Motherboards Support 48 GB of DDR5-7000 modules
summary: ASUS has released BIOS updates for its Intel 700 and 600 series motherboards, adding support for up to 192 GB of DDR5 memory. This includes support for new Corsair Vengeance DDR5 48 GB (2 x 24 GB) 7000 MT/s modules, allowing for up to 48 GB of DDR5 RAM at 7000 MT/s. The update caters to enthusiasts and professionals who require high-capacity, high-speed memory for demanding workloads like gaming, video editing, and 3D modeling.

title: Global Intelligent Vending Machines Market to Reach $21.4 Billion by 2030
summary: The global Intelligent Vending Machines market is projected to grow from US$9.2 Billion in 2022 to US$21.4 Billion by 2030, exhibiting an 11.1% CAGR. This growth is driven by various factors despite global economic uncertainties, with particular segments like 'Hot Drinks' and key regions like China showing strong growth potential. The report also highlights the impact of technological advancements and changing consumer behaviors on the market.

title: ASML, China customers haunted by uncertainty on new Dutch chip export rules
summary: The Dutch government's newly announced restrictions on chip-technology exports to China have left ASML Holding NV and its Chinese customers uncertain about crucial details, including whether ASML can service existing chip-printing machines in China. While potentially aligning with U.S. goals, the lack of clarity on servicing limits and affected machine models poses a significant challenge for ASML's outlook and its substantial sales and service agreements in the Chinese market. The situation could lead Chinese chipmakers to focus on older technology, shifting ASML's demand to other regions.

title: Centura Sculpta chip-making tool puts China further behind
summary: US firm Applied Materials has introduced a new pattern-shaping tool called Centura Sculpta, which is expected to significantly reduce the cost and complexity of advanced node chip production by decreasing the need for EUV lithography steps. This innovation further widens the technology gap for China, which is already restricted from accessing EUV tools due to US sanctions. While China can produce older-generation chips, the Sculpta tool could accelerate progress towards smaller process nodes, challenging China's ability to compete in next-generation semiconductor manufacturing.

title: Exclusive: Chip equipment maker ASML's suppliers eye Asia plants outside China amid tensions
summary: Suppliers to ASML, a major chip-making machine company, are exploring establishing manufacturing plants in Southeast Asia, specifically Vietnam and Malaysia, instead of China. This shift is driven by geopolitical tensions between Beijing and Western nations, and a long-term strategy to reduce exposure to China. The move follows U.S. pressure on the Netherlands to restrict sales of advanced chip machinery to China, impacting companies like TSMC, Samsung, and Intel.

title: Ssangyong E&C wins contract for building ASML Korea campus
summary: Ssangyong Engineering & Construction secured a 200-billion-won ($170-million) contract to build a new campus for ASML Holding NV in Hwaseong, South Korea. The campus, with two buildings totaling 74,418 square meters, will house offices and research facilities for ASML, specializing in extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography equipment. This project aims to expand ASML's local supply chain and customer support by enabling more parts procurement in Korea and expanding its partner firm base.

title: Federal Circuit Allows Apple’s Fintiv Challenge To Proceed
summary: The Federal Circuit Court of Appeals has revived Apple's challenge to the PTAB's Fintiv practice of discretionary denials, allowing it to proceed to the district court. This decision questions the USPTO's ability to issue rules through precedential decisions rather than traditional notice-and-comment rulemaking. The resolution of this case could significantly impact how the USPTO operates regarding Inter Partes Review practices.

title: ArcelorMittal to produce torches, cauldrons, rings, agitos in Paris 2024 partnership
summary: ArcelorMittal has partnered with the Paris 2024 Olympic and Paralympic Games to produce key steel items including torches, cauldrons, and the Olympic and Paralympic logos. The company aims to demonstrate the low-carbon potential of steel in line with Paris 2024's environmental goals. This marks another instance of ArcelorMittal's involvement in major multi-sport events, having previously sponsored the London 2012 Olympics and the 2015 Pan American Games.

title: Artificial Intelligence in China: Implications and Opportunities
summary: The rapid adoption of generative AI platforms like ChatGPT has highlighted China's strong position to benefit from the AI trend. Driven by massive data, a skilled workforce, supportive government policies, and an advanced manufacturing base, Chinese companies like Baidu, ByteDance, Alibaba, and Tencent are poised to become leaders in generative AI. The article details the foundational strengths of China's AI ecosystem and the potential beneficiaries within its tech and semiconductor industries.

title: AMD Shoots Down EPYC Genoa Memory Bug Claims, Says Update On Track
summary: AMD has refuted recent claims of a memory bug affecting its EPYC Genoa processors that would necessitate a costly redesign to support 2DPC memory configurations. The company stated that all shipped fourth-gen EPYC processors fully support 2DPC and that BIOS updates have already been issued to OEM partners to enable this functionality by the end of Q1 2023. This clarifies previous vague statements and assures that no respin is required for the anticipated memory capacity boost.

title: Raja Koduri, Main Driver Of Intel’s GPU Efforts, To Leave Company
summary: Raja Koduri, Intel's chief architect and the main driver behind its discrete GPU efforts, is leaving the company at the end of March to start a new software company focused on generative AI for gaming, media, and entertainment. Koduri had been with Intel for over five years, leading the buildout of its graphics capabilities and serving as chief architect for most of his tenure. His departure comes as Intel announced cuts to its data center GPU roadmap and delays to Falcon Shores.

title: Liquid Cooled AI Development Platform
summary: Supermicro has launched a new liquid-cooled AI development platform, developed in partnership with NVIDIA, designed for advanced AI developers. This system features four liquid-cooled NVIDIA A100 Tensor Core GPUs, offering over 2 Petaflops of AI performance in a quiet, self-contained unit suitable for various environments. It is available with optional NVIDIA AI Enterprise software and can be customized to specific requirements.

title: Supermicro Expands GPU Solutions Portfolio with Deskside Liquid-Cooled AI Development Platform, Powered by NVIDIA
summary: Supermicro has launched a new liquid-cooled AI development platform, the SYS-751GE-TNRT-NV1, designed for deskside use. This platform features four NVIDIA A100 Tensor Core GPUs and two 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable CPUs, providing a powerful, quiet, and energy-efficient solution for AI/HPC workloads. It also includes a three-year subscription to NVIDIA AI Enterprise, simplifying software setup for developers.

title: Givinga Appoints Drew St. Germain as Chief Operating Officer
summary: Givinga, a philanthropic technology company based in Boston, has appointed Drew St. Germain as its first Chief Operating Officer. St. Germain, who previously served on the executive team at InfoArmor and facilitated its sale to Allstate Identity Corporation, will lead Givinga's operational core platform and system integrations to support the company's next phase of growth. He is known for building strong operational teams and customer value, and is also involved in volunteer work with Habitat for Humanity and co-founded a nonprofit focused on carbon sequestration.

title: Gordon E. Moore, Intel Co-Founder Behind Moore’s Law, Dies at 94 (Published 2023)
summary: Gordon E. Moore, co-founder of Intel and the visionary behind Moore's Law, has died at the age of 94. His 1965 prediction about the exponential growth of transistors on computer chips guided the development of the high-tech industry for decades. Moore, along with Robert Noyce, built Intel into a dominant force, transforming electronics and making microprocessors ubiquitous in modern devices.

title: Gordon Moore, Intel Co-Founder, Dies at 94
summary: Gordon Moore, co-founder of Intel and originator of Moore's Law, has passed away at the age of 94. His vision profoundly impacted the semiconductor industry and technology development, and he dedicated his later years to philanthropy through the Gordon and Betty Moore Foundation, which has donated over $5.1 billion to various causes. Intel's current CEO and other leaders recognize his immense contributions to the company and the technological world.

title: Intel co-founder and philanthropist Gordon Moore has died at 94
summary: Gordon Moore, the visionary co-founder of Intel and profound philanthropist, has passed away at the age of 94. He was widely recognized for his groundbreaking "Moore's Law," which accurately predicted the exponential growth of computing power, significantly influencing the trajectory of the digital age. Beyond his technological contributions, Moore, alongside his wife, established a foundation that has donated over $5.1 billion to environmental conservation, science, and patient care.

title: AutoCAD for Mac 2024: New Collaboration Enhancements and Improved Performance With M-Series Chips
summary: AutoCAD for Mac 2024 and AutoCAD LT for Mac 2024 have been released, introducing significant performance enhancements, particularly for Apple Silicon M-series chips, which can double performance compared to the previous version. The new release focuses on improving collaboration and automation with features like Markup Import and Markup Assist, Smart Blocks for automatic block placement, and updates to the Trace environment. Additionally, it includes 3D Color Background customization, 3D Connexion device support, and new language support for Italian and Spanish.

title: Micron Could Report Largest Ever Loss in Q2
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) is projected to report its largest-ever loss in the second fiscal quarter of 2023, with an estimated net loss of nearly $1.2 billion and EPS of -$1.06, a significant drop from the prior year. This financial struggle is attributed to weak consumer demand, high inflation, inventory issues, and industry-wide supply constraints. Analysts predict negative gross margins for the first time in 15 years, with an expected inventory write-down in the range of a billion dollars.

title: Intel’s stock set for best month in 22 years as execs lay out plans to take on AMD’s rise in servers
summary: Intel's stock experienced its best month since 2001, with shares jumping 7.6% on Wednesday after an investor presentation detailed the company's strategy to regain market share in data-center chips. The company aims to reverse its manufacturing decline and compete more effectively against AMD. This surge pushed Intel's March gain to 26.4%.

title: The Netherlands Joins US Effort To Deny China Semiconductor Technology
summary: The Netherlands has announced plans to ban the export of semiconductor technology to China, aligning with US efforts to limit China's access to advanced chipmaking capabilities due to national security concerns. This move is significant as Dutch suppliers, particularly ASML, are crucial for essential chipmaking tools. While ASML states the ban will not materially affect its 2023 financial outlook, China has condemned these "bullying acts" as harmful to market principles and global supply chains.

title: 3 Best Desktop Computers for Business from HP India
summary: This article identifies and reviews the three best HP desktop computers for business use: the HP Workstation Z4 G4 Tower, the HP Z1 G9 Tower Desktop PC, and the HP Z2 Mini G9 Workstation Wolf Pro Security Edition. It outlines key features to consider when purchasing a business desktop, including form factor, processor, graphics, memory, storage, and security. The article emphasizes that the ideal business computer depends on specific needs like raw processing power, upgradability, or a compact design.

title: WSJ News Exclusive | China’s New Tech Weapon: Dragging Its Feet on Global Merger Approvals
summary: China is reportedly using delayed antitrust reviews of proposed global mergers involving American companies as a new tactic in its technology competition with the U.S. Officials are demanding that U.S. companies make products available in China that they sell elsewhere, creating a difficult position for American firms due to existing U.S. export controls. This strategy is seen as a counter to increased U.S. sanctions targeting China's tech sector.

title: Intel Foundry and Arm Announce Multigeneration Collaboration on Leading-Edge SoC Design
summary: Intel Foundry Services (IFS) and Arm have announced a multigeneration agreement to enable chip designers to build low-power compute system-on-chips (SoCs) on Intel's 18A process technology. This collaboration will initially focus on mobile SoC designs, with potential expansion into other sectors like automotive and data centers. The partnership aims to provide customers with advanced Arm CPU IP and Intel's leading-edge manufacturing capabilities, fostering a more balanced global supply chain.

title: Intel to Work With Arm to Boost Its Outsourced Production Effort
summary: Intel Corp. is partnering with Arm Ltd. to strengthen its outsourced chip manufacturing efforts. This agreement will enable mobile-phone chip designers to utilize Intel's 18A production technique and aims to expand into other sectors like automotive and data centers in the future.

title: Empowering The Industry with Open System Firmware – AMD openSIL
summary: AMD has introduced openSIL, an open-source silicon initialization library, to address challenges in firmware such as security vulnerabilities and limited transparency. This initiative aims to improve platform security, functionality, and traceability by opening up firmware architecture and development to the open-source community. AMD is collaborating with several organizations to develop and demonstrate openSIL, which reorganizes its existing AGESA framework into a more flexible and scalable solution for various host firmware types.

title: 3 Best HP Pavilion Laptops For Entertainment | HP® Tech Takes
summary: This article outlines the key features to consider when choosing an entertainment laptop, focusing on display size, audio, storage, memory, and graphics. It then presents three top HP Pavilion laptop models—the Pavilion x360, Pavilion 15, and Pavilion Gaming laptop—highlighting their specifications and suitability for various entertainment needs. The guide aims to help users find a budget-friendly and high-performance device for gaming, streaming, or general multimedia consumption.

title: Tata Electronics Appoints Dr. Randhir Thakur as Chief Executive Officer & Managing Director
summary: Tata Electronics Private Limited (TEPL) has appointed Dr. Randhir Thakur, a prominent leader in the semiconductor industry, as its Chief Executive Officer & Managing Director. Dr. Thakur brings over 40 years of experience from global manufacturing, R&D, and P&L management, including his recent role as President of Intel Foundry Services. N. Chandrasekaran, Chairman of Tata Sons, expressed confidence that Dr. Thakur's leadership will significantly advance Tata Electronics' role in India's semiconductor and precision manufacturing sectors.

title: HiddenLayer Announces Machine Learning Security Collaboration with Intel
summary: HiddenLayer, a Gartner-recognized AI Application Security company, has announced a strategic collaboration with Intel Corporation. This partnership aims to provide an advanced hardware and software-based machine learning (ML) security solution for organizations, particularly those in regulated industries. The collaboration integrates HiddenLayer's ML Model Scanner with Intel's Confidential Computing, powered by Intel Software Guard Extensions (Intel SGX), to offer an end-to-end ML model protection platform.

title: GlobalFoundries sues IBM, says trade secrets were unlawfully given to Japan's Rapidus
summary: GlobalFoundries has filed a lawsuit against IBM, alleging that IBM unlawfully shared confidential intellectual property and trade secrets with Japan's Rapidus and Intel Corp. GlobalFoundries claims IBM is unjustly receiving substantial licensing income from these disclosures and is also seeking to halt IBM's recruitment of its engineers. IBM denies the allegations, calling the lawsuit "meritless."

title: Analog Devices Appoints Alan Lee as Chief Technology Officer
summary: Analog Devices, Inc. (ADI) has appointed Alan Lee as its new Chief Technology Officer (CTO). In this role, Lee will lead the identification and advancement of next-generation technologies for the semiconductor industry, collaborating with customers, universities, and partners. He brings extensive experience from his previous role as Head of Research and Advanced Development at AMD and will report directly to ADI CEO and Chair Vincent Roche.

title: Johnson & Johnson Unleashes Kenvue: Tylenol to Neutrogena, $40B Revenue Unit Spinoff Preps for IPO Blitz
summary: Johnson & Johnson is preparing to launch Kenvue, its consumer-healthcare spinoff business, with an investor roadshow aiming to raise over $3.5 billion in an IPO and achieve a valuation of approximately $40 billion. Kenvue, which includes brands like Tylenol and Neutrogena, generated nearly $15 billion in revenue in 2022. This IPO could be the largest in the U.S. since Mobileye's 2022 offering, though Kenvue is expected to face significant competition from other consumer-product giants.

title: Analog Devices names former AMD exec Alan Lee as CTO
summary: Analog Devices has appointed Alan Lee, formerly head of research and advanced development at AMD, as its new Chief Technology Officer. In this role, Lee will focus on identifying and advancing next-generation technologies to disrupt and shape the semiconductor industry. He will report directly to Analog Devices' CEO Vincent Roche and brings extensive experience in hardware, software, AI, and high-performance computing from his tenure at AMD, Intel, and IBM.

title: Analog Devices appoints former AMD executive Alan Lee as CTO
summary: Analog Devices has appointed Alan Lee, former head of research and advanced development at AMD, as its new Chief Technology Officer (CTO). In this role, Lee will focus on next-generation semiconductor technologies and collaborate with various partners to drive innovation. He will report directly to ADI's CEO and is expected to accelerate the company's leadership in areas such as analog, mixed-signal, power, software, and AI capabilities.

title: SK Hynix sees H2 memory chip rebound; outlook helps shares brush off record loss
summary: SK Hynix reported a record operating loss of $2.54 billion in the first quarter but expects market conditions for memory chips to improve in the second half of 2023 due to production cuts across the industry. The company also forecast that artificial intelligence applications would drive a significant increase in memory chip revenue over the next five years. This positive outlook helped its shares rise despite the substantial loss.

title: Alibaba Cloud cuts prices by up to 50% for core, storage products
summary: Alibaba Cloud, the cloud computing division of Alibaba Group, announced price cuts of up to 50% on its core products and services to increase its market share in China amidst growing competition. This move, effective Wednesday, includes reductions for services using Arm, Intel-based chips, and Nvidia GPUs. The company also revealed that over 200,000 enterprises have requested beta testing for its AI-powered large language model, Tongyi Qianwen.

title: Intel names Deepak Patil as new chip architect
summary: Intel Corp. has appointed Deepak Patil as its new chip architect. Patil currently serves as the chief technology and strategy officer for Intel's Data Center and AI Group. The detail about his current role provides context to his new appointment within the semiconductor company.

title: Intel says margins will recover in second half of 2023, shares rise
summary: Intel announced that its gross margins are expected to improve in the second half of 2023, after hitting historic lows in the first half of the year. The chipmaker also reported shipping its delayed Sapphire Rapids data center chip in volume and observed signs of stability in the PC market, leading to a 4% rise in its shares in after-hours trading. Despite forecasting adjusted losses for the second quarter, the company's Q1 revenue slightly exceeded estimates, and CEO Pat Gelsinger discussed the ongoing Tower Semiconductor acquisition with Chinese officials.

title: Green Computing at Scale – Interview Recap
summary: This article recaps a "Green Computing at Scale" webinar from November 2022, highlighting efforts by Intel and Supermicro to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and operating expenses in data centers. It discusses adopting energy-efficient technologies, improving asset utilization, and implementing methods like liquid cooling, repurposing equipment, and extending IT equipment lifecycles. The goal is to lower data centers' total cost to the environment (TCE) and power usage effectiveness (PUE) through affordable and practical solutions.

title: Warren Buffett's lessons about dividends
summary: Warren Buffett emphasizes the significance of dividends as a "secret sauce" for investment returns, citing Coca-Cola and American Express as examples of long-term dividend growth. The article highlights how dividends contribute substantially to total stock market returns, often overlooked by investors solely focused on stock price appreciation. It also stresses the importance of selecting dividend-paying companies with strong economic moats, rather than just high yields, to ensure dividend consistency and growth potential.

title: Goldman Sachs Maintains Intel (INTC) Sell Recommendation
summary: Goldman Sachs has reaffirmed its "Sell" recommendation for Intel (NASDAQ: INTC), with an average one-year price target suggesting a 5.45% downside from its last closing price. The report also highlights Intel's projected annual revenue increase of 10.29% and details its recent dividend declaration of $0.12 per share, which is lower than its historical average yield. Institutional ownership shows slight decreases in portfolio allocation by major funds, though total shares owned by institutions generally increased.

title: IBM to Replace 7,800 Jobs Using Automation and AI
summary: IBM plans to replace approximately 7,800 jobs over the next five years using automation and AI technology. CEO Arvind Krishna stated that this would primarily impact non-customer-facing roles, with about 30% of back-office positions expected to be replaced. The company will also slow or suspend hiring in departments like human resources, while still looking for engineering and customer-facing talent.

title: Intel tells employees it wants a better ‘balance’ on remote work
summary: Intel has informed its employees that it is seeking a better "balance" between remote work and in-office presence to foster more face-to-face interaction and collaboration. While not issuing a companywide mandate, CEO Pat Gelsinger indicated that individual business units would determine their specific operating models, emphasizing the desire for employees to split their time between home and the office. This move comes as other major companies like Amazon and the city of Portland have also tightened remote work policies, reflecting a broader trend of employers seeking more on-site presence amidst a shifting labor market.

title: Amkor Technology seeks to open Vietnam factory in fourth quarter
summary: U.S.-based Amkor Technology Inc. expects its mega facility in Bac Ninh, Vietnam, to begin first-phase manufacturing in the fourth quarter of this year. This factory, covering 1.9 million square feet, will be among Amkor's largest globally and represents a $1.6 billion investment until 2035. The move highlights Vietnam's growing importance as a manufacturing and R&D hub for major foreign electronics manufacturers.

title: AMD to Make 'New' 2019-Era 3000G APUs to Sell Old Motherboards
summary: AMD is reportedly resuming production of its 2019-era 12nm Zen+ Ryzen 3000G APUs, specifically the Ryzen 3 3200G and Ryzen 5 3400G, with plans to produce 30,000 new chips. This move is believed to be an effort to stimulate sales of older AM4 motherboards, particularly in the Chinese market. The article notes that price adjustments will be necessary for these dated APUs to be competitive against newer, similarly priced options like the Ryzen 5 5600G.

title: Intel layoffs 2023: Targeting $10B in annual savings
summary: Intel is reportedly planning significant layoffs, potentially affecting up to 20% of employees in its client computing and data center divisions, as part of a strategy to achieve $10 billion in annual savings by 2026. These workforce reductions come amidst a challenging macroeconomic environment, slowed chip demand, and increasing competition from rivals like AMD. Despite a record first-quarter loss, Intel anticipates chip demand recovery in the second half of 2023 and is focusing on advanced manufacturing processes to regain market share.

title: CEO pay continues to soar – new study
summary: A new study by Equilar reveals that median CEO pay at top US public companies increased by 7.7% to a record $22.3 million in 2022, despite negative S&P 500 returns. This rise was primarily driven by stock awards, leading to a median "pay ratio" of 288 times the median employee's compensation, significantly outpacing the 3.6% increase in average US private sector employee earnings. The study highlights a trend of incentivizing executives through substantial stock awards during uncertain economic times.

title: Beating the S&P 500 at Its Own Game
summary: This article examines the performance of equally weighted S&P 500 indexes compared to traditional cap-weighted indexes. While an early attempt by Dean Witter Reynolds in the 1980s failed due to high expenses and poor timing, the equally weighted strategy has significantly outgained the conventional index since 1998, primarily driven by its exposure to smaller companies rather than value stocks. The author concludes that equally weighted portfolios have merit despite their initial tarnished reputation.

title: Juniper Networks launches R&D initiative to help to shape next-generation networks
summary: Juniper Networks has launched Juniper Beyond Labs, an R&D initiative to drive innovation in networking and IT through research and experimental technology projects. This initiative will focus on various networking domains including AI, 5G/6G, and quantum computing, with a strong emphasis on sustainability. It aims to collaborate with academic and industry partners, offering customers and partners an opportunity to influence research directions and access new innovations.

title: Implications and Updates From China’s National People’s Conference
summary: China's annual "Two Sessions" parliamentary meeting revealed strategic policies for economic growth, trade, and technology, targeting a 5% GDP growth in 2023 driven by domestic consumption. The conference highlighted key implications for sectors like EVs, clean energy, cloud computing, biotechnology, and semiconductors, emphasizing self-reliance and technological innovation. Government restructuring and increased focus on digital infrastructure and R&D are expected to significantly impact various industries and investment themes in China.

title: Supermicro Launches Industry's First NVIDIA HGX H100 8 and 4-GPU H100 Servers with Liquid Cooling -- Reduces Data Center Power Costs by Up to 40%
summary: Supermicro has launched the industry's first NVIDIA HGX H100 8 and 4-GPU servers featuring liquid cooling, designed to significantly reduce data center power costs by up to 40%. These new rack-scale solutions accelerate deployment and increase performance for AI and HPC workloads, while also lowering operating expenses and improving Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) by up to 86% in direct cooling costs. The systems offer up to 30x performance improvement over previous generations for large transformer models and boast advanced features like PCIe 5.0 and NVIDA H100 SXM5 Tensor Core GPUs.

title: Applied Materials Launches Multibillion-Dollar R&D Platform in Silicon Valley to Accelerate Semiconductor Innovation
summary: Applied Materials, Inc. announced a multibillion-dollar investment to build the Equipment and Process Innovation and Commercialization (EPIC) Center in Silicon Valley. This facility will be the world’s largest and most advanced for collaborative semiconductor process technology and manufacturing equipment R&D, aiming to accelerate innovation and reduce the time to commercialization for new technologies. The center will provide dedicated space for chipmakers and university researchers, fostering collaboration and addressing critical industry challenges like talent shortages and R&D costs.

title: Applied Materials to invest $4 bln in Silicon Valley chip research center
summary: Applied Materials announced plans to invest up to $4 billion in a new chip research center in Silicon Valley, aiming to accelerate semiconductor manufacturing advances. The facility, scheduled to open in 2026, will create up to 2,000 engineering jobs and host extensive research work in collaboration with universities and chipmakers. This investment aligns with the U.S. government's efforts to boost domestic semiconductor manufacturing through the CHIPS and Science Act.

title: Applied Materials plans to build US$4bn R&D center
summary: Applied Materials plans to invest up to US$4 billion to construct a new Research and Development (R&D) center in Silicon Valley, aiming to accelerate semiconductor manufacturing innovation. This "EPIC Center" would be the largest R&D facility in the semiconductor industry and is envisioned to speed up the transition of academic research to factory production, while also potentially leveraging funding from the US CHIPS and Science Act. The project seeks to address technical challenges and attract talent crucial for the industry's growth.

title: Applied Materials plans $4B Silicon Valley chip research facility
summary: Applied Materials plans to build a $4 billion chip research facility in Sunnyvale, California, part of a larger potential investment of up to $25 billion over 10 years, contingent on support from the CHIPS Act. This facility is expected to create 1,500 construction jobs and aims to accelerate semiconductor technology and manufacturing equipment research and development. The project signifies the CHIPS Act's success in attracting significant private investment back to the U.S. semiconductor industry.

title: Applied Materials' Big Bet: A ‘Unique’ $4B Chip R&D Center
summary: Applied Materials is investing up to $4 billion to build the "EPIC Center," a 180,000-square-foot semiconductor R&D facility in Silicon Valley. This unique center will bring together chipmakers, academics, and ecosystem partners to accelerate manufacturing innovation, reduce costs, and shorten product development timelines, especially crucial in the complex 'angstrom era' of chipmaking. The investment's final size is contingent on subsidies from the U.S. government’s CHIPS and Science Act.

title: Intel's Arc A750 graphics card is only $200 at Newegg
summary: Intel's Arc A750 graphics card is currently available for $200 on Newegg, a $50 discount, making it an excellent option for affordable 1080p gaming. This deal is significant as GPU prices are decreasing due to increased competition from Intel's Arc series. The card performs best with motherboards supporting Resizeable BAR, especially for DirectX 12 titles.

title: Why Intel’s stock is falling as Nvidia leads the rest of the semiconductor sector on a massive surge
summary: While Nvidia and other semiconductor stocks saw a massive surge following Nvidia's optimistic outlook on AI-fueled demand, Intel's stock moved in the opposite direction, declining over 5%. An analyst suggested that Intel is a "major loser and share donor" in the competitive artificial intelligence chip market.

title: Cadence Stock vs Synopsys Stock: The Winner Is?
summary: The article compares Cadence (CDNS) and Synopsys (SNPS), two leading electronic design automation (EDA) software providers, to determine which stock is a better investment. It analyzes their market share, financial performance, valuation, and their use of AI in a mature, well-penetrated market. The authors conclude that Synopsys has a slight edge due to its higher EDA market share, strong position in intellectual property, and marginally better valuation, despite both being considered overvalued.

title: Microsoft shares fix for cameras not working on Surface laptops
summary: Microsoft has released a temporary fix for an issue preventing built-in cameras from working on some ARM-based Surface devices, including the Surface Pro X. The problem, caused by a buggy driver, affects devices running Windows 10 22H2 or Windows 11 21H2/22H2 with specific Qualcomm processors. While a troubleshooter is being deployed automatically, users can apply a manual registry edit or await a permanent driver update.

title: Intel inside? Tests show Chinese firm’s ‘home-grown’ chip was a rebadged device
summary: A Chinese computer hardware producer, Powerleader, is suspected of rebadging Intel Corp's Core i3-10105 Comet Lake CPU as its own "home-grown" Powerstar P3-01105 chip. Benchmark testing on Geekbench revealed identical parameters, raising questions as Powerleader has no prior semiconductor development track record. Neither Powerleader nor Intel has commented on the findings.

title: ISMC's India chip plan stalls after Tower-Intel deal in setback to Modi
summary: India's ambitious drive to establish itself as a significant semiconductor manufacturing hub is facing considerable hurdles. A proposed $3 billion chip facility by the ISMC consortium has stalled following Intel's acquisition of Tower Semiconductor, a key technology partner. Additionally, a $19.5 billion joint venture between Vedanta and Foxconn is slow to progress, as talks to secure European chipmaker STMicroelectronics as a partner have reached an impasse.

title: Inside ADI’s Catalyst Centre
summary: Analog Devices (ADI) has established its $100 million Catalyst Centre in Limerick, Ireland, as a hub for co-development and accelerating the adoption of new technologies with ecosystem partners. The center focuses on industrial automation, 5G/6G systems, and wireless battery management systems, aiming to support end customers with modules and reference designs. This initiative helps customers integrate ADI’s wide range of products across various applications, including automotive and communications, and supports the "green shoring" trend of localized production.

title: PowerVia Test Shows Industry-Leading Performance
summary: Intel has announced that its PowerVia backside power technology has achieved industry-leading performance in testing, marking a significant milestone in chip design. PowerVia, set to be introduced with the Intel 20A process node in the first half of 2024, addresses interconnect bottlenecks by routing power to the backside of the wafer, achieving over 90% cell utilization. This innovation is expected to provide substantial performance and efficiency gains for chip designers, including Intel Foundry Services customers, well ahead of competitors.

title: With PowerVia, Intel Achieves a Chipmaking Breakthrough
summary: Intel has achieved a significant chipmaking breakthrough with its new PowerVia technology, which rethinks how chips are powered by moving power wires to the backside of the chip. This innovative approach, set to debut with the Intel 20A manufacturing node in 2024, is the industry's first implementation of backside power in silicon, addressing long-standing interconnect bottlenecks. It promises substantial improvements in performance, power efficiency, and manufacturing cost, positioning Intel two years ahead of competitors in this area.

title: Intel Reveals Fastest Arc Pro Workstation GPUs Yet
summary: Intel has launched its fastest Arc Pro workstation GPUs to date, the A60 for desktops and A60M for laptops, significantly increasing specifications over previous models. These new GPUs are designed for applications like CAD, AI inferencing, and media processing, aiming to challenge the market dominance of Nvidia and AMD. While Intel hopes to attract workstation users with improved performance and quarterly driver updates, some partners note a lack of demand and suggest Intel needs a more comprehensive solution to compete effectively.

title: Applied Materials Announces $4 Billion Investment In EPIC Center For R&D Collaboration
summary: Applied Materials is investing $4 billion into its new Equipment and Process Innovation and Commercialization (EPIC) Center, a collaborative R&D project designed to accelerate semiconductor production techniques. This initiative aims to reduce the time from foundational research to mass commercialization by 30% through overlapping R&D phases and fostering direct collaboration between universities, equipment makers, and chip companies. The EPIC Center will strengthen Applied Materials' position in the industry, benefit chip makers with faster product roadmaps, and provide invaluable learning opportunities for university students.

title: Intel sells Mobileye stake for $1.6b
summary: Intel Corp. has sold 35 million shares of its stake in Mobileye Global Inc. for $1.6 billion in a secondary offering, pricing the shares at $42 each. Following this sale, Intel retains an 88% stake in Mobileye. The share sale occurs after Mobileye's stock price doubled since its IPO in October 2022, and with an option for the underwriters to purchase an additional 5.25 million shares.

title: AMD launches new data-center AI chips, software to go up against Nvidia and Intel
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) unveiled new data-center AI chips and software, including the Instinct MI300X accelerator and MI300A, to compete with Nvidia Corp. and Intel Corp. Despite these announcements at its Data Center & AI Technology Premiere event, AMD's stock declined, finishing down 3.6%. The new products are specifically aimed at handling generative-AI workloads.

title: AMD's AI chip challenge to Nvidia remains uphill fight with no big customers named
summary: AMD unveiled new details about its MI300X artificial intelligence chip, designed to challenge Nvidia's market dominance, but did not announce any major customers for the product. While the chip offers significant memory capacity aimed at cost reduction for AI services, the lack of customer commitments disappointed Wall Street, causing AMD's stock to fall. Analysts noted that Nvidia's strong market position is reinforced by its software ecosystem, which AMD is attempting to counter with its Rocm platform.

title: Invesco Semiconductors ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (PSI) $PSI
summary: MarketBeat has assigned the Invesco Semiconductors ETF (PSI) an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $95.38. This rating is derived from analyzing 505 analyst ratings over the past year covering 25 companies within the ETF, which represent 89.4% of its portfolio. The report provides a detailed breakdown of consensus ratings and price targets for the ETF's top 25 holdings, alongside their individual share prices, market rank, and potential upside.

title: Intel, German Government Agree on Increased Scope for Wafer Fabrication Site in Magdeburg
summary: Intel and the German federal government have finalized an agreement to expand Intel's wafer fabrication site in Magdeburg. The investment now exceeds 30 billion euros for two semiconductor facilities that will utilize advanced Angstrom-era technology, making it the largest foreign direct investment in German history. This project, known as “Silicon Junction,” aims to create a robust and resilient semiconductor supply chain in Europe, generating thousands of jobs and operating with high environmental standards.

title: Cisco Silicon One Breaks the 51.2 Tbps Barrier
summary: Cisco has announced its fourth-generation Silicon One devices, the G200 and G202, three and a half years after the initial launch, demonstrating a rapid pace of innovation. The G200 is a 5 nm, 51.2 Tbps device focusing on enhanced Ethernet-based AI/ML and web-scale spine deployments, while the G202 offers similar benefits at 25.6 Tbps. These new devices leverage Cisco's high-speed ADC-based SerDes technology and a converged architecture to reduce costs, power consumption, and latency in web-scale and AI/ML networks.

title: Intel pairs its hardware with HPE solutions to drive enterprise AI success
summary: Intel Corp. and Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co. have partnered for 30 years and are now collaborating to deliver AI-focused solutions for enterprises. Intel's strategy involves powerful hardware like its GPU Max Series with 100 billion transistors and a comprehensive software suite called OpenVINO for training video-based AI use cases. This partnership aims to make advanced AI technology accessible to various sectors, including government, cloud companies, and general enterprises.

title: Applied Materials: 2023 TIME100 Most Influential Companies | TIME
summary: Applied Materials, the largest maker of machines that produce semiconductors, was recognized in TIME's 2023 TIME100 Most Influential Companies list. The company announced plans to invest up to $4 billion to build the EPIC Center in Silicon Valley, an R&D campus aimed at rebuilding U.S. influence in chipmaking. This facility will provide advanced tools and space for university researchers and chipmakers like Intel and Samsung, potentially housing $25 billion of research in its first decade with anticipated federal CHIPS Act funding.

title: Artificial Intelligence Opens a Generational Opportunity for Chipmakers
summary: The article discusses how the expansion of AI applications, especially large language models, is creating a "generational opportunity" for chipmakers. It highlights the projected growth of AI chip spending to nearly $165 billion by the end of the decade, primarily benefiting GPU-based chip providers like Nvidia, and notes that the demand for specialized AI hardware is leading to significant investments, partnerships, and a potential "long capital investment cycle" for the semiconductor industry. The article also examines how this shift will impact traditional CPU providers and accelerate the replacement of legacy computing infrastructure with AI-first hardware.

title: Intel Corp., city of New Albany enter into development and supply agreement
summary: Intel Corp. and the city of New Albany have entered into a development and supply agreement for Intel's $20 billion processor plant project in Licking County, Ohio. This agreement formalizes the city's commitment to provide essential infrastructure like roads, water, and sewer to the site. It also outlines key milestones for the initial phases of the project, which represents the largest single private-sector investment in Ohio's history.

title: HP Laptop Buying Guide
summary: This HP laptop buying guide helps consumers navigate the wide array of HP laptops available, offering tailored recommendations for gaming, entry-level use, business, and convertible needs. It highlights specific models like OMEN for gaming, Chromebooks and Stream for budget-friendly basic use, EliteBook and ZBook for business professionals, and Spectre x360 and ENVY x360 for versatile convertible options. The guide encourages users to explore various features to find the perfect laptop for their unique requirements.

title: Oracle is no longer buying Intel processors for its data centers
summary: Oracle, one of the largest data center operators globally, has stopped purchasing Intel processors for its data centers. This decision was confirmed by Oracle Chairman Larry Ellison. Instead of Intel, Oracle is now buying billions of dollars worth of Nvidia and AMD chips.

title: Stock of the week - Advanced Micro Devices (29.06.2023)
summary: This article discusses the potential impact of new US chip export restrictions to China on Advanced Micro Devices (AMD). While such restrictions negatively affected semiconductor stocks, AMD's exposure may be limited as China's share of its revenue has significantly decreased. The article also compares AMD's stock performance to Nvidia and Intel, noting Nvidia's stronger recovery from the 2022 sell-off.

title: [Latest] Global Semiconductor Packaging Market Report
summary: The global Semiconductor Packaging Market was valued at approximately USD 27 Billion in 2022 and is projected to reach USD 55 Billion by 2032, exhibiting a CAGR of 7%. Key growth drivers include increasing demand for advanced electronic devices, technological advancements in packaging solutions, miniaturization trends, and the need for high-performance, low-power solutions. The report also highlights the impact of COVID-19 on supply chains and demand fluctuations, and covers regional analysis with Asia Pacific identified as a dominant market.

title: China puts the choke on raw materials used in semiconductors
summary: China is imposing export restrictions on gallium and germanium, two elements crucial for semiconductors and other electronics, effective August 1. This move is widely seen as retaliation for Western restrictions on chip technology sales to China. While these controls might not cause an immediate global shortage, they highlight the rising geopolitical tensions over critical supply chains, particularly in the semiconductor industry.

title: Netherlands-based ASML Holding Remains the Central Player in the Global Chip Supply Chain
summary: ASML Holding, a Netherlands-based company, is the sole provider of crucial photolithography systems and extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography machines essential for manufacturing computer chips, making it a central player in the global chip supply chain. The company faces significant challenges and opportunities amidst ongoing "Chip Wars" and export controls, particularly regarding its sales to the Chinese market. ASML is considering creating China-specific versions of its lithography tools to comply with export regulations while maintaining market access, highlighting the complex geopolitical landscape influencing semiconductor manufacturing.

title: China, chips & US dispute: Wolfspeed, Triad-based Qorvo paying the price
summary: Wolfspeed CEO Gregg Lowe is navigating renewed challenges with China regarding semiconductor exports, reminiscent of issues in 2019. This comes as China bans exports of key materials like gallium and germanium, which are crucial for Wolfspeed and Qorvo's chip production. The ban, effective August 1st, has already impacted the stock prices of both companies and is a major point of discussion during Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen's visit to Beijing.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. – More market share gains expected
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is expected to gain further market share in the data center CPU market and capitalize on opportunities to expand its presence in the data center GPU market due to increased generative AI adoption. The company's margins are projected to grow post-acquisition of Xilinx and Pensando, driven by increasing revenue contributions from its Data Centre and Embedded segments. Phillip Securities Research initiates coverage with an Accumulate recommendation and a target price of US$125.00, anticipating marginal revenue growth in FY23e and re-acceleration in FY24e.

title: Under pressure, but thriving: What makes Druck pivot pioneers?
summary: Druck, a pressure sensing device manufacturer, has successfully navigated recent market uncertainties by pivoting into new industries like semiconductors and electric vehicles, adapting from its traditional aviation focus. The company has invested in automation, tackled supply chain disruptions, and is committed to talent development and achieving net-zero emissions, demonstrating resilience and strategic growth in challenging times.

title: Schneider Electric Partners with Intel and Applied Materials to Help Decarbonize the Semiconductor Value Chain with New Catalyze Program
summary: Schneider Electric has launched Catalyze, a new program in partnership with Intel and Applied Materials, aimed at accelerating the decarbonization of the global semiconductor value chain. This initiative seeks to increase the adoption of renewable energy among semiconductor suppliers by combining purchasing power and providing access to utility-scale Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs). The program will initially focus on specific markets where renewable energy is readily available, with plans for global expansion, encouraging thousands of suppliers to take action towards a low-carbon future.

title: Aehr Reports Record Revenue and Profit for Fiscal 2023 and Guides for Over 50% Increase in Revenue for Fiscal 2024 on Strength from Semiconductor Wafer Level Test and Burn-in Demand
summary: Aehr Test Systems announced record financial results for fiscal 2023, with net sales reaching $65.0 million and GAAP net income of $14.6 million, driven by demand for semiconductor wafer level test and burn-in products. The company also provided strong guidance for fiscal 2024, projecting revenue of at least $100 million, a 50% increase, and GAAP net income of at least $28 million, reflecting over 90% growth. This growth is attributed to increased adoption of their FOX systems and WaferPak Contactors for silicon carbide in electric vehicles, as well as new opportunities in silicon photonics and gallium nitride markets.

title: Arizona State University, Applied Materials partner to create $270M Materials-to-Fab Center
summary: Arizona State University and Applied Materials, Inc. are partnering to create a $270 million Materials-to-Fab Center at ASU Research Park in Tempe. This facility will provide hands-on learning and research opportunities on leading-edge semiconductor equipment, bridging the gap between academic research and prototyping. The initiative aims to accelerate discovery, develop next-generation processes, and strengthen the semiconductor workforce in Arizona and the U.S.

title: Intel Corp. stock rises Monday, outperforms market
summary: Intel Corp. (INTC) stock rose 3.68% on Monday, closing at $34.37. This performance outpaced the broader market, as the S&P 500 Index increased by 0.39% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 0.22%. The rise marked the end of a two-day losing streak for Intel.

title: Humanoid robot startup Figure nabs $9M investment from Intel Capital
summary: Intel Capital has invested $9 million in Figure AI Inc., a startup focused on developing a commercially viable humanoid warehouse robot.
