This investment follows a prior $70 million funding round and supports Figure's goal to build autonomous humanoid robots for various applications, including e-commerce fulfillment centers. The company is developing its first prototype, Figure 01, with plans for rapid hardware and software iterations.

title: Schneider Electric Partners with Intel and Applied Materials to Decarbonize Semiconductor Value Chain
summary: Schneider Electric has launched "Catalyze," a new partnership program with Intel and Applied Materials, aimed at accelerating access to renewable energy and decarbonizing the global semiconductor value chain. The program, unveiled at SEMICON West 2023, seeks to unite thousands of suppliers to combine energy purchasing power, facilitate participation in utility-scale Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs), and educate on sustainable operational models to achieve net-zero ambitions. This collaboration emphasizes the critical need for industry-wide effort to reduce the growing carbon footprint of semiconductor manufacturing amidst increasing global demand.

title: Boeing and Intel to collaborate, focus on advancing semiconductor technology for aerospace
summary: Boeing and Intel have announced a strategic collaboration to advance semiconductor technology for the aerospace industry. The partnership will focus on developing next-generation microelectronics applications for AI, secure computing, and advanced flight capabilities. They plan to leverage Intel 18A technology to create state-of-the-art Si CMOS fabrication processes for national security-relevant capabilities.

title: NXP Semiconductors Executive Reelected Board Chair of Silicon Integration Initiative for 2023-2024
summary: Pankaj Kukkal, executive vice president of MCU/MPU Engineering for NXP Semiconductors, has been reelected to a second term as board chair of the Silicon Integration Initiative (Si2). Si2 is an R&D joint venture focused on advancing design solutions for semiconductor manufacturers. The article also lists the members of the 2023-2024 Si2 board of directors and provides information about Si2's primary initiatives.

title: TSMC: Chip giant delays Arizona production in blow to Biden
summary: TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker, has delayed the start of production at its Arizona factory until 2025 due to a shortage of skilled workers. This setback impacts President Biden's plans to boost US domestic chip production amidst intensifying trade tensions with China. The company also reported a significant drop in profits and forecast lower sales for the year.

title: Nvidia is ‘dominating’ and could unlock $300 billion in AI revenue by 2027, analyst says
summary: Mizuho analyst Vijay Rakesh is increasingly optimistic about Nvidia's prospects, projecting the company could tap into $300 billion in AI-related revenue by 2027. Rakesh believes Nvidia will maintain a significant share in the AI server market despite potential future competition. This outlook comes as Nvidia's stock has already seen a 200% increase this year.

title: Micron Delivers Industry's Fastest, Highest-Capacity HBM to Advance Generative AI Innovation
summary: Micron Technology announced it has begun sampling the industry's first 8-high 24 GB HBM3 Gen2 memory, boasting bandwidth greater than 1.2 TB/s and pin speed over 9.2 Gb/s, a significant improvement over existing HBM3 solutions. This new memory offers a 2.5 times performance per watt improvement, setting new benchmarks for AI data center metrics and reducing training times for large language models like GPT-4. Micron's 12-high stack with 36 GB capacity is expected to sample in early 2024, further enhancing capacity and efficiency for generative AI and HPC applications.

title: Intel stock rallies after earnings show AI data-center beat, strong PC sales
summary: Intel's stock surged after reporting a surprise profit in its second-quarter earnings. While data-center sales exceeded expectations, the significant beat in PC product sales was the primary driver of improved gross margins. Shares rose by approximately 8% in extended trading following the announcement.

title: UMC forecasts utilization rate to fall to 65%
summary: United Microelectronics Corp (UMC) anticipates its factory utilization rate will drop further to about 65% in the current quarter due to prolonged inventory correction and weak end-market demand. This represents the lowest utilization since Q4 2011, and the company finds it difficult to predict a bottom given customer inventory destocking through next quarter. Despite this, UMC plans to expand its 28-nanometer capacity and is doubling its silicon interposer wafer capacity to meet growing demand for AI chips.

title: Odisha is all set to be a Global IT & Semiconductor hotspot, here’s how
summary: Odisha is poised to become a global IT and Semiconductor hotspot, attracting significant investment intentions from major IT and Semiconductor MNCs. A delegation from the Government of Odisha met with top Silicon Valley industry leaders, resulting in commitments for an HDI-PCB manufacturing facility worth approximately Rs. 4,360 crore (~530 million USD) and Offshore Development Centers from Integreon and iOPEX. The state also plans collaborations for 5G, Industry 4.0, and semiconductor design education, aiming to create numerous employment opportunities.

title: U.S. chipmaker AMD to invest $400 million in India by 2028
summary: U.S. chipmaker Advanced Micro Devices (AMD.O) announced a significant investment of $400 million in India over the next five years, including the establishment of its largest design center in Bengaluru, creating 3,000 new engineering jobs. This move aligns with India's efforts to become a chipmaking hub, attracting other major players like Applied Materials and Micron with its incentive programs. AMD's expansion underscores the country's growing importance in the global semiconductor industry.

title: AMD earnings preview: Is the company ceding ground to Intel?
summary: This article previews AMD's upcoming earnings report, questioning whether the company is losing market share to Intel. Historically, Intel's struggles have benefited AMD, but Intel's recent performance suggests a potential shift. Wall Street will be looking for confirmation of this trend in AMD's latest quarterly results.

title: Intel plans massive Oregon factory expansion, filings show
summary: Intel plans a significant, multi-billion-dollar expansion of its research factories in Oregon, including a fourth phase for the D1X factory in Hillsboro and an overhaul of the D1A factory in Aloha, over the next five years. This investment, potentially one of the largest capital projects in Oregon's history, aims to strengthen the state's role in Intel's technology development and is supported by $90 million in state incentives. Despite current challenges for the chipmaker, the expansion is expected to create hundreds or thousands of new jobs, though it will also lead to increased air emissions.

title: Intel signals expansion plans in Hillsboro
summary: Intel is planning a significant expansion of its semiconductor manufacturing operations in Hillsboro, Oregon, according to recent filings with state air quality regulators. This move comes as Oregon dedicates substantial funds to boost its semiconductor industry. Governor Tina Kotek plans to provide $90 million to Intel as part of a broader effort to secure the state's position in advanced microchip research and development.

title: Prioritise school buses, HazMat CVs for Road Safety Tech Sols, FTI
summary: FTI Consulting, in collaboration with various stakeholders including the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways, has released a report advocating for road safety technology solutions in India. The report, titled 'Making Roads Safer in Indian Cities,' specifically prioritizes the adoption of such technologies in school buses and commercial vehicles transporting hazardous materials. Recommendations include developing a roadmap for tech adoption, installing and retrofitting safety devices, defining minimum safety levels for school buses, and continuous driver upskilling and retraining.

title: Mobile chip giant Qualcomm shares slide amid worst smartphone slump in years
summary: Qualcomm shares fell after the company gave a tepid sales forecast for the current quarter, indicating continued weak demand for mobile devices, especially in China which accounts for over 60% of its sales. The company plans further job cuts, having already recorded US$285 million in restructuring charges, as it takes a conservative view of the market amidst an industry downturn. This performance underperformed a broader chip industry rally, raising concerns about the smartphone market's recovery.

title: AMD's stock breaks with chip selloff to finish week higher as analyst cites 'important buying opportunity'
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares unexpectedly rose last week, up 2.6%, despite a broader chip sector selloff that saw Nvidia and the PHLX Semiconductor Index decline significantly. An analyst pointed to this performance as an "important buying opportunity" for AMD. Nvidia's earnings report is anticipated on August 23rd.

title: Micron Launches Memory Expansion Module Portfolio to Accelerate CXL 2.0 Adoption
summary: Micron Technology announced the sample availability of its CZ120 memory expansion modules, designed to accelerate CXL 2.0 adoption. These modules, available in 128 GB and 256 GB capacities, enhance server systems by providing incremental memory capacity and bandwidth, particularly beneficial for AI, SaaS, and HPC workloads. Micron is collaborating with Intel and AMD to validate the CZ120 modules on their CXL-compatible platforms.

title: Germany spends big to win $11 billion TSMC chip plant
summary: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC has committed 3.5 billion euros ($3.8 billion) to build its first European factory in Germany, a move heavily supported by the German government with up to 5 billion euros in subsidies for the $11 billion plant. This investment is part of Germany's and the EU's broader strategy to boost domestic semiconductor production and enhance supply chain resilience, especially for the automotive industry. The plant in Dresden aims to produce 40,000 wafers monthly starting in 2027, with TSMC owning 70% and partners Bosch, Infineon, and NXP each holding 10%.

title: The Case For Intel: An Illustration Of The ‘Value’ Investing Mindset
summary: This article argues for Intel as a "value investment" due to its current undervaluation despite strong long-term prospects. It delves into strategic market trends, Intel's strong industry position, favorable political and geopolitical factors, and a revived company culture under CEO Pat Gelsinger. The author emphasizes that true "value investing" focuses on a company's ability to correct its problems and adapt to strategic environmental shifts over a multi-year horizon, rather than short-term financial details.

title: Opinion: Intel is Poised to Unleash America’s Innovation Boom, Fueled by the CHIPS Act
summary: This opinion piece by Al Thompson, VP of U.S.-Canada Government Affairs for Intel, highlights how Intel is leveraging the CHIPS and Science Act to drive an innovation boom in America. The company is doubling down on R&D, making significant private-sector investments in manufacturing facilities across Arizona, New Mexico, and Ohio, and developing workforce training programs. Intel aims to regain U.S. leadership in semiconductor technology and foster the next generation of talent.

title: Supermicro Announces High Volume Production of E3.S All-Flash Storage Portfolio with New CXL Memory Expansion Offerings
summary: Supermicro is commencing high-volume production of its E3.S all-flash storage portfolio, which includes new CXL memory expansion offerings. These solutions support PCIe Gen5 drives and CXL memory modules to meet the demands of large AI Training and HPC clusters. The company's Petascale systems are a new class of storage servers designed for high-throughput, low-latency storage, available with Intel Xeon Scalable or AMD EPYC processors.

title: Blockchain Technology Market to grow by USD 11,047.61 million between 2022 and 2027
summary: The blockchain technology market is projected to grow by USD 11,047.61 million between 2022 and 2027, at a CAGR of 32.72%. This growth is significantly driven by blockchain's application in enhancing power grid reliability and the increasing adoption of Blockchain-as-a-Service (BaaS). The European region, particularly the UK, Germany, and Russia, is expected to contribute 38% to this market expansion.

title: GDS launches data center campus in Johor, Malaysia
summary: GDS has officially launched its Nusajaya Tech Park (NTP) data center campus in Johor, Malaysia, ahead of schedule. Phase 1 of the campus offers 22,500 sqm of net floor area and 69.5MW of capacity. GDS views the Sijori region as a strategic location and aims for the NTP campus to become an AI computing hub, capitalizing on its first-mover advantage.

title: Intel’s Terminated $5.4B Tower Acquisition: 5 Big Things To Know
summary: Intel terminated its $5.4 billion acquisition of Tower Semiconductor due to regulatory approval issues, a setback for CEO Pat Gelsinger's IDM 2.0 strategy and Intel Foundry Services. The deal's failure, possibly due to U.S.-China geopolitical tensions, resulted in a $353 million termination fee. Despite this, Intel remains committed to its foundry business, aiming to become the second-largest global external foundry by 2030, though Tower's capacity and customer base would have provided a significant boost.

title: Synopsys names insider Ghazi as CEO, forecasts stronger fourth quarter
summary: Synopsys, a chip design software company, has appointed Sassine Ghazi, a 25-year veteran, as its new CEO and president, succeeding co-founder Aart de Geus in January. The company also provided an optimistic fourth-quarter forecast, exceeding analyst expectations for both adjusted earnings per share and revenue, driven by increased demand for custom semiconductor design due to AI innovations. This announcement follows a recent deal with Intel for advanced manufacturing processes, indicating strategic moves to strengthen its market position.

title: LX International liquidates Yantai LCD component warehouse in China
summary: LX International has liquidated its Yantai LCD component warehouse in China due to decreased demand for LCD panels, particularly from key customer LG Display. This move follows LG Display's reduction in LCD module production at its Yantai plant as the market shifts from LCD to OLED technology for small panels. The liquidation reflects the evolving display market where OLED is expected to significantly increase its share in IT devices.

title: Intel Corp. to host engineering hiring event in Rio Rancho
summary: Intel Corp. is set to host a hiring event in Rio Rancho, New Mexico, with plans to recruit hundreds of engineers. The company is looking to fill three distinct engineering roles at its facility. This initiative follows recent news regarding Intel's forecast for AI-driven growth and discussions about expanding its advanced manufacturing capabilities in the state.

title: GraniteShares 2x Short TSLA Daily ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:TSDD)
summary: The GraniteShares 2x Short TSLA Daily ETF (TSDD) provides 2x inverse leveraged exposure to the daily price movement of Tesla stock. As of March 3, 2026, TSDD was trading at $9.65, representing a 24.8% increase since the beginning of 2026. The ETF has an expense ratio of 1.15% and launched on August 21, 2023.

title: Why Ireland is well positioned to capitalise on chip ‘gold rush’
summary: Ireland is well-positioned to capitalize on the booming semiconductor industry, which is projected to double by 2030, thanks to its long-standing presence of major players like Analog Devices and Intel. However, the country faces challenges including intense global competition for investment, capacity constraints for manufacturing, and a scarcity of electronic engineering talent. To maintain its competitive edge, Ireland must continue to invest in R&D, upgrade its infrastructure, and foster international collaboration within the semiconductor ecosystem.

title: Fortinet Makes Cuts To Sales, Channel, Business Development Groups
summary: Fortinet has announced layoffs affecting employees in sales, business development, and channel partner roles, with several affected individuals sharing the news on LinkedIn. These cuts come after Fortinet reported lower-than-expected earnings for the quarter ending June 30th, attributed to enterprise customers postponing deals amidst an uncertain economic environment. The company, which relies entirely on channel sales, had seen its employee count grow by 9% year-over-year to 13,677 as of June 30.

title: New Albany Co. reveals more details about proposed Intel gateway project in Johnstown
summary: New Albany Co. has released further details regarding its proposed 407-acre Intel gateway project in Johnstown, which could include up to 1,220 multifamily units. The comprehensive development aims to incorporate data centers, retail, restaurants, manufacturing, warehousing, and office spaces. This project is designed to support the Intel Corp. site and its surrounding area.

title: 10 Best Semiconductor ETFs
summary: This article identifies and discusses the 10 best semiconductor ETFs based on 5-year share price performance, providing insight into the semiconductor market's recent performance and future outlook. It highlights key companies within these ETFs and analyst perspectives on the industry's recovery and growth drivers such as AI and automotive demand.

title: AI In Medical Devices Global Market Report 2023: Unveiling
summary: The "AI In Medical Devices Global Market Report 2023" indicates significant growth in the AI in medical devices market, projected to reach $66.35 billion by 2027 from $10.64 billion in 2022. Key drivers include technological advancements like Nvidia's Clara Holoscan MGX platform and increasing demand for AI-integrated wearables for health monitoring. Major players are also expanding through acquisitions to enhance their AI capabilities in healthcare.

title: Intel, Tower make New Mexico foundry deal
summary: Intel Foundry Services (IFS) and Tower Semiconductor Ltd. have announced a deal where Intel will provide 300mm wafer manufacturing capacity to Tower from its New Mexico facility. Tower will invest up to $300 million to acquire equipment and secure manufacturing capacity of over 600,000 photolithographic layers per month, with full process flow qualification planned for 2024. This collaboration follows the recent cancellation of Intel's proposed takeover of Tower due to Chinese regulatory issues, and is seen as a first step towards future synergistic solutions between the companies.

title: Tower and Intel reach chip production agreement
summary: Following the canceled acquisition by Intel, Tower Semiconductor and Intel Foundry Services have formed a new agreement for chip production. Tower will invest up to $300 million to install equipment at Intel's New Mexico facility, providing significant manufacturing capacity for advanced analog processing. This collaboration aims to expand their foundry footprints and support Tower's customer demand, with Intel manufacturing Tower's 65-nanometer power management flows.

title: Which AI Stocks Could Drive Digital Transformation in Latin America?
summary: Latin American economies are increasingly prioritizing digital transformation and the responsible use of AI, with countries like Brazil, Colombia, and Uruguay publishing national AI strategies. Companies such as Intel and Cisco are actively supporting digital infrastructure development in the region. Investors can gain exposure to this trend through ETFs like the Pacer Data and Digital Revolution ETF, the Digital Infrastructure and Connectivity UCITS ETF, and the Amundi MSCI Digital Economy and Metaverse ESG Screened UCITS ETF, all of which include major tech players.

title: Intel to offer Tower foundry services after failed acquisition
summary: Intel announced a new deal to offer foundry services to Tower Semiconductor, providing 300mm chip-manufacturing capacity at its Rio Rancho, New Mexico facility. This collaboration comes after Intel's $5.4bn acquisition bid for Tower Semiconductor fell through due to a lack of approval from Chinese antitrust regulators. The deal enables Tower to meet customer demand for next-generation chips, while Intel continues its IDM 2.0 strategy to expand its foundry services.

title: SOXQ ETF: Huawei's Latest Phone And Implication For Semis Stocks
summary: Huawei has launched the Mate 60, a new 5G smartphone, signaling its recovery from past sanctions and strengthening China's semiconductor industry. This development poses competitive risks for holdings within the Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ). Given these implications for the semiconductor market, the author maintains a "Hold" position on SOXQ.

title: Nvidia Says It Will Invest In Arm’s IPO After Failed Acquisition
summary: Nvidia has confirmed its intention to invest in Arm's initial public offering, despite its failed acquisition attempt of the company. The GPU giant was named as a potential "cornerstone investor" in Arm's updated IPO filing, alongside other tech companies like Intel, AMD, Apple, and Google. This investment highlights the continued importance of Arm's architecture to Nvidia's products and the broader tech ecosystem.

title: US, Vietnam firms talk business during Biden visit; AI and Boeing deals unveiled
summary: During U.S. President Joe Biden's visit to Hanoi, executives from top American and Vietnamese firms in technology, semiconductors, and aviation met to establish new business partnerships. The meeting led to significant deals, including Vietnam Airlines purchasing 50 Boeing 737 Max jets and plans for Microsoft and Nvidia to advance AI solutions and chip design centers in Vietnam. These collaborations aim to deepen cooperation in key technological sectors and reinforce Vietnam's role in global supply chains, especially in chipmaking.

title: GlobalFoundries opens $4 billion Singapore expansion fab to meet 'demand for essential semiconductor chips'
summary: GlobalFoundries has opened a $4 billion fabrication plant in Singapore to meet the increasing global demand for essential semiconductor chips, particularly for automotive, AI, and industrial sectors. This expansion will significantly boost the company's manufacturing capacity and create approximately 1,000 new jobs in Singapore. Despite current weakness in consumer-related demand, GlobalFoundries CEO Thomas Caulfield anticipates a doubling of the industry over the next decade due to new applications and AI's impact.

title: Mobileye completes follow on offering
summary: Mobileye Global Inc. announced the pricing of a previously announced underwritten secondary public offering of 38,500,000 shares of its Class A common stock by Intel Overseas Funding Corporation at $42.00 per share, totaling US$1.6 billion. The offering is expected to close on June 12, 2023, with RBC Capital acting as joint bookrunner. The selling stockholder also granted underwriters a 30-day option for an additional 5,775,000 shares.

title: Intel to sell 10% stake in IMS Nanofabrication to TSMC
summary: Intel announced it would sell a 10% stake in its IMS Nanofabrication business to Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC), a deal that values IMS at $4.3 billion. Intel will remain the majority owner of IMS, an Austrian company that creates essential components for next-generation lithography tools. This move comes after Intel previously sold a 20% stake in IMS to Bain Capital earlier in the year.

title: Netgear's Nighthawk MK93S Mesh Router Arrives As iPhone 15 Readies A Big Wi-Fi 6E Push
summary: Netgear has launched its Nighthawk MK93S, a tri-band Wi-Fi 6E mesh router system, timed with the release of the iPhone 15 series. This system offers speeds up to 50% faster than its predecessor and covers homes up to 7,500 square feet. Netgear anticipates that the iPhone 15's adoption of Wi-Fi 6E will significantly boost the technology's market presence and consumer adoption.

title: Chipmaker Arm is valued at $55 billion after a blockbuster IPO that is this year's biggest by far
summary: Arm Holdings successfully priced its IPO at the top of its range, raising $4.87 billion and achieving a valuation of approximately $54.5 billion. This marks the largest listing of the year globally and is expected to invigorate the equity markets and a pipeline of other tech IPOs. SoftBank will retain about 90% ownership after the offering, which saw significant investment from major tech companies like Intel, Apple, and Nvidia.

title: How Applied Materials and Intel are helping chipmakers buy clean power
summary: Applied Materials and Intel are co-sponsoring the Catalyze program, run by Schneider Electric, to accelerate access to renewable energy for semiconductor supply chain companies. The program aims to help chipmakers decouple their growth from environmental impact by making it easier for their suppliers to purchase clean power through aggregated power purchase agreements. This initiative seeks to educate suppliers and provide a platform for smaller entities to participate in the renewable energy market, ultimately reducing Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions across the industry.

title: Goldman Sachs Physical Gold ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (CBOE:AAAU)
summary: The Goldman Sachs Physical Gold ETF (AAAU) tracks the LBMA Gold Price index, investing in physical gold bars held in Perth, Australia. It offers investors exposure to gold spot prices, with shares redeemable for gold coins and small bars. AAAU was launched on July 26, 2018, and has seen its shares increase by 65.1% this year, trading at $42.82.

title: Intel pitches the 'AI PC' at software developer event
summary: Intel announced a new "Meteor Lake" chip, expected in December, that will enable generative AI chatbots to run directly on laptops without needing cloud data centers. This development, showcased at a software developer conference, aims to offer businesses and consumers greater privacy and faster AI responses for ChatGPT-style technologies. Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger positioned this as a "sea change moment" for tech innovation, with the company also announcing plans for a successor chip and partnerships in the AI space.

title: Better Buy: ASML vs. Aehr Test Systems
summary: This article compares two semiconductor equipment makers, ASML and Aehr Test Systems, to determine which is a better investment. ASML dominates the lithography system market, including unique EUV technology, while Aehr specializes in testing equipment for silicon carbide chips. Despite Aehr's higher recent stock performance, the author concludes that ASML is the better buy due to its market dominance, stable growth, and lower valuation, contrasting with Aehr's customer concentration and potential competition risks.

title: Supermicro Future Direction Based on the Intel Processor Roadmap
summary: Supermicro is aligning its future server designs with Intel's announced CPU roadmap, which includes the 5th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable processors (Emerald Rapids) and upcoming Granite Rapids and Sierra Forest Xeon CPUs. These new Intel processors will feature P-cores for high performance and E-cores for energy efficiency, using a modular SoC design for scalability. Supermicro plans to integrate these innovations into its product lines to offer solutions optimized for both high-performance and energy-efficient workloads from edge to data center.

title: Cisco to buy cybersecurity firm Splunk for $28 billion
summary: Cisco Systems agreed to acquire cybersecurity firm Splunk for approximately $28 billion, marking its largest-ever deal aimed at bolstering its software business and leveraging the AI boom. This acquisition is expected to reduce Cisco's reliance on its networking equipment business and accelerate revenue growth and gross margin expansion. Despite potential antitrust scrutiny, both companies anticipate the deal, valued at $157 per share for Splunk, to close by the end of the third quarter of 2024.

title: Intel sees massive demand for AI chips designed for China: report
summary: Intel is experiencing high demand for its Gaudi2 AI chips in China, which were designed to comply with US export restrictions. This surge is attributed to Chinese companies developing large language models, leading Intel to increase orders with TSMC. The company's CFO has noted a general trend of customers seeking Gaudi chips as an alternative due to supply shortages.

title: Intel hit with $400 million EU antitrust fine in decades-old case
summary: Intel has been fined 376 million euros ($400 million) by the EU for anti-competitive practices nearly two decades ago. This penalty stems from an original 2009 fine for blocking rivals like AMD, which was largely overturned by Europe's second-highest court last year. The new fine specifically addresses Intel's actions between 2002 and 2006, where it paid companies like HP, Acer, and Lenovo to delay or halt competitor products.

title: Oracle spends more than $100 million on Ampere chips
summary: Oracle Corp has agreed to prepay $104.1 million for processor chips from startup Ampere Computing and also invested $400 million in Ampere in its fiscal 2023. This investment and chip purchases are intended to give Oracle's cloud services a competitive edge against rivals like Amazon.com and Google, which develop their own server chips. Ampere designs custom server chips based on Arm technology, competing with Intel and Advanced Micro Devices.

title: Gov. Hobbs visits TSMC facilities, meets with semiconductor industry leaders in Taiwan
summary: Arizona Governor Katie Hobbs visited TSMC facilities and met with semiconductor industry leaders in Taiwan to strengthen economic ties and promote Arizona as a leader in semiconductor manufacturing. During her trip, Hobbs toured TSMC's fabrication and water treatment facilities and the Hsinchu Science Park, emphasizing the importance of partnerships for job creation and investment in Arizona's booming semiconductor sector. She also attended a signing ceremony for a collaboration between the Arizona Science Center and the National Taiwan Science Education Center.

title: Astera Labs Leo Memory Connectivity Platform Enables Data Center Servers with Performance for Memory Intensive Workloads
summary: Astera Labs announced its Leo Memory Connectivity Platform, a CXL memory controller designed to enhance performance for memory-intensive workloads in data centers. Collaborating with Intel, the platform increases server memory bandwidth by 50% and reduces latency by 25% when integrated with upcoming 5th Gen Xeon Scalable Processors. This innovation aims to overcome the "memory wall" by allowing existing applications to leverage augmented memory resources without software changes, thus improving efficiency, especially in AI servers.

title: Intel begins mass-producing chips using cutting-edge EUV technology
summary: Intel has commenced mass production of chips using extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography at its Fab 34 plant in Leixlip, Ireland, marking a significant advancement in its semiconductor manufacturing capabilities. This move, part of a €17 billion investment, aims to catch up with rivals like TSMC and Samsung who have long used EUV technology. The chips, specifically Meteor Lake laptop processors, are based on Intel's 4 process and utilize equipment exclusively provided by Dutch company ASML.

title: Intel’s New Fab in Ireland Begins High-Volume Production of Intel 4 Technology
summary: Intel's new Fab 34 in Leixlip, Ireland, has commenced high-volume production of computer chips using Intel 4 technology, marking the first time extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography is used for mass production in Europe. This milestone is crucial for Intel's strategy to deliver five process nodes in four years and regain leadership in process technology. The facility will produce chips for upcoming Intel® Core™ Ultra processors and future-generation Intel® Xeon® processors, while Intel also announced an Ireland Climate Action Plan to reduce its environmental footprint.

title: Intel shares updated construction video from Licking County site
summary: Intel has released an updated construction video showcasing the progress of its semiconductor facility in Licking County, Ohio. The project has moved from excavation to concrete pouring, with 16,000 cubic yards already laid. This advancement comes after Intel's initial groundbreaking over a year ago and significant investments in education initiatives to attract skilled local talent.

title: ASUS Officially Takes Over Intel NUC Product Lines at Signing Ceremony
summary: ASUS has officially completed the handover of Intel's Next Unit of Computing (NUC) product lines, following a signing ceremony with Intel Corporation. ASUS will now manufacture and sell Intel NUC 10th to 13th generation systems and develop future NUC products. This move aims to expand ASUS's capabilities in AI and IoT, particularly for industrial, commercial, and prosumer markets, ensuring a smooth transition for existing NUC customers.

title: Intel Corp. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
summary: Intel Corp. (INTC) shares rose by 0.65% to $35.69 on a generally negative trading day for the stock market, outperforming the S&P 500 Index and the Dow Jones Industrial Average, both of which saw declines. The company's stock closed $4.38 below its 52-week high of $40.07, achieved on September 12th.

title: UT’s Texas Institute for Electronics and Infleqtion Launch Quantum Manufacturing Center of Excellence
summary: The University of Texas at Austin and Infleqtion have partnered to establish a new center of excellence for quantum manufacturing, named qNexus. This collaboration aims to scale the domestic production of quantum-enabled products for various sectors, including energy, defense, and healthcare, while also developing the future workforce for quantum and photonic manufacturing. The initiative is supported by significant funding from the Texas Legislature and builds on UT's expertise in quantum materials and algorithms.

title: Supermicro Accelerates Everything at ISC23
summary: Supermicro showcased its latest High-Performance Computing (HPC) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions at ISC23 in Hamburg, Germany, emphasizing its leadership in AI infrastructure. Key highlights included liquid-cooled NVIDIA HGX H100 GPU servers, an AI development platform with NVIDIA, and an 8U PVC system featuring Intel Data Series GPUs. The company also presented high-performance storage solutions optimized for AI workloads, demonstrating its commitment to accelerating AI adoption.

title: Harnessing the Power of the Ecosystem in the Era of Custom Silicon on Arm
summary: Arm has launched Arm Total Design, an ecosystem initiative built around its Neoverse Compute Subsystems (CSS), to simplify and accelerate the development of custom silicon for infrastructure. This program brings together ASIC design houses, IP vendors, EDA tool providers, foundries, and firmware developers to address the increasing demand for specialized, efficient, and performant solutions in areas like AI, cloud, and edge computing. Total Design aims to lower development costs and speed up time to market by providing pre-integrated IP, optimized tools, design services, and support for advanced manufacturing techniques.

title: MaxLinear joins Broadcom in DOCSIS 4.0 chip race with 'Puma 8'
summary: MaxLinear has entered the DOCSIS 4.0 chip market with its new "Puma 8" chipset, aiming to provide an alternative to Broadcom. The company states it will not require restrictive joint development agreements, unlike Broadcom, and expects its chip to go into production in the second half of 2024. MaxLinear is focusing on the frequency division duplex (FDD) option for DOCSIS 4.0 and highlights its fan-less, power-efficient design as a key differentiator.

title: ASML warns of flat 2024 sales as chipmakers slow orders
summary: ASML, a major semiconductor equipment manufacturer, issued a warning of flat sales for 2024 and reported lower-than-expected orders, leading to a dip in its share price. This caution stems from chipmakers conserving cash due to economic uncertainty, despite the industry nearing a market trough. However, ASML maintains a strong long-term outlook with a substantial order backlog and anticipated recovery in 2025.

title: Intel’s Arizona Expansion Marks Construction Milestone
summary: Intel has achieved a significant construction milestone for its two new leading-edge chip factories in Chandler, Arizona, with the initial portion of the cleanroom being "weather tight" and the "blow down" phase underway. This expansion, part of a $20 billion investment, reinforces Intel's commitment to U.S. semiconductor manufacturing, creating thousands of high-tech and construction jobs, and contributing significantly to Arizona's economy. The company is also emphasizing sustainability in its construction and operations, implementing initiatives for waste reduction, water conservation, and renewable energy use.

title: ASML voices common concerns of global chip industry over US’ new export curbs
summary: ASML and other international semiconductor companies are concerned about the US' escalating export curbs on advanced AI chips to China, as these restrictions threaten their sales and market share in the vast Chinese market. The article highlights that these US policies are not only impacting American businesses like Nvidia, Intel, and Qualcomm but are also pushing China towards accelerated independent chip development, ultimately leading to a lose-lose situation for all involved.

title: Intel Launches Industry’s First AI PC Acceleration Program
summary: Intel has launched its AI PC Acceleration Program, aiming to enable artificial intelligence on over 100 million PCs by 2025, driven by the upcoming Intel Core Ultra processors. The program partners with more than 100 independent software vendors (ISVs) to integrate over 300 AI-accelerated features, enhancing various PC experiences. This initiative provides IHVs and ISVs with Intel resources, including AI toolchains and co-engineering, to maximize AI and machine learning application performance.

title: Nvidia and AMD reportedly developing Arm-based PC processors
summary: Nvidia and AMD are reportedly developing new Arm-based PC processors, aiming to enter the Windows computer market as early as 2025. This move aligns with Microsoft's efforts to foster competition and introduce more energy-efficient CPUs with advanced AI features, similar to Apple's Arm-based Macs. Qualcomm currently holds an exclusivity agreement with Microsoft for Arm-based Windows chips, which is set to expire by the end of the year, potentially opening the door for Nvidia and AMD.

title: Intel’s Oregon Investments Fuel US Silicon Innovation Leadership
summary: Intel announced significant investments in its semiconductor technology development facilities at Gordon Moore Park in Hillsboro, Oregon, solidifying the state's role as a global R&D and manufacturing hub. These advancements are crucial for Intel's goal to regain process technology leadership and are supported by the U.S. CHIPS and Science Act, as well as state and local partnerships. The investments are part of Intel's broader plan to invest over $100 billion in the U.S. over five years, promising thousands of new jobs and boosting Oregon's economy.

title: Nvidia to take on Intel, Qualcomm with Arm-based processors for PC; availability pegged for 2025
summary: Nvidia is developing Arm-based processors for personal computers, aiming to compete with Intel and Qualcomm, with availability expected as soon as 2025. This move intensifies competition in the PC chip market, especially as AMD is also reportedly working on Arm-based processors for PCs. The initiative follows Apple's successful transition to in-house Arm-based chips, boosting Mac sales, and poses a significant challenge to Intel's dominant position.

title: Analysts Offer Insights on Technology Companies: Confluent (CFLT), Western Digital (WDC) and Intel (INTC)
summary: This article summarizes recent analyst ratings for several technology companies: Confluent (CFLT), Western Digital (WDC), and Intel (INTC). J.P. Morgan maintained a Buy rating on Confluent, Rosenblatt Securities reiterated a Buy on Western Digital, and Morgan Stanley held a Hold rating on Intel. The article details price targets, analyst success rates, and consensus ratings for each company.

title: Intel cheers foundry wins, AI traction, and its stock is roaring after earnings
summary: Intel's stock surged nearly 8% after the company provided an upbeat fourth-quarter forecast, surpassing analyst expectations for revenue and adjusted earnings per share. The chip maker also highlighted new customer wins for its foundry business and increasing traction in the artificial intelligence sector, contributing to its positive market performance.

title: Automotive Digitalization Sparks Big Growth For Chips And Design Tools
summary: The automotive industry is experiencing significant growth in semiconductor dependency due to innovations like autonomous driving, advanced driver-assist features, and infotainment systems. This surge is driven by the adoption of a "software-defined vehicle" concept and a scalable, zonal digital chassis architecture. Companies like Cadence Design Systems are playing a crucial role by providing tools for chip design, system modeling, simulation, and multiphysics analysis to support this technological transformation.

title: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger scoops up $250,000 in stock
summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger recently purchased nearly $250,000 worth of company stock in open-market transactions. He bought 3,400 shares at $36.79 and an additional 3,375 shares at $36.82 on Wednesday, according to disclosures a day later. This action signals a significant personal investment by the CEO in Intel's future.

title: Jabil takes on Intel's silicon photonics transceiver module business
summary: Jabil has acquired Intel's silicon photonics pluggable transceiver module business, integrating it into Jabil Photonics to expand its presence in the data center supply chain. This move allows Intel to focus on higher-value silicon photonics components for AI infrastructure, as part of its broader cost-cutting and portfolio optimization strategy. The acquisition aims to better position Jabil to address evolving data center demands, particularly those driven by artificial intelligence.

title: SolarWinds hack explained: Everything you need to know
summary: The SolarWinds hack, identified as one of the largest cybersecurity breaches, involved malicious code injected into its Orion IT monitoring software, affecting thousands of organizations including the U.S. government. Suspected Russian nation-state hackers, referred to as Nobelium, gained widespread access, leading to significant investigations and legal actions against SolarWinds. The incident highlighted critical vulnerabilities in supply chain security and spurred new cybersecurity initiatives and regulations, such as the mandating of Software Bill of Materials (SBOMs).

title: Intel CEO Admits to and Details The Company's Three Biggest Mistakes
summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger openly discussed what he considers Intel's three biggest missteps: the failed smartphone business, the cancellation of the early AI-oriented GPU Larrabee, and the historical lack of focus on building a robust foundry. He believes these errors led to a disadvantage in the current AI market and impacted the company's overall direction. Gelsinger is now driving Intel's IDM 2.0 initiative, opening its fabs to external customers to generate revenue and invest in manufacturing and R&D.

title: On-device AI chips: New battleground for chipmakers
summary: Global chipmakers like Samsung Electronics, Qualcomm, Intel, and AMD are intensifying their focus on on-device AI chips for smartphones, laptops, and PCs, recognizing its potential for customized services, lower costs, and enhanced privacy compared to generative AI. This shift is expected to drive significant growth in AI-enabled consumer electronics and contribute to rising memory chip prices. Both on-device AI and generative AI are seen as complementary, with companies like Samsung planning to integrate both into future smart devices.

title: Investing in Semiconductor ETFs & Stocks
summary: This article explores investing in semiconductor ETFs and stocks, detailing the sector's evolution, market dynamics, and future outlook, especially concerning AI. It highlights semiconductors' crucial role in modern technology, the economic powerhouse of the industry, and the benefits of investing via ETFs for diversification and accessibility. The piece also discusses factors influencing the sector, including geopolitical efforts to secure supply chains and the profound impact of AI on semiconductor demand and innovation.

title: Penguin Solutions to Showcase Expertise in AI and HPC at SC23
summary: Penguin Solutions will exhibit its AI and High-Performance Computing (HPC) solutions at Supercomputing 2023 (SC23) in Denver from November 12-17. The company's booth will feature technology demonstrations on designing, building, deploying, and managing AI/HPC infrastructures, including insights into immersion cooling and hybrid cloud solutions. Penguin experts will also participate in speaking engagements discussing AI factories and sustainable deployment environments like immersion cooling.

title: Intel building geographically balanced supply chain
summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger stated that the company is expanding its semiconductor capacity in the US, Europe, and Asia to create a geographically balanced supply chain. This is part of Intel's IDM2.0 strategy, aiming to become a leading foundry service supplier, even while acknowledging Taiwan's strong existing foundry industry. Gelsinger emphasized the need for diversified manufacturing facilities globally and highlighted Intel's investments in new fabs and expansions in various regions.

title: Micron Announces 128GB DRAM Low-Latency, High-Capacity RDIMMs
summary: Micron Technology has launched new 128 GB DDR5 RDIMM memory modules featuring 32Gb monolithic die technology, offering best-in-class performance up to 8000 MT/s. These high-capacity, low-latency modules are designed for data center and cloud environments, particularly for AI, in-memory databases, and high-performance computing, providing significant improvements in bit density, energy efficiency, latency, and AI training performance over competitive products. AMD and Intel have expressed interest in these new RDIMMs for their latest server platforms due to the enhanced capacity and bandwidth benefits.

title: After Earnings, Is AMD Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar assesses AMD's stock post-Q3 earnings, evaluating its fair value, economic moat, and future prospects, particularly in the AI accelerator market. The company's target of over $2 billion in AI accelerator revenue for 2024 positions it as a significant player. While fair value is estimated at $125 per share, high uncertainty exists due to competition from Nvidia and potential shifts in the x86 architecture dominance.

title: China's Zeekr reveals wider loss in US IPO paperwork, Geely shares fall
summary: China's Zeekr Intelligent revealed wider losses for the first half of the year as it made its US IPO paperwork public, intending to use the funds to expand its product line. Shares in its parent company, Geely, fell on the news. Zeekr reported a net loss of 3.87 billion Chinese yuan ($531.34 million) for the six months ended June 30, though net revenue more than doubled.

title: Intel Corp. stock falls Monday, underperforms market
summary: Intel Corp. (INTC) shares fell 1.62% to $38.23 on Monday, underperforming a mixed market session where the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose and the S&P 500 Index declined slightly. The stock closed $1.84 below its 52-week high established in September.

title: AMD-powered Frontier system wins first place in Top500 supercomputer ranking
summary: The Top500 computing organization has again named Frontier, an AMD-powered system at Oak Ridge National Laboratory, as the world's fastest supercomputer, achieving 1.194 exaflops. This marks the system's continued dominance since June, showcasing its high performance and impressive power efficiency. The ranking also highlights the rise of other powerful systems like Aurora and Eagle, while demonstrating AMD's growing presence in the supercomputing sector.

title: Intel upgraded by Mizuho Securities as PC, data center improve (INTC)
summary: Mizuho Securities upgraded Intel (INTC) to a "buy" rating from "neutral" due to anticipated improvements in the PC and data center markets. Analyst Vijay Rakesh positively adjusted his outlook on the semiconductor giant's shares. This upgrade reflects Mizuho's confidence in Intel's future performance in these key segments.

title: AES in talks to sell major coal-fired power plant in Vietnam: sources
summary: US energy firm AES Corp. is reportedly in discussions to sell its majority stake in Vietnam's 1.2 gigawatt Mong Duong 2 coal-fired power plant to Czech energy firm Sev.en Global Investments as part of its global strategy to divest coal assets by 2025. South Korea's Posco International, the second-largest shareholder, is also considering selling its 30% stake. The potential sale aligns with Vietnam's goal to end coal power generation by 2050, and AES itself plans to build an LNG terminal and gas-fired power plant in the country.

title: 2 Toll properties break ground a week apart in Arizona
summary: Toll Brothers Apartment Living has broken ground on two new multifamily projects, Navona and Lumara, in the Phoenix metropolitan area. These developments add 856 units to the firm's pipeline, bringing its total Phoenix metro unit count to 1,317 across four properties. The Phoenix market continues to be a strong area for new housing and employment, particularly in the tech sector.

title: Ohio forms supply chain group, anchored by Intel, to capture CHIPS funding
summary: Ohio has created the "Silicon Heartland Supplier consortium," anchored by Intel, to build a semiconductor supply chain ecosystem in the state and compete for federal CHIPS and Science Act funding. The initiative aims to support existing suppliers, attract new ones, and create thousands of jobs, leveraging the $52 billion federal program. This move is a strategic step to maximize secondary job creation from Intel's new microchip fabrication facilities in Ohio.

title: Broadcom and VMware Intend to Close Transaction on November 22, 2023
summary: Broadcom Inc. (NASDAQ: AVGO) and VMware, Inc. (NYSE: VMW) announced that they have received all necessary regulatory approvals and intend to finalize Broadcom's acquisition of VMware on November 22, 2023. This transaction follows approvals from various global regulatory bodies across multiple juristictions. The announcement includes details about both companies, cautionary statements regarding forward-looking information, and contact information for investor relations.

title: Diamond Sleet supply chain compromise distributes a modified CyberLink installer
summary: Microsoft Threat Intelligence has uncovered a supply chain attack by the North Korea-based threat actor Diamond Sleet (formerly ZINC). This attack involves a modified CyberLink application installer that includes malicious code and has impacted over 100 devices globally. Microsoft has taken steps to protect customers, including notifying CyberLink and adding the malicious certificate to its disallowed list, while also providing detection and mitigation guidance.

title: Gaming PCs under $1k: Black Friday makes gaming more budget-friendly at Newegg
summary: This article highlights several Black Friday deals on gaming PCs available at Newegg, all priced under $1,000. It presents three specific pre-built options from MXZ, Skytech, and IPASON, detailing their specifications, discounted prices, and suitability for various gaming needs. The article emphasizes that these deals make PC gaming more accessible for budget-conscious consumers and advises checking Newegg's full selection or using their PC Finder.

title: Market Leaders Collaborate with Synopsys to Realize Gains of Generative AI Across Synopsys.ai Full EDA Stack
summary: Synopsys announced the expansion of its Synopsys.ai EDA suite to integrate generative AI capabilities across its full stack, aiming to significantly enhance engineering productivity for chipmakers. This initiative, starting with Synopsys.ai Copilot, builds on its existing AI-driven optimization solutions. Leading companies like AMD, Intel, and Microsoft are collaborating with Synopsys to leverage these GenAI tools for various aspects of chip design, from RTL creation to formal verification.

title: Is Automotive Embedded Systems Market Really a Strong Market to Invest in?
summary: Advance Market Analytics has released a new research report on the "Automotive Embedded Systems Market Insights, to 2028." The study highlights the market's significant expansion driven by increasing R&D spending and identifies key players such as Robert Bosch, Continental, and Infineon Technologies. The report covers market trends like AI and Machine Learning integration, opportunities in electric vehicles, and drivers such as consumer awareness of vehicle safety.

title: Amkor to build $2 billion semiconductor testing plant in Peoria. 2,000 jobs promised
summary: Amkor Technology Inc. plans to build a $2 billion advanced semiconductor packaging and testing facility in Peoria, Arizona, creating approximately 2,000 jobs. The plant will partner with TSMC, testing chips produced for Apple Inc., and is expected to begin production within two to three years. This investment is seen as a significant step in bolstering the U.S. semiconductor supply chain and is supported by the CHIPS and Science Act.

title: ASML boss Wennink to retire in April; veteran Fouquet to step up
summary: ASML Chief Executive Peter Wennink will retire in April 2024 after overseeing a decade of significant growth, during which the company became Europe's largest technology firm. He will be succeeded by company veteran Christophe Fouquet, who has extensive experience with ASML's critical extreme ultraviolet (EUV) product lines. Additionally, Chief Technology Officer Martin van den Brink will also retire, and Jim Koonmen will join the management board as Chief Customer Officer.

title: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) Morgan Stanley Raises Target Forecast
summary: Morgan Stanley has increased its 12-month target price for NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) from $235.00 to $250.00, while re-iterating an "Overweight" rating. This upgrade indicates strong confidence in NVIDIA's fundamentals, driven by AI and data-center demand, despite market concerns about U.S.–China export restrictions and competitive pressures. The target hike acts as a counterweight to bearish sentiment, suggesting the recent stock pullback may be overdone relative to NVIDIA’s long-term earnings potential.

title: Amkor to invest US$2bn in US chip packaging
summary: Amkor Technology Inc. plans to invest US$2 billion to build an advanced semiconductor packaging facility in Peoria, Arizona. This new plant will create up to 2,000 jobs and is expected to start production within two to three years, primarily packaging chips made for Apple by TSMC in Phoenix. The investment aims to bolster the US’s chipmaking capabilities, reduce reliance on foreign packaging capacity, and has applied for Chips Act funding.

title: Meta Platforms, IBM create industrywide AI alliance to share technology
summary: Meta Platforms and IBM are leading an industry-wide AI Alliance of over 40 companies and organizations to promote open-source artificial intelligence development. The coalition aims to share technology, reduce risks, and focus on responsible AI development, including safety and security tools. This initiative seeks to increase open-source AI models and foster collaboration with academic researchers.

title: AMD releases new chips to power faster AI training
summary: AMD announced the availability of its new MI300 chips, including the Instinct MI300X accelerator and Instinct M1300A APU, designed to power demanding AI training and large language models. The company also unveiled the Ryzen 8040 mobile processors, which will bring enhanced AI capabilities to laptops in 2024. These releases position AMD to compete more directly with Nvidia in the booming AI chip market.

title: Nvidia set to discuss chip deals in Vietnam next week
summary: U.S. chip giant Nvidia is scheduled to discuss potential semiconductor cooperation deals with Vietnamese tech companies and authorities in Hanoi. This meeting is part of Vietnam's efforts to expand into chip design and manufacturing, capitalizing on global trade tensions. Nvidia's CEO, Jensen Huang, will meet with government representatives and companies like FPT, Vingroup, and Viettel to explore partnerships and boost Vietnam's semiconductor industry.

title: Themes Generative Artificial Intelligence ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:WISE)
summary: The Themes Generative Artificial Intelligence ETF (WISE) tracks an index of companies involved in AI-related industries, with a concentrated portfolio of 89 holdings. Launched on December 8, 2023, the ETF currently has $33.55 million in assets under management and a net expense ratio of 0.35%. Its top holdings include major tech companies like Advanced Micro Devices, Intel, Apple, and NVIDIA.

title: STMicroelectronics speeds up Edge AI adoption for product transformation
summary: STMicroelectronics has launched the ST Edge AI Suite, a free software suite aimed at simplifying the integration of Edge AI into products using ST hardware. This initiative seeks to accelerate the adoption of AI at the edge, offering tools like NanoEdge AI Studio freely for STM32 and other ARM Cortex-M microcontrollers. The suite supports various applications, from enhancing appliance efficiency to optimizing EV motors, and is expected to be released in the first half of 2024.

title: Cooperation with ASML in Netherlands becomes important for AI chip war
summary: The article highlights the growing importance of the Netherlands, specifically ASML, in the global semiconductor supply chain, especially with the surge in demand for AI chips. South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol's visit, accompanied by top executives from Samsung and SK Group, underscores the critical need for cooperation with ASML, the sole manufacturer of essential photolithography systems. This alliance is seen as crucial for the success of the South Korea-U.S.-Japan semiconductor partnership as the high-performing chip market expands rapidly due to AI.

title: Lisa Su’s net worth: How she saved AMD & built her wealth
summary: This article details Lisa Su's background, her impact as CEO of Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and how she built her net worth. It covers her engineering career, her strategies to diversify AMD's product portfolio and financial recovery, and her compensation, highlighting that her wealth primarily comes from AMD stock holdings. The article also touches upon AMD's stock performance under her leadership and her status relative to being a billionaire.

title: Despite Chinese Market Controls, Taiwan’s Semiconductor Supply Chain Remains Secure
summary: Despite China's recent export controls on gallium and germanium, critical elements for advanced semiconductors, Taiwan's semiconductor supply chain, particularly TSMC, is expected to remain secure. While China's dominance in gallium production gives it a potential chokepoint, market dynamics, including new investment interest in non-Chinese gallium production and TSMC's diversified supply chain, are likely to limit the long-term effectiveness of these trade weapons. The article highlights how these controls prompted broader awareness and a potential global response to reduce reliance on Chinese supply.

title: Intel unveils new AI chip to compete with Nvidia and AMD
summary: Intel has announced new computer chips, including the Gaudi3 AI chip designed to compete with Nvidia and AMD in the generative AI market, launching next year. The company also introduced Core Ultra chips for Windows laptops and PCs, and fifth-generation Xeon server chips, all featuring specialized AI parts (NPUs) to enhance AI program performance. Intel aims to increase its market share in AI hardware amidst Nvidia's dominance, with Gaudi3 targeting high-performance AI applications and Core Ultra processors focusing on AI capabilities in personal computing.

title: ASML, Samsung to spend US$760m to build S Korea plant
summary: ASML and Samsung Electronics will jointly invest US$760 million to construct a plant in South Korea dedicated to developing advanced semiconductor processing technology, utilizing ASML's next-generation extreme ultraviolet equipment. This initiative expands ASML's presence in South Korea and aims to strengthen the country's technology sector, with South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol actively promoting stronger tech ties and domestic innovation. The collaboration also includes plans for a "Korea-Netherlands Advanced Semiconductor Academy" to address labor shortages and enhance educational opportunities in the field.

title: CACI International (CACI) Secures $64M U.S. Air Force Contract
summary: CACI International (CACI) has secured a five-year task order valued at up to $64 million from the U.S. Air Force to provide life cycle hardware and systems engineering support for the Distributed Common Ground System (DCGS). This contract aims to enhance agile and effective quick reaction capabilities for ground stations and improve intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance operations. This win is part of a continuous flow of significant contracts for CACI from various government agencies, bolstering its reputation as a key defense contractor.

title: Sony at CES2024 - The World's Largest Technology Event - Sony Group Portal
summary: Sony will be exhibiting at CES 2024, the world's largest technology event, showcasing initiatives that support creators. The company has a long history with CES, dating back to 1977, and has evolved its presence from a consumer electronics trade show to a platform for introducing cutting-edge technologies. A press conference will be streamed on January 8, 2024, at 5:00 p.m. PST, detailing their announcements.

title: Are the Chips Down for First Trust Nasdaq Semiconductor ETF?
summary: The First Trust Nasdaq Semiconductor ETF (FTXL) has seen a significant 52.7% rise year-to-date, fueled by strong performances from top holdings like Intel and Qualcomm. Despite a challenging period for chipmakers, the global chip sector is forecasted to grow by 13.1% in 2024, though some analysts and investors express concerns about the sector being overheated. Intel's new Core Ultra processors and substantial investments in R&D and manufacturing are seen as positive drivers for the industry.

title: Folsom Intel Campus To Cut 235 More Jobs By End Of The Month
summary: Intel Corporation is set to cut an additional 235 jobs at its Folsom campus by the end of December, bringing the total reductions for the year to 784 positions. These layoffs are part of Intel's strategy to reduce costs and accelerate its business amid declining revenue and increased competition. The Folsom campus, which primarily focuses on office, research, development, and support, remains the largest technology employer in the local region despite the cuts.

title: Federal Circuit Vacates $2.18 Billion Verdict As WilmerHale Receives Big Win for Intel
summary: The Federal Circuit reversed a $2.18 billion jury verdict against Intel in a patent infringement case brought by VLSI Technology. WilmerHale successfully represented Intel, leading to the case being remanded for a damages trial on only one of the two patents initially at issue. This victory highlights WilmerHale's long-term strategy and litigation capabilities in complex, high-stakes intellectual property disputes.

title: Intel, Marvell, Agilent Technologies advise state of layoffs
summary: Three Silicon Valley technology companies — Intel, Marvell, and Agilent Technologies — have notified California officials of upcoming job cuts. A total of 166 positions are set to be eliminated across these companies.

title: Intel’s $25 billion investment in Israel ‘important and significant’ says country’s finance minister
summary: Intel Corp. is making a $25 billion investment in Israel, which the country's government has described as "important and significant." This investment follows Intel securing $3.2 billion in incentives from Israel. The funds will support a new chip manufacturing plant in Kiryat Gat, expected to begin operations by 2028 and create thousands of jobs.

title: Israel grants Intel $3.2bn for new $25bn chip plant
summary: Israel has approved a $3.2 billion grant for Intel to build a new $25 billion chip manufacturing plant in southern Israel, marking the largest investment ever in the country. In addition to the grant, Intel has committed to purchasing $16.6 billion worth of goods and services from Israeli suppliers over the next decade. This investment comes amid the ongoing conflict in Gaza and is seen as a significant show of support by a major US company for Israel's economy.

title: Astera Labs IPO, an overview
summary: Astera Labs, founded in 2017, specializes in connectivity solutions for data-centric systems, particularly for cloud and AI infrastructure. The company is preparing for an IPO in 2024, with Morgan Stanley and JPMorgan Chase & Co. as lead underwriters. Astera Labs' competitive advantage lies in its innovative products for accelerated computing and its strategic use of cloud environments for product design, distinguishing it from competitors like Credo, Trenton Systems, and Magma.

title: Twelve Japanese companies join forces to develop automotive SoCs using chiplet technology
summary: A consortium named ‘Advanced SoC Research for Automotive’ (ASRA) comprising twelve leading Japanese companies, including major automakers, electrical component manufacturers, and semiconductor firms, has been established to research and develop high-performance automotive System-on-Chip (SoC) using chiplet technology. The goal is to integrate these SoCs into mass-production vehicles from 2030 onwards. Keiji Yamamoto of Toyota Motor Corporation will chair ASRA, with Nobuaki Kawahara of Denso Corporation serving as executive director.

title: Netherlands Blocks ASML Exports of Some Chip-Making Equipment to China
summary: The Netherlands has partially blocked ASML's exports of certain lithography systems to China, affecting the shipment of NXT:2050i and NXT:2100i systems in 2023. This decision follows U.S. export restrictions and impacts China's ability to produce advanced microprocessors. ASML, a crucial chip-equipment manufacturer, confirmed the Dutch government's partial revocation of the export license.

title: The U.S.-China Chip War: Who Dares to Win?
summary: The U.S. and China are engaged in a semiconductor industry rivalry, with the U.S. aiming to boost domestic chip production through the CHIPS and Science Act while imposing sanctions on China to hinder its self-reliance in the sector. However, the CHIPS Act faces challenges like high production costs and specific conditions that may deter leading companies. Other nations like the EU and Japan are also implementing strategies to increase their semiconductor manufacturing capabilities amid global supply chain concerns.

title: Intel and DigitalBridge Launch Articul8, an Enterprise Generative AI Company
summary: Intel and DigitalBridge announced the formation of Articul8 AI, Inc., an independent company offering a full-stack, vertically-optimized, and secure generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) software platform for enterprise customers. Articul8 was created with intellectual property and technology developed at Intel, and the two companies will collaborate on go-to-market opportunities. The platform keeps customer data, training, and inference within enterprise security perimeters and supports cloud, on-prem, or hybrid deployment.

title: Justin Hotard to Lead Intel’s Data Center and AI Group
summary: Intel Corporation announced the appointment of Justin Hotard as executive vice president and general manager of its Data Center and AI Group (DCAI), effective February 1, 2024. Hotard brings over 20 years of experience in computing and data center businesses, focusing on driving transformation and growth, and will be responsible for Intel's data center products and their mission to bring AI everywhere. He will report directly to CEO Pat Gelsinger and lead Intel's suite of data center products including Xeon processors, GPUs, and accelerators.

title: Microchip wins $72 million federal CHIPS Act grant, Oregon’s first, for Gresham expansion
summary: Microchip Technology received a $72 million federal CHIPS Act grant for its $800 million Gresham factory expansion, marking Oregon's first such grant. This funding is part of a larger $162 million subsidy Microchip received, which also includes $90 million for a Colorado plant expansion, aiming to triple the domestic production of semiconductors essential for various industries. The federal grant complements an earlier $11 million award from Oregon and is expected to boost the state's economy and create 300 jobs.

title: US plans $162 million award to Microchip Technology to boost production
summary: The U.S. Commerce Department plans to award Microchip Technology $162 million in government grants to increase domestic production of semiconductors and microcontroller units. This funding aims to triple production at two U.S. factories, reducing reliance on foreign supply chains for crucial components used in various industries. This award is part of the "Chips for America" program, designed to boost U.S. semiconductor manufacturing and research.

title: Nvidia Gains as Bank of America Names It Top Sector Pick on 'GenAI Dominance'
summary: Nvidia's shares increased after Bank of America named it a "top sector pick" due to its powerful position in generative AI (genAI). Analysts predict Nvidia's genAI dominance could lead to $100 billion in incremental free cash flow over two years, suggesting potential for strategic partnerships or acquisitions in genAI software. The company's solid free cash flow generation provides flexibility for future growth initiatives.

title: CES 2024: Intel Delivers New High-Level Compute Solutions in Mobile, Desktop and Edge
summary: Intel announced its full lineup of 14th Gen mobile and desktop processors at CES 2024, including new HX-series mobile processors, mainstream 65-watt and 35-watt desktop processors, and the new Intel Core mobile processor Series 1. These new offerings are designed to provide enhanced performance, efficiency, and platform features for gamers, creators, professionals, and mainstream PC users, extending from mobile systems to desktop and edge devices. The announcement emphasizes Intel's commitment to delivering a wide range of computing solutions, complementing the introduction of Intel Core Ultra-powered systems.

title: Why Intel has declined to participate in the Science Based Targets initiative
summary: Intel has chosen not to formally participate in the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) despite aligning with its net-zero guidance. This decision stems from the company's long history of emissions reductions, which SBTi's near-term requirements do not fully account for, and the framework's inability to credit emissions reductions from new technologies like AI. While Intel has significantly reduced emissions historically, its absolute emissions are currently increasing due to expanded manufacturing output.

title: Aehr Reports Strong Revenue and Earnings Growth for the Second Quarter and First Six Months of Fiscal 2024
summary: Aehr Test Systems announced strong financial results for its second quarter and first six months of fiscal 2024, with revenue up 45% and 65% respectively year-over-year. Despite the growth, the company revised its full-year fiscal 2024 revenue guidance downward due to a temporary slowing in the electric vehicle market's impact on silicon carbide orders and shifts in customer product mix impacting order timing. Aehr remains confident in long-term demand for its test solutions in various markets, including gallium nitride applications and silicon photonic optical I/O.

title: Intel Drives ‘AI Everywhere’ into Automotive Market at CES
summary: Intel announced its expansion into the automotive market, unveiling plans to acquire Silicon Mobility SAS for EV energy management and introducing a new family of AI-enhanced software-defined vehicle (SDV) SoCs. Zeekr will be the first OEM to adopt these new SoCs for generative AI experiences in their next-generation vehicles. Intel also committed to developing an open automotive chiplet platform and chairing a new SAE standard for vehicle power management.

title: Press Kit: Intel at CES 2024: Bringing ‘AI Everywhere’
summary: At CES 2024, Intel highlighted its commitment to "AI Everywhere" with new mobile and desktop processors and advancements in the automotive industry. The company showcased its Core Ultra and 14th Gen Core processors, emphasizing their role in AI PCs and software-defined vehicles. Intel also reinforced its partnerships and technological contributions for a more sustainable and inclusive future.

title: Top Performing Leveraged/Inverse ETFs: 01/07/2024
summary: This article outlines the top-performing leveraged and inverse ETFs for the week of January 7, 2024, highlighting their weekly returns and the underlying market factors that contributed to their performance. It features funds like MSOX, BOIL, and SOXS, explaining the reasons for their gains such as federal legalization support, natural gas price increases, and semiconductor sector declines. The piece emphasizes the volitile nature of these leveraged funds and advises investors to conduct thorough research.

title: SHOC Stock Fund Price and Chart — NYSE:SHOC
summary: The SHOC (Strive U.S. Semiconductor ETF) tracks the Bloomberg US Listed Semiconductors Select Index and invests in US-listed semiconductor companies. The fund has assets under management of $165.06 million, an expense ratio of 0.40%, and has shown significant price increases over the last year. Detailed performance, holdings, and technical analysis are available, alongside answers to frequently asked questions about ETFs.

title: Intel Corp. stock falls Friday, underperforms market
summary: Shares of Intel Corp. (INTC) fell 1.09% on Friday, closing at $47.12. This underperformed the broader market, which saw the S&P 500 Index rise slightly and the Dow Jones Industrial Average fall. The stock closed $4.16 below its 52-week high of $51.28 reached in late December.

title: Ayar Labs Appoints Ganesh Moorthy and Craig Barratt to Board of Directors
summary: Ayar Labs, a silicon photonics provider, has expanded its Board of Directors with the appointment of Ganesh Moorthy, President and CEO of Microchip Technology Inc., and Craig Barratt, former CEO of Atheros. Additionally, co-founder Vladimir Stojanovic has been named Chief Technology Officer. These appointments are expected to bolster Ayar Labs' leadership in optical I/O solutions for AI, high-performance computing, and data centers.

title: Infosys announces its first acquisition of the year 2024, here are the details
summary: Infosys has announced its first acquisition of 2024, acquiring InSemi, a Bengaluru-based provider of semiconductor design and embedded services, for Rs 280 crore. The acquisition is expected to close in Q4 of fiscal 2024 and aims to strengthen Infosys' engineering R&D capabilities. This move follows a significant jump in Infosys' stock price after its December quarter earnings met market expectations.

title: Optimizing Communication and Data Sharing in Multi-Core SoC Designs
summary: This article discusses the evolution of interconnects in Systems-on-Chip (SoCs), from shared buses to Network-on-Chip (NoC) architectures, highlighting their role in managing data flow and enhancing performance. It delves into the importance of cache coherence for data consistency in multi-core designs and the challenges engineers face in optimizing communication and data sharing as semiconductor technology advances. The article also mentions Arteris' Ncore Cache Coherent Interconnect as a solution.

title: Is eVTOL sector about to take off?
summary: At CES 2024, Hyundai Motor unveiled its eVTOL aircraft, the S-A2, signaling a potential takeoff for the Electric Vertical Takeoff and Landing (eVTOL) sector. The article discusses the technological advancements, cost efficiencies, and supply chain dynamics of eVTOLs, highlighting the role of companies like Molicel and the opportunities for Taiwanese businesses in this emerging market. It also emphasizes the need for regulatory frameworks to support urban air mobility (UAM) and the ongoing trend toward vehicle electrification.

title: Mobileye, Execs Allegedly Hid Inventory Issues From Investors
summary: Mobileye Global Inc. and its executives are facing a shareholder class-action lawsuit for allegedly concealing information about excess inventory purchases. The complaint states that the company bought inventory exceeding demand in fiscal year 2023, including up to 7 million EyeQ System-on-Chip units, which negatively impacted its financial results. The lawsuit, filed in the US District Court for the Southern District of New York, claims Mobileye misrepresented its inventory and supply chain situation.

title: S&P 500 Rises as US Stocks Build on Record High: Markets Wrap
summary: US stocks, including the S&P 500, continued to build on record highs, with traders evaluating robust economic signals and upcoming corporate earnings reports. Despite warnings of an overheated market, equities are gaining momentum due to expectations of Fed rate cuts and the sustained growth driven by artificial intelligence. Key earnings reports from companies like Netflix, Tesla, and Intel are anticipated this week.

title: Direct RF system-on-module introduced by Mercury Systems
summary: Mercury Systems has launched a new Direct RF system-on-module (SOM) that utilizes Intel Agilex Field Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) to directly digitize RF signals. This technology aims to improve size, weight, power, and cost efficiency (SWaP-C) and reduce latency by eliminating the need for analog signal down-conversion. The DRF2580 SOM is designed for applications in radar, communications, and electronic warfare, enhancing security and speed in aerospace and defense operations.

title: Intel Opens Fab 9 in New Mexico
summary: Intel has officially opened Fab 9 in Rio Rancho, New Mexico, representing a $3.5 billion investment in advanced semiconductor packaging technologies, including its Foveros 3D packaging technology.
