This new facility, co-located with Fab 11X, creates an end-to-end manufacturing process in the US for advanced packaging, fostering supply chain resilience and furthering Intel's innovation in heterogeneous computing using multiple chiplets. The investment has also generated thousands of jobs and reinforces Intel's commitment to corporate responsibility, including environmental sustainability and community engagement in New Mexico.

title: ASML orders triple in fourth quarter amid soaring demand for advanced chip gear, especially in China
summary: ASML Holding NV saw its order bookings more than triple in the fourth quarter of last year, reaching €9.19 billion, signaling a recovery in the semiconductor industry. This surge was driven by strong demand for its most advanced chip-making machines, particularly from China, as companies there hastened purchases ahead of new export restrictions. ASML's unique position as the sole producer of equipment for sophisticated semiconductors makes its performance a key indicator for the industry's health.

title: Intel stock sinks more than 10% as early 2024 outlook comes up short
summary: Intel's stock dropped over 10% after the company issued a first-quarter 2024 outlook that fell short of analyst estimates, despite beating Wall Street expectations for the latest quarter. While CEO Pat Gelsinger stated the core PC and server chip businesses are healthy, overall sales are impacted by weakness in subsidiaries like Mobileye and its programmable chip unit. The company also broke a seven-quarter streak of declining revenue, with a 10% sales growth in Q4 2023.

title: Intel Gives Bleak Forecast in Sign Comeback Is Going Slowly
summary: Intel's shares tumbled more than 12% after the company provided a disappointing sales and profit forecast for the first quarter, significantly missing Wall Street estimates. CEO Pat Gelsinger claimed the market's reaction was "overblown." This marks Intel's biggest single-day decline since July 2020, suggesting its comeback efforts are progressing slowly.

title: Intel collaborates with UMC to develop new 12nm process node
summary: Intel Foundry Services (IFS) is partnering with United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC) to develop a new 12nm semiconductor process platform. This new node, aimed at high-growth markets like communication infrastructure and networking, will be manufactured at Intel's Arizona fabs, with production anticipated to start in 2027. The collaboration is expected to leverage UMC's capabilities and market access to bolster Intel's foundry business and global semiconductor supply chain presence.

title: SES AI Board of Directors Taps Former Intel CEO
summary: SES AI Corporation has appointed Brian Krzanich, former CEO of Intel Corp. and CDK Global Inc., to its board of directors as an independent director, effective January 26, 2024. Krzanich's extensive leadership experience in commercializing next-generation technologies for automotive applications is expected to be vital as SES AI develops its Li-Metal batteries, especially in integrating AI and machine learning for material discovery and manufacturing. This board expansion follows the company's recent B-sample Joint Development Agreement with a major automotive OEM and its ongoing innovations in battery technology and urban air mobility.

title: Intel and UMC announce collaboration to develop and manufacture in Arizona
summary: Intel Corp. and United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC) have announced a strategic collaboration to develop and manufacture a 12-nanometer semiconductor process platform. This initiative targets high-growth markets and leverages Intel's existing fabrication facilities in Chandler, Arizona, with production anticipated to begin in 2027. The partnership aims to broaden semiconductor access for global customers and enhance Intel's goal of becoming a leading foundry.

title: Intel, UMC to collaborate on chip fabs
summary: Intel and United Microelectronics Corp. are partnering to develop a 12-nanometer semiconductor process platform, aiming to target high-growth markets. This collaboration will leverage Intel’s U.S.-based manufacturing capacity and UMC’s Process Design Kit, with production expected to begin in 2027 at Intel’s Arizona facility. The partnership supports Intel's goal of becoming the world's second-largest foundry by 2030 and addresses the need for secure and geographically diversified supply chains.

title: Why the Intel-UMC chip foundry deal is a win-win
summary: Intel has partnered with United Microelectronics Corp. (UMC) to develop 12 nm semiconductor processes, aiming to expand Intel's foundry business and diversify the global semiconductor supply chain. This collaboration allows UMC to leverage advanced manufacturing capabilities without significant capital investment and helps Intel gain foundry market experience while focusing on more advanced process technologies. Both companies stand to benefit from this strategic alliance in the competitive semiconductor industry.

title: The Ripple Effect - Finding Company Connections from Detailed Estimates
summary: This article explores how sell-side analyst co-coverage can identify strong economic linkages between companies, creating investment opportunities. It demonstrates a lead-lag return relationship among connected firms, showing that buying or selling stocks based on the performance of their connected companies can generate alpha across developed markets. The research also highlights that information processing for companies with complex networks takes longer, leading to higher long-short returns.

title: Fund selectors flock to AI and semiconductor ETFs in January
summary: Investors dramatically increased their allocation to semiconductor and artificial intelligence ETFs in January, betting on the continued strong performance of these themes throughout 2024. The Xtrackers Artificial Intelligence & Big Data UCITS ETF (XAIX) and the iShares MSCI Global Semiconductors UCITS ETF (SEMI) saw significant inflows, indicating sustained investor appetite for AI and chip-related investments despite a decrease in overall thematic ETF inflows in 2023.

title: Exclusive | Intel Delays $20 Billion Ohio Project, Citing Slow Chip Market
summary: Intel is delaying its $20 billion chip-manufacturing project in Ohio, pushing the completion of its factories to late 2026. This delay is attributed to challenging market conditions and the slow distribution of U.S. government grants intended to boost the domestic semiconductor industry. The company's stock symbol is INTC.

title: Intel's Lunar Lake reference laptops aim to improve power efficiency with Cirrus Logic hardware
summary: Cirrus Logic has announced that its audio and power converter chips will be integrated into reference laptops powered by Intel's upcoming Lunar Lake CPUs, aiming to significantly enhance power efficiency and battery life. The new CP9314 power converter chip is expected to achieve record-breaking efficiency and enable thinner laptop designs. This move highlights Intel's focus on making its next-generation mobile CPUs as efficient as possible, potentially challenging Apple's M-series chips in the portable computing market.

title: News | Mall Landlord Simon Plans To Spend $800 Million on Redevelopments This Year
summary: Simon Property Group plans to invest $800 million in 2024 for redevelopment projects across the U.S., diversifying its retail centers and attracting more shoppers. These mixed-use developments will be funded by internally generated cash flow and include new features like multifamily housing, restaurants, and entertainment venues. The REIT aims to transform its malls to meet evolving consumer demands, with projects spanning from Orange County to Boston and Seattle to Roosevelt Field.

title: Manufacturer positions itself for semiconductor success - Buffalo Business First
summary: Materion Corp. is strategically investing in its Buffalo plant to capitalize on the growing semiconductor industry. With approximately 200 local employees, the company produces specialty materials for major semiconductor manufacturers like Micron and Intel. This positioning allows Materion to benefit from the increasing momentum in the region's semiconductor sector.

title: ASML's next chip challenge: rollout of its new $350 mln 'High NA EUV' machine
summary: ASML is ramping up production of its new $350 million "High NA EUV" machine, a critical device for advanced chipmaking, with plans to ship "a number" this year. The company anticipates an inflection point for customer adoption around 2026-2027, despite some analysts suggesting economic viability might be further in the future. Intel has already received a pilot device, while other major chipmakers like TSMC and Samsung intend to use the technology.

title: Israel’s chipmaker Tower Semiconductor proposes to set up $8 billion plant in India: report
summary: Israel's Tower Semiconductor has proposed an $8 billion chipmaking plant in India, focusing on 65nm and 40nm chips for various sectors like automotive and wearable electronics. This initiative aligns with India's $10 billion chip manufacturing scheme, offering a 50% capital expenditure subsidy. The proposal follows a previous suspended plan and comes as other companies, like CG Power, are also establishing semiconductor facilities in India.

title: CEO of the Year 2023: Meet Our 12 Finalists From the For-Profit and Nonprofit Sectors
summary: Columbus CEO announced the 12 finalists for its 13th annual CEO of the Year awards, recognizing leaders from large and small for-profit and nonprofit organizations in Central Ohio. These C-suite executives were selected by their peers for outstanding leadership and contributions to their companies and communities. The article details each finalist's background, leadership philosophy, and key achievements in their respective roles.

title: US considering more than $10 billion in subsidies for Intel, Bloomberg reports
summary: The Biden administration is reportedly in talks to provide Intel Corp with over $10 billion in subsidies, including loans and direct grants, under the CHIPS Act. This funding aims to boost semiconductor manufacturing in the U.S., supporting Intel's plans for chip factories in Arizona, New Mexico, and Ohio, and potentially impacting other chipmakers like TSMC, Micron, and Samsung also seeking funds. The move comes as Intel faces delays in its Ohio plant due to market slowdowns and the slow rollout of federal dollars.

title: Exclusive: Amgen's New Albany biopharma packaging plant is now open - Columbus Business First
summary: Amgen's new biopharma packaging plant in New Albany, Ohio, is now operational, featuring the company's most advanced digital and automation technology globally. Currently, two of the eventual eight production lines are running, with the plant poised to play a significant role in Amgen's $28 billion biotech operations. This opening marks a key development for the New Albany International Business Park and the local Columbus area.

title: US awards $1.5 bln to GlobalFoundries for semiconductor production
summary: The U.S. government is awarding $1.5 billion to GlobalFoundries to subsidize semiconductor production, marking one of the first major awards from a 2022 congressional fund. This funding aims to bolster domestic chip production, with GlobalFoundries planning a new facility in New York and expanding existing operations to generate $12.5 billion in overall potential investment and produce essential chips for national security. The chips manufactured will support various sectors, including satellite communications, defense, and automotive safety features.

title: Expanding the Edge of Wireless Innovation with Analog Devices
summary: Analog Devices (ADI) is collaborating with partners to enhance wireless connectivity, focusing on reducing energy consumption and complexity in 5G and future 6G networks. ADI's solutions, such as their software-defined RF transceiver family, integrate complex signal chains into single microchips to improve efficiency and reduce the carbon footprint of Radio Access Networks. They will showcase these innovations, including significant energy savings with Vodafone, at MWC 2024 in Barcelona.

title: Nvidia CEO Wears Fuchsia Leather Jacket At Insight Sales Event
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang wore a fuchsia leather jacket, Insight's signature color, at the company's Amplify sales event, signaling a strong partnership in generative AI. An Insight executive stated that Huang's virtual presence and choice of attire underscored the significance of their collaboration, especially as Nvidia expands its AI solutions. The event also featured Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger and focused on generative AI use cases, infrastructure upgrades, and the growing demand for AI PCs amidst a broader PC refresh cycle.

title: Intel Launches World’s First Systems Foundry Designed for the AI Era
summary: Intel has launched Intel Foundry, positioning it as the world's first systems foundry tailored for the AI era, with an expanded process roadmap targeting leadership by the end of the decade. The company announced new process technologies, ASAT capabilities, key customer design wins including Microsoft, and strong support from ecosystem partners. Intel Foundry also emphasized its commitment to sustainability, aiming for 100% renewable electricity and net-zero emissions by specific target dates.

title: Intel Launches World’s First Systems Foundry Designed for the AI Era
summary: Intel has launched Intel Foundry, positioning it as the world's first systems foundry for the AI era, and announced an expanded process roadmap including Intel 14A and specialized node evolutions. The company affirmed its "five-nodes-in-four-years" process roadmap is on track and highlighted significant customer momentum, including Microsoft choosing Intel's 18A process for a chip design, alongside strong support from ecosystem partners. Intel outlined its commitment to sustainability, aiming to be the industry's most sustainable foundry with goals for 100% renewable electricity, net-positive water, and net-zero waste by 2030, and net-zero Scope 1 & 2 GHG emissions by 2040.

title: Intel’s CEO Says AI Is the Key to the Company’s Comeback
summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger states that AI and renewed investment in cutting-edge manufacturing are crucial for the company's comeback, allowing it to become a leading supplier of AI chips. He announced a rebrand of Intel's foundry business with Microsoft as a major customer for its new manufacturing technology. Gelsinger expects Intel's 18A manufacturing process to compete with TSMC and highlights advancements like backside power and being first in line for next-generation lithography machines as key differentiators.

title: How Rio Rancho turned dust into a growing city
summary: Rio Rancho, New Mexico, transformed from dusty land into a rapidly growing city, initially marketed for retirement but evolving into a hub for young families and businesses. Key developments include the establishment of Intel's operations and the growth of its public school system, positioning it to potentially become the second-largest city in New Mexico. Despite challenges like relying on nearby Albuquerque for commerce, efforts to attract investment, improve infrastructure, and foster local businesses are driving its continued expansion and identity.

title: Intel Releases 2023 Product Security Report Highlighting Commitment to Security Assurance
summary: Intel has released its 2023 Product Security Report, demonstrating a strong commitment to security assurance with proactive investments accounting for 94% of vulnerability disclosures, the highest in five years. The report highlights a 39% reduction in hardware and firmware vulnerabilities compared to 2022 and notes Intel's leadership in product security assurance as validated by ABI Research. This commitment is supported by a record 256 researchers participating in Intel's Bug Bounty program, underscoring the company's transparent approach to security.

title: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon to Deliver Keynote Speech at COMPUTEX 2024
summary: Cristiano Amon, President and CEO of Qualcomm Incorporated, will deliver a keynote speech at COMPUTEX 2024 on June 3. He will discuss how on-device generative AI and next-generation computing, particularly advanced AI PCs powered by Snapdragon® X Series, will transform productivity and creativity. The event, themed "Connecting AI," will feature other industry leaders and showcase 1,500 exhibitors.

title: How Nvidia grew to a trillion-dollar company fueling the AI revolution
summary: Nvidia has grown into a nearly $3 trillion company by specializing in Graphics Processing Units (GPUs), which have become crucial for the artificial intelligence revolution. Initially used for gaming, GPUs' ability to perform concurrent calculations makes them ideal for AI, giving Nvidia a significant first-mover advantage and market dominance. The company's unique technology and supply chain have led to widespread adoption by major tech firms, with discussions emerging about potential shortages and the need for continued investment in chip manufacturing.

title: Notice of Funding Opportunity: CHIPS National Advanced Packaging Manufacturing Program (NAPMP) Materials and Substrates Research and Development
summary: The CHIPS National Advanced Packaging Manufacturing Program (NAPMP) has distributed $300 million in funding for advanced substrates and material research. Awards of $100 million each went to Absolics, Inc., Applied Materials, Inc., and Arizona State University for projects focused on glass-core packaging, silicon-core substrate technology, and fan-out-wafer-level-processing, respectively. This funding aims to accelerate domestic R&D, establish robust manufacturing capacity, and develop a skilled workforce for the advanced packaging industry in the U.S.

title: Intel unveils Sierra Forest and Granite Rapids-D Xeon processors to support ‘AI Everywhere’
summary: Intel has announced two new Xeon processor platforms, Sierra Forest and Granite Rapids-D, alongside a new Edge Platform, to support "AI Everywhere" initiatives. The Granite Rapids-D processor, due in 2025, aims to extend Intel's leadership in virtual radio access network (vRAN) workloads, while Sierra Forest, launching in the second half of this year, will offer up to 288 cores for 5G workloads. These advancements are designed to improve total cost of ownership, operational efficiency, and enable customers to leverage AI and automation across 5G, edge, and enterprise infrastructures.

title: Intel’s new Intel vPro platform to power the next generation of AI PCs
summary: Intel has launched its new Intel vPro platform, integrating Intel Arc GPUs and Intel Core 14th Gen CPUs, to accelerate artificial intelligence capabilities on personal computers. This platform aims to introduce the "AI PC" era, offering significant productivity gains and enhanced security features for businesses and educational institutions. Over 100 new device models from various partners will be powered by this technology.

title: American tech is still being found in Russian weapons used in Ukraine
summary: Despite sanctions and export controls, American technology continues to be found in Russian weapons used in Ukraine. A report given to Senator Richard Blumenthal by Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy listed 211 U.S.-manufactured chips and semiconductors from companies like Intel, AMD, Analog Devices, and Texas Instruments in Russian missiles and high-tech products. Companies claim they've halted shipments to Russia, suggesting illicit diversion, while experts dismiss the idea that these components are merely repurposed household appliance parts.

title: Intel Launches Altera, Its New Standalone FPGA Company
summary: Intel announced the official launch of Altera, its new standalone FPGA company, aiming for leadership in the over $55 billion market opportunity. Led by CEO Sandra Rivera and COO Shannon Poulin, Altera plans to expand its portfolio with FPGAs featuring built-in AI and enhance its Quartus Prime software. The company introduced new products like Agilex 9, Agilex 7 F-series/I-series, Agilex 5, and the upcoming Agilex 3, targeting various market segments from military-aerospace to embedded and edge applications.

title: Aehr Announces Shipment of New High-Power Configured FOX-XP(TM) System for Wafer Level Burn-in and Stabilization of Next Generation Silicon Photonics Integrated Circuits
summary: Aehr Test Systems announced the shipment of a new high-power configured FOX-XP™ system to a major silicon photonics customer. This system is designed for high-volume wafer-level burn-in and stabilization of next-generation silicon photonics integrated circuits, which require significantly more power than previous generations. The FOX-XP system, capable of testing up to 8,192 high-power optical devices in parallel across nine wafers, expands Aehr's market opportunities in optical I/O and co-packaged optics for data centers and telecommunications.

title: As CHIPS Act Money Is Set to Be Distributed, Experts See Pivotal Point in Establishing U.S. Market Structure for the Long Term, Creating High Stakes for Intel, TSMC, Broadcom, Qualcomm, Nvidia, Micron, and Texas Instruments
summary: The article discusses the significant impact of the CHIPS Act funding distribution on the U.S. semiconductor market, with experts viewing it as a pivotal moment for long-term market structure. The Department of Commerce has recently disbursed $1.5 billion to GlobalFoundries Inc (GFS), and further billions are expected for other domestic manufacturers, notably Intel (INTC). This development is seen as having high stakes for major companies like TSMC, Broadcom, Qualcomm, Nvidia, Micron, and Texas Instruments.

title: Tower Semiconductor plans three temporary shutdowns at Newport Beach site
summary: Tower Semiconductor is planning three week-long furlough periods for 699 employees at its Newport Beach, California site in April, July, and October 2024. Employees will use accrued time off or file for unemployment during these shutdowns, which come after a 12.4 percent drop in Q4 2023 revenue due to decreased demand from the automotive sector. This news follows Intel's pulled acquisition of Tower Semiconductor and prior reports of the company proposing an $8 billion chip fabrication plant in India.

title: HP Inc. CEO: Security Will Be A ‘Key Advantage’ In Our AI PC Strategy
summary: HP CEO Enrique Lores announced that security will be a "key advantage" in the company's AI PC strategy, emphasizing local large language model processing for enhanced security, lower costs, and faster performance compared to cloud solutions. HP plans to leverage its Wolf Security technologies to protect AI models running on these new PCs, which are expected to launch in the second half of 2024. Lores believes AI PCs represent a significant opportunity for channel partners due to device sales and the coming wave of AI-optimized software.

title: Tower plans temporary closures of US wafer fab
summary: Tower Semiconductor is implementing three week-long closures of its US operations, including its wafer fab in Newport Beach, California, in an effort to avoid longer shutdowns and job cuts. This decision impacts 699 employees and is attributed to a drop in customer orders due to previous overstocking. The news follows recent reports of Tower seeking to build a wafer fab in India and a previous acquisition attempt by Intel that fell through.

title: Intel’s Dominance in the Chip Market: An Analysis
summary: This article analyzes Intel's enduring dominance in the chip market, highlighting its strategic use of pricing, market segmentation, and significant R&D investments. It details Intel's chip advancements, its competitive landscape with rivals like AMD and NVIDIA, and the impact of the CHIPS Act on its growth. The piece also covers the rebranding of Altera and elaborates on Intel's product strategies, including its new Xeon processors and various Core i-series price points.

title: IWD 2024: From Chennai to California: What I've learned from a lifetime in STEM
summary: Gayathri Natarajan, a Program Manager at Synopsys, shares her journey in STEM from Chennai, India, to California, highlighting the often-unseen applications of STEM in daily life. She emphasizes the importance of women supporting women, crediting her grandmother's encouragement for her academic path and decision to study abroad. Natarajan advocates for more women in STEM, detailing her efforts in founding and joining women's networking groups to empower and mentor future generations.

title: TSMC to Win More Than $5 Billion in Grants for US Chip Plant
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) is expected to receive over $5 billion in federal grants for its chipmaking project in Arizona, a significant move for President Biden's bid to revitalize American semiconductor manufacturing. This award, part of the 2022 Chips and Science Act, is not yet finalized but marks a major step in distributing funds to leading chipmakers like Intel, Micron Technology, and Samsung. TSMC is investing $40 billion in two Arizona fabrication facilities, though the project has faced delays due to labor shortages and technological considerations.

title: Intel Releases Continuous Profiler to Increase CPU Performance
summary: Intel has released Continuous Profiler to open source, a tool developed by Intel Granulate that unifies multiple profilers into a flame graph view. This solution helps developers, performance engineers, and DevOps continuously identify runtime inefficiencies and CPU bottlenecks to optimize applications, improve throughput, and reduce latency. The profiler is already being used by companies like ironSource, ShareChat, and Snap Inc. and integrates with Intel Granulate's continuous optimization services.

title: HP's 6 Biggest Announcements At Amplify Partner Conference 2024
summary: At its Amplify Partner Conference 2024, HP unveiled several major advancements focusing on hybrid work and AI integration. Key announcements included new business laptops and mobile workstations featuring AI capabilities, an expanded partnership with Nvidia for AI Studio software, and a revamped Workplace Experience Platform (WEX) with AI-powered management. HP also introduced new conferencing solutions, an AI MasterClass program for partners, and new Color LaserJet Pro 3000 printers for SMBs.

title: Intel Appoints Stacy Smith to Board of Directors
summary: Intel Corporation has appointed Stacy Smith, executive chairman of Kioxia Corporation and chair of Autodesk Inc., to its board of directors, effective immediately. Smith will serve as an independent director and join the board’s Audit & Finance Committee. With nearly 30 years at Intel prior to his 2018 retirement, Smith brings deep semiconductor industry knowledge, finance expertise, and extensive public company board experience to Intel's ongoing transformation.

title: Intel Appoints Stacy Smith to Board of Directors
summary: Intel Corporation announced the appointment of Stacy Smith to its board of directors, effective immediately. Smith, who previously spent nearly 30 years at Intel in various leadership roles including CFO, will serve as an independent director and join the board’s Audit & Finance Committee. His extensive experience in the semiconductor industry and finance is expected to be a significant asset to Intel's ongoing transformation and IDM 2.0 strategy.

title: Intel’s chief people officer: Humans need to be at the center of AI
summary: Intel's chief people officer, Christy Pambianchi, emphasizes the importance of keeping humans at the center of artificial intelligence development and integration. The company, an early pioneer in microprocessors, is now deeply invested in AI through initiatives like its AI Inside program, proprietary iGPT, and a significant AI upskilling program. Pambianchi explains how Intel plans to responsibly incorporate AI into its workforce, encourage employee adoption, and prepare staff for future AI-driven roles.

title: Mobileye to close aftermarket unit, lay off 130
summary: Mobileye Global Inc. announced its decision to close its aftermarket solutions unit, leading to the layoff of 130 employees globally, including about 90 in Israel. This move comes as the demand for retrofitted advanced driver assistance technology has declined due to the increased integration of ADAS solutions in new vehicles. The company aims to reallocate resources to more advanced driver assist technologies and autonomous driving.

title: NVIDIA Launches Blackwell-Powered DGX SuperPOD for Generative AI Supercomputing at Trillion-Parameter Scale
summary: NVIDIA has announced its next-generation AI supercomputer, the NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD, powered by GB200 Grace Blackwell Superchips. This new system is designed for processing trillion-parameter models with constant uptime for generative AI training and inference, featuring liquid cooling, 11.5 exaflops of AI supercomputing, and up to 576 Blackwell GPUs. Additionally, NVIDIA unveiled the DGX B200 system, an air-cooled platform for AI model training, fine-tuning, and inference, providing 15x faster real-time inference for trillion-parameter models.

title: Gallium Nitride Market Size 2024 Industry Analysis, Key Players, Regional Demand, Revenue, Opportunity and Forecast 2032
summary: The global gallium nitride (GaN) market is projected to reach US$ 298 million by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 9.5% from 2021 to 2030, driven by its superior properties for electronic devices, energy efficiency, and applications in hybrid electric vehicles and 5G infrastructure. North America leads the market, while Asia Pacific, particularly China, shows substantial growth due to R&D and technological advancements. Key players include Cree Inc., Infineon Technologies AG, and GaN Systems Inc.

title: Governor Abbott Announces Appointments To Texas Semiconductor Innovation Consortium Executive Committee
summary: Governor Greg Abbott announced the inaugural members of the Texas Semiconductor Innovation Consortium Executive Committee (TSIC EC) at the University of Texas Dallas. The TSIC EC will guide the consortium's efforts and collaborate with industry experts to establish Texas as a national leader in advanced semiconductor research, design, and manufacturing. These appointments follow the Texas CHIPS Act, signed by Governor Abbott, which aims to solidify Texas's leading position in the semiconductor industry both nationally and globally.

title: Intel Wins Almost $20 Billion in US Awards for Chip Plants
summary: Intel is set to receive $8.5 billion in grants and up to $11 billion in loans from the US government to support its domestic semiconductor manufacturing expansion. This funding, totaling nearly $20 billion, is the largest award from a US program aimed at revitalizing the chip industry. The package will help finance over $100 billion in Intel's US investments, including large-scale facilities in Arizona and Ohio, and R&D projects in Oregon and New Mexico.

title: Intel awarded up to $19.5 billion in grant, loan for US chip production
summary: The Biden administration has awarded Intel Corp. a federal grant of up to $8.5 billion and a loan of up to $11 billion to expand semiconductor manufacturing in Arizona, New Mexico, Ohio, and Oregon. This is the largest award from the 2022 CHIPS and Science Act, aiming to reduce U.S. dependence on foreign chip suppliers. The funding is expected to stimulate over $100 billion in private investment from Intel and create 10,000 manufacturing jobs and 20,000 construction jobs.

title: Corning's latest innovation described as 'an essential consideration for increasing data usage'
summary: Corning has introduced Multifiber Pushlok Technology, a new "click and stick" fiber-dense connector, aiming to provide cost savings and help network operators meet sustainability goals by allowing more fiber deployment in compact spaces. This innovation is deemed "an essential consideration for increasing data usage," addressing challenges like cost constraints and labor shortages. Despite a 2% sales decrease in optical communications in 2023 due to carrier inventory drawdowns, Corning projects over $3 billion in sales growth medium-term and has also released a 5G RAN Compute Platform and the Everon 5G Enterprise Radio Access Network (ERAN) this year.

title: Intel announces $36B Hillsboro investment following federal funding commitments
summary: Intel announced a $36 billion investment in Hillsboro, Oregon, to modernize and expand its research and development hub, making it the largest chunk of the company's $100 billion planned spending across four states. This investment follows a commitment from the Biden Administration of $8.5 billion from the CHIPS and Science Act and up to $11 billion in federal loan guarantees, aimed at advancing future process nodes and creating thousands of jobs. Oregon's leaders expressed enthusiasm for the federal investment transforming into significant local spending, bolstering the state's semiconductor industry.

title: Inside Micron's most advanced memory fab - we visited the colossal control room and newest A3 fab in Taiwan
summary: This article details a visit to Micron's highly advanced DRAM manufacturing facilities in Taiwan, specifically highlighting the scale of operations at Fab 16, the cutting-edge A3 facility, and the Remote Operations Center. It touches upon Micron's sustainability efforts, diverse memory product portfolio, and the company's optimistic outlook amid the AI boom, contrasting it with previous industry downturns. The article also provides a profile of Dr. Donghui Lu, Head of Micron Taiwan, emphasizing his passion for PC DIY and background in the tech industry.

title: Swiss semiconductor company to establish U.S. headquarters in the Valley
summary: Swiss semiconductor company SEALSQ, based in Geneva, plans to open its U.S. headquarters with an assembly and testing center in the Phoenix metro area by 2025. The company chose Arizona over two other states, attracted by the state's growing semiconductor industry. This move follows recent major investments and developments in Arizona's semiconductor sector.

title: AMD’s Lisa Su to speak at Computex
summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su is scheduled to deliver the opening keynote speech at Computex Taipei on June 3, discussing the future of high-performance computing and AI. She will explore how AMD and its partners are advancing AI and HPC from data centers to end-user devices. The Computex event, themed "Connecting AI," will also feature other prominent tech leaders and is expected to include an AI cooperation announcement between Nvidia and Hon Hai.

title: The Top 3 ETFs to Profit From the AI Surge
summary: This article identifies three top AI ETFs—Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT), Invesco AI and Next Gen Software ETF (IGPT), and Global X Data Center REITs & Digital Infrastructure ETF (VPN)—that can help investors profit from the AI surge while minimizing risk. The author highlights the active management and strong holdings of CHAT, IGPT's exposure to undervalued AI stocks like Qualcomm and Intel, and VPN's strategic position to benefit from the increased demand for data centers due to AI. The article emphasizes minimizing risk by diversifying across several AI-exposed companies through ETFs.

title: Opinion: How Intel is Refining Its Approach to Responsible AI
summary: Intel Fellow and director of the Intelligent Systems Research Lab, Lama Nachman, discusses how Intel is refining its approach to Responsible AI (RAI). Started in 2017, Intel's RAI program has evolved to address new challenges posed by generative AI, focusing on rigorous governance, multidisciplinary research, and broad partnerships to ensure AI is developed and used safely, sustainably, and ethically, as well as being accessible to everyone.

title: U.S. Election Preview: Infrastructure Development is Something Both Sides Can Agree On
summary: The article discusses the bipartisan support for infrastructure development in the U.S., emphasizing that significant government funding and private investment are likely to continue regardless of the upcoming election outcomes. It highlights the resilience of the IIJA and CHIPS Act due to their bipartisan nature, while noting that the climate-focused IRA might face implementation slowdowns rather than full repeal. Overall, the piece suggests that clean energy transition and manufacturing reshoring will provide ongoing opportunities in U.S. infrastructure.

title: Chip maker SkyWater cancels plans for Indiana plant—at least for now
summary: SkyWater Technology has canceled its plans for a $1.8 billion semiconductor R&D and fabrication facility in West Lafayette, Indiana, which was expected to create 750 jobs. The decision comes as the project was dependent on funding from the CHIPS and Science Act, which SkyWater did not receive. Despite this, Purdue University states SkyWater remains a valued partner for research opportunities, and the Indiana Economic Development Corp. committed incentives, though none were claimed.

title: Intel and Altera Announce Edge and FPGA Offerings for AI at Embedded World
summary: Intel and Altera announced new edge-optimized processors, FPGAs, and market-ready solutions at Embedded World, extending powerful AI capabilities to edge computing markets like retail, industrial, and healthcare. These new offerings include Intel Core Ultra, Intel Core, and Intel Atom processors, along with Intel Arc GPUs and Agilex™ 5 FPGAs, bringing advanced AI acceleration, visual computing, and media processing to next-generation edge devices. Altera's FPGA AI Suite and broadly available Agilex 5 SoC FPGAs are designed to accelerate customer AI innovations with best-in-class performance per watt and developer usability.

title: TSMC will build one of world's most advanced chips plants in Phoenix
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) announced plans to build a third advanced semiconductor fabrication plant in Phoenix, Arizona, with production expected by the end of the decade. This expansion, supported by up to $6.6 billion from the CHIPS and Science Act, marks a total investment of $65 billion by TSMC in Arizona. The new fab will produce 2-nanometer chips, the most sophisticated semiconductors available, and is expected to create 6,000 tech jobs and 20,000 construction jobs.

title: TSMC's $6.6bn Arizona deal the latest in the CHIPS Act rollout
summary: The Biden administration has announced a preliminary agreement for up to $6.6 billion in grants and $5 billion in loans for Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) under the CHIPS program. This investment, combined with TSMC's planned expansion in Arizona, totals $65 billion and aims to restart the semiconductor industry in the U.S. The Commerce Department expects the U.S. to produce about 20% of the world’s most advanced chips by 2030, a goal supported by the CHIPS funding and noted for its potential political implications in swing states.

title: Intel challenges Nvidia with new Gaudi 3 AI chip as AMD expands processor lineup
summary: Intel has launched its new Gaudi 3 AI chip, promising significantly improved performance over its predecessor and competitive speeds against Nvidia's H100 and H200 chips for training and inference of large language models. Simultaneously, AMD expanded its AI processor lineup with new Versal AI Edge Series Gen 2 and Prime Series Gen 2 chips, targeting edge computing devices and leveraging customizable FPGA technology. Both companies are pushing boundaries in the AI hardware space, with Intel also focusing on an open platform for enterprise AI and AMD enhancing its offerings for connected devices.

title: Radcom extends collaboration with Rakuten Mobile
summary: Radcom has renewed its multi-year collaboration with Rakuten Mobile, focusing on advanced AI-powered analytics for network operations and automation monitoring. This expanded partnership will enhance the identification and prevention of network degradations to maintain service quality. Additionally, Rakuten Mobile announced it has surpassed 6.5 million mobile subscribers and plans to boost 5G coverage using its new 700 MHz spectrum.

title: 20 Chip Manufacturing Companies in India to Know
summary: This article highlights 20 key chip manufacturing companies in India, emphasizing the country's growing importance in the semiconductor industry across design, fabrication, and materials development. It details how the rise of AI has heightened the demand for advanced chipsets, positioning India as a crucial hub for global semiconductor firms. The listed companies include major players like NVIDIA, AMD, Samsung, and Intel, alongside specialized Indian firms contributing to various aspects of semiconductor technology.

title: Updates: Intel’s 10 Largest Construction Projects
summary: Intel is investing significantly in expanding its global manufacturing network to meet future semiconductor demand and build a resilient supply chain. This article highlights the company's 10 largest construction projects across Arizona, New Mexico, Ohio, Oregon, Ireland, Israel, Germany, Malaysia, and Poland, detailing their progress and future expectations. These projects involve massive scale construction and contribute to advanced chip manufacturing, R&D, and packaging technologies.

title: Samsung wins $6.4 billion CHIPS grant for Texas project. Is Micron next?
summary: Samsung Electronics has secured a $6.4 billion federal CHIPS Act grant to expand its chip manufacturing and research facilities in Texas, committing nearly $45 billion in investment. This agreement makes Samsung the sixth company to receive CHIPS funding, with attention now turning to Micron Technology and Texas Instruments, who are the last major applicants yet to finalize deals. Micron is planning a massive $100 billion chip plant complex in upstate New York and is a key candidate for the remaining CHIPS Act funds, particularly for advanced memory chip production.

title: AMD and Nvidia unveil new processors for AI PCs
summary: AMD and Nvidia have both launched new processors designed for AI PCs, aiming to capitalize on the growing market for computers capable of running AI workloads on-device. AMD introduced its Ryzen PRO 8040 and 8000 Series processors, emphasizing their AI-enabled features for business users, while Nvidia unveiled new RTX A400 and A1000 GPUs with enhanced Tensor and RT Cores for accelerating AI and graphics tasks. This move by both companies highlights the intensifying competition in the AI PC sector, which analysts predict will drive new growth in the overall PC market.

title: Intel Builds World’s Largest Neuromorphic System to Enable More Sustainable AI
summary: Intel has developed Hala Point, the world's largest neuromorphic system, featuring 1.15 billion neurons and 1,152 Loihi 2 processors to address the efficiency and sustainability challenges of AI. Deployed at Sandia National Laboratories, this system aims to advance brain-inspired AI research and future real-time continuous learning for various applications. It achieves state-of-the-art computational efficiencies, rivaling and exceeding GPU and CPU architectures, and promises significant energy savings for future AI deployments.

title: Onshoring Semiconductor Production: National Security Versus Economic Efficiency
summary: The article explores the trade-off between national security and economic efficiency in the U.S. effort to onshore semiconductor production, driven by concerns over reliance on Taiwan. It details the CHIPS and Science Act's goals and potential drawbacks, such as increased costs and reduced innovation. Alternative strategies like "friendshoring" and strengthening Taiwan's defense are also discussed.

title: US to Provide $6.1 Billion for Micron Technology Chip Plants
summary: The Biden administration will provide $6.1 billion in government support for Micron Technology to produce advanced memory computer chips in New York and Idaho. This funding, from the 2022 CHIPS and Science Act, aims to revive the manufacturing sector, create 50,000 jobs, and bolster domestic semiconductor production. Micron plans to invest a total of $100 billion in upstate New York over two decades and $15 billion in Boise, Idaho.

title: Micron set to get $6.1 bln in chip grants from US
summary: Micron Technology is expected to receive $6.1 billion in grants from the U.S. Commerce Department to support its domestic chip factory projects in New York and Idaho. This funding, part of the CHIPS & Science law, aims to boost U.S. chip production and reduce reliance on foreign manufacturing. The award follows similar grants to Intel, TSMC, and Samsung, reinforcing the U.S. strategy to revitalize its semiconductor industry.

title: Huge CHIPS Grants Awarded to TSMC, Samsung, and Micron
summary: The U.S. government has announced significant CHIPS Act grants to Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC), Samsung, and Micron, following an earlier grant to Intel. These grants, totaling billions of dollars, are intended to support the expansion and construction of semiconductor manufacturing facilities within the U.S. The Commerce Department plans to disburse all $39 billion in manufacturing incentives under the CHIPS and Science Act by the end of the year.

title: Semiconductor Market size is set to grow by USD 156.39 billion from 2023-2027, Analog Devices Inc., Broadcom Inc. and Infineon Technologies AG, and more to emerge as Some of the Key Vendors, Technavio
summary: The global semiconductor market is projected to grow by USD 156.39 billion from 2023 to 2027, with a CAGR of 5.08%, driven by increasing technology integration, renewable energy, and IoT. Key vendors such as Analog Devices Inc., Broadcom Inc., and Infineon Technologies AG are capitalizing on this growth through strategic alliances and partnerships. APAC is expected to hold the largest market share, experiencing significant incremental growth during the forecast period.

title: ASML has started shipping second high-NA EUV litho machine
summary: ASML has begun shipping its second commercial high numerical aperture extreme ultraviolet lithography scanner to an unnamed customer, following the first unit sent to Intel. The identity of the second recipient is unknown, with possibilities including Samsung, TSMC, or another machine for Intel, given the high cost and strategic importance of this technology for advanced chip manufacturing. TSMC notably continues to favor existing 0.33 NA EUV machines for cost-effectiveness.

title: Express To Close Over 100 Stores as Mall Titans Simon, Brookfield Seek To Buy Its Assets
summary: Mall retailer Express plans to close over 100 stores and has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection. A consortium including landlords Simon Property Group and Brookfield Properties, along with brand manager WHP Global, intends to buy a substantial majority of Express's assets to keep the retailer afloat and avoid vacancies in their malls. The move reflects broader challenges in the retail sector and a strategy by mall owners to acquire stakes in troubled tenants.

title: Advanced Packaging Market Aims for Remarkable Growth, Targeting USD 79.9 Billion by 2033 at 8.4% CAGR
summary: The advanced packaging market is projected to grow from USD 35.7 billion in 2023 to USD 79.9 billion by 2033, achieving an 8.4% CAGR. This growth is driven by the increasing demand for miniaturization of devices and advanced packaging solutions for integrated circuits in various sectors, especially consumer electronics and automotive. The Asia Pacific region is anticipated to dominate the market due to its prominent semiconductor manufacturing industry.

title: Intel Tumbles Most in Almost Four Years After Tepid Forecast
summary: Intel Corp. experienced its largest stock drop in three months following a weak forecast for the current quarter, indicating ongoing struggles in its semiconductor turnaround efforts. The company projected second-quarter sales of approximately $13 billion and profit of 10 cents per share, both falling short of analyst estimates. This performance suggests a slower-than-expected return to the top tier of the chip industry for the biggest PC processor maker.

title: Intel’s bad year worsens, with analyst decrying company as ‘profoundly broken’
summary: Intel's stock experienced another sharp decline following its latest earnings report, with shares down about 9% in Friday's midday trading. This downturn stems from the company missing its revenue and margin outlooks for the current quarter, marking the second significant post-earnings selloff in as many quarters. An unnamed analyst has described the company as "profoundly broken" as its difficult year continues.

title: Arqit announces collaboration to deliver out-of-the-box post-quantum cryptography solutions
summary: Arqit Quantum Inc. and Intel have partnered to create the "World’s First Quantum-Safe, 1.89 Tb IPsec products." This collaboration combines Intel's Xeon Scalable processors with Arqit's Symmetric Key Agreement Platform (SKA-Platform™) to deliver high-performance IPsec encryption solutions that are quantum-safe. The initiative addresses modern cyber threats by providing off-the-shelf products that harden networks against quantum attacks while adhering to core standards.

title: Disclosure and Divestment: A look at U of O protesters’ demands
summary: This article details the demands of U of O protesters, organized by INSAF uOttawa and the Palestinian Student Association, regarding the university's investments and academic relationships. Key demands include disclosure and divestment from companies involved in the Israeli occupation, adoption of an anti-Palestinian racism definition, and breaking ties with academic Zionist institutions. The article lists specific companies targeted for divestment and highlights the university's initial response to academic boycotts.

title: AI In Chip Design Market Size, Share | CAGR of 31.4%
summary: The global AI in chip design market is projected to reach USD 27.6 billion by 2033, growing from USD 1.8 billion in 2023 at a CAGR of 31.4%. This growth is driven by the increasing integration of AI in consumer electronics and the expansion of AI applications in sectors like healthcare, despite challenges such as high development costs and technological complexity. North America currently dominates the market due to its robust technological infrastructure and significant investments in R&D.

title: Who’s making chips for AI? Chinese manufacturers lag behind US tech giants
summary: Chinese researchers are reportedly 5-10 years behind their US counterparts in AI chip manufacturing due to US export restrictions. The rapid advancements in generative AI have intensified a global race for computer chips, highlighting China's struggle to keep pace with key technologies under these limitations.

title: Alchip says revenue to grow in 2026
summary: AI chip designer Alchip Technologies Ltd expects significant revenue growth in 2026, driven by a new major AI chip customer, despite a stock price drop and increasing competition. The company emphasizes its strong partnerships with cloud service providers and its unique capabilities in leading-edge process technologies (5nm, 4nm, 3nm) for ASIC design. Alchip also reported a 104% increase in net profit for the last quarter and is prioritizing advanced packaging capacity from TSMC to meet AI chip production demands.

title: China’s emerging markets ties are huge opportunity for B2B payments space: Ebanx
summary: Ebanx, a Brazilian fintech company, sees significant growth opportunities in the B2B payments sector, particularly due to increasing business ties between China and other emerging markets. The company notes a rising demand for digitized payment solutions, paralleling the digital revolution previously seen in the consumer market. Ebanx is leveraging its presence in 29 countries to improve cross-border B2B digital payment infrastructure, aiming to streamline transactions and expand its merchant base.

title: CEO Letter: 2023-24 Intel CSR Report
summary: Pat Gelsinger, Intel's CEO, details the company's 2023-24 Corporate Responsibility Report, highlighting its RISE strategy focused on creating a more responsible, inclusive, and sustainable world. The report emphasizes advancements in sustainable manufacturing, efforts to secure resilient supply chains by increasing semiconductor production in the U.S. and Europe, and the development of open, ethical ecosystems in technology, including responsible AI. Intel aims to leverage its innovations and expertise to address global challenges and drive human progress.

title: The rise of TSMC: How one chipmaker started making everyone's chips
summary: This article traces the journey of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) from its founding in 1987 by Morris Chang to becoming the world's leading chip manufacturer, producing 90% of cutting-edge logic chips. It highlights key milestones including Chang's early career at Texas Instruments, TSMC's unique business model focusing solely on manufacturing, its crucial partnership with Apple, and recent expansions with significant investments in the U.S. and Japan.

title: ST rolls a new FD-SOI microcontroller with phase-change memory (PCM) for automotive applications
summary: STMicroelectronics has announced a new generation of automotive microcontrollers featuring embedded phase-change memory (ePCM), fabricated by Samsung using a jointly developed 18nm FD-SOI process. These microcontrollers target the automotive industry with its stringent temperature requirements, addressing a historical challenge for PCM technology. ST plans to sample these devices to automotive customers in the second half of 2024, with production slated for the latter half of 2025.

title: Robert Dennard, Inventor of Computer DRAM at IBM, Dies at 91
summary: Robert Dennard, the IBM engineer credited with inventing dynamic random access memory (DRAM) in the late 1960s, has passed away at the age of 91. His groundbreaking invention was crucial in the development of personal computers, mobile phones, tablets, and video game players. Dennard's death was announced on April 23, according to a notice in the Journal News.

title: Should You Buy Nvidia Stock Before the AI Chipmaker Reports Earnings on May 22?
summary: Nvidia's stock has seen a significant surge due to demand for its GPUs driven by generative AI. With first-quarter earnings approaching on May 22nd, analysts have high expectations for the chipmaker. Investors are advised to be cautious due to the stock's current pricey valuation, despite its strong market position and growth potential in the AI economy.

title: Nvidia's next-generation GPU revealed: integrating eight HBM 4, TSMC N3 process
summary: Nvidia's upcoming R100 AI chip is expected to enter mass production in Q4 2025, utilizing TSMC's N3 process and integrating eight HBM4s. The company is also focusing on improving energy consumption alongside AI computing power in its next-generation chips. This aggressive roadmap reflects Nvidia's strategy to accelerate product release cycles to maintain market leadership against competitors like AMD.

title: Kevin O’Buckley to Lead Foundry Services at Intel
summary: Intel Corporation announced the appointment of Kevin O’Buckley as senior vice president and general manager of Foundry Services, effective immediately. He will lead Intel’s systems foundry growth strategy for the AI era and reports directly to CEO Pat Gelsinger. O’Buckley succeeds Stuart Pann, who will retire after 35 years of service.

title: Intel In Advanced Talks With Apollo For $11B Ireland Plant Partnership: Report
summary: Intel is reportedly in advanced talks with Apollo Global Management for a transaction where Apollo would provide over $11 billion to fund a chip manufacturing plant in Ireland. This deal would support Intel's expansion plans, which include building or enhancing facilities in multiple locations to meet increasing chip demand and bolster its contract chip-making business. Facing high costs for these new projects and a slowdown in its core business, Intel is seeking partners for financing, similar to a previous $30 billion deal with Brookfield Asset Management.

title: Embrace the AI era with NetApp new AFF A-Series | NetApp Blog
summary: NetApp has introduced new AFF A-Series storage systems (A70, A90, A1K) designed to handle demanding workloads like AI and GenAI while accelerating existing mission-critical applications. These all-flash systems offer significant performance increases, improved efficiency with features like Intel QAT for data reduction, and enhanced security with multilayered protection against ransomware. They are built to provide a unified, intelligent, and secure data foundation for enterprise AI and are available for purchase or as a service via NetApp Keystone.

title: NetApp refreshes storage hardware, software for AI era
summary: NetApp has introduced new hardware and software solutions aimed at the AI and cybersecurity markets, including refreshed high-end all-flash FAS (AFF) A-Series arrays, updated ONTAP software, and StorageGRID hardware. The new A-Series models (A70, A90, A1K) offer up to double the performance of previous generations, achieving up to 40 million IOPS and 1 TBps throughput, powered by Sapphire Rapids generation controller processors. Additionally, NetApp has enhanced its AIPod offerings, including a new "GenAI-in-a-box" system with Lenovo and Nvidia L40S GPUs for fine-tuning and inference.

title: Creator Of The Bitcoin ASIC Says Next Wave Of Mining Efficiency Is Coming
summary: Nangeng Zhang, CEO of Canaan Inc., discusses the state of the Bitcoin mining industry, focusing on the historical impact of ASICs, the future of chip efficiency amidst the challenges of Moore's Law, and the growing importance of environmental sustainability. He also highlights emerging opportunities in the Middle East and the convergence of Bitcoin mining with artificial intelligence. Zhang believes significant efficiency gains are still possible for ASICs in the next 3-5 years, emphasizing heat monetization and strategic energy development.

title: NetApp Unveils Unified Data Storage Built for the AI Era
summary: NetApp has announced new leading AFF A-Series systems designed for unified data storage in the AI era, capable of handling demanding IT workloads including GenAI and VMware. The company is expanding its portfolio with enhanced capabilities and new features like StorageGRID models and a cyber vault reference architecture to help organizations efficiently manage data and drive innovation. These advancements aim to provide powerful, intelligent, and secure enterprise data infrastructure for modern workloads.

title: ASML’s new machines are too expensive: TSMC
summary: TSMC Senior Vice President Kevin Zhang stated that ASML's new high-NA extreme ultraviolet (EUV) chip machines are too expensive, despite their advanced capabilities. While Intel has already ordered these machines, TSMC indicated their A16 node technology, due in late 2026, would not require them, continuing to rely on existing EUV equipment. Zhang emphasized that integrating the new technology would depend on economic sense and technical balancing, highlighting the collective industry challenge of rising manufacturing costs.

title: HPE supercomputer push continues with Aurora
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has launched Aurora, its second exascale supercomputer, which is the "largest AI-capable system in the world." Built in collaboration with Intel for the United States Department of Energy’s Argonne National Laboratory, Aurora will support research in areas like neuroscience, particle physics, and pharmacology. This launch reinforces HPE's strategic pivot towards integrating AI with high-performance computing (HPC), following similar projects like Isambard-AI and Helios.

title: How NetApp Unified Data Storage is Powering the AI Era
summary: NetApp has introduced a new generation of unified data storage systems, including the AFF A-Series, designed to accelerate AI innovation and manage demanding AI workloads. These solutions integrate with major public cloud providers and incorporate advanced features like ransomware detection and hybrid cloud capabilities. Companies like T-Systems and Intel are leveraging NetApp's infrastructure to power their critical AI initiatives.

title: Liqid composability tech aims to turn Dell servers into GPU powerhouses
summary: Liqid's composability technology aims to transform Dell servers into powerful AI model training systems by enabling a single Dell R760 server to access up to 30 NVIDIA L40S GPUs. This UltraStack configuration, developed with Dell and NVIDIA, promises significant reductions in capital and operational expenses while boosting AI performance, efficiency, and sustainability. Liqid's SmartStack technology also allows efficient allocation of GPU resources to multiple clients or modular server nodes, supporting various Dell PowerEdge systems.

title: HP Unlocks New Era of AI Experiences Powering Work and Creation
summary: HP has unveiled new AI PCs, the HP OmniBook X AI PC and HP EliteBook Ultra AI PC, designed with ARM architecture and Snapdragon X Elite processors to power AI experiences locally. These devices aim to enhance productivity and creativity with features like Copilot+ PC and improved collaboration tools. HP highlights their sleek design, long battery life, sustainability, and robust security for both consumer and commercial users.

title: Intel Powers China’s Intelligent Cockpit Market
summary: Intel is collaborating with Neusoft and ThunderSoft to advance intelligent cockpit solutions for next-generation vehicles in the Chinese market. These partnerships leverage Intel's AI-enhanced software-defined vehicle (SDV) system-on-chip (SoC) to deliver personalized, intuitive driving experiences with advanced AI capabilities. This initiative aims to equip Chinese automakers with cutting-edge technology to compete globally.

title: Opinion: Intel and AMD take a backseat as Qualcomm plays starring role in Microsoft’s AI PCs
summary: Microsoft unveiled a new line of AI-enabled PCs, branded as Copilot + PCs, which are powered by Qualcomm's Snapdragon processors instead of Intel's. This move positions Qualcomm as a key player in Microsoft's AI PC strategy, potentially shifting market dynamics for traditional chip partners Intel and AMD. The article highlights Qualcomm's significant headway in the AI PC arena.

title: Cynthia Bowman Departs Bank Of America, Named Apple Inc’s New Head Of Diversity
summary: Cynthia Bowman, previously from Bank of America, has been appointed as Apple Inc.'s new head of diversity, becoming the fourth individual to hold this position. She will report to Apple’s chief people officer and support the company's diversity initiatives. Bowman's appointment comes as Apple continues its efforts to increase the representation of diverse groups within its workforce.

title: China's largest chipmaker SMIC is now the No. 3 foundry in the world, Counterpoint says
summary: China's largest chipmaker, SMIC, has become the world's third-largest foundry by revenue in the first quarter of 2024, according to Counterpoint Research. SMIC secured a 6% market share, surpassing GlobalFoundries and United Microelectronics Corporation, and is now behind only TSMC and Samsung Foundry. This rise is attributed to recovering demand in China for various chip applications, with SMIC reporting increased revenue and expecting further growth in the second quarter.

title: With the rise of Ethernet, Nvidia Infiniband is being encroached upon
summary: Nvidia's InfiniBand sales are being encroached upon by the growing demand for Ethernet in data centers, particularly from hyperscale enterprises and cloud builders. While InfiniBand still accounts for a large portion of Nvidia's network sales, the company is actively pushing its Spectrum X Ethernet products, expecting them to become a multi-billion dollar product line. Despite this internal cannibalization, Nvidia anticipates continued strong growth, with CEO Jensen Huang emphasizing the company's commitment to both InfiniBand and Ethernet as computing structures, alongside NVLink.

title: HP CEO: AI PCs To Make Up A Tenth Of Shipments In Fiscal Year’s Second Half
summary: HP CEO Enrique Lores predicts that AI PCs will constitute 10% of the company's client computer shipments in the latter half of its fiscal year, driven initially by consumer sales. He anticipates a significant increase in AI PC demand over the next few years, forecasting that AI PCs will represent 40-50% of total PC shipments within three years. Despite a decline in printing revenue, HP's Personal Systems business saw growth in commercial PC sales.

title: Rising commercial PC sales help HP beat earnings and revenue targets
summary: HP Inc. surpassed its earnings and revenue targets, driven by a recovery in the global PC market and increasing commercial PC sales. The company's stock rose in after-hours trading as it looks to benefit from the new generation of AI-enabled PCs. HP anticipates AI PCs will constitute 40-50% of total PC shipments within three years, aligning with analyst forecasts for significant growth in the coming years.

title: Marvell Expands Connectivity Portfolio With New PCIe Retimer Product Line to Scale Compute Fabrics of Accelerated Infrastructure
summary: Marvell Technology Inc. has launched its new Alaska P PCIe retimer product line, designed to scale data center compute fabrics in accelerated servers, CXL systems, and disaggregated infrastructure. These 8- and 16-lane PCIe Gen 6 retimers, built on Marvell's 5nm PAM4 technology, are crucial for connecting AI accelerators, GPUs, and CPUs as AI applications demand significantly higher bandwidth and faster data rates, necessitating reliable communication over greater distances. The new product line extends Marvell's PAM4 leadership beyond Ethernet and InfiniBand, addressing the growing market for PCIe and CXL connectivity within server systems with industry-leading low power consumption.

title: Local programs create training pipeline for Intel plant's jobs
summary: The Dayton Daily News highlighted training programs at the University of Cincinnati and other Ohio universities designed to prepare students and workers for the 3,000 new jobs at Intel Corp.'s microchip fabrication plant. UC's College of Engineering and Applied Science has organized the Ohio Southwest Alliance on Semiconductors and Integrated Scalable Manufacturing, offering microcredentials and hands-on experience in superconductor design and manufacturing, supported by a $1 million federal grant. Intel is collaborating with universities to build a skilled workforce for its new plants outside Columbus, emphasizing the need for regional high-tech talent.

title: EU approves Italian aid for $5.4 bln STMicro chip plant, more to come
summary: The European Commission has approved Italian state aid of approximately 2 billion euros for STMicroelectronics to build a 5 billion euro ($5.4 billion) microchip plant in Catania, Sicily. This move is part of Europe's strategy to reduce its reliance on Asian chip imports and boost domestic semiconductor production, with more investments expected under the EU Chips Act. The new plant will produce energy-efficient silicon carbide chips primarily for the electric vehicle market, creating 3,000 jobs.

title: VMware IPO to Raise $741M, Intel Invests
summary: Virtualization software specialist VMware, Inc. expects to raise approximately $741 million in its upcoming Initial Public Offering. Intel (INTC) plans to invest $218 million in VMware, securing a seat on the company's board of directors. VMware, owned by EMC Corp., will list its Class A common stock on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol VMW, highlighting the growing importance of virtualization in data center management.

title: Computer Industry Joins NVIDIA to Build AI Factories and Data Centers for the Next Industrial Revolution
summary: NVIDIA, in collaboration with top computer manufacturers, unveiled a new array of NVIDIA Blackwell architecture-powered systems, featuring Grace CPUs and NVIDIA networking, designed for building AI factories and data centers. These systems aim to drive the next wave of generative AI breakthroughs, with offerings ranging from single to multi-GPUs and various cooling technologies. Key industry players like ASRock Rack, ASUS, GIGABYTE, and Supermicro are among those delivering these advanced AI systems.

title: STMicroelectronics receives $2.2B to build the world’s first silicon carbide factory
summary: STMicroelectronics has secured a €2 billion ($2.2 billion) grant from the Italian government to establish a €5 billion ($5.4 billion) chip production facility in Sicily. This factory is slated to become the world's first fully integrated silicon carbide (SiC) semiconductor factory, designed to produce microchips primarily for electric vehicles. The investment is part of the EU's broader strategy to bolster domestic chip manufacturing and reduce reliance on single suppliers, with STMicroelectronics expecting the facility to begin production by 2026 and reach full capacity with 15,000 SiC wafers per week in seven years.

title: AMD announces new AI chips amid intensifying competition with Nvidia, Intel
summary: AMD announced new AI chips at the Computex tech conference in Taipei, emphasizing AI as its top priority and a transformative force in the computing market. This move intensifies its competition with rivals Nvidia and Intel, both of whom are also rapidly introducing new AI-focused processors. AMD plans to release new AI chip technology annually, outlining a roadmap for Instinct accelerators and EPYC server processors through 2026.

title: Intel Accelerates AI Everywhere at Computex 2024; Redefines Compute Power, Performance and Affordability with new Xeon 6, Gaudi Accelerators and Lunar Lake Architecture to Grow AI PC Leadership
summary: At Computex 2024, Intel launched its new Xeon 6 processors with Efficient-cores, announced pricing for Gaudi 2 and Gaudi 3 AI accelerators, and unveiled details for the Lunar Lake client processor architecture. These innovations aim to redefine compute power and performance across data centers and AI PCs, offering improved efficiency and lower total cost of ownership. The company is collaborating with numerous global system providers to bring these technologies to market, emphasizing open standards and a robust ecosystem to accelerate AI adoption.

title: Intel gets $11B from Apollo for stake in its Fab 34 chipmaking plant in Ireland
summary: Intel has sold a 49% stake in its new Fab 34 chip manufacturing facility in Ireland to Apollo Global Management Inc. for $11 billion. This deal aims to fund the ongoing development of the site and other global chip factories, as Intel aggressively expands its production capacity to compete with TSMC. The chipmaker will maintain a 51% stake and full operational control of Fab 34, which is crucial for manufacturing its most advanced process nodes and supporting its Intel Foundry business.

title: Intel and Apollo Agree to Joint Venture Related to Intel’s Fab 34 in Ireland
summary: Intel and Apollo have announced a definitive agreement for an $11 billion investment by Apollo-managed funds to acquire a 49% equity interest in a joint venture related to Intel’s Fab 34 in Leixlip, Ireland. This transaction, Intel's second Semiconductor Co-Investment Program (SCIP) arrangement, aims to provide financial flexibility and redeploy capital while Intel retains 51% controlling interest and operational control of the facility. The joint venture will manufacture wafers for Intel and its Foundry customers, enhancing Intel's strategy to build a resilient semiconductor supply chain.

title: Arm aims to win 50% of PC market in five years: Haas
summary: Arm Holdings PLC CEO Rene Haas announced the company's goal to capture over 50% of the Windows PC market within five years. This ambition is fueled by Microsoft's commitment to use Arm-based chips for its new line of AI-powered PCs and the development of robust software tools for Arm-based systems, aiming to challenge Intel's long-standing dominance in the PC industry. Apple's success with its Arm-based "M Series" processors in terms of battery life and performance is cited as a precedent.

title: 5 things to know about Intel’s new Ohio plants and workforce training efforts
summary: Intel is building two massive semiconductor fabrication plants in Licking County, Ohio, expected to create 3,000 jobs. The project involves a $50 million statewide training program and could eventually see Intel invest up to $100 billion for eight plants. Intel is also set to receive substantial federal funding and tax credits for its projects across several states, including Ohio.

title: VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) 2024 Holdings and Performance Recap
summary: The VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) experienced strong performance in Q1 2024, with a 28.9% increase, largely driven by NVIDIA Corp. and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. Intel Corporation and STMicroelectronics were the main detractors during this period. The report provides a detailed overview of SMH's top holdings, their contributions to the ETF's performance, and key financial highlights for Q1 2024.

title: VIDEO: See what happens inside Intel Corp. factories in Oregon
summary: This article features a video showcasing the operations inside Intel Corp.'s D1D and D1X Assembly and Test Technology Development factories in Hillsboro, Oregon. Intel is expanding its manufacturing capabilities with new plants in Ohio and expansion projects in other states. The company is also investing in statewide training efforts in Ohio to prepare a workforce for its new semiconductor plants there.

title: Coherent Corp. Gets a New CEO
summary: Coherent Corp. announced James R. Anderson as its new CEO, replacing Vincent D. Mattera, Jr. Anderson's appointment, following his successful tenure at Lattice Semiconductor, caused Coherent's stock to jump 23% while Lattice's fell 15.5%. The move signals Coherent's focus on AI, datacom, and industrial markets, building on Mattera's legacy of significant growth and acquisitions.

title: Nand Flash Market size is set to grow by USD 18.8 billion from 2024-2028, Rising demand for nand flash in automotive industry boost the market, Technavio
summary: The global NAND flash market is projected to grow by USD 18.8 billion from 2024-2028, driven by rising demand in the automotive industry and the proliferation of cloud computing services. Technavio reports a CAGR of 5.02% during this period, despite challenges like demand-supply fluctuations. Key players include Samsung, Micron, and Kioxia, with APAC expected to contribute 60% of the market growth.

title: Chipmaker Intel to halt $25-billion Israel plant, news website says
summary: Intel is reportedly halting plans for a $25-billion chip manufacturing plant in Israel, according to Israeli financial news website Calcalist. While Intel did not directly confirm or deny the report, they stated that managing large-scale projects often involves adapting to changing timelines based on business conditions and market dynamics. Intel emphasized its continued commitment to Israel as a key global manufacturing and R&D site.

title: Intel suspends planned expansion of its Israeli semiconductor manufacturing plant
summary: Intel has reportedly suspended the planned $25 billion expansion of its semiconductor manufacturing plant in Kiryat Gat, Israel, with suppliers receiving contract cancellation notices. The company had previously secured a $3.2 billion grant from the Israeli government for this expansion. While Intel stated its commitment to Israel as a manufacturing and R&D site, it cited business conditions and market dynamics as factors influencing timelines for large-scale projects.

title: 3 Stocks to Buy in the Wake of Nvidia’s 10-for-1 Split
summary: Following Nvidia's 10-for-1 stock split, this article highlights three companies poised for significant growth in the booming AI market: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Marvell Technology (MRVL), and Arista Networks (ANET). TSM is a key chip producer for major AI players, offering an attractive P/E ratio and dividend yield. Marvell Technology is expected to see strong revenue growth from custom AI chips, while Arista Networks' networking solutions are crucial for AI infrastructure, with its AI solutions business rapidly growing.

title: Nvidia surpasses Microsoft to become the world’s most valuable company
summary: Nvidia Corp. has surpassed Microsoft Corp. to become the world's most valuable publicly traded company, reaching a market capitalization of $3.34 trillion. This milestone follows a surge in Nvidia's stock, driven by its dominance in the AI chip market, particularly with its GPUs essential for generative AI development. The company's exceptional growth contrasts with Microsoft and Apple, which have also benefited from AI but not to the same extent, solidifying Nvidia's unique position in the tech landscape.

title: To address talent shortage, chipmakers skilling engineering students
summary: Semiconductor companies in India, including Intel India, AMD, Applied Materials, and Tessolve, are actively addressing the industry's talent shortage by partnering with engineering students and academic institutions. They are establishing labs, driving industry-academia collaborations for research in core design and AI training, and sponsoring student research. These initiatives aim to develop fresh talent, build a skilled workforce, and improve diversity within the rapidly growing Indian semiconductor sector, which is projected to more than double by 2026.

title: Former TSMC and UMC leadership unite to chart future semiconductor ecosystem
summary: The Semi Impact Forum 2024 in London brought together leaders from Taiwan's semiconductor industry, including former TSMC R&D Director Konrad Young and former UMC CEO Shih-Wei Sun, with UK investors and policymakers. The event focused on collaboration opportunities and insights into Taiwan's semiconductor ecosystem, emphasizing the importance of international partnerships, early-stage technology investment, and the future role of education and AI in the industry. Both sides discussed their respective strengths, with Taiwan excelling in manufacturing and the UK in innovation, aiming for mutual growth.

title: Semiconductor cycle: Going up or down?
summary: The article explores the current state of the semiconductor industry, noting that while the AI frenzy has propelled companies like Nvidia to new heights, the recovery is uneven across the value chain, particularly for Southeast Asian firms focused on lower-value segments. It details the challenges and strategies of Singapore-listed companies AEM Holdings, UMS Holdings, Frencken Group, and Grand Venture Technology as they navigate the cyclical downturn and aim to capitalize on the AI boom and regional shifts in the supply chain. Frencken Group is highlighted as a top pick for recovery due to its diversified business and strong customer relationships despite geopolitical headwinds.
