title: Not all semiconductor ETFs are the same. What investors need to know before betting on Nvidia and other chip stocks.
summary: This article discusses the nuances of semiconductor ETFs, highlighting that not all are structured the same. It aims to inform investors about different weighting schemes that could impact returns, especially in the context of Nvidia's strong performance. The piece emphasizes the importance of understanding these differences before investing in chip stocks through ETFs.

title: Why Lam Research funds startups to disrupt the semiconductor industry | Audrey Charles interview
summary: Lam Research recently hosted its Lam Capital Venture Competition, highlighting its efforts to fund semiconductor startups. Audrey Charles, Senior Vice President of Corporate Strategy at Lam Research and head of Lam Capital, discussed the competition, the impact of the CHIPS and Science Act, and the increasing role of AI in driving innovation within the semiconductor industry. The event saw Crystal Sonic win a $250,000 prize, reflecting Lam Capital's early-stage investments typically ranging from $1 million to $10 million.

title: Intel Demonstrates First Fully Integrated Optical I/O Chiplet
summary: Intel Corporation's Integrated Photonics Solutions (IPS) Group has demonstrated the industry's first fully integrated bidirectional optical compute interconnect (OCI) chiplet, co-packaged with an Intel CPU and running live data. This OCI chiplet is designed to enhance AI infrastructure for data centers and high-performance computing by providing higher bandwidth, lower power consumption, and longer reach than traditional electrical or pluggable optical solutions. This breakthrough is expected to revolutionize high-speed data processing for AI, enabling greater scalability and more efficient resource utilization.

title: Biden awards up to $75 million in CHIPS Act grant to Entegris
summary: The Biden administration has awarded up to $75 million in CHIPS Act funding to Entegris to support the development of a new manufacturing facility in Colorado Springs, Colorado. This grant is part of the U.S. effort to boost domestic semiconductor production and bolster suppliers. The facility will produce essential components for chip manufacturing, including liquid filter membranes and specialized containers, primarily for major chipmakers.

title: Rags to Riches: 3 Hydrogen Stocks That Could Make Early Investors Rich
summary: The article highlights three hydrogen stocks—Bloom Energy (BE), Plug Power (PLUG), and the Global X Hydrogen ETF (HYDR)—that could offer substantial returns to early investors. It suggests that potential changes to the restrictive 45V tax credits by the Biden administration could significantly boost the hydrogen sector. The author recommends these investments as opportunities to capitalize on the growing focus on clean energy and net-zero emission goals.

title: Entegris to get $75M in CHIPS funding for Colorado plant
summary: Entegris will receive $75 million in CHIPS and Science Act funding to support the construction of its $600 million semiconductor materials manufacturing center in Colorado Springs, set to open in early 2025. The facility is expected to create nearly 600 direct manufacturing jobs and will focus on producing specialized containers for semiconductor wafers, known as front opening unified pods, which are currently only produced abroad. Entegris aims to recruit 50% of its workforce from veterans and military families, partnering with local educational institutions and military bases.

title: Better Chip Stock: Arm Holdings vs. Intel
summary: This article compares Arm Holdings and Intel, two major chipmakers. Arm, dominating mobile CPU design, has seen significant stock growth due to its flexible approach and expansion into cloud and auto markets, despite a high valuation. Intel, a major PC and server CPU producer, has struggled with manufacturing delays and competition, leading to a stock decline, though analysts expect some recovery. The author concludes that Arm is the better buy due to its faster growth, wider market moat, and higher-margin business.

title: Applied Materials reveals chip wiring innovations for energy-efficient computing
summary: Applied Materials has introduced new chip wiring innovations designed to enhance energy efficiency in computing, particularly for 2-nanometer node manufacturing and beyond. These advancements utilize new materials like Ruthenium Cobalt liners and enhanced low-k dielectrics, which reduce electrical resistance by up to 25% and capacitance by up to 3%, ultimately supporting the development of trillion-transistor chips for AI and graphics processing. The company's solutions aim to improve performance-per-watt, strengthen chips for 3D stacking, and address challenges in scaling Moore's Law, with adoption already seen by leading logic and memory chipmakers including Samsung and TSMC.

title: Helen of Troy Plunges After It Misses Q1 Estimates and Cuts Outlook
summary: Helen of Troy (HELE) shares plunged over 30% after missing fiscal Q1 estimates for profit and sales and cutting its full-year outlook. The consumer products firm cited slumping demand for beauty and wellness products, lower replenishment orders, and technical issues at its Tennessee distribution facility as reasons for the poor performance. CEO Noel Geoffroy noted that challenges intensified late in the quarter and are continuing to evolve.

title: Resonac Announces New US-JOINT Consortium; Ten Partners to Collaborate on Next-Generation Semiconductor Packaging in Silicon Valley
summary: Resonac has launched US-JOINT, a new consortium of ten American and Japanese companies, to advance semiconductor back-end process R&D in Silicon Valley. The partners, including KLA and ULVAC, will collaborate at a new R&D center in Union City, California, aiming to accelerate the development of advanced packaging technologies for applications like AI and autonomous driving. This initiative will bring packaging R&D closer to major semiconductor device makers, fostering faster evaluation and development.

title: Where does eBay do most of its AI development? You might be surprised
summary: EBay primarily conducts its AI development and training in its own on-premises data centers rather than relying heavily on public cloud resources. This strategy allows the company to maintain high standards of customer privacy and compliance, achieve faster time to market, and leverage its extensive engineering talent. While public cloud is used for seasonal demand spikes, eBay believes its core competency for large-scale AI workloads is best served by its dedicated HPC infrastructure despite challenges like rapidly evolving GPU technology and power/cooling demands.

title: News | Simon Property Group, BP To Add Electric Vehicle Chargers at US Retail Locations
summary: BP Pulse, the electric vehicle charging arm of BP, has partnered with Simon Property Group to install more than 900 ultra-fast EV charging stations at 75 of Simon's retail locations across the U.S. The first stations are expected to open in early 2026. This collaboration is part of BP's larger plan to invest $1 billion in U.S. EV infrastructure by 2030, expanding its presence in the public EV charging market.

title: Optimism abounds as semiconductor industry takes aim at AI-fueled trillion-dollar market
summary: The semiconductor industry is poised for significant growth, aiming for a trillion-dollar market, driven by the widespread adoption of artificial intelligence. This expansion is further fueled by substantial U.S. government investments through the CHIPS Act, which is boosting domestic chip production and attracting new talent. Industry leaders also emphasize the importance of addressing sustainability and exploring advanced computing areas like quantum computing amidst this AI-driven boom.

title: Should I Buy Intel's Shares After Their Big Sell-Off?
summary: Intel's stock has significantly underperformed its semiconductor peers despite its historical dominance, largely due to struggles in competing with AI-focused chipmakers like Nvidia and losing market share in its core PC business. While Intel faces high uncertainty and financial challenges, Morningstar analysts consider its stock fairly valued at $30 per share, with potential upside dependent on its successful execution of an aggressive advanced manufacturing roadmap. The company needs to overcome manufacturing delays and increasing competition to regain its competitive edge.

title: Intel Capital has a stake in 43 Chinese technology companies – report
summary: Intel Capital, Intel's venture capital arm, holds investments in 43 Chinese tech startups, despite increasing US government legislation aimed at limiting funding to Chinese firms, especially those developing advanced technologies for military purposes. Although Intel recently received significant CHIPS Act funding and has a license to supply certain chips to Huawei, its business in China accounted for 27% of its 2023 revenue, highlighting the complex geopolitical challenges it faces. The US Treasury is considering exemptions for some venture capital transactions, but Intel Capital may be forced to sell its stakes in Chinese companies once new regulations come into force.

title: Dutch chip manufacturing quality control startup Nearfield Instruments raises €135M
summary: Nearfield Instruments, a Dutch startup specializing in quality control systems for chip manufacturing, has secured €135 million in Series C funding. The company develops metrology and inspection systems, like its Quadra 3D, to ensure the quality of silicon wafers, aiming to improve production yields for advanced chips. This significant funding round, led by Walden Catalyst and Temasek, highlights the growing demand for innovative quality control within the semiconductor industry, which is typically dominated by a few large players.

title: Nvidia’s other billionaire: Director Mark Stevens joins Huang in 10-figure club
summary: Mark Stevens, a director at Nvidia and long-time venture capitalist, has joined the billionaire ranks with an estimated net worth of $8.8 billion, largely due to his early investment in Nvidia in 1993. His fortune also includes a minority stake in the Golden State Warriors and returns from over two decades in venture capital. Stevens' journey with Nvidia highlights the company's tumultuous early years before the AI boom propelled its stock to unprecedented levels, making him and other early board members billionaires alongside CEO Jensen Huang.

title: Survey of CXL Implementations and Standards (Intel, Microsoft)
summary: A new technical paper, "An Introduction to the Compute Express Link (CXL) Interconnect," co-authored by researchers from Intel, Microsoft, and the University of Washington, provides a comprehensive overview of the CXL standard. The paper details CXL 1.0, 2.0, and 3.0, surveys existing implementations, and discusses CXL's impact on datacenter landscapes and future directions. CXL is highlighted as an industry-standard interconnect enabling coherency and memory semantics for devices like accelerators and persistent memory, crucial for supporting efficient accelerators, server memory expansion, and multi-server resource pooling.

title: Portland General CEO: "We're seeing really quite significant growth" in data center load demand
summary: Portland General Electric (PGE) CEO Maria Pope noted significant growth in data center load demand in Oregon, alongside semiconductor manufacturing development. Data centers account for 20 percent of PGE's industrial customers and are driving the need for increased renewable energy and infrastructure investments. While chip manufacturing growth is slower, it is expected to continue into the next decade.

title: Amkor scores $400M in CHIPS funding
summary: Amkor Technology Inc. has been offered up to $400 million in direct funding and $200 million in loans under the CHIPS and Science Act for its new $2 billion semiconductor assembly and test facility in Peoria, Arizona. This facility, which will be Amkor's first in the U.S., is set to package and test chips for Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC), with Apple as its primary customer. The investment aims to boost domestic semiconductor manufacturing, address the shortage of 2.5D advanced packaging capacity crucial for AI chips, and create approximately 2,000 jobs.

title: Marvell Introduces Breakthrough Structera CXL Product Line to Address Server Memory Bandwidth and Capacity Challenges in Cloud Data Centers
summary: Marvell Technology, Inc. has launched its new Structera product line of Compute Express Link (CXL) devices to tackle memory performance and scaling issues in cloud data centers. The line includes Structera A CXL near-memory accelerators, optimized for high-bandwidth applications like deep learning with integrated Arm Neoverse processors, and Structera X CXL memory-expansion controllers, designed to add terabytes of memory for high-capacity applications and enable recycling of DDR4 DIMMs. These products are the first to support four memory channels, integrate inline compression, and are manufactured using a 5nm process, aiming to improve efficiency, reduce e-waste, and lower operational costs for cloud operators.

title: Intel will cut ‘thousands’ more jobs, Bloomberg reports
summary: Intel is reportedly planning another round of job cuts, with Bloomberg indicating "thousands" of positions could be eliminated across the company. These potential layoffs come as Intel faces declining sales and intense market competition, despite significant government subsidies and investments in new factories. The move reflects Intel's strategy to reduce expenses during its multi-year turnaround effort.

title: Newegg accepts more items for trade-in
summary: Newegg is expanding its Refreshed refurbishment and trade-in program to now accept CPUs for credit towards new CPUs. Additionally, Newegg has partnered with Intel for a limited-time offer allowing customers to trade in qualifying PCs for a Newegg gift card when purchasing a new Intel-powered PC. These initiatives aim to provide customers with pathways to upgrade their computer components and systems.

title: Marvell presents Structera CXL product line targeting cloud data centres
summary: Marvell Technology has launched the Structera CXL product line designed to help cloud data center operators address memory performance and scaling challenges. The line includes Structera A CXL near-memory accelerators for high-bandwidth applications like DLRM, and Structera X CXL memory-expansion controllers for high-capacity applications such as in-memory databases. These products aim to optimize resource utilization, lower energy consumption, and allow for the repurposing of older DDR4 DIMMs, thus reducing CapEx and promoting sustainability.

title: Intel to cut 15% jobs, suspend dividend in turnaround push; shares plummet
summary: Intel announced on Thursday that it will cut over 15% of its workforce, approximately 17,500 employees, and suspend its dividend starting in the fourth quarter as part of a turnaround strategy. The chipmaker aims to focus on its manufacturing business and reduce costs while battling increased competition in the AI chip market. This news caused Intel's shares to plummet by 20% in extended trading, reflecting investor concerns about its financial outlook and its struggle to compete with rivals like Nvidia and AMD in the AI sector.

title: Intel Shares Fall by Most Since at Least 1982 on Grim Forecast
summary: Intel Corp. (INTC) shares experienced their largest decline in over 40 years, falling 26%, after providing a grim growth forecast and announcing plans to cut 15,000 jobs. This move signals the chipmaker's struggles to compete in the artificial intelligence era and resulted in a $32 billion loss in market value. The company also confirmed its revenue would fall short of expectations.

title: Intel has worst day on Wall Street in 50 years, falls to lowest price in over a decade
summary: Intel's shares experienced their largest drop since 1974, plummeting 26% to their lowest price since 2013, after the chipmaker reported a significant earnings miss and announced a restructuring that includes laying off over 15% of its workforce. This downturn dragged down other global chip stocks like TSMC and Samsung, and contributed to a 2.4% drop in the Nasdaq. The company also reported a net loss of $1.61 billion, missed revenue expectations, and suspended its dividend, implementing a $10 billion cost-reduction plan.

title: Intel shares slump 26% as turnaround struggle deepens
summary: Intel's shares dropped 26% in their worst day since 1974 after the company suspended its dividend and announced a 15% workforce reduction to fund a costly turnaround strategy. The move, aimed at catching up with competitors like TSMC, resulted in a market value loss of over $30 billion. The disappointing forecast and job cuts have deepened investor concerns about Intel's ability to regain its once-dominant position in the chip manufacturing industry.

title: Intel Corporation (New Mexico)
summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce has awarded Intel up to $7.865 billion in direct funding through the CHIPS and Science Act to expand its capacity and capabilities across four U.S. states. This funding supports Intel's plan to invest nearly $90 billion to create a robust domestic semiconductor ecosystem, anticipating 10,000 manufacturing and 20,000 construction jobs. Specifically, the Rio Rancho, New Mexico facility will receive $500 million to modernize two fabs into the largest advanced packaging facility in the U.S., creating 1,800 manufacturing jobs and 2,500 construction jobs.

title: The 3 Best 6G Stocks to Buy in August 2024
summary: This article identifies InterDigital (IDCC), SK Telecom (SKM), and Apple (AAPL) as the top three 6G stocks to consider buying in August 2024. It highlights 6G technology's potential for ultra-high speeds and near-instantaneous response times, projecting significant market growth. The piece details each company's relevance to 6G development and their strong financial performance, making them attractive investments in the burgeoning 6G market.

title: 挑战英伟达，AI 芯片公司 Groq 成功融资 6.4 亿美元：估值飙升至 28 亿美元
summary: AI芯片初创公司Groq近日成功完成6.4亿美元融资，使其总融资额超过10亿美元，公司估值飙升至28亿美元。本轮融资旨在加速开发其LPU推理引擎，以更快处理AI任务，有望挑战英伟达在AI芯片领域的领先地位。Groq还宣布Meta首席人工智能科学家Yann LeCun将担任技术顾问，并计划扩大产能和增加模型支持。

title: Top 10 Drone Analytics Companies | Best Aero Tech Provided
summary: The global drone analytics market is projected to reach $53.9 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 26.4% from 2024. This growth is driven by technological advancements, increased adoption across various industries like agriculture and construction, and a rising demand for real-time data. The article highlights the top 10 companies in this sector, including DJI, Parrot, AeroVironment, and Intel, detailing their overviews, financial performance, geographical presence, and recent developments.

title: NSF invests $7.6M in educational projects to build a skilled semiconductor manufacturing workforce in partnership with Intel Corporation
summary: The U.S. National Science Foundation and Intel Corporation announced a $7.6 million investment across six projects to enhance STEM education and training, aiming to strengthen the nation's semiconductor workforce. This initiative is part of a larger 10-year, $100 million collaboration between NSF and Intel to address semiconductor design and manufacturing challenges and workforce shortages in the U.S. The projects will provide scholarships and develop technician training programs at two-year and four-year institutions, with Intel experts offering guidance and support.

title: Corsair ditches 80 Plus for PSUs: here are the Cybenetics ratings it'll use instead
summary: Corsair is discontinuing its use of 80 Plus ratings for power supplies and will instead exclusively adopt Cybenetics efficiency standards. The new system categorizes efficiency with similar metallic classifications (Gold, Platinum, Titanium) but includes a more comprehensive assessment and introduces noise level ratings. This move follows a growing trend among manufacturers like Thermaltake, FSP, MSI, and Intel, who have also started recognizing Cybenetics certification.

title: 3 Semiconductor Stocks Feeling the Pressure from Intel's Big Miss
summary: Following Intel's significant Q2 earnings miss and subsequent stock drop, the semiconductor sector is facing pressure, leading Citi to lower its spending forecasts for chip equipment. This article examines three semiconductor companies—Lam Research (LRCX), KLA Corp (KLAC), and Applied Materials (AMAT)—that are feeling the impact and assesses whether their current valuations present a buying opportunity despite the industry headwinds. Each company's financial performance, analyst ratings, and target prices are detailed.

title: Cisco could reportedly let go 4,000+ workers in new round of job cuts
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. is reportedly preparing for a new round of layoffs that could affect over 4,000 employees, six months after a similar restructuring cut. These job cuts are attributed to slow demand for networking equipment and macroeconomic uncertainty. Despite a recent drop in networking revenue, Cisco has raised its full-year sales forecast and anticipates growth in fiscal 2025, partly due to its acquisition of Splunk Inc.

title: Synopsys' $35 bln Ansys deal under UK regulator scrutiny
summary: The UK's Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is examining Synopsys' $35 billion acquisition of Ansys to assess its potential impact on competition in Britain. The deal, which combines Synopsys' chip design tools with Ansys' broader electronic systems software, has already faced concerns from analysts regarding regulatory challenges and scrutiny from Chinese regulators. The CMA will decide whether to proceed with a formal Phase 1 investigation or clear the deal based on its information-gathering process.

title: Infillion Named to Inc. 5000 List for 8th Consecutive Year
summary: Infillion, an advanced media buying platform, has been named to the 2024 Inc. 5000 list of fastest-growing private companies in America for the eighth consecutive year. The company's consistent growth is attributed to its ability to adapt to changing advertising landscapes and its recent acquisition of MediaMath, expanding its multiplatform digital advertising solutions. Infillion's founder and executive chair, Rob Emrich, and CFO, Angela Birkemeier, highlighted the team's dedication and rapid innovation.

title: Cisco cuts thousands of jobs, 7% of workforce, as it shifts focus to AI, cybersecurity
summary: Cisco Systems is set to lay off 7% of its employees, approximately 5,900 jobs, marking its second round of job cuts this year. This move is part of the company's strategy to refocus on high-growth areas like artificial intelligence and cybersecurity. Despite a 10% drop in quarterly revenue and a 45% decline in earnings, Cisco's shares rose 6% after hours, reflecting investor confidence in its strategic shift and recovery in product orders.

title: 5 Top FPGA Companies to Know
summary: This article highlights five top FPGA companies: Qualcomm, Microchip Technology, AMD, BAE Systems, and Intel. It explains that FPGAs are re-programmable microchips used in various sectors like defense and telecommunications, and these companies are developing innovative FPGA-capable products. Many of these companies are actively hiring FPGA experts.

title: Applied Materials’ Q3 FY2024 net income increases by 9% to $1.7bn
summary: Applied Materials reported a 9% increase in its Q3 FY2024 net income, reaching $1.7 billion, with revenues rising by 5% to $6.78 billion. This growth is largely attributed to the increasing demand for AI services, prompting the company to focus on improving the performance-per-watt of its chips significantly over the next 15 years through advancements in semiconductor technologies. The company forecasts continued revenue growth for Q4 FY2024, with diluted EPS projected between $2 and $2.36.

title: GPUs: The backbone of artificial intelligence
summary: Graphics Processing Units (GPUs), initially developed for gaming, have become crucial to artificial intelligence (AI) due to their parallel processing capabilities. The GPU market is projected to grow significantly, reaching an estimated $274 billion by 2029, driven by high demand from various technological advancements like AI and autonomous driving. The Invesco QQQ ETF offers exposure to major GPU developers such as Nvidia, Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and Intel, highlighting their roles in the rapidly expanding AI landscape.

title: AMD Plans ZT Systems Acquisition To Challenge NVIDIA AI Ecosystem
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has announced its intention to acquire AI infrastructure firm ZT Systems for $4.9 billion in a strategic move to challenge NVIDIA's dominance in the AI market. This acquisition aims to boost AMD's presence in cloud computing and data center operations by integrating ZT Systems' expertise in servers and custom hyperscaling data centers. The deal, expected to close by the first half of 2025, represents AMD's largest acquisition since 2022 and is a critical step in its long-term AI strategy.

title: 5 Stocks With the Largest Fair Value Estimate Cuts After Q2 Earnings
summary: Morningstar analysts identified five US-listed stocks—Fastly, Plug Power, Lyft, Intel, and Arcadium Lithium—that experienced significant fair value estimate cuts of 10% or more following their second-quarter earnings reports. Despite an overall positive earnings season for most companies, these firms faced reductions due to factors like slowing demand, reduced guidance, competitive disadvantages, and changes in revenue forecasts. Some of these stocks, like Lyft and Arcadium Lithium, are still considered attractively priced by analysts for long-term investors even after the valuation cuts.

title: Chemours expands Teflon PFA production in West Virginia
summary: Chemours is expanding its Teflon PFA production at its Washington Works plant in West Virginia to meet surging demand from the semiconductor industry, driven by the CHIPS and Science Act. The company plans to begin operations at the expanded facility next month after receiving its wastewater permit. The expansion reflects the critical role of Teflon PFA in chip manufacturing, though Chemours seeks more policy and regulatory certainty for future investments in U.S. manufacturing.

title: Mobileye Global: CEO Makes $10 Million Bet on Future Strength
summary: Mobileye Global Inc. (NASDAQ: MBLY) CEO Amnon Shashua recently made a significant insider purchase of over $10 million in company stock, signaling confidence despite a recent stock drop and lowered guidance due to a China slowdown. The company, a dominant player in ADAS, reported mixed Q2 2024 results but highlighted the clearing of excess inventory at customers. Analysts maintain a positive outlook with a strong consensus price target upside.

title: What Are the Benefits of Windows 11 for AI?
summary: Windows 11 leverages AI and machine learning to enhance productivity, security, and user experience across various applications. Key benefits include optimized UI with features like Snap Assist and Copilot, improved performance in productivity tools and gaming, and robust cybersecurity measures. HP and other manufacturers are integrating these AI capabilities into their hardware, such as the HP Spectre and ENVY lines, to deliver powerful and secure computing experiences.

title: US to award HP $50 mln for semiconductor tech project
summary: The U.S. Commerce Department plans to award $50 million to HP to expand and modernize an Oregon facility, boosting semiconductor technologies crucial for life sciences and AI applications. This funding is part of a larger $39 billion subsidy program for U.S. semiconductor manufacturing approved by Congress in 2022. The project specifically leverages HP's expertise in microfluidics and microelectromechanical systems to produce silicon devices vital for drug discovery and other life sciences research.

title: Biden-Harris Administration Announces Preliminary Terms with HP to Support Development and Commercialization of Cutting-Edge Semiconductor Technologies
summary: The Biden-Harris Administration has announced a preliminary memorandum of terms with HP Inc., proposing up to $50 million in direct funding under the CHIPS and Science Act. This investment aims to expand and modernize HP's facility in Corvallis, Oregon, focusing on semiconductor technologies that support life sciences instrumentation and AI hardware. The project is expected to create over 250 manufacturing and construction jobs and bolster the U.S. semiconductor ecosystem.

title: HP’s Oregon fab receives CHIPS funding
summary: HP Inc. is set to receive up to $50 million in CHIPS and Science Act funding from the Department of Commerce to expand and modernize its Corvallis, Oregon, facility. This investment will support the manufacturing of silicon devices for drug discovery and medical applications, creating nearly 150 construction jobs and over 100 manufacturing jobs. The project demonstrates a public-private partnership, with HP also seeking the Department of the Treasury’s Investment Tax Credit and having already secured $9.5 million from the state of Oregon.

title: Top 10: Quantum Computing Companies
summary: This article highlights the top 10 quantum computing companies in 2024, detailing their unique approaches, key innovations, and applications. From D-Wave Systems' quantum annealing to IBM's superconducting transmon qubits, the list covers a diverse range of technologies and their impact on fields like drug discovery, financial modeling, and AI. The companies are ranked based on their contributions and advancements in the competitive quantum landscape.

title: CXL memory drives the future of data centers with low-latency solutions
summary: CXL memory is emerging as a critical solution for modern data centers, offering high speed and low latency to boost data throughput and scalability for applications like generative AI and autonomous driving. Industry leaders from Micron Technology, Supermicro, Intel, and MemVerge discussed the transformative impact of CXL on data center architectures, highlighting its ability to leverage existing PCIe interfaces for lower latency and its potential to repurpose older memory technologies like DDR4. Supermicro is playing a key role in CXL adoption by integrating it into its latest platforms, supporting up to eight CXL memory modules to expand memory capacity efficiently.

title: Marvell sees 5G sales slump but guides for second-half revival
summary: Marvell Technology, a US chipmaker, has experienced a significant slump in its 5G sales due to telecommunications spending cuts and inventory build-up, particularly affecting its largest 5G customer, Nokia. Despite a challenging first half of the year with plummeting revenue in its carrier infrastructure unit, Marvell's CEO Matt Murphy projects a revival in the second half, especially in carrier infrastructure and enterprise networking, aiming for these divisions to collectively reach $2 billion to $2.2 billion annually. The company has also found some relief in its data-center business, which nearly doubled in size thanks to AI-related demand, helping to narrow overall losses.

title: Intel working with bankers to present board with strategic options, sending shares up 10%
summary: Intel is reportedly working with bankers, including Morgan Stanley, to explore strategic options for its struggling businesses, which may include splitting off or selling units. This news caused Intel's shares to jump 10% on Friday. CEO Pat Gelsinger acknowledged investor skepticism and emphasized the need for urgency in addressing the company's challenges, as it faces competition from Nvidia and has been largely left out of the AI boom, leading to a significant stock decline and recent layoffs.

title: 10 Best Additive Manufacturing Vendors
summary: This article identifies and describes the top 10 additive manufacturing companies globally, crucial for industries like automotive, healthcare, and aerospace. It details the services and specializations of each vendor, ranging from 3D Systems' comprehensive offerings to Materialise NV's full-spectrum solutions, highlighting their role in the rapidly growing additive manufacturing market projected to reach USD 74.21 billion by 2031.

title: Best- and Worst-Performing Stocks of August 2024
summary: The article highlights the best and worst-performing stocks of August 2024 within the Morningstar US Large-Mid Cap Index. Fortinet and Kellanova were among the top performers, while Intel and Moderna were among the worst. The analysis includes financial metrics, sector, industry, and economic moat for each company.

title: Robotic surgery to benefit from advanced processors and AI, says AMD
summary: AMD states that its advanced processors and AI technologies are crucial for making robotic surgery more accessible and cost-effective. The company is actively collaborating with leading surgical robot providers, including Intuitive Surgical, to integrate its high-performance computing solutions. AMD expects AI to enhance various aspects of robotic surgery, from diagnostics and real-time visualization to surgeon training, while emphasizing that AI will assist rather than make autonomous decisions.

title: Semiconductor Bonding Market Size, Growth Report, 2034
summary: The global semiconductor bonding market was valued at USD 991.1 million in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 1363.7 million by 2034, growing at a CAGR of 3.60%. This growth is driven by the increasing demand for advanced, miniaturized semiconductor devices in consumer electronics, EVs, autonomous vehicles, and AI/ML applications, despite challenges related to technological complexity and high equipment costs. North America currently leads the market share, while Asia Pacific is expected to exhibit the highest CAGR.

title: How Nvidia doubled earnings, lost almost $300 billion in value and shook the stock market
summary: Nvidia experienced a significant stock market shake-up, losing almost $300 billion in value in a single day, despite having doubled its earnings. This downturn is attributed to growing fears of an economic slowdown and renewed skepticism about the immediate payoff from AI investments, causing broader market indexes to also fall. Analysts highlight Nvidia's dependence on continued massive AI investments from major tech companies and warn of stock volatility.

title: Nvidia Stock Is Down 15% Since Earnings. Is It a Buy?
summary: Nvidia's stock has dropped 15% since its Q2 earnings report, despite strong results and an optimistic forecast for AI investments and data center revenue. Morningstar maintains a "fairly valued" rating with a $105 fair value estimate, citing Nvidia's wide economic moat due to its GPU technology and CUDA software, and robust financial health. The primary risks include increasing competition in the AI chip market and potential diversification efforts by tech giants.

title: [News] 300mm Wafer Fab Construction Picks up Steam Worldwide
summary: The global 12-inch wafer production is accelerating due to rising demand for high-performance computing, HBM, CoWoS packaging, and high-performance storage in the AI market, prompting major companies like TSMC, Intel, and UMC to expand their fabrication facilities worldwide. Several companies, including VIS, TSMC, UMC, Toshiba, Powerchip, Texas Instruments, Intel, GlobalFoundries, and various Chinese firms, are investing heavily in new 12-inch wafer fab construction, with many projects expected to begin production between 2024 and 2029, significantly boosting global semiconductor manufacturing capacity.

title: IBM Gains 35% in the Past Year: Reason to Buy the Stock?
summary: IBM has seen a 35% gain over the past year, driven by strong demand for hybrid cloud and AI solutions, particularly through its partnership with Intel for Gaudi 3 AI accelerators and the Watsonx platform. Despite robust growth and strategic initiatives, the company faces stiff competition, margin pressures, and a premium valuation, leading Zacks Investment Research to recommend caution for new investors due to its Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

title: Intel reaches deal to make chips for US military
summary: Intel Corp. has secured a deal expected to provide up to $3.5 billion in federal grants to produce semiconductors for the Pentagon under a secretive program called "Secure Enclave." This initiative aims to establish domestic production of advanced chips for military and intelligence applications, adding to Intel's potential $8.5 billion in grants and $11 billion in loans from the Chips and Science Act. Despite recent financial struggles and a reevaluation of its manufacturing ambitions, Intel was chosen due to its status as the only American maker of advanced processors, showcasing the Biden administration's commitment to domestic sourcing for critical defense technology.

title: Intel (INTC) Solidifies $3.5 Billion Deal to Make Chips for US Military
summary: Intel Corp. has secured up to $3.5 billion in federal grants to manufacture semiconductors for the Pentagon's Secure Enclave program. This deal aims to bolster the production of advanced chips with military and intelligence applications, with support for facilities in Arizona and other US states. The agreement highlights efforts to strengthen domestic chip manufacturing for defense purposes.

title: Pat Gelsinger on Foundry Momentum, Progress on Plan
summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger outlined the next phase of Intel's transformation following a Board of Directors meeting. Key takeaways include building on Foundry momentum, driving cost efficiencies to achieve a $10B savings target, and refocusing on the x86 franchise with an AI strategy. The company announced an expanded strategic collaboration with AWS for custom chip designs, a $3B award under the CHIPS and Science Act for the U.S. Secure Enclave program, and plans to establish Intel Foundry as an independent subsidiary.

title: Intel awarded up to $3 bln from US for national security related chip production
summary: The Biden Administration has awarded Intel Corp up to $3 billion under the CHIPS and Science Act to develop a "Secure Enclave" for microelectronics crucial for national security. This investment aims to fortify the domestic semiconductor supply chain and maintain U.S. leadership in advanced manufacturing, and it sent Intel's shares up 5.7% after the announcement. The CHIPS Program Office is also discussing a separate potential award for commercial fabrication facilities.

title: Top 10 Nvidia Competitors & Alternatives
summary: This article lists the top 10 competitors and alternatives to Nvidia, a leading manufacturer of GPUs and chips. It details each company's origins, revenue, employee count, and specific competitive advantages or areas of direct competition with Nvidia. The article highlights key players like AMD, Intel, TSMC, and Samsung, emphasizing their roles in the semiconductor and graphics processing markets.

title: Yangtze Memory advances chip tech with Chinese tools, TechInsights says
summary: Yangtze Memory Technologies Co (YMTC) is making strides in replacing foreign chipmaking technology with domestic alternatives, with its latest storage products rivaling global leaders, according to TechInsights. While YMTC still relies on some foreign equipment, Chinese suppliers are increasing their share. However, the company faces technical challenges with its latest Xtacking 4.0 technology, resulting in fewer layers due to lower production yields when using Chinese tools.

title: Dow drives innovation for a sustainable electronics future
summary: Dow is focused on driving innovation for a sustainable electronics future, addressing the challenges of a rapidly digitalizing world. The company develops solutions that enhance product performance, reliability, and efficiency while reducing environmental impact, particularly concerning data center energy consumption. Dow offers cooling technologies like DOWSIL™ Immersion Cooling Technology and DOWFROST™ LC Heat Transfer Fluid to minimize the environmental footprint of data centers and enable more energy-efficient and safer electronic devices.

title: 2 Semiconductor Stocks to Buy
summary: Morningstar analyst Phelix Lee identifies two undervalued semiconductor stocks—Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and GlobalWafers (6488)—despite the sector's significant growth driven by artificial intelligence. Lee emphasizes the continued importance of cyclical trends and economic moats, predicting an improved demand picture by 2025. Both companies are highlighted for their strategic positions, with TSMC dominating cutting-edge contract manufacturing and GlobalWafers establishing a monopoly in US 300mm wafer production.

title: Intel Gets Multibillion-Dollar Apollo Offer as Qualcomm Circles
summary: Apollo Global Management has offered Intel a multibillion-dollar, equity-like investment of up to $5 billion, signaling confidence in Intel's turnaround strategy. This offer provides Intel with an alternative amid a potential takeover bid from rival Qualcomm. The investment could bolster Intel's efforts against the backdrop of increasing competition in the chip industry.

title: Up 6%, What's Next For Applied Materials Stock?
summary: Applied Materials stock recently gained 6% and is up 22% year-to-date, driven by a Fed interest rate cut, an upswing in the semiconductor cycle, and robust demand from the AI sector. The company's Q3 2024 results exceeded expectations, with revenue growth and strong earnings, and it is guiding for continued growth in Q4 FY’24 despite risks from U.S. restrictions on semiconductor exports to China. Forbes values Applied Materials stock at $222 per share, indicating a potential 10% upside from the current market price.

title: Report: Apollo Global Management Offers $5 Billion Investment in Intel
summary: Apollo Global Management has reportedly offered Intel a $5 billion investment, which Intel executives are currently considering. This potential investment could serve as an alternative to a possible takeover by rival chipmaker Qualcomm, which recently initiated discussions with Intel regarding an acquisition. Both Intel's declining sales and Qualcomm's regulatory challenges are key factors in these ongoing negotiations.

title: Intel Unveils Next-Generation AI Solutions with the Launch of Xeon 6 and Gaudi 3
summary: Intel has launched its new Xeon 6 with Performance-cores (P-cores) and Gaudi 3 AI accelerators to address the growing demand for cost-effective and high-performance AI infrastructure. The Xeon 6 processors are designed for compute-intensive workloads with enhanced efficiency and AI acceleration, while Gaudi 3 accelerators are optimized for large-scale generative AI, offering significant performance improvements and TCO benefits. These new products aim to support an open ecosystem for AI development and deployment, leveraging Intel's x86 infrastructure and collaborations with OEMs.

title: TSMC and Cadence Collaborate to Deliver AI-Driven Advanced-Node Design Flows, Silicon-Proven IP and 3D-IC Solutions
summary: TSMC and Cadence announced an expanded collaboration to deliver AI-driven advanced-node design flows, silicon-proven IP, and 3D-IC solutions. This partnership focuses on optimizing productivity and performance for AI-driven designs, supporting TSMC's latest process technologies like N3, N2P, and A16, and enabling sophisticated 3D-IC solutions for complex data processing. The collaboration aims to accelerate time to market and enhance silicon performance for the rapidly growing demands of AI applications.

title: Micron rises 13% as strong forecast impresses AI-hungry investors
summary: Micron Technology's shares jumped over 13% after its strong first-quarter revenue forecast indicated high demand and favorable pricing for HBM chips used in AI. The company's forecast and impressive quarterly revenue growth, exceeding Wall Street estimates, suggest a robust market for memory chips, despite earlier concerns about HBM pricing. This positive outlook also influenced other chip firms, though some gains were pared due to news of a U.S. Department of Justice probe into Super Micro Computer.

title: PCI Express Market to Grow by USD 53.74 Billion from 2024-2028, Driven by PCIE Integration with IEEE 1394 (FireWire) and AI's Role in Market Transformation - Technavio
summary: The global PCI Express market is projected to grow by USD 53.74 billion from 2024-2028, at a CAGR of over 28.22%, according to Technavio. This growth is primarily driven by the integration of PCIe with IEEE 1394 (FireWire) and continuous technological upgrades, including the increasing adoption of AI and ML. Key challenges include managing inventory fluctuations due to changing customer demand and technological advancements.

title: COO interview: SkyWater’s Sakamoto on turning up the volume
summary: John Sakamoto, President and COO of SkyWater Technology Inc., aims to significantly increase the foundry's volume output and expand its advanced packaging operations. Despite soft wafer revenues, SkyWater is transitioning from primarily R&D services to high-volume manufacturing, supported by new e-beam lithography technology and a substantial DoD contract for advanced packaging in Florida. The company seeks to be a versatile alternative for advanced packaging in North America, addressing a critical gap in the domestic chip production supply chain.

title: Ingram Micro Pulls the Trigger on its IPO Filing
summary: Global technology distributor Ingram Micro, owned by Platinum Equity, has filed for an Initial Public Offering (IPO) with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, signaling its return to public markets. The company plans to list its common stock on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol "INGM" and intends to use the proceeds to repay outstanding loans. This move follows the company generating $48.1 billion in global net sales in 2023 and working with numerous partners and MSPs.

title: Cerence CEO Stefan Ortmanns to Depart; Ex-Intel Chief Krzanich Named as Replacement
summary: Cerence CEO Stefan Ortmanns is stepping down and will be replaced by Brian Krzanich, former chief of Intel and CDK Global. This change comes as the company undergoes a restructuring aimed at cost reduction. Ortmanns is recognized for his role in transitioning Cerence into a public company after its split from Nuance Communications in 2019.

title: AMD Up on Strong August Semiconductor Sales: Is the Stock a Buy?
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares rose significantly on the back of strong August semiconductor sales, which saw global sales reach a record $53.1 billion. The article highlights AMD's strong performance relative to peers, its robust data center business, strategic acquisitions, and positive earnings outlook. Despite trading at a premium, AMD's expanding portfolio and market position suggest its valuation is justified.

title: New law that exempts chip plants from U.S. environmental review won’t affect Micron in Clay
summary: A new federal law exempts chipmaking plants from rigorous U.S. environmental review, but it will not impact Micron Technology's planned facility in Clay, New York. This is because Micron's project still requires a federal permit for wetlands and is subject to New York State's stringent environmental quality review act, which is comparable to federal standards. Micron's $100 billion plant will undergo both state and specific federal reviews regardless of the new legislation.

title: Analog Devices Executive VP to depart in 2025
summary: Analog Devices Inc. announced that Anelise Sacks, Executive Vice President and Chief Customer Officer, will resign effective March 31, 2025. This departure is amicable, and Sacks will stay on for a smooth transition if a successor is appointed early. The news comes amid various financial assessments and strategic discussions for Analog Devices, including potential manufacturing in India and scrutiny from the U.S. Senate.

title: AMD: We Maintain Fair Value Estimate as ‘Advancing AI’ Event Highlights Strong Road Map
summary: Morningstar maintains its fair value estimate of $145 per share for AMD, viewing its shares as modestly overvalued despite progress in AI. This stance follows AMD's "Advancing AI" event, which highlighted significant advancements across processors, software, and networking hardware, and an increased forecast for the AI accelerator industry to $500 billion in revenue by 2028. The company also reported gains in server processor market share against Intel, suggesting continued growth in this segment.

title: Jabil to close Kentucky plant, lay off 108 workers
summary: Electronics maker Jabil is closing one of its three facilities in Florence, Kentucky, resulting in 108 layoffs, effective December 6th. The closure is due to a customer relocating operations to another Jabil site. The company plans to offer severance benefits and career outplacement assistance to impacted employees, and two other Florence facilities will remain open.

title: Jabil Introduces New High-Performance, Purpose-Built AMD and Intel Servers Optimized for AI, FinTech, and Cloud Applications
summary: Jabil Inc. has expanded its server portfolio with the introduction of the J421E-S and J422-S servers, featuring AMD 5th Generation EPYC™ and Intel® Xeon® 6 processors. These new servers are designed for scalability and customization across various cloud data center applications, including AI, HPC, and FinTech. Jabil emphasizes its collaborative approach with customers to develop tailored solutions, including Precision Time Measurement optimized motherboards, and will showcase these products at the 2024 OCP Global Summit.

title: Intel and AMD Form x86 Ecosystem Advisory Group to Accelerate Innovation for Developers and Customers
summary: Intel and AMD have announced the formation of an x86 Ecosystem Advisory Group to drive innovation and simplify software development across the x86 architecture. This group, including major tech companies like Google, Microsoft, and Dell, aims to enhance compatibility and consistency for developers and customers. The initiative seeks to ensure the x86 architecture evolves to meet emerging needs, particularly in areas like AI workloads and custom chiplets.

title: 1,300 jobs will be eliminated at Intel’s Hillsboro campus, company tells state
summary: Intel announced it will eliminate 1,300 jobs at its Hillsboro campus in Oregon, following a more than $1 billion loss and a global plan to cut 15% of its workforce. These layoffs are part of a broader strategy to reduce costs and make the company leaner and more agile to regain its position as a technology leader. Employees will be notified at least two months before their jobs are eliminated, with the first separations scheduled for November 15, 2024.

title: ASML Earnings: Stock Slumps on Weaker Guidance, Lower Orders
summary: ASML's shares declined 14% after the company lowered its 2025 revenue guidance to EUR 30 billion-EUR 35 billion and weakened 2025 gross margin expectations due to lower sales and EUV order postponements. Third-quarter orders were significantly weaker than expected, primarily attributed to slower ramp-ups in logic foundries and weak memory capacity additions, especially impacting Intel. Despite the pullback, Morningstar analysts see ASML as a good buying opportunity, having cut their fair value estimate to $935 per share but believing the stock is currently discounting an overly pessimistic long-term scenario.

title: Intel Vs. Micron Technology: Which Chipmaker Holds The Edge?
summary: This article compares Intel and Micron Technology, assessing which chipmaker currently holds an advantage. Intel faces challenges with stock performance, geopolitical scrutiny from China, and the complexity of its partnerships, while analysts have a cautious outlook. Micron, conversely, is experiencing strong growth driven by its focus on next-gen memory technologies crucial for AI and its global expansion efforts, particularly in India, with analysts predicting significant upside.

title: Alphabet appoints new Chief Technologist
summary: Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ: GOOGL) has appointed Prabhakar Raghavan, formerly Senior Vice President, Knowledge and Information, as the new Chief Technologist of Google LLC, effective immediately. This new role comes as the tech industry continues to evolve, prompting companies to adjust their leadership structures. Alphabet's robust financial health, with a market capitalization of $2.01 trillion and strong cash flows, positions it well for future technological initiatives under Raghavan's guidance.

title: Analog Devices sees India as key R&D hub: Company's India head
summary: Analog Devices (ADI) considers India a pivotal hub for its research and development efforts, recognizing the country's engineering talent and burgeoning market opportunities. With its Bengaluru R&D center being one of the largest globally, ADI aims to significantly contribute to the semiconductor industry's growth, which is projected to double to over a trillion dollars in seven years. ADI has also signed an MoU with Tata Electronics, Tata Motors, and Tejas Networks to explore semiconductor manufacturing in India and integrate ADI's products into Tata applications like electric vehicles.

title: Amplify ETFs Announces the Amplify Bloomberg AI Value Chain ETF (NYSE: AIVC)
summary: Amplify ETFs has launched the Amplify Bloomberg AI Value Chain ETF (AIVC), providing equal-weighted global exposure to companies crucial for the AI ecosystem. The ETF invests in semiconductor, cloud/software, and hardware companies identified by the Bloomberg AI Value Chain Index. AIVC aims to capitalize on the projected growth of the global AI market, which is expected to reach $3.6 trillion by 2034.

title: How recent Intel layoffs may affect the Oregon economy
summary: Intel announced 1,300 job cuts in Oregon, part of a nationwide effort to reduce staffing by 15% and save $10 billion. Despite these layoffs, the company plans to invest billions in modernizing and expanding its advanced factories in Hillsboro to maintain technological leadership. Oregon is also vying for a federal research center, which could further anchor the chip industry in the state.

title: How Intel Got Left Behind in the A.I. Chip Boom (Published 2024)
summary: Intel, once a dominant chip company, missed significant opportunities and made strategic errors that led to it falling behind in the artificial intelligence chip boom. Despite early insights into the potential of graphics chips for AI, the company's valuation has plummeted compared to Nvidia, now the AI chip leader. Mismanagement, a rigid corporate culture focused on its PC-era architecture, and failed in-house projects contributed to its inability to capitalize on the AI revolution.

title: UAE investors snap up AI stocks after price drop
summary: During the third quarter, UAE investors significantly increased their holdings in artificial intelligence stocks, taking advantage of price drops in companies like CrowdStrike, Nvidia, and Intel. This investment trend also extended to blockchain stocks, with Marathon Digital Holdings seeing a substantial increase in UAE holders. Despite the focus on emerging tech, big tech companies such as Tesla, Amazon, and Apple continue to form a stable core of UAE investor portfolios.

title: Applied Materials, Lam Research, Or Tokyo Electron: Which Generalist Is The Most Likely To Outperform?
summary: This article compares Applied Materials, Lam Research, and Tokyo Electron, the three major generalists in the Wafer Fab Equipment sector, highlighting their wide moats and market dominance. The author rates Tokyo Electron as a "Strong buy" due to its very low valuation compared to its peers and the broader market. The piece emphasizes the increasing importance of semiconductor equipment companies in enabling technological progress.

title: ASML Holding N.V. (ASML): Institutional Investors Are Shorting This Semiconductor Stock Now
summary: Institutional investors are shorting ASML Holding N.V. (ASML) due to recent earnings leaks and downward guidance, signaling continued weakness in the semiconductor industry. The company, a crucial supplier of chip manufacturing equipment, saw a significant stock drop after forecasting lower 2025 net sales and weak bookings. This reflects a broader industry bifurcation where AI-driven chip stocks are performing better than those tied to other slowing sectors like electric vehicles.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Stock Sinks as Q4 Guidance Disappoints. What to Know
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock fell after its Q4 guidance disappointed Wall Street, despite meeting Q3 expectations. Analysts are mostly bullish, with an average target price suggesting upside, but some like Oppenheimer maintain a Perform rating due to competition and valuation concerns.

title: Intel Posts $16.6 Billion Quarterly Loss, Its Biggest Ever (Published 2024)
summary: Intel announced its largest-ever quarterly loss of $16.6 billion, primarily due to asset valuation write-downs and restructuring charges from cutting 15,000 workers. The company's CEO, Pat Gelsinger, is attempting to turn around its fortunes amidst shrinking profit margins and struggles in the AI chip market, leading to a 60 percent drop in share price since his tenure began. Intel's revenue for the quarter also dropped 6 percent from the previous year.

title: NVIDIA and Sherwin-Williams Set to Join Dow Jones Industrial Average; Vistra to Join Dow Jones Utility Average
summary: NVIDIA Corp. and The Sherwin-Williams Co. are set to join the Dow Jones Industrial Average, replacing Intel Corp. and Dow Inc., respectively. This change aims to provide more representative exposure to the semiconductor and materials sectors. Additionally, Vistra Corp. will replace The AES Corp. in the Dow Jones Utility Average to better represent the Independent Power Producers and Renewable Electricity Producers industry.

title: Nvidia to take Intel's spot on Dow Jones Industrial Average
summary: Nvidia, a leading force in AI chips, is set to replace Intel on the Dow Jones Industrial Average after Intel's 25-year run, reflecting a significant shift in the chipmaking market. This change comes as Intel's shares have declined significantly and Nvidia's valuation has soared due to its pivotal role in generative AI. Sherwin-Williams will also join the index, replacing Dow, effective next week.

title: Tech Shake-Up: NVIDIA Supplants Intel In The Dow Jones Industrial Average
summary: NVIDIA is replacing Intel in the Dow Jones Industrial Average after Intel's 25-year tenure on the index. This change comes as NVIDIA's shares have soared due to the AI boom, while Intel recently reported a significant loss. The move aims to increase the index's exposure to the semiconductor industry, with NVIDIA's market cap now second only to Apple among publicly traded companies.

title: Wells Fargo To Close Portland, Hillsboro, Salem Offices
summary: Wells Fargo announced it will close its offices in Portland, Hillsboro, and Salem, Oregon, by the end of 2025, consolidating global operations to 13 major geographic areas. This move will result in hundreds of layoffs in Oregon, although the company states it will offer relocation opportunities and severance packages. Other major employers in Oregon, including Nike and Intel, have also experienced significant layoffs recently, and several other banks have left downtown Portland.

title: Sherwin-Williams will join Dow Jones Industrial Average on Nov. 8
summary: The Sherwin-Williams Co. (NYSE: SHW) is set to join the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) on November 8, replacing Dow Inc. (NYSE:DOW). This change, along with NVIDIA Corp. replacing Intel Corp., aims to better represent the chemicals/materials and semiconductor industries within the 30-stock index. Sherwin-Williams' inclusion highlights its significant market capitalization and reputation.

title: New companies are coming to the Dow Jones
summary: Nvidia and Sherwin-Williams are set to replace Intel and Dow in the Dow Jones Industrial Average, reflecting changes in key economic sectors. This move highlights Nvidia's rise in semiconductors and Sherwin-Williams' relevance, while being removed can negatively impact a company's image and stock value, especially with the prevalence of ETF investing.

title: Nvidia briefly overtakes Apple as world's most valuable company—it began in a breakfast booth at Denny's
summary: Nvidia briefly surpassed Apple as the world's most valuable company with a market cap of $3.38 trillion driven by its advanced AI chips. The company's journey began with its co-founders at a Denny's in 1993, overcoming early failures to become a dominant force in AI and computing. CEO Jensen Huang attributes their success to a combination of vision, luck, and 61 years of hard work.

title: Rackspace Brings GPU-as-a-Service to Spot Cloud Platform
summary: Rackspace Technology is introducing a GPU-as-a-service offering to its Rackspace Spot platform, an online auction for cloud servers, to address the growing demand for compute power in AI. This new service, including access to Nvidia GPUs, is designed to provide cost-effective and scalable cloud infrastructure, particularly for AI, machine learning, and data analytics workloads. Rackspace is also expanding its physical presence with a new location in San Jose, California, to support this initiative.

title: Big Changes Ahead For Analog Design
summary: The analog design landscape is undergoing significant changes due to challenges like heterogeneous integration, rapid foundry PDK evolution, and the increasing complexity of advanced nodes. Traditional in-house tools are struggling to keep up, pushing designers towards commercial EDA solutions despite initial resistance. The shift necessitates a new approach to design, focusing more on early post-layout simulation and continuous verification, while also highlighting a growing shortage of experienced analog engineers and the difficulties in transferring crucial design knowledge.

title: As PayPal’s ‘AI Bump’ Fades Away, Should You Buy or Sell PYPL Stock?
summary: This article discusses PayPal's stock performance following an "AI bump" that quickly faded, contrasting it with other companies that retained their gains. It evaluates PayPal's underperformance, its recent business growth and turnaround strategy under CEO Alex Chriss, and its current valuation, ultimately recommending PYPL as a buy due to reasonable pricing and future growth drivers like increased TAM and agentic commerce services.

title: 3 Ways Stock-Split Stocks Nvidia and Sherwin-Williams Are Modernizing the Dow Jones Industrial Average
summary: This article discusses how the additions of Nvidia and Sherwin-Williams to the Dow Jones Industrial Average are modernizing the index. It highlights that stock splits are crucial for inclusion in the price-weighted Dow, unlike market-cap-weighted indexes. The changes signify a shift towards a more tech-focused and growth-oriented Dow, moving away from its traditional emphasis on blue-chip dividend stocks.

title: Intel fuels R&D Surge: Chip wars amid Asian AI expansion
summary: The R&D World Index saw a modest climb, driven by Intel's gains and strategic expansion in Asia, while Honda experienced setbacks. The article highlights significant R&D developments including CATL's advancements in solid-state batteries, Apple's new subsidiary in India, TSMC facing U.S. export restrictions on AI chips to China, and DuPont's growth fueled by AI-based semiconductor products. It also touches on Toyota's revised sales forecast and China's large debt rescue package.

title: Is First Trust NASDAQ Semiconductor ETF (FTXL) a Strong ETF Right Now?
summary: The article analyzes the First Trust NASDAQ Semiconductor ETF (FTXL), highlighting its smart beta strategy and performance. It notes FTXL's significant asset size ($1.45 billion), expense ratio of 0.60%, and strong year-to-date and 12-month returns of 16.05% and 40.77% respectively. The fund's primary sector exposure is Information Technology, with Intel, Broadcom, and Applied Materials as top holdings.

title: Vietnam expands chip packaging footprint as investors reduce China links
summary: Foreign semiconductor companies are increasing their chip testing and packaging capacities in Vietnam due to a shift away from China amid trade tensions. Major players like Amkor, Hana Micron, and Intel are expanding operations, with some equipment being transferred from China. This trend is expected to boost Vietnam's share in global chip assembling, testing, and packaging capacity significantly by 2032, with domestic firms like FPT and Sovico Group also investing.

title: Booking Holdings Announces Job Cuts Amid Restructuring
summary: Booking Holdings is implementing job cuts as part of a restructuring effort to enhance operational efficiency and reduce costs, following a 13.6% rise in operating expenses in Q3 2024. This move aligns with a broader industry trend of major companies streamlining operations in response to rising costs, contributing to increased uncertainty in the job market. The company plans to modernize processes and optimize procurement, though specific details on job cuts are yet to be released.

title: Applied Materials Announces New Collaboration Model for Advanced Packaging at Summit on Energy-Efficient Computing
summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) has announced an expansion of its global EPIC innovation platform to accelerate the commercialization of advanced chip packaging technologies, specifically targeting energy-efficient computing for AI. The company convened over two dozen semiconductor R&D leaders in Singapore to foster collaboration across the industry. This initiative, EPIC Advanced Packaging, aims to address challenges in developing next-generation packaging technologies by facilitating co-innovation and providing early access to new equipment and expertise.

title: Nvidia Corp. (NVDA)-Makes Big Beat on Earnings, But Stock May Need Santa to Propel to New All Time High
summary: Nvidia Corp. (NVDA) reported strong third-quarter earnings, surpassing Wall Street expectations and easing concerns about AI-related valuations, with CEO Jensen Huang emphasizing a foundational transformation in computing. While the beat indicates strong momentum, the stock might remain range-bound in the near term between $190 and $212. A "Santa Claus rally" or positive macro data, alongside sustained demand and successful platform rollouts, could propel the stock to new highs.

title: Stock Split Watch: Is Applied Materials Next?
summary: Applied Materials has not split its stock in over two decades, leading some investors to wonder if another split is imminent. While stock splits don't change a company's fundamental value, they still attract retail investors. The article suggests that Applied Materials' current valuation makes it a reliable investment for long-term investors, regardless of a future stock split.

title: To understand Nvidia, take a look at its CEO and culture
summary: A new book, "The Nvidia Way," by Tae Kim, explores the critical role of CEO Jensen Huang and the company's unique work culture in its significant success, particularly in the AI industry. The excerpt highlights Huang's intense work ethic, his use of "fear and anxiety" as motivational tools, and the demanding environment that shaped Nvidia's early years, including a fierce competitive challenge from Intel. The article emphasizes that beyond technology, the distinct culture fostered by Huang has been a key driver of Nvidia's dominance.

title: The State Of The EDA Industry In 2024
summary: This article provides an in-depth discussion with Jay Vleeschhouwer of Griffin Securities about the Electronic Design Automation (EDA) industry. Key topics include continued consolidation, the expansion of EDA into broader engineering software, opportunities for startups amidst market dominance by a few large players, the alignment of EDA products with silicon and software development, and the industry's engagement with AI/ML trends. The conversation also covers the significant growth in hardware-assisted verification and the strategic investments EDA companies are making for future growth, such as Cadence's Intelligent System Design strategy and Synopsys's proposed acquisition of Ansys.

title: Interim chief at Wolfspeed poised to make $375K in monthly compensation
summary: Thomas H. Werner has been appointed interim executive chairman of Wolfspeed following the termination of previous CEO Gregg Lowe. Werner will receive $375,000 in monthly compensation, consisting of a $150,000 base salary and $225,000 in restricted stock, while the Durham chipmaker searches for a permanent CEO. The company's shares have significantly declined over the past year amidst decreased demand and a complex strategic transition.

title: Lam Research Is Overvalued Amid Geopolitical Risks
summary: Lam Research (LRCX) is considered overvalued due to geopolitical risks, particularly Sino-American tensions, and its current stock price significantly exceeds its intrinsic value. The article highlights the company's essential role in the semiconductor industry but points to U.S. restrictions on sales to China and rising domestic competition within China as significant challenges. Despite potential tailwinds from AI and cloud computing, the author's valuation model suggests the stock will not appreciate significantly by June 2027, leading to a "hold" rating.

title: Washington Curtails Intel’s Chip Grant After Company Stumbles
summary: The Biden administration is reducing Intel's preliminary federal CHIPS grant from $8.5 billion to less than $8 billion, partly due to a $3 billion military contract offered to Intel and the company's investment delays and financial struggles. This reduction follows Intel's decision to postpone the completion of its Ohio chip facilities and its recent significant quarterly loss, signaling challenges to the administration's domestic chip manufacturing ambitions. The change also considers Intel's technology roadmap and customer demand as it lags behind rivals.

title: Intel AI Platforms at the Olympic Games Paris 2024
summary: Intel, as the Official Worldwide AI Platform Partner, utilized its artificial intelligence technology to enhance the Olympic and Paralympic Games Paris 2024 for fans, athletes, organizers, and viewers. The AI platforms provided immersive fan experiences, revolutionized broadcasting with 8K streaming and automatic highlight generation, and improved on-site operations for athletes through a conversational AI platform. This showcased the potential of AI in sports and demonstrated Intel's "AI Everywhere" strategy for consumers and industries alike.

title: Intel to sell Folsom campus and lease back only space it needs, company says
summary: Intel announced its decision to sell its Folsom campus and lease back only the necessary space as part of a cost-saving strategy aimed at generating $10 billion in savings by 2025. This move follows recent layoffs of 272 employees at the Folsom site, adding to approximately 800 employees laid off between December 2022 and December 2023. The company aims for greater in-person collaboration at larger sites and a leaner, more agile structure for sustainable growth, while a finance professor noted shareholder pressure for improved financial performance due to Intel's stagnant stock price since 2000.

title: Intel, Biden-Harris Administration Finalize $7.86 Billion Funding Award Under US CHIPS Act
summary: Intel and the Biden-Harris Administration have finalized a $7.86 billion funding award under the US CHIPS and Science Act. This funding supports Intel's $100 billion investment plans in American semiconductor manufacturing and advanced packaging projects across Arizona, New Mexico, Ohio, and Oregon. The award, coupled with a 25% investment tax credit, aims to strengthen the U.S. semiconductor supply chain, create jobs, and advance domestic technology leadership.

title: CHIPS Act funding released to Intel for Ohio plants, jobs
summary: The Biden administration has awarded Intel nearly $8 billion in CHIPS Act funding, with $1.5 billion specifically designated for its semiconductor fabrication plants in Ohio. This funding supports Intel's anticipated $90 billion investment in various states, including Ohio, by the end of the decade, as part of a larger $100+ billion expansion strategy. Ohio leaders, including Gov. Mike DeWine, welcomed the announcement, emphasizing its importance for economic and national security and for making advanced computer chips in America.

title: Yext, Inc. (YEXT) Positioned for Stronger Growth with Expansion into Social Media and Usage-Based Pricing
summary: Yext, Inc. (YEXT) is poised for stronger growth through its recent acquisition of Hearsay Systems, expansion into social media with "Yext Social," and adoption of usage-based pricing for resellers. These strategic moves aim to accelerate innovation and create long-term value, enhancing Yext's position in the cloud-based digital knowledge management and search solutions market. The company is leveraging AI to drive cloud computing growth and focusing on sustainability within the cloud industry.

title: Dogs of Tech: 3 Semiconductor Stocks Set for a 2025 Rebound
summary: This article identifies three semiconductor stocks—GlobalFoundries (GFS), Microchip Technologies (MCHP), and Intel (INTC)—as "Dogs of Tech" poised for a rebound in 2025. These companies, which experienced significant declines in 2024, are expected to benefit from favorable economic tailwinds, including lower interest rates and business-friendly policies. The analysis suggests that despite recent struggles, these foundational tech companies are well-positioned for growth, partly due to government initiatives like the CHIPS Act.

title: Intel Announces Retirement of CEO Pat Gelsinger
summary: Intel Corporation announced the retirement of CEO Pat Gelsinger after a 40-plus-year career, effective December 1, 2024. David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus have been named interim Co-CEOs, with Holthaus also appointed CEO of Intel Products. Frank Yeary will serve as interim executive chair while the board searches for a permanent successor.

title: Bank of America Maintains Underperform Rating on Intel Corporation (INTC), Sees Increased Likelihood of Business Separation Amid CEO Transition
summary: Bank of America has maintained an Underperform rating on Intel Corporation (INTC) and sees an increased likelihood of a business separation following CEO Pat Gelsinger's unexpected retirement. The advisory suggests that separating Intel's Products and Foundry arms could provide much-needed operational and financial independence, despite significant hurdles. This assessment comes amidst Intel's IDM 2.0 strategy not yielding desired results and highlights the competitive issues both divisions face.

title: Wells Fargo to trim Oregon workforce by 700 next year amid office closures in the state
summary: Wells Fargo announced it will trim approximately 700 jobs in Oregon next year as part of office closures in Salem, Hillsboro, and Portland. This move follows previous layoffs and a company strategy to consolidate non-customer facing positions. Other major Oregon employers like Intel and Nike have also announced significant layoffs recently, though state economists maintain the labor market remains strong.

title: Intel Appoints Semiconductor Leaders Eric Meurice and Steve Sanghi to Board of Directors
summary: Intel Corporation announced the appointment of Eric Meurice, former CEO of ASML Holding N.V., and Steve Sanghi, chairman and interim CEO of Microchip Technology Inc., to its board of directors. Both semiconductor industry veterans will serve as independent directors, bringing extensive technical expertise, executive experience, and operational rigor to Intel's board. Their appointments are expected to provide valuable perspectives as Intel focuses on its customer priorities in Intel Products and Intel Foundry, while also driving efficiency and improving profitability.

title: Intel Shores Up Board With Microchip CEO, Former ASML Chief
summary: Intel (INTC) has appointed former ASML Holding NV CEO Eric Meurice and Microchip Technology Inc. head Steve Sanghi to its board of directors. This move aims to bring significant semiconductor industry experience and operational vigor to the embattled chipmaker. The appointments come as Intel seeks a new chief executive officer and attempts a significant comeback.

title: Apollo Hedge Fund Jumps 6% on Well-Timed Shorts, High-Grade Debt
summary: Apollo Global Management Inc.'s flagship credit hedge fund, the Apollo Credit Strategies Fund, has seen a 6% gain this year due to well-timed short positions and strategic investments in high-grade debt. The fund profited from shorts against the CMBX 6 index, energy companies, and SoftBank, while also acquiring beaten-down investment-grade debt from companies like JPMorgan Chase & Co. and Intel Corp. These gains come at a time when many rival hedge funds are experiencing losses amid market turmoil.

title: The Inside Story of Apple Intelligence
summary: This article delves into Apple's strategy for generative AI, dubbed Apple Intelligence, highlighting the company's "best, not first" approach. Key executives, including John Giannandrea and Craig Federighi, explain that Apple has been preparing for this moment for years and focused on integrating AI at a systems level with a strong emphasis on privacy. The piece also contrasts Apple's approach with competitors and discusses the future trajectory of AI at Apple.

title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Challenges NVIDIA with Faster, Cheaper AI Chip Alternatives
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is emerging as a credible alternative to NVIDIA in the AI chip market, offering faster speeds at lower prices, according to industry analysts. This development comes amid new U.S. government curbs on chip exports to China, aiming to limit Chinese access to advanced AI technology. While AMD shows promise, the article suggests other AI stocks might offer higher returns in a shorter timeframe.

title: AI's growth is just getting started, BlackRock's thematic ETF head says
summary: BlackRock's U.S. head of thematic and active ETFs, Jay Jacobs, believes the AI boom is still in its early stages, highlighting infrastructure and cybersecurity as key areas for growth in 2025. He emphasizes that the theme extends beyond megacap tech to include various semiconductor, data center, and software companies. Jacobs also points out the need for physical infrastructure, including energy, materials, and real estate, to support AI's expansion.

title: The next wave of AI: Demand and adoption
summary: Barclays Research analysts predict that AI is entering a new era, moving beyond text-based chatbots to "Agent Era" systems capable of autonomous, end-to-end tasks by 2025-2026. This shift is expected to drive significant enterprise and consumer adoption, with consumer AI potentially reaching one billion daily active users by 2026.
