Despite immense capital investment requirements for computing resources like GPUs and ASICs, rapidly falling inference costs are projected to make AI systems profitable sooner than anticipated, with demand for compute power significantly exceeding current forecasts.

title: Why Marvell Technology is the next AI winner
summary: Marvell Technology has seen its market value nearly double in the past year, largely due to its significant role in the booming AI infrastructure market. The company partners with all hyperscalers, particularly AWS, providing custom AI chips and infrastructure solutions. While not consistently profitable, its strategic positioning in AI has driven its stock market performance, with expectations to convert this into future profits.

title: Nvidia slapped with a Chinese antitrust probe as Beijing collects ‘bargaining chips’ ahead of Trump’s return
summary: China's State Administration for Market Regulation (SAMR) has launched an antitrust investigation into Nvidia regarding its 2020 acquisition of Mellanox Technologies, alleging potential violations of monopoly regulations. This move is seen as Beijing's effort to gain leverage ahead of Donald Trump's potential return to the White House, which is expected to intensify U.S. tech export controls against China. Despite U.S. restrictions, China remains a significant market for Nvidia and other chipmakers, making the company vulnerable to pressures from both the U.S. and China.

title: News | Micron edges closer to getting billions from government to build, expand US chip plants
summary: Micron Technology is moving closer to receiving billions in federal funding to construct new chip facilities in New York and Idaho, and expand an existing plant in Virginia. The U.S. Department of Commerce has finalized a $6.1 billion award and preliminarily agreed to expand the Manassas, Virginia, facility with up to $275 million, with the Biden administration aiming to secure these funds before the incoming Trump administration takes office. These investments are part of the CHIPS and Science Act, aiming to increase the U.S. share of advanced memory manufacturing from nearly 0% to 10% over the next decade.

title: Mobileye Partners with Innoviz for Next-Gen Autonomous Driving Tech, Major Automakers Already On Board
summary: Mobileye and Innoviz Technologies have partnered for Mobileye to integrate Innoviz's LiDARs into its Mobileye Drive™ autonomous vehicle platform, with production scheduled for 2026. Major automakers like VW Commercial Vehicles and Schaeffler/VDL have already adopted the platform. This collaboration aims to enhance L4 autonomous driving capabilities by combining Innoviz's LiDAR technology with Mobileye's existing imaging radars and high-resolution cameras, leading to safer and more efficient autonomous transportation.

title: Lenovo, HP And Dell Are Betting Big On A PC Refresh In 2025 (So Is The Channel)
summary: Major PC manufacturers like Lenovo, HP, and Dell, along with their channel partners, are highly optimistic about a significant PC refresh in 2025. This anticipation is driven by several factors, including the Windows 10 end-of-life, the increasing demand for AI-capable PCs, and a large installed base of aging computers purchased during the COVID-19 pandemic. While some industry analysts predict the full surge of AI PC sales might extend into 2026, the consensus among these tech giants and solution providers points to 2025 as a pivotal year for PC market recovery, especially in the commercial sector.

title: TSMC says first advanced U.S. chip plant 'dang near back' on schedule. Here's an inside look at the Arizona fab
summary: TSMC's first advanced U.S. chip fabrication plant in Arizona is "dang near back on the original schedule" after delays and an increased investment from $12 billion to $20 billion. The plant will produce 4-nanometer chips and has committed to building two more fabs by the end of the decade, totaling $65 billion. Challenges include securing a skilled workforce, managing significant water and power demands, and global supply chain complexities, but the U.S. Commerce Department finalized $6.6 billion in CHIPS Act funding for the project.

title: Intel Corporation (NASDAQ:INTC) is favoured by institutional owners who hold 62% of the company
summary: Institutional investors hold 62% of Intel Corporation (NASDAQ:INTC), indicating their significant influence on the company's share price. BlackRock, Inc., The Vanguard Group, Inc., and State Street Global Advisors, Inc. are among the largest shareholders. While insiders hold less than 1% of the stock, their ownership is seen as a positive alignment with shareholders, and the general public holds a 37% stake.

title: A decade ago, she took over a struggling chipmaker—now she's worth $1 billion: 'Everything takes time'
summary: Lisa Su took over Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) a decade ago when the company was struggling, with its stock at $3 per share and significant staff cuts. Under her leadership, AMD's market cap has grown to over $205 billion and her net worth has reached $1.3 billion. Su, named Time's CEO of the Year for 2024, attributes her success to a long-term strategy focused on high-quality products, customer trust, and simplified operations, emphasizing that "everything takes time."

title: Suppliers to Samsung and other chip firms play waiting game amid slow grants and industry slump
summary: Suppliers to Samsung and other major chip manufacturers like Intel and TSMC are experiencing delays due to the slow distribution of grants and an industry slump. These smaller companies, which had invested millions to increase capacity following expansion announcements from the chip giants, are now facing the challenge of timing their operations correctly to avoid idle equipment and personnel. The project delays are creating ripple effects throughout the entire supply chain.

title: Viavito to buy Inertial Labs for up to $350 million
summary: Viavi Solutions is acquiring Virginia-based Inertial Labs for up to $350 million, including $150 million at closing and up to $175 million in contingencies. This acquisition will expand Viavi's presence in aerospace, defense, and industrial markets, particularly for autonomous delivery and transportation, by integrating Inertial Labs' precise positioning and navigation technologies. The transaction is expected to close in Q1 2025 and add approximately $50 million in annual revenue to Viavi's Network and Service Enablement segment.

title: Qualcomm claims it only copied "1% or less" of Arm technology for the Snapdragon X
summary: A legal battle has begun between Qualcomm and Arm Holdings over allegations that Qualcomm's Snapdragon X chipsets copied significant aspects of Arm's architecture. Qualcomm, having acquired Nuvia, claims only "1% or less" of Arm technology was used in their Snapdragon X chips, while Arm alleges copyright infringement and is seeking the destruction of Nuvia's designs. The outcome could significantly impact the future of Snapdragon-based AI PCs and royalty payments for both companies.

title: UMC says has no plans to build in US
summary: United Microelectronics Corp (UMC) has stated it has no immediate plans to build a production site in the US, despite speculation regarding potential pressure to invest there, especially with Donald Trump's prospective return to the White House. UMC is currently focused on an ongoing joint project with Intel Corp to develop a 12-nanometer semiconductor process platform in Arizona. This contrasts with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co's (TSMC) significant investment in US manufacturing.

title: Intel faces derivative lawsuit over foundry business challenges
summary: Intel Corporation is facing a derivative class-action lawsuit from LR Trust, a shareholder, targeting former CEO Pat Gelsinger and other executives for alleged mismanagement of its foundry business, breaches of fiduciary duty, and misleading financial statements. The lawsuit follows substantial operating losses exceeding $7 billion in 2023 for Intel Foundry Services (IFS) and aims to recover damages for the company, not individual shareholders. The complaint also highlights concerns over executive compensation amidst the company's financial struggles.

title: Broadcom CEO Hock Tan claims he has no interest in buying Intel
summary: Broadcom CEO Hock Tan has stated he is not interested in acquiring Intel Corp., focusing instead on making Broadcom an AI powerhouse. Tan cited his company's efforts to increase market share in the AI chip sector and his reluctance to pursue hostile takeovers after a past failed attempt with Qualcomm. This news comes as Broadcom's market capitalization has soared due to AI enthusiasm, while Intel's value has significantly declined.

title: Eric King joins 3M spinoff Solventum’s CVC
summary: Eric King, formerly an investor at Intel Capital, has joined Solventum Ventures, the corporate venturing arm of 3M's healthcare spinoff, Solventum. King will focus on investments in medical equipment, healthcare IT, and dental technologies. Solventum became a publicly listed company in April 2024, specializing in medical and oral care equipment.

title: Jim Cramer Says Intel Corporation (INTC)’s Balance Sheet Is ‘Something That Looks Like A Nightmare’
summary: Jim Cramer criticized Intel Corporation's (INTC) balance sheet, calling it a "nightmare" due to high debt and challenged cash flows, despite the company's status as the world's largest integrated chip manufacturer. He argued that Intel's business model, particularly its expensive foundries and inability to capitalize on the AI market, has negatively impacted its financial health. Cramer also discussed broader market trends, urging investors to buy during market dips based on historical data and cautioning against addictive zero-day options trading.

title: Press Kit: Intel Vision 2024
summary: The Intel Vision 2024 event focused on advances in artificial intelligence for the enterprise, featuring Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger and industry leaders. Intel introduced a scalable systems strategy for AI, new enterprise AI solutions, and unveiled the Intel® Gaudi® 3 AI accelerator, which promises significant performance improvements. The event included keynotes, discussions, demos, and media assets showcasing Intel's commitment to transforming business with AI.

title: Steve Sanghi
summary: This article introduces Steve Sanghi, the Chairman, Interim CEO, and President of Microchip Technology Inc., who joined Intel Corporation's board of directors in December 2024 as an independent director. It details his extensive career at Microchip Technology, highlighting his long tenure as CEO and president, and his previous roles at Waferscale Integration Inc. and Intel. The piece also mentions his board memberships, published business books, and educational background.

title: Nvidia Supplier Ibiden Weighs Faster Expansion for AI Demand
summary: Ibiden Co., a key supplier of chip package substrates for Nvidia, is considering accelerating its production capacity expansion due to robust demand from the AI sector. CEO Koji Kawashima indicated that current expansion plans might not be sufficient to meet customer needs, prompting discussions for further investment. This expansion comes as Ibiden's shares rose and despite past reliance on Intel, the company is seeing increased demand driven by AI chips used by Nvidia and potentially other chipmakers.

title: Unbelievable: How China’s Outsmarting US Chip Ban to Dominate AI
summary: Despite US chip embargoes aimed at stifling its AI progress, China has innovated strategies to advance its AI capabilities. The country is focusing on local chip manufacturing, developing efficient code for smaller AI models, and leveraging cloud services and open-source models as workarounds. These efforts, supported by national strategies and significant investments, have allowed China to continue its rapid progress in AI, turning the embargo into a catalyst for self-reliance and innovation.

title: Palantir Stock Went 4.5x This Year: Time To Rethink?
summary: Palantir Technologies stock has seen a significant 4.5x surge this year, reaching $80 per share, driven by discussions of a consortium to bid for U.S. defense contracts, the AI market frenzy, strong earnings, and optimism surrounding a potential Trump administration increasing national security spending. Despite these gains and its inclusion in the Nasdaq-100, the Trefis Team suggests the stock presents a high-risk trade due to its high valuation, reliance on government sales, and insider selling, recommending caution for investors. The article compares Palantir's valuation to Snowflake and Nvidia, highlighting its potentially disconnected share price from fundamental growth rates.

title: Management That Matters: 2024’s Best U.S. Companies Unveiled
summary: The 2024 Drucker Institute Company Rankings identify the 30 best-managed U.S. companies based on corporate effectiveness across five categories: customer satisfaction, financial strength, social responsibility, innovation, and employee engagement. Apple secured the top spot as the best-managed company with an effectiveness score of 92.1, surpassing Microsoft from its 2023 position. Nvidia and Microsoft followed, with Intel and MasterCard rounding out the top five, demonstrating the importance of innovation and financial strength for high effectiveness scores.

title: Palantir and Duolingo: The AI Powerhouses Driving AGIX’s Success
summary: This article highlights the impressive performance of the KraneShares Artificial Intelligence and Technology ETF (AGIX) since its inception, driven by key holdings like Palantir and Duolingo. It details how these companies leverage AI to achieve significant success in their respective sectors, contributing to AGIX's outperformance compared to major market benchmarks. The article also provides a 2025 AI market outlook, projecting significant growth in enterprise AI adoption and infrastructure expansion.

title: Samsung, Texas Instruments, and Amkor finalize CHIPS Act funding agreements
summary: Samsung, Texas Instruments, and Amkor have finalized their CHIPS and Science Act funding agreements with the US Department of Commerce. Samsung will receive $4.745 billion for its $37 billion investment in Texas, Texas Instruments will get $1.61 billion for its $18 billion project across Texas and Utah, and Amkor will receive $407 million for its $2 billion investment in an Arizona OSAT plant. These agreements are part of a broader effort by the US to secure a domestic supply of advanced semiconductors and are finalized before a new presidential term which may see changes to CHIPS Act support.

title: HP Inc. Unleashes New AI PCs At CES 2025, Wins Three Innovation Awards
summary: HP Inc. unveiled a new lineup of AI-powered PCs at CES 2025, winning three innovation awards for devices like the Omnibook Ultra Flip and its embedded AI Companion. The company is targeting various user types, from entry-level workers to on-the-go executives, with new models such as the OmniStudio X Series, EliteBook Ultra, and EliteBook X, all featuring enhanced NPUs and AI capabilities. HP emphasizes its commitment to providing tools that boost productivity, creativity, and connectivity for every user.

title: Intel Extends Leadership in AI PCs and Edge Computing at CES 2025
summary: At CES 2025, Intel unveiled new Intel Core Ultra (Series 2) processors, including the 200V series with Intel vPro for businesses, and 200HX and H series for creators and gamers, all featuring cutting-edge AI enhancements and performance improvements. Intel also introduced a new suite of processors for edge computing, showcasing significant performance gains in AI workloads compared to previous generations and competitors. The availability of these new systems is set to begin in January and February 2025, with further releases planned for the first half of the year.

title: Arizona will host third CHIPS R&D flagship facility
summary: Arizona State University Research Park in Tempe has been selected as the site for the third CHIPS for America research and development facility, focusing on semiconductor prototyping and packaging. This facility, which will be operational by Q4 2028, will involve the highest financial investment among the three R&D centers. It aims to bridge lab-to-production challenges by providing a manufacturing-like setting for diverse R&D activities, leveraging Arizona's existing microelectronics ecosystem.

title: Dell picks AMD for business PCs
summary: Dell Technologies will start using Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) chips for its business PCs for the first time, a move announced at CES in Las Vegas. This decision is seen as a blow to Intel's dominance in the PC chip market, especially in the more profitable commercial sector. AMD unveiled new Ryzen AI Max series processors and 9000 series desktop processors, claiming significant performance improvements for AI workloads, while competitors Intel and Qualcomm also showcased their AI-focused chips.

title: Intel Stock Plunge 4%, Hits 5-Month Low After Mizuho Lowers Price Target
summary: Intel's stock plunged nearly 4% to a five-month low of $19 after Mizuho analysts downgraded the chipmaker from 'neutral' to 'negative' and reduced its price target from $23 to $21. This setback comes amid increasing uncertainty due to stricter chip export controls expected from President Biden and reports of Qualcomm abandoning its bid to acquire Intel. Despite the company's struggles, retail investor sentiment on Stocktwits has shifted to "extremely bullish," though analysts at Citi suggest a new CEO transition is not out of the question as the long-term recovery remains unclear.

title: IoT Technology Industry worth $1,148.62 billion by 2030
summary: The IoT technology market is projected to reach USD 1,148.62 billion by 2030, growing from USD 959.30 billion in 2025 at a CAGR of 3.7%. This growth is driven by increased adoption of connected devices, advancements in 5G, cloud, and AI, and government initiatives for smart cities. The Asia Pacific region is expected to lead this growth due to rapid digital transformation and significant investments.

title: HP finalizes $53 million in CHIPS Act money for Oregon expansion
summary: HP Inc. has finalized a $53 million award from the CHIPS Act to expand its research and manufacturing operations in Corvallis, Oregon. The funding will support innovation in microfluidics and micromechanics, adapting inkjet printing technology for drug discovery, and is expected to create 150 temporary construction jobs and 100 long-term positions. This award is part of the larger federal effort to revitalize the domestic semiconductor industry, though other CHIPS Act initiatives in Oregon have seen mixed results.

title: Magneto Resistive RAM Market Share to Surge At 38.3% CAGR, Is Projected To Reach USD 91.29 Billion by 2034 | PMR
summary: The Magneto Resistive RAM (MRAM) market is projected to surge at a CAGR of 38.3%, reaching USD 91.29 billion by 2034, driven by its benefits of non-volatility, accelerated speed, and low power intake. Technological advancements, growing demand in electronic gadgets, and increased usage in aerospace and defense sectors are key growth drivers. North America currently dominates the market, with Asia Pacific expected to show the highest CAGR during the forecast period.

title: Supermicro Empowers AI-driven Capabilities for Enterprise, Retail, and Edge Server Solutions
summary: Supermicro is showcasing its latest AI server, storage, and edge computing solutions for the retail industry at the National Retail Federation (NRF) annual show, in collaboration with NVIDIA. These solutions aim to enhance retail operations, security, and efficiency by leveraging generative AI for personalized shopping, logistical automation, and shrinkage prevention. Supermicro highlights its broad portfolio of edge AI solutions and various server systems designed to deliver high-end performance at retail locations, demonstrating use cases like digital human customer service, AI shopping assistants, and video search for loss prevention.

title: AMD: Trading at a 20% Discount, This Misunderstood Stock Is a Buy | Morningstar UK
summary: Morningstar believes Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is undervalued, trading at a 20% discount to its fair value estimate despite a challenging 2024. The company is well-positioned in the lucrative AI chip market, second only to Nvidia, and its strong data center business is expected to drive significant growth. Morningstar maintains a "narrow economic moat" rating for AMD, citing its chip design expertise and its unique x86 instruction set architecture license.

title: High-tech company with operations in Beaverton wins $105 million in CHIPS funding
summary: Analog Devices, Inc., a semiconductor company with operations in Beaverton, Camas, Washington, and Chelmsford, Massachusetts, is set to receive up to $105 million in federal CHIPS and Science Act funding. This investment is planned for facility expansions, particularly a "significant" one at its Beaverton plant, and is expected to create 500 new jobs across all sites while also focusing on environmental management and community partnerships. This funding is part of a broader federal effort to revitalize semiconductor manufacturing in the U.S., with other Oregon companies like HP, Intel, and Microchip Technologies also receiving CHIPS funds.

title: Should You Steer Clear of Intel and Enphase Stocks in 2025?
summary: This article examines whether Intel (INTC) and Enphase Energy (ENPH) stocks, both of which have seen significant declines, represent a buying opportunity or should be avoided in 2025. Intel faces challenges from strong competition and slow AI adoption, while Enphase struggles with high interest rates and European market weakness. Both companies have strategies for recovery, but face considerable headwinds and have received cautious ratings from Wall Street analysts.

title: Governor Hochul Announces Corning to Invest More Than $315 Million and Create up to 300 Jobs at Semiconductor Glass Manufacturing Facility in North Country
summary: Governor Kathy Hochul announced that Corning Incorporated will invest up to $315 million in its Canton, St. Lawrence County facility, creating up to 130 new manufacturing jobs and 175 construction jobs. This investment will increase the production of High Purity Fused Silica and EXTREME Ultra Low Expansion glass, critical for advanced semiconductor manufacturing, and supports New York State's efforts to grow the semiconductor industry. Empire State Development is providing up to $7 million in Excelsior Jobs Program tax credits to support Corning's expansion.

title: Advanced Semiconductor Packaging Market to Generate US$ 40.3 Billion in Revenue by 2031, Driven by the Surge in Demand for Miniaturized Electronics | Latest Report by TMR
summary: The advanced semiconductor packaging market, valued at US$ 30.1 billion in 2022, is projected to reach US$ 40.3 billion by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 5.2%. This growth is fueled by increasing demand for compact, high-performance electronic devices, driven by innovations in packaging technologies like 3D and wafer-level packaging, and the rising adoption of 5G, IoT, and AI applications. Key drivers include miniaturization trends, 5G technology, IoT and edge computing, automotive electronics, and AI/high-performance computing, with major companies investing in R&D and strategic partnerships.

title: Tesla Price Target Bumped To $500 By Bank With 120x P/E Multiple Stemming From “Nebulous” Business
summary: Investment bank Piper Sandler has raised its price target for Tesla shares to $500, citing the company's potential in "real-world" AI and future opportunities like Optimus robots, despite a mixed performance in 2024. The bank acknowledges that immediate financial estimates for Tesla are highly uncertain due to the nascent nature of these business segments, leading them to use a 120x fiscal year 2026 P/E valuation.

title: Baya Systems raises $36M to transform AI chiplet design
summary: Baya Systems, a system-on-chip design startup, has secured $36 million in Series B funding to advance its software for the emerging "chiplet" economy. The company aims to address challenges in AI chiplet design by developing a "revolutionary" chiplet-optimized network-on-chip (NoC) interconnect solution. This technology creates a unified fabric for NoC-to-NoC communications, improving efficiency and flexibility for complex multi-die designs required by AI.

title: Joby vs. Archer Aviation: Which Is the Better Buy in 2025?
summary: This article compares Joby Aviation and Archer Aviation, two leading eVTOL companies, to determine which is the better investment for 2025. It examines their investor backing, partnerships, financial profiles, and market valuations, concluding that Archer Aviation holds a slight edge due to its more bullish growth profile and reasonable valuation.

title: C-RAN Market Size to Hit USD 293.84 Billion by 2035
summary: The global C-RAN market is projected to grow from USD 26.22 billion in 2025 to USD 293.84 billion by 2035, exhibiting a CAGR of 27.34%. This significant growth is driven by the increasing demand for 5G networks, cloud computing, IoT, and smart devices, alongside government initiatives for digital transformation. Asia Pacific currently dominates the market, with North America expected to show the highest CAGR, propelled by advancements in AI and network optimization.

title: Intel 2024 revenue dips amid corporate overhaul
summary: Intel's annual revenue for 2024 fell by 2% year over year to $53.1 billion, with its internal foundry business incurring a $13 billion loss, though the company aims for it to break even by late 2027. Amidst these financial challenges, Intel is undergoing a corporate overhaul, including a search for a new CEO after Pat Gelsinger stepped down. The company is also implementing cost-cutting measures, such as a 15% workforce reduction, and navigating market volatility caused by new tariffs while striving to compete in the AI chip sector.

title: Intel Announces Another 58 Layoffs For Folsom Campus
summary: Intel has announced an additional 58 layoffs at its Folsom campus, continuing its cost-cutting measures due to stagnant sales and increased competition. This new round of job cuts, expected by March 31, follows previous significant reductions, with the Folsom workforce decreasing from over 6,000 in 2018 to under 4,000. The company is also planning to sell its 150-acre Folsom campus in a sale-leaseback deal as part of its broader restructuring efforts and aims to cut approximately 15,000 jobs globally.

title: US chipmaker GlobalFoundries names new CEO
summary: GlobalFoundries, the world's third-largest contract chipmaker, has appointed Tim Breen as its new chief executive officer, succeeding Thomas Caulfield. Breen has been with the company since 2018 and previously served as chief operating officer. Caulfield will transition to executive chairman, and Niels Anderskouv will take over as president and COO, overseeing manufacturing and product strategy.

title: The Nvidia Way: How Nvidia Became the Most Valuable Company in History
summary: This article reviews "The Nvidia Way: Jensen Huang and the Making of a Tech Giant" by Tae Kim, detailing Nvidia's journey to becoming the world's most valuable company. It highlights CEO Jensen Huang's leadership, the company's commitment to excellence, strategic hiring, and generous stock programs as key factors. The book emphasizes that Nvidia's success was not a fluke but the result of foresight, hard work, and perseverance through early struggles, particularly in anticipating and capitalizing on the AI revolution.

title: AIST and Intel Strengthen Collaboration for Industrialization of Silicon Quantum Computers
summary: The National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology (AIST) and Intel Corporation have signed an MOU to enhance their collaboration in industrializing quantum technology, particularly silicon quantum computers. This partnership aims to leverage AIST's G-QuAT research capabilities and Intel's semiconductor processes to develop a system with tens of thousands of qubits by the early 2030s. Their focus areas include material optimization, implementation techniques, advanced refrigeration, qubit integration, and cryogenic electronics for large-scale quantum computing.

title: Is Intel now a Trump stock? Its shares surge after upbeat comments by J.D. Vance.
summary: Shares of Intel Corp. surged by 6% after Vice President J.D. Vance made bullish comments about semiconductor manufacturing in the U.S. at an AI summit in Paris. This positive outlook on U.S. chip making also benefited GlobalFoundries Inc., whose shares also jumped over 6%. The article suggests a potential political alignment for Intel's stock given Vance's comments.

title: Lattice Semiconductor Stock Rises as Q1 Projections Top Estimates
summary: Lattice Semiconductor shares rose after the company announced new CFO Lorenzo Flores and released Q1 projections that exceeded analyst estimates. While Q4 revenue narrowly beat expectations, EPS fell short. CEO Ford Tamer noted signs of improvement in the broader market, contributing to a positive outlook for the company despite a year-over-year decline in Q4 revenue and EPS.

title: Intel’s stock-market run is unlike anything it’s seen in more than two decades
summary: Intel's stock has experienced its most significant three-day percentage gain since April 2001, rising 17.7% in three days, including a 7.2% jump on Wednesday. This rally is attributed to a favorable political environment and positive analyst commentary regarding its CPU business.

title: Trade Desk Stock, AppLovin, Intel, Tesla, Robinhood, Deere, MGM, and More Movers
summary: Stocks, including Trade Desk, AppLovin, Intel, Tesla, Robinhood, Deere, and MGM, saw movement on Thursday. This occurred despite persistent wholesale inflation in January and President Donald Trump's directive to propose reciprocal tariffs.

title: The Intel Challenge and Trump’s Foolhardy Plans for Semiconductor Tariffs
summary: This article discusses Intel's ongoing struggles as a semiconductor "national champion" despite government subsidies from the CHIPS and Science Act. It proposes strategies for a new Trump administration, including encouraging Intel to separate its chip design from manufacturing and avoiding tariffs on critical semiconductor imports from companies like TSMC. The author argues that Trump's proposed tariffs are "foolhardy and dangerous" given the US reliance on foreign manufacturers for advanced chips.

title: After Earnings, Is Advanced Micro Devices Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) reported strong fourth-quarter results with growth in client and data center revenue, though its AI GPU growth forecast was disappointing compared to competitor Nvidia. Morningstar has cut its fair value estimate for AMD to $140 per share from $160 due to less optimism about AI GPU growth, but still considers the stock undervalued. The company holds a narrow economic moat based on intangible assets in chip design and expects significant long-term growth, particularly in the data center segment for AI applications.

title: INTW Stock Fund Price and Chart — NASDAQ:INTW
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the GraniteShares 2x Long INTC Daily ETF (INTW), including its price, chart, key statistics like AUM and expense ratio, and investment strategy. It highlights that INTW is a short-term tactical tool aiming for 2x the daily price return of Intel Corporation stock and provides technical analysis and frequently asked questions about the ETF.

title: Silicon Valley Conquers the Pentagon as Defense Tech Explodes
summary: The article discusses the growing influence of Silicon Valley in defense technology, driven by a "new red Cold War" centered on AI. It highlights how companies like Palantir are outperforming traditional defense contractors and how the U.S. and Europe are increasing investment in defense tech. The author suggests investment opportunities in AI-defense intersection companies, domestic semiconductor manufacturers, and emerging defense tech firms.

title: Micron Redefines Performance for AI PCs, Gamers and Professionals
summary: Micron Technology announced the new Micron 4600 PCIe Gen5 NVMe SSD, designed to significantly boost performance for AI PCs, gamers, creators, and professionals. Leveraging Micron G9 TLC NAND, this SSD delivers sequential read speeds of 14.5 GB/s and write speeds of 12.0 GB/s, effectively halving AI model loading times compared to Gen4 SSDs and offering improved energy efficiency. The 4600 SSD is compatible with leading processors from AMD and Intel and integrates advanced security features.

title: Going Into Earnings, Is Nvidia Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar assesses Nvidia (NVDA) stock ahead of its Q4 earnings report, maintaining a "fairly valued" rating with a long-term fair value estimate of $130 per share. The analysis highlights the critical role of Nvidia's data center business, its wide economic moat due to GPU technology and CUDA software, and the "Very High" uncertainty rating given competitive pressures and market dynamics. Despite potential slowdown concerns and competition, Morningstar projects robust growth for Nvidia's data center revenue in the near-to-medium term.

title: GraniteShares 2x Long INTC Daily ETF (NASDAQ: INTW) - Share Price
summary: This article provides the current share price and historical performance data for the GraniteShares 2x Long INTC Daily ETF (NASDAQ: INTW). As of the article, INTW's share price is $57.45, reflecting a 7.79% increase from seven days prior. The report includes detailed daily share price history and performance over various periods, showing significant movements.

title: Chip design software maker Cadence revenue skyrockets with AI demand
summary: Cadence Design Systems reported strong Q4 2024 revenues, up 27% to $1.36 billion, driven by surging demand for AI-driven chip design software and expanded partnerships with major chipmakers like Nvidia and Intel. Despite robust growth and a 2024 fiscal year revenue of $4.6 billion, the company's 2025 financial outlook fell below Wall Street expectations due to uncertainties in its China business amid evolving trade restrictions. Cadence expects flat growth in China for 2025 and plans to be prudent with its guidance given the market's unpredictability.

title: F5 unveils ADC 3.0 to enhance AI application security | SC Media
summary: F5 has launched ADC 3.0, a new framework designed to improve application delivery and security for AI-powered, hybrid, and multicloud environments. This initiative addresses the security gaps in AI adoption among enterprises, with ADC 3.0 integrating high-performance load balancing and advanced security to tackle AI-related risks like model theft and data poisoning. F5 has also partnered with Nvidia, Intel, and NetApp to enhance AI application delivery.

title: Apple’s $500B US expansion includes chips from TSMC Phoenix
summary: Apple pledged $500 billion for US expansion, including sourcing chips from TSMC's Phoenix factory, where mass production began recently. This investment is set to create 20,000 new US jobs and expand manufacturing and data centers across several states. Former President Trump and the Biden administration both claim credit for stimulating domestic chip production, with Trump citing tariffs and Biden highlighting the CHIPS and Science Act.

title: Apple Plans Houston Plant to Produce AI Servers
summary: Apple Inc. plans to establish a new 250,000-sq-ft manufacturing facility in Houston, Texas, to produce servers for its Apple Intelligence and Private Cloud Compute AI services, with an expected opening next year. This initiative is part of a larger $500 billion investment in the U.S. over four years, including expanding data centers and doubling its "Advanced Manufacturing Fund" to $10 billion. The move comes as companies face new tariffs on goods imported from China and indicates a shift towards more U.S.-based manufacturing, potentially with Foxconn identified as the manufacturer for the Houston plant.

title: Micron starts shipping 1γ DRAMs ...
summary: Micron Technology has announced it has begun shipping 1γ (1-gamma) 16Gbit DDR5 DRAMs to partners and customers, claiming to be the first to reach this sixth generation of DRAM process technology. This new memory offers up to a 15 percent speed increase and more than 20 percent power reduction compared to its predecessor, with a 30 percent increase in bits-per-wafer, though it requires EUV lithography. Micron plans to integrate 1γ across its DRAM range for various applications, including data centers, AI-PCs, mobile products, and automotive.

title: Navitas Semiconductor Full Year 2024 Earnings: Misses Expectations
summary: Navitas Semiconductor (NASDAQ:NVTS) reported its Full Year 2024 earnings, showing a revenue of US$83.3m, an increase of 4.8% from FY 2023, and a net loss of US$84.6m, a 42% reduction from the previous year. Both revenue and earnings per share missed analyst estimates by 1.3% and 17% respectively. The company's shares dropped 28% in the past week, and analysts forecast a 21% annual revenue growth for the next three years.

title: Focus on Execution as Synopsys Reports Solid 1Q FY 2025 Financial Results
summary: Synopsys reported solid 1Q FY 2025 financial results, with revenue of $1.455 billion andGAAP EPS of $1.89, demonstrating strong performance driven by design activity and its قیادت in various technology sectors. The company's focus on hardware-assisted verification, EDA software, and AI innovations, particularly for 2nm projects, positions it for continued growth. Synopsys expects 2Q FY 2025 revenue between $1.585 billion and $1.615 billion, anticipating a 10.0% year-over-year growth.

title: MWC 2025: Intel Showcases Foundational Network Infrastructure with Xeon 6
summary: At MWC 2025, Intel presented its Xeon 6 system-on-chip (SoC) with integrated AI, designed to modernize network infrastructure. The new SoC offers significant improvements in RAN capacity, performance-per-watt, and AI RAN performance compared to previous generations, aiming to reduce total cost of ownership for operators. Intel is collaborating with over 50 partners and customers, including Vodafone, AT&T, Samsung, and Verizon, to accelerate the adoption of these AI-powered networking solutions and drive sustainability in the telecommunications industry.

title: BioXcel Therapeutics Soars: Analyzing Sustained Gains
summary: BioXcel Therapeutics saw its stock soar by over 108% following its regaining compliance with Nasdaq's minimum closing bid price rule. This compliance has significantly boosted investor confidence, despite the company's financial results showing substantial operating losses and negative equity. While the market response is optimistic, the article highlights the importance of strategic improvements and operational efficiency to translate this momentum into sustainable financial viability.

title: Top AI Execs to Watch in 2025: Intel Corp.’s Melvin Greer
summary: Melvin Greer, Fellow & Chief Data Scientist at Intel Corp., is a top AI executive to watch in 2025. His recent achievements include developing strategies for prompt engineering to improve generative AI tools and reduce risks for Intel's customers. Greer is focused on technical achievement, expanding technical leadership, and maturing AI adoption strategies to solve complex business problems.

title: HPE boss optimistic Juniper deal will happen, Wi-Fi and all
summary: HPE CEO Antonio Neri remains optimistic about the company's acquisition of Juniper Networks, despite a lawsuit from the US Department of Justice to block the deal. Neri believes the DoJ has misunderstood the acquisition by narrowly focusing on the wireless local area network (WLAN) market, and he insists that divestment of Juniper's Mist Wi-Fi assets is "unacceptable." He argues the merger will create a strong competitor to Huawei outside the US and is confident in HPE's legal case, with a trial set for July 9.

title: Trump calls Chips Act that funds Ohio Intel plant 'a horrible, horrible' thing
summary: Donald Trump has called for the repeal of the $52 billion Chips and Science Act, despite it funding projects like the Intel plant in Ohio and being critical for U.S. national and economic security. Trump argues that tariffs would be a better approach and has indicated that import levies on chips could be implemented soon, leading to uncertainty for companies that have committed to multibillion-dollar projects based on the Act's incentives. While many lawmakers and companies view the agreements as ironclad, a review by the Commerce Secretary indicates potential modifications or challenges to existing terms.

title: Intel Products Unveils Assured Supply Chain Program
summary: Intel Products has launched the Intel® Assured Supply Chain (ASC) program, offering enhanced transparency and assurance in silicon manufacturing. This program provides a digitally attestable chain of custody for chips, focusing on dedicated manufacturing pathways through specific Intel locations. Initially available for select Intel® Core™ Ultra (Series 2) mobile and desktop SKUs, it aims to protect customers from counterfeit-related hardware risks and supply chain vulnerabilities.

title: Malaysia-Arm Deal To Drive Semiconductor Growth
summary: Malaysia's partnership with United Kingdom-based Arm Holdings Plc (ARM) involves a US$250 million investment to develop proprietary intellectual property (IP) for integrated circuit (IC) design, aiming to elevate Malaysia into a high-value economy and foster local semiconductor firm growth. This collaboration, believed to be the world's first between a nation and a technology giant for IP development, is expected to accelerate the formation of front-end semiconductor firms and help achieve a semiconductor export target of RM1.2 trillion by 2030. Industry analysts have offered mixed outlooks, with some maintaining 'neutral' stances due to current market conditions but acknowledging long-term positive spillover effects for the technology sector.

title: Is ON Semiconductor Corporation (ON) the Most Undervalued Semiconductor Stock to Buy Now?
summary: The article discusses the semiconductor industry's projected growth and key trends for 2025, highlighting ON Semiconductor Corporation (ON) as a potentially undervalued stock. It details ON's financial standing, analyst ratings, and strategic initiatives, placing it fourth on a list of most undervalued semiconductor stocks based on hedge fund sentiment and forward P/E ratios.

title: Point72 buys 5% stake in Mobileye
summary: US hedge fund Point72 Asset Management has acquired a 5% stake in Mobileye Global Inc (Nasdaq: MBLY), causing the Israeli advanced driving assistance systems company's share price to rise 5.2% on Wall Street. This investment, valued at $78 million, is seen as a positive development for Mobileye, despite a recent 1% weekly decline in its share price and a 20.4% revenue fall in 2024. Mobileye anticipates a return to growth in the current year.

title: AMAT - Applied Materials to Ride the AI Boom with a 7% Revenue Growth Outlook
summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) is projected to achieve 7-12% CAGR revenue growth, primarily driven by the expanding AI and high-performance computing sectors. Despite challenges like trade restrictions and industry cyclicality, AMAT's leadership in semiconductor manufacturing equipment and advanced technologies positions it for sustained demand. The company anticipates profit margin expansion to around 30% due to R&D efficiencies and advanced technology needs.

title: Valens Semiconductor to Showcase High-Performance Connectivity Innovations for AI Applications at Embedded World 2025
summary: Valens Semiconductor will present its high-performance connectivity technology for AI applications at Embedded World 2025, featuring collaborations with various companies to demonstrate solutions based on Valens' standard-compliant chipsets. The showcase will highlight MIPI A-PHY and HDBaseT USB3 standards, offering flexible and efficient connectivity for robotics and extended USB 3.2 product lines. Valens will also have speaking engagements on connectivity innovations in industrial and medical sectors.

title: Super Micro Computer, Inc. - Supermicro Brings Superior Performance and Efficiency to AI at the Edge
summary: Supermicro is expanding its range of AI-optimized servers, introducing new systems for edge and embedded workloads that leverage Intel Xeon 6 and Intel Core Ultra processors. These new servers are designed to deliver enhanced performance, efficiency, and real-time AI inferencing capabilities from the data center to the far edge. The offerings include solutions for telecom, networking, IoT, and enterprise edge use cases, supporting higher core counts, increased memory bandwidth, and integrated AI acceleration.

title: Intel found someone brave enough to be its new CEO—but is Lip-Bu Tan a change maker or more of the same?
summary: Intel has appointed Lip-Bu Tan as its new CEO, a move that prompted a 10% jump in stock price. Tan, a semiconductor industry veteran and former CEO of Cadence Design Systems, brings a unique blend of design and manufacturing expertise. However, the author questions if he will truly shake up Intel's long-standing ambivalence regarding its identity and strategic direction, or if his appointment is merely a safe "Goldilocks" choice that attempts to please everyone without committing to radical change.

title: Intel appoints Lip-Bu Tan as CEO to orchestrate turnaround at struggling chipmaker, stock jumps 12%
summary: Intel has appointed Lip-Bu Tan as its new CEO, with the stock jumping 12% on the news. Tan, formerly CEO of Cadence Design Systems and a former Intel board member, is tasked with turning around the struggling chipmaker after a tumultuous period under previous leadership. His appointment aims to accelerate Intel's recovery amidst declining sales and increasing competition, particularly in the AI market where Nvidia has a strong lead.

title: Intel’s new CEO brings ‘immediate credibility’ on Wall Street but warns employees of more ‘hard decisions’
summary: Lip-Bu Tan has been named the new CEO of Intel, bringing "immediate credibility" to the company on Wall Street due to his extensive experience in the semiconductor industry. However, Tan warned employees of more "hard decisions" ahead, causing some concern among staff demoralized by previous job cuts. Investors, meanwhile, reacted positively to the appointment, with Intel's shares rising significantly.

title: Analysts Offer Insights on Technology Companies: Intel (INTC), Adobe (ADBE) and SentinelOne (S)
summary: Analysts have provided new ratings for technology companies Intel (INTC), Adobe (ADBE), and SentinelOne (S). Intel received a "Hold" rating from TD Cowen and an upgrade to "Hold" from Bank of America Securities, with price targets indicating a modest upside. Adobe and SentinelOne both received "Buy" ratings from analysts at BMO Capital and Loop Capital Markets, respectively, with their price targets suggesting significant upside potential for investors.

title: Silicon Photonics Modules Market: Driving Innovation in High-Speed Communication with Intel, Cisco, IBM
summary: HTF Market Intelligence has published a comprehensive study on the Global Silicon Photonics Modules Market, forecasting its growth from USD 1.29 billion in 2022 at a CAGR of 25.8% from 2024 to 2032. The market, driven by high data demand and energy consumption reduction, focuses on integrating photonic devices onto silicon chips for high-speed data transmission in telecommunications and data centers. Key players include Cisco Systems, Intel Corporation, and IBM.

title: Tesla launches free trial of assisted driving service in China
summary: Tesla is offering a free trial of its Full Self-Driving (FSD) service in China from March 17 to April 16. This initiative aims to expand FSD's reach in the country, with Tesla collaborating with Baidu to enhance the system's performance. Unlike in the U.S., Tesla faces data localization challenges in China which limit its ability to train the FSD system using local EV data.

title: Dell’s new RTX Pro AI PC boasts an ‘unlimited turbo’ mode
summary: Dell has announced its new "Pro Max" lineup of workstation desktops and laptops, featuring Nvidia's RTX Pro Blackwell GPUs and an innovative "unlimited turbo" mode for sustained high performance in its Tower T2 desktop. This series targets AI developers and power users, offering advanced cooling, large displays, and significant memory and storage capacities, including options with tandem OLED screens and modular USB-C ports. The range will include both Intel Core Ultra Series 2 and AMD Threadripper CPUs, with some models shipping in March and others in July.

title: Speeding AI and HPC Workloads with Composable Memory and Hardware Compression / Decompression Acceleration
summary: This article discusses how composable memory and hardware-accelerated compression/decompression can significantly improve efficiency and reduce the total cost of ownership (TCO) for AI and HPC workloads in data centers. It highlights the collaboration between Rambus and ZeroPoint Technologies to address the high cost of memory and the issue of "stranded memory" by using CXL-enabled disaggregated memory pools combined with hardware-based data compression. This approach aims to reduce memory requirements and free up XPU cycles, ultimately speeding up workloads.

title: Broadcom Defeats Juniper, F5 Vanquishes Eaton In Round 1: Channel Madness
summary: The first round of CRN's 2025 Channel Madness tournament has concluded, revealing the winners and losers across various regions. Notable outcomes include Broadcom's Tara Fine narrowly defeating Juniper Networks' Gordon Mackintosh and F5's Lisa Citron triumphing over Eaton's Steve Loeb. Other key advancements include Cloudflare's Tom Evans and IBM's Kate Woolley, setting the stage for the Solutions 16 round with new matchups defined.

title: Great Shots: Lip-Bu Tan Joins Intel as CEO
summary: Lip-Bu Tan officially began his tenure as Intel’s ninth CEO on March 18, 2025, a week after his appointment. His first day involved receiving his badge, meeting employees, and engaging with the leadership team at Intel's Santa Clara headquarters. Tan expressed excitement about leading the company into its next era despite acknowledging challenges.

title: From Demo to Dream: Intel PCs Give African Entrepreneurs New Life
summary: Intel is donating retired demo PCs to the Amsha Africa Foundation, providing critical technology to young African entrepreneurs. This initiative, spearheaded by Intel's Tony Abuta and Nick Blair, gives these laptops a second life and helps individuals in Africa access online job opportunities and essential digital tools. The program has already seen success in Kenya, with more donations planned to empower talent in underrepresented communities.

title: GCT Semiconductor Holding, Inc. Provides Business Update and Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Financial Results
summary: GCT Semiconductor Holding, Inc. announced its "202 5G CT" program, signaling the "Year of 5G" with forthcoming 5G chipset shipments in the first half of 2025. The company reported a net loss of $5.0 million for Q4 2024 and $12.4 million for the full year 2024, emphasizing a shift towards 5G chipset sales for future growth. GCT Semiconductor also highlighted significant debt reduction and ongoing discussions for capital funding.

title: Beijing Yunji Tech Applies for Chapter 18C Listing, Backed by TENCENT & BABA-W
summary: Beijing Yunji Technology, a robotics company, has applied for a main board listing on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under Chapter 18C, with CITIC Securities and CCBI as joint sponsors. The company focuses on composite multi-modal robots and embodied intelligence, having deployed robots in over 30,000 locations worldwide and generating RMB245 million in revenue last year. Significant pre-IPO investors include Alibaba, Tencent, and Ctrip.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Can Double in Price: Here’s Why
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is projected to double in price due to its increasing market share against NVIDIA in GPU and CPU markets, driven by lower-cost, better-performing products. Insider transactions, strong institutional buying, and an undervalued stock price relative to its growth potential support this optimistic outlook. The company is actively addressing its software infrastructure gap to further compete with NVIDIA.

title: How Sustainable Is US Exceptionalism?
summary: US stocks have significantly outperformed global markets for 17 years, but an analysis suggests that much of this "exceptionalism" is due to expanding valuations and a strengthening dollar, rather than superior earnings growth. European policy changes, including fiscal stimulus and regulatory reforms, along with a declining share of the USD in global reserves, could shift this balance and favor international equities in the future. Investors are advised to consider international diversification to mitigate risk and capture potential opportunities.

title: Supermicro Ships Over 20 New Systems that Redefine Single-Socket Performance and Deliver Data Center Power, Space, and Cost Savings
summary: Supermicro announced the availability of over 20 new single-socket server systems supporting Intel Xeon 6 with P-Cores, designed to reduce data center power, space, and cost. These systems offer performance previously requiring dual-socket configurations, providing advantages like increased core counts, faster memory, and enhanced PCIe 5.0 lanes for diverse workloads. The new servers aim to optimize TCO and environmental impact across various applications from cloud computing to AI.

title: ASP Isotopes Begins Commercial Production of Enriched Silicon-28 for Quantum and Semiconductor Applications
summary: ASP Isotopes Inc. has started commercial production of enriched Silicon-28 at its facility in Pretoria, South Africa, with initial shipments expected in Q2 2025 to U.S.-based customers. Silicon-28 is crucial for quantum computing due to its spin-free nature which aids in protecting quantum information, and for advanced semiconductors due to its superior thermal conductivity for AI applications. The company utilizes a proprietary Aerodynamic Separation Process (ASP) technology to directly enrich silane, promising higher purity material compared to competitors.

title: 12 companies building quantum computers
summary: This article highlights 12 leading companies, including Alice & Bob, Amazon, D-Wave Systems, and Google, that are actively developing quantum computing hardware and software. It details their key contributions, technologies, and roadmaps towards making quantum computing widely available and fault-tolerant. The article also discusses the potential impact of quantum computing on various industries and the challenges that still need to be overcome.

title: Game-Changing News for Advanced Micro Devices
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is integrating Rapt.AI into its MI300X, MI325X, and upcoming MI350 GPUs, which is expected to enhance its AI semiconductor capabilities and market share. The company also completed the acquisition of ZT Systems to offer end-to-end AI infrastructure solutions. Despite a recent stock price correction, analysts are still bullish on AMD, seeing it as a deep-value opportunity with significant growth catalysts.

title: Intel and IBM Announce the Availability of Intel Gaudi 3 AI Accelerators on IBM Cloud
summary: IBM has announced the availability of Intel Gaudi 3 AI accelerators on IBM Cloud, aiming to provide better cost performance for AI innovation in public cloud environments. This collaboration intends to help clients more cost-effectively scale and deploy enterprise AI solutions. The accelerators are currently available in certain IBM Cloud regions, with plans for expanded availability and integration with Red Hat OpenShift AI and IBM watsonx.ai software.

title: Jabil Launches 1.6T Pluggable Transceiver to Support Growing Demand for Intra-Data Center and AI Connectivity
summary: Jabil Inc. has launched its new 1.6T pluggable transceivers designed to meet the increasing demand for AI/ML workloads, high-performance computing, cloud computing, and high-speed data center interconnects. These transceivers, built with an Intel® Silicon Photonics engine, can effectively double the bandwidth capacity of data center racks without infrastructure modifications and offer low power consumption. Jabil will be demonstrating this solution and other photonics products at the OFC 2025 conference.

title: Vision Highlights: Intel Spotlights Customer Deployments on the Main Stage
summary: At Intel Vision, executives, customers, and partners showcased real-world applications of Intel technology, emphasizing a customer-centric approach. Christoph Schell outlined three focus areas for Intel, including reinforcing x86 architecture, optimizing organizational structures, and revamping go-to-market strategies. Several companies like EdgeRunner AI, Inflection AI, Kamiwaza AI, and Softtek presented their successful deployments, highlighting the diverse applications and performance efficiencies achieved with Intel processors, AI accelerators, and software tools.

title: Postcard from Vision: A Refreshed Intel Brand Takes Center Stage
summary: Intel unveiled its refreshed brand and new platform, "That's the power of Intel Inside," at the Intel Vision 2025 event in Las Vegas. This initiative aims to highlight Intel's essential role in the world while honoring its popular "Intel Inside" campaign from the 1990s. The company intends to leverage this new brand message to drive differentiation, revenue growth, and strengthen equity with customers and partners.

title: Mobileye selects Valens Semiconductor's VA7000 MIPI A-PHY chipsets for automated and autonomous driving projects
summary: Mobileye has chosen Valens Semiconductor's VA7000 MIPI A-PHY-compliant chipsets for the in-car, sensor-to-compute connectivity infrastructure in its EyeQ™ 6 High automated and autonomous production programs. This collaboration marks a significant advancement for the MIPI A-PHY standard in the automotive industry, validating its high performance and reliability for next-generation ADAS requirements. The VA7000 chipsets were selected after extensive testing, demonstrating superior performance benefits crucial for accurate sensor data delivery in autonomous systems.

title: Semiconductor Industry Outlook 2025
summary: The semiconductor industry is projected for strong growth in 2025, reaching $697 billion, an 11% year-over-year increase, driven by demand in AI and data centers. Key challenges include geopolitical tensions, supply chain disruptions, talent shortages, and cybersecurity threats, while technological advancements in advanced packaging and new materials will be crucial. The industry is expected to allocate $185 billion to capital expenditures to expand manufacturing capacity, aiming for a $1 trillion valuation by 2030.

title: Co-Packaged Optics Market Report 2025-2030 | $2.43B to
summary: The Co-Packaged Optics Market is projected to grow from $2.43 billion in 2025 to $4.67 billion by 2030, exhibiting a CAGR of 13.74%. This growth is driven by the increasing adoption of smart devices, rising data traffic, and the need for high-performance, energy-efficient computing solutions. The report provides a comprehensive analysis of market dynamics, regional insights, competitive landscape, and actionable recommendations for industry leaders.

title: The Impact of New Tariffs on the Machine Vision Industry: Winners, Losers, and What Comes Next
summary: New tariffs by the United States, including a 10% baseline duty and higher rates on key trading partners like China (34%), Vietnam (46%), and the EU (20%), are significantly altering the machine vision industry. This article identifies companies facing increased costs and supply chain disruptions as "losers" and those benefiting from reduced competition and shifts in production as "winners." It meticulously details how these tariffs affect various players, from optics manufacturers to AI software providers, and outlines future trends to watch, such as retaliation, supply chain shifts, and the growth of AI-based vision.

title: Trump's tariffs on chipmaking tools could make U.S.-made processors more expensive
summary: The Trump administration's new import tariffs on wafer fabrication equipment (WFE) are set to increase the cost of chipmaking tools for American companies by 20% to 32%. This move will impact major chipmakers like Intel, GlobalFoundries, Samsung Foundry, and TSMC, potentially making U.S.-made processors more expensive. While U.S. companies dominate about 50% of the chipmaking tool market, essential equipment from European and Asian manufacturers, such as ASML's lithography tools, will now incur significant additional costs, inevitably leading to higher production costs for chips made in America.

title: Globalstar expands leadership leam
summary: Globalstar, Inc. has announced the appointment of two new leaders to head its terrestrial spectrum and network solutions and wholesale satellite capacity businesses. Dr. Tamer Kadous will lead the terrestrial spectrum and network solutions, focusing on private wireless network solutions, while Daaman Hejmadi will lead the wholesale satellite capacity business, aiming to expand access to Globalstar’s satellite services through partnerships. These strategic hires bring extensive experience in wireless communications and aim to bolster Globalstar's growth and market presence.

title: Intel Announces 2025 EPIC Supplier Award Recipients
summary: Intel has announced the recipients of the 2025 Intel EPIC Supplier Award, recognizing suppliers that demonstrate excellence, partnership, inclusion, and continuous improvement. The award highlights the critical role these partners play in advancing Intel's technology roadmap, enhancing supply chain resilience, and enabling Intel Foundry. Over 30 companies were honored across various categories for their exceptional contributions.

title: Intel Stock Price Forecast 2025–2026: INTC Targets $48 After 83% Rally
summary: The article "Intel Stock Price Forecast 2025–2026: INTC Targets $48 After 83% Rally" appears to be mistitled based on its content. The actual article focuses on the Virtus InfraCap U.S. Preferred Stock ETF (PFFA), evaluating its high 10.3% dividend yield and sustainability for income investors. It discusses PFFA's portfolio of preferred stocks, its exposure to the real estate sector, and how a declining interest rate environment could benefit the ETF despite a recent 10% year-to-date drop.

title: Tigress Financial raises Intel stock price target to $52 on AI growth
summary: Tigress Financial Partners has increased its price target for Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) to $52, maintaining a Buy rating due to the company's AI innovation, strategic partnerships, and manufacturing expansion. Intel's return to profitability in Q3, driven by AI accelerator demand and an improved product mix, plus supportive U.S. policies, are expected to fuel multiyear growth. The stock has seen a 75.4% return over the past year and trades at a P/E ratio of 842.35, with 11 analysts revising earnings upwards for the upcoming period.

title: Keysight tools tackle data center deployment efficiency
summary: Keysight Technologies has introduced Keysight Artificial Intelligence (KAI), a suite of tools designed to improve the deployment efficiency of AI data centers. KAI identifies performance bottlenecks in large GPU deployments by emulating workload profiles. The suite aims to accelerate design, development, deployment, and operations for AI providers, semiconductor fabricators, and network equipment manufacturers.

title: HP Holiday Gift Guide 2025: Top Tech Gifts for Everyone | HP® Tech Takes SEO
summary: HP's 2025 Holiday Gift Guide features a wide range of tech products, including desktops, laptops, printers, monitors, and accessories, designed to suit various needs and budgets. The guide highlights specific HP and OMEN models, detailing their features and ideal users, from serious gamers and creative professionals to students and home office workers. It also offers shopping tips, such as checking for deals, protecting purchases with Care Packs, and personalizing gifts based on the recipient's preferences.

title: After Altera, is Intel preparing to sell Mobileye?
summary: Intel's recent sale of a 51% stake in its Altera unit for $4.46 billion under new CEO Lip-Bu Tan signals a broader strategy to shed non-core assets and refocus. This divestment, though a financial loss, provides crucial cash for Intel's recovery. Attention is now shifting to Mobileye, which analysts believe could be Intel's next divestment, allowing the company to concentrate on core operations like custom silicon, AI, and foundry services.

title: Europe FPGA Market Size, Share & Trends, 2033
summary: The Europe Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) market, valued at USD 2.24 billion in 2024, is projected to reach USD 5 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 9.34%. This growth is primarily driven by the expanding adoption of FPGAs in automotive electronics, 5G networks, and AI/machine learning applications, despite challenges like high development costs and design complexity. Germany currently dominates the market, while France and the UK show significant growth potential due to thriving tech and innovation sectors.

title: Nvidia stock dives after chipmaker discloses $5.5 billion hit from 'surprise' China chip controls
summary: Nvidia's stock dropped almost 7% after the company revealed a $5.5 billion hit due to new US government restrictions on its semiconductor exports to China. The restrictions, which require a special license for H20 chips designed for the Chinese market, effectively act as a ban, surprising Wall Street given previous reports of a potential rollback of such measures. Other chipmakers like AMD also saw declines, with analysts questioning the logic of the H20 ban since its performance is considered low compared to existing Chinese alternatives.

title: Intel Accelerates Software-Defined Vehicles with Next-Gen SoC and Partners
summary: Intel unveiled its second-generation AI-enhanced software-defined vehicle (SDV) system-on-chip (SoC) at Auto Shanghai 2025, featuring the automotive industry’s first multi-process node chiplet architecture. The company also announced new strategic collaborations with ModelBest and Black Sesame Technologies to expand its automotive ecosystem. These developments aim to accelerate innovation in AI-powered cockpits, advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS), and energy-efficient vehicle compute platforms, driving the transformation toward intelligent, software-defined vehicles.

title: Intel’s new CEO plans to cut another 20% of its jobs, Bloomberg reports
summary: Intel reportedly plans to eliminate 20,000 or more jobs, following previous layoffs and the installation of a new CEO, Lip-Bu Tan. These cuts aim to address the company's financial struggles and technological missteps, which have led to declining sales and a significant loss. The move could severely impact Oregon, Intel's largest operating hub, but may also be an effort to increase efficiency under the new CEO.

title: Intel to Announce Plans This Week to Cut Over 20% of Staff
summary: Intel Corp. (INTC) is expected to announce plans this week to lay off more than 20% of its staff. This move aims to streamline management and rebuild an engineering-driven culture, marking the first major restructuring under new Chief Executive Officer Lip-Bu Tan. The cuts are intended to eliminate bureaucracy at the struggling chipmaker.

title: Report: Intel to lay off 20%+ of its workforce in efficiency push
summary: Intel Corp. is reportedly planning to lay off over 20% of its workforce, impacting more than 20,000 roles, as part of an efficiency drive led by CEO Lip-Bu Tan. The company's shares rose on the news, which is seen as an effort to streamline management, rebuild an engineering-driven culture, and enhance competitiveness, particularly in AI and chip manufacturing. This move follows previous layoffs and aims to save costs and improve aspects like fab network efficiency and AI processor development.

title: Lip-Bu Tan: Our Path Forward
summary: Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan addressed employees following Q1 2025 results, outlining a path forward that includes becoming a more engineering-focused company, flattening the organizational structure, streamlining processes, and requiring four days per week in the office. He stressed the need for significant change, including cost reductions and a potential workforce reduction, to improve execution and regain industry leadership. Tan expressed confidence in the team's ability to reinvent Intel and achieve a significant comeback.

title: UMC forecasts growth in chip wafer shipments despite tariff uncertainty
summary: United Microelectronics Corp (UMC) anticipates a sequential growth of up to 7% in wafer shipments and a 75% utilization rate for the current quarter, despite uncertainties from US tariff policies. The company's CFO noted that while trade tensions create uncertainty for the latter half of the year, customer ordering behavior has not significantly altered in the short term. UMC also reported its lowest profit in approximately 19 quarters but expects gross margin to rebound, driven by demand in communications, computers, and consumer electronics.

title: Intel Beats on Q1 Earnings, Offers Weak Outlook: ETFs in Focus
summary: Intel reported first-quarter 2025 earnings and revenues that surpassed estimates, but its shares dropped 5.1% after it issued a weak outlook for the current quarter. The company announced new initiatives, including staff cuts and capital expenditure reductions, under its new CEO to improve performance. Investors are advised to monitor ETFs with high Intel allocation, such as FEPI, TINY, CHPS, WISE, and SMH, as potential investment opportunities.

title: Layoffs are coming to major chipmaker Intel, but Oregon impact is unclear
summary: Intel is facing impending layoffs as CEO Lip-Bu Tan announced workforce reductions to improve agility and competitiveness, aiming to shave half a billion dollars in operating expenses. The company, a major employer in Oregon, is working to catch up in the AI technology race after reporting a significant loss of nearly $19 billion last year. Despite federal funding to expand domestic manufacturing, Intel's leadership emphasizes the need for fundamental changes to its internal culture and operations.

title: Alphabet Sets Positive Tone Ahead of Tech Earnings
summary: This article discusses the Q1 earnings season with a focus on big tech companies. While Tesla reported a decline in earnings and revenue, Alphabet surpassed analyst estimates due to strong performance in its search and advertising units, driven by AI investments. Intel also beat estimates but provided weak forward-looking guidance and announced workforce reductions.

title: Arqit delivers quantum-safe protection enhanced by confidential computing
summary: Arqit Quantum Inc. announced the delivery of quantum-safe protection enhanced by confidential computing, powered by Intel Trust Domain Extensions (Intel TDX) and Arqit NetworkSecure™. This solution strengthens data protection in cloud environments by ensuring no third party, including the cloud provider, can access a customer’s encryption keys or workloads. It addresses long-standing cloud security challenges, offering enhanced data sovereignty and secure collaboration across various sectors, including telecom, enterprise, and critical infrastructure.

title: 5 HR pros on the move in April
summary: This article highlights five significant human resources leadership changes that occurred in April. Companies like Associated Banc-Corp, Hyatt Hotels Corp., Mars Petcare, Comviva, and Caterpillar Inc. announced new CHRO appointments, with many seasoned HR professionals stepping into these roles. The movements underscore a dynamic period for HR leadership across various industries.

title: Intel Foundry Gathers Customers and Partners, Outlines Priorities
summary: At its Direct Connect event, Intel Foundry shared significant progress on its core process and advanced packaging technologies, including the introduction of Intel 14A and new variants of Intel 18A. The company highlighted momentum in domestic U.S. manufacturing with its Fab 52, and announced new ecosystem programs and partnerships, such as the Intel Foundry Chiplet Alliance, to foster collaboration and innovation for customers. Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan emphasized the company's commitment to building a world-class foundry by listening to customers and strengthening partnerships.

title: Adani pauses talks with Israel's Tower for $10 billion India chip foray, sources say
summary: The Adani Group has reportedly paused discussions with Israel's Tower Semiconductor for a $10 billion chip manufacturing project in India. Sources indicate that Adani found the venture strategically and commercially unviable due to uncertain demand in India and Tower Semiconductor's insufficient financial commitment to the partnership. This decision marks a potential setback for India's aspirations to become a global chipmaking hub, following a previous collapse of a similar venture between Vedanta and Foxconn.

title: With Intel’s latest layoffs, will the Ohio plant ever be built?
summary: Ohio's top union leader and workers are expressing significant concern after Intel announced further layoffs, casting doubt on the future of its multi-billion-dollar manufacturing plant in Ohio. Despite Intel's stock troubles and reduced workforce, Ohio Governor Mike DeWine remains optimistic the plant will eventually be built, citing the substantial investment already made by Intel. The company itself has acknowledged financial difficulties and a need for change, declining to comment directly on the Ohio project's impact.

title: From Canada to Paraguay: HIVE Digital Technologies’s CEO Aydin Kilic on Global Bitcoin Mining, Trump’s Policies, and the Future of the Electro-Dollar
summary: Aydin Kilic, CEO of HIVE Digital Technologies, discusses the company's strategic shift to international Bitcoin mining, moving its HQ to San Antonio while expanding operations in Paraguay. He highlights the impact of Trump's pro-Bitcoin policies and proposes the "American electro-dollar" concept. Kilic also details HIVE's significant investment in Paraguay's renewable hydro-energy and its community initiatives.

title: Qualcomm Beats Analyst Forecasts
summary: Qualcomm (Nasdaq: QCOM) reported better-than-estimated revenue for its second quarter and provided guidance for the third quarter, projecting $9.9 billion to $10.7 billion in revenue. Despite beating analyst consensus for diluted earnings per share and revenue, shares saw a dip due to Wall Street's cautious outlook regarding economic uncertainty and challenges in global trade. The company highlighted strong performance in automotive chips and IoT, along with a significant increase in smartphone handset revenue.

title: Synopsys and Intel Foundry Collaborate to Advance Angstrom-Scale Chip Designs
summary: Synopsys and Intel Foundry are collaborating to advance angstrom-scale chip designs, focusing on multi-die design innovation. They are providing certified AI-driven digital and analog design flows for Intel 18A and 18A-P process nodes, including support for RibbonFET Gate-all-around transistor architecture and PowerVia backside power delivery. This partnership aims to accelerate the development of highly optimized AI and HPC chip designs by leveraging advanced packaging technologies like Intel's EMIB-T and Synopsys' 3DIC Compiler, alongside a broad portfolio of IP.

title: Chipmakers AMD, GlobalFoundries and Cirrus Logic post solid earnings and guidance, easing tariff fears
summary: U.S. chipmakers AMD, GlobalFoundries, and Cirrus Logic reported strong quarterly earnings and optimistic guidance, easing concerns about the impact of President Donald Trump's tariffs. Despite challenges like new export regulations for AI chips to China affecting AMD, and a slowdown in the smartphone market for GlobalFoundries, all three companies expressed confidence in their ability to navigate current market conditions. GlobalFoundries, in particular, anticipates benefiting from tariffs on foreign-made chips, projecting increased demand for its U.S.-manufactured products.

title: Gold-plated billionaires
summary: Billionaires like John Paulson, Ray Dalio, and Stanley Druckenmiller are increasingly investing in gold, driven by geopolitical instability, trade tensions, central bank buying, and gold's role as an inflation hedge. Despite Warren Buffett and Charles Munger's view of gold as unproductive, these investors see it as a "safe haven" and a hedge against the devaluation of fiat currencies and systemic risks. The trend is also fueled by strategic investments in gold mining companies that offer leveraged exposure to rising gold prices.

title: ASML vs. TSM: Which Semiconductor Stock is the Smarter Buy?
summary: The article compares ASML Holding (ASML) and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM), both critical to the semiconductor industry. It concludes that ASML is currently the smarter investment due to its monopoly on EUV technology and strong earnings growth, despite TSM's impressive growth in AI chips. TSM faces greater geopolitical, cost, and supply-chain risks, reflected in its recent downward earnings revisions and increased capital spending.

title: Winning the AI race: Strengthening U.S. capabilities in computing and innovation
summary: Brad Smith, Vice Chair and President of Microsoft, testified before the Senate Commerce Committee on the critical issue of artificial intelligence, emphasizing three strategic priorities for the U.S. to win the AI race: innovation, diffusion, and exporting AI to allies. He highlighted the need for federal support in expanding datacenters and electrical grids, training a skilled AI workforce, and investing in research and development. The article stresses that winning the AI race requires a robust ecosystem including both private sector efforts and government policies, focusing on both technological advancement and widespread adoption of AI across all sectors.

title: What Happens to the CHIPS Act Now?
summary: The article discusses the uncertain future of the CHIPS Act under a potential Trump presidency, highlighting concerns about renegotiation, tariffs on foreign-made semiconductors, and the potential impact on global manufacturing and the tech sector. It details how the Biden administration leveraged the Act to boost domestic chip production and attract major investments, contrasting this with Trump's view of the Act as wasteful and his preference for avoiding tariffs over subsidies. The piece also touches on efforts to counter China's influence and the potential economic consequences of new tariffs on consumers and the semiconductor industry.

title: Intel’s new CEO ‘isn’t thinking about massive changes’
