summary: Intel's new CEO, Lip-Bu Tan, is focused on organizational restructuring and cost-cutting measures, including job cuts and flattening management layers, rather than a significant change in strategy. The company aims to improve execution and decision-making, which includes increasing engineers' involvement in product design and attracting top talent to rebuild the workforce after past losses. Despite a challenging period with sales decline and a large loss, Intel is committed to its plan of opening its factories to make chips for other companies, aiming for its factory business to break even by 2027.

title: Rambus Launches Client Chipsets for Advanced AI PC Memory Modules
summary: Rambus Inc. has released new client chipsets for next-generation AI PC memory modules, including two new Power Management ICs (PMICs), the PMIC5200 and PMIC5120. These chipsets, which also include a Client Clock Driver and SPD Hub, offer complete solutions for LPDDR5 CAMM2, DDR5 CSODIMMs, and CUDIMMs, addressing the performance and power efficiency demands of AI PCs. Rambus now provides complete memory interface chipsets for all JEDEC standard DDR5 and LPDDR5 memory modules for both server and client applications.

title: Tower Semiconductor Reports 9% Year-over-Year Revenue Growth for Q1 2025 and Maintains Growth Outlook Throughout the Year
summary: Tower Semiconductor announced a 9% year-over-year revenue increase for Q1 2025, reaching $358 million. Despite this, net profit declined to $40 million from $45 million in Q1 2024, partly due to fixed costs from a new facility and a prior non-recurring tax benefit. The company projects Q2 2025 revenues of approximately $372 million and maintains a stable corporate credit rating while focusing on growth in RF infrastructure, power management, and emerging technologies.

title: NetApp’s strategy aligns AI and data with real-time demand
summary: NetApp is focusing on a strategy that aligns AI and data with real-time demand, emphasizing nuance over hype to help enterprises derive meaning from vast amounts of data. CEO George Kurian highlights four key market drivers—Flash, AI, block, and cloud—and a customer-first approach that prioritizes long-term responsibility and innovation. The company's Intelligent Data Infrastructure strategy aims to manage risk, empower AI, ensure performance at scale, and deliver real-time ransomware detection, with applications seen in partnerships with organizations like the San Francisco 49ers and Aston Martin Formula One Team.

title: Applied Materials’ chips are down following revenue miss and light guidance
summary: Applied Materials Inc. reported mixed second-quarter results, with earnings beating expectations but revenue slightly missing estimates at $7.1 billion. The company's guidance for the current quarter also fell short, leading to a more than 5% drop in stock price after hours. Despite the cautious outlook, CEO Gary Dickerson emphasized that AI computing remains a major growth driver and that demand hasn't changed, though investor concerns persist regarding ongoing U.S.-China trade tensions.

title: Intel spars with EU regulators over $421.4 million antitrust fine
summary: U.S. chipmaker Intel is challenging a 376 million euro ($421.4 million) antitrust fine imposed by EU regulators, arguing it is disproportionate and unfair. The fine stems from payments made by Intel to HP, Acer, and Lenovo to delay or halt rival products between 2002 and 2006. Intel's lawyer argued that these were "narrow, tactical moves" and did not represent a market-wide foreclosure strategy, while the EU watchdog maintains the fine is appropriate and not disproportionate.

title: Applied Materials’ sales slip
summary: Applied Materials Inc., a leading US chip-manufacturing equipment maker, provided a cautious forecast for the current quarter, reflecting concerns about the US trade dispute with China. The company's sales are projected to be around US$7.2 billion, with profit at roughly US$2.35 per share. Despite market uncertainties and evolving export restrictions impacting sales to China, Applied Materials reported strong fiscal second-quarter results and noted continued high demand for AI-related processor equipment.

title: Computex: Intel Unveils New GPUs for AI and Workstations
summary: Intel announced new GPUs and AI accelerators at Computex 2025, including the Intel Arc Pro B-Series GPUs for professional workstations and AI inference, and Intel Gaudi 3 AI accelerators in PCIe and rack scale systems. The company also launched the Intel AI Assistant Builder on GitHub, enabling developers to create localized AI agents. These announcements coincide with Intel's 40th anniversary of collaboration with ecosystem partners in Taiwan.

title: Intel Gaudi 3 Expands Availability to Drive AI Innovation at Scale
summary: Dell's AI platform, featuring Intel Gaudi 3 AI accelerators, is now available to help enterprises with AI adoption. This end-to-end solution combines Intel Gaudi 3 hardware with an open-source software stack and Dell's infrastructure, offering high performance, flexibility, and cost-efficiency for various AI workloads. The platform boasts competitive generative AI price-performance, a scalable architecture, and an optimized environment for developers and IT teams.

title: Extreme bolsters AI support for its integrated network and security platform
summary: Extreme Networks has enhanced its Platform ONE, an integrated network and security management system, with new AI-driven features. These additions, AI Canvas and Service AI Agent, aim to simplify network and security operations, reduce manual tasks by 90%, and cut resolution times by up to 98% through automated diagnostics and natural language processing. The platform integrates Extreme's wired, wireless, and security products with AI and analytics, offering a unified dashboard for efficient control of distributed resources.

title: Press Kit: Intel at Computex 2025
summary: Intel celebrated 40 years of collaboration with the Taiwan ecosystem at Computex 2025 by announcing new Intel® Arc™ Pro B-Series GPUs, new deployment capabilities for Intel® Gaudi® 3 AI accelerators, and the availability of Intel® AI Assistant Builder on GitHub. The company emphasized its dedication to advancing computing architectures for AI, data centers, client workloads, prosumers, workstations, and edge computing. Intel also showcased Project Battlematrix, a configurable workstation platform designed to reduce AI developer friction points, and gave a sneak peek at its upcoming Panther Lake processors.

title: New Intel Xeon 6 CPUs to Maximize GPU-Accelerated AI Performance
summary: Intel has announced three new Xeon 6 series CPUs with Performance-cores designed to enhance GPU-accelerated AI systems. These processors feature Priority Core Turbo technology and Intel Speed Select Technology – Turbo Frequency to optimize performance for demanding AI workloads. One of these new Xeon 6 processors, the Intel Xeon 6776P, is already integrated into NVIDIA's latest DGX B300 AI systems.

title: North America’s chip supply chain needs Canada, whatever Trump says
summary: Canada's critical role in the North American semiconductor supply chain is highlighted through the specialized pellicle production at Teledyne's Bromont plant and IBM's packaging facility in the same area. Despite "America First" rhetoric from the Trump administration, Canadian hardware executives argue that Canada should focus on its niche strengths, such as packaging, sensor production, and optical connectors, to complement rather than directly compete with U.S. chip manufacturing efforts. This strategy aims to preserve and develop Canadian intellectual property and expertise within the broader semiconductor ecosystem.

title: SQQQ vs. SPXS: Which Inverse ETF Is a Better Buy?
summary: This article compares two triple-leveraged inverse ETFs, ProShares UltraPro Short QQQ (SQQQ) and Direxion Daily S&P 500 Bear 3X Shares (SPXS), for bearish investors. It explains that these funds are designed for short-term trading due to daily rebalancing and discusses their expense ratios and underlying indexes. The author generally prefers SPXS due to its lower expense ratio and the S&P 500's greater diversification, making it comparatively less risky than SQQQ which tracks the more volatile NASDAQ 100.

title: Intel, Micron, Qualcomm, TI join call for chip tariff exemptions ...
summary: US chip giants Intel, Micron, Qualcomm, and Texas Instruments have asked the US Department of Commerce for semiconductor import tariff exemptions or lighter burdens. Their submissions are a response to a "Section 232" investigation into semiconductor imports, coming after TSMC also appealed to the Trump administration. The companies, while supporting reshoring, warn that poorly designed tariffs could harm US interests and the complex semiconductor supply chain.

title: HP shares fall over 7% as earnings miss and tariffs weigh on outlook
summary: HP Inc. shares fell after the company missed earnings expectations for its fiscal second quarter of 2025 and issued a cautious outlook. The miss was attributed to increasing costs from U.S. tariffs and macroeconomic uncertainties, despite revenue surpassing analyst estimates. HP also highlighted its strategic focus on AI, evidenced by the acquisition of assets from Humane Inc. and the launch of new AI-powered products.

title: 9th Circuit Affirms Dismissal of Long-Running Intel Investment Lawsuit
summary: The 9th Circuit Court of Appeals has affirmed the dismissal of a lawsuit against Intel Corp. fiduciaries regarding their retirement plan investments. The court ruled that the plaintiffs failed to plausibly allege a breach of fiduciary duties under ERISA concerning the inclusion of alternative investments like hedge funds and private equity funds. This decision provides some reassurance for plan sponsors considering private market investments in defined contribution plans, though caution remains due to the extended nature of such litigation.

title: UMC warns of risks from NT dollar’s appreciation
summary: United Microelectronics Corp (UMC) has warned that the appreciation of the New Taiwan dollar poses a significant threat to its revenue and profitability in the second half of this year. For every 1% the NT dollar gains against the US dollar, where UMC's revenue is booked, its gross margin drops by 0.4 percentage points. The company is focusing on 22-nanometer technology adoption, specialized services, and a joint 12-nanometer development with Intel to mitigate these impacts and differentiate itself from competitors.

title: Malaysia's delicate balancing act
summary: Malaysia is facing increasing pressure to abandon its neutral stance amidst the escalating US-China tech war, particularly concerning semiconductor supply chains. The US's efforts to reshore chip production are reconfiguring the global industry, impacting Malaysia, which is a significant semiconductor exporter. A recent incident involving Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching and Huawei’s Ascend chips highlighted the difficulties Malaysia faces in maintaining its neutrality given geopolitical tensions.

title: Intel-Backed AI PC Helps Cut Eye Lesion Detection from Weeks to Seconds
summary: An Intel-backed AI PC solution developed by Acer Medical, featuring Intel Core Ultra processors and OpenVINO toolkits, dramatically reduces the time for eye lesion detection from weeks to seconds. This technology, developed with Dr. Hsieh Yi-Ting from National Taiwan University Hospital, allows for early diagnosis of conditions like diabetic retinopathy and age-related macular degeneration, improving patient care and accessibility, especially in remote areas. The AI-powered tools enhance doctor productivity and act as a valuable aid for training non-specialist medical professionals.

title: GlobalFoundries boosts investment plans to $16 billion, with research focus
summary: GlobalFoundries announced plans to increase its investment to $16 billion, allocating an additional $1 billion to capital spending and $3 billion to research in emerging chip technologies. This expansion is driven by the booming artificial intelligence hardware market and is expected to support factory expansions in New York and Vermont, as well as significant research in chip packaging, silicon photonics, and gallium nitride. The company is also collaborating with the Trump administration to fortify the U.S. chip manufacturing supply chain.

title: Top 10: Autonomous Vehicle Companies
summary: This article ranks the top 10 autonomous vehicle companies that are revolutionizing the transport industry through significant investments in self-driving technology. Key players like Waymo, NVIDIA, Tesla, and Mercedes-Benz are highlighted for their contributions to improving safety, scaling operations, and enhancing vehicle connectivity. The companies are advancing different levels of automation, from advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) to fully autonomous robotaxis.

title: GlobalFoundries plans to spend US$16 billion to boost US chip production
summary: GlobalFoundries, the largest US-based provider of made-to-order chips, plans to invest US$16 billion to increase domestic chip production, with US$13 billion allocated for expanding existing facilities in New York and Vermont and US$3 billion for research into advanced packaging and other technologies. This strategic investment is aimed at meeting the increasing demand for chips, driven partly by the artificial intelligence boom, and ensuring supply security for customers like Apple, Qualcomm, and General Motors. The company emphasizes its focus on essential chips and carving out lucrative niches, rather than competing in the most advanced production market.

title: Top researchers leave Intel to build startup with ‘the biggest, baddest CPU’
summary: Four former top Intel chip architects, with nearly a century of combined experience, have departed the company to launch AheadComputing, a Beaverton-based startup. They aim to revolutionize semiconductor technology by designing a new class of microprocessor based on the open-source RISC-V architecture, which they believe will be the "biggest, baddest CPU" in the world. The company has already raised $22 million in venture capital and boasts prominent semiconductor engineer Jim Keller on its board, positioning itself to capitalize on the ongoing upheaval in the semiconductor industry and Oregon's evolving tech landscape.

title: Lenovo Vs. HP Vs. Dell PC Sales Showdown In 2025 So Far
summary: The article details the 2025 PC sales performance of Lenovo, HP, and Dell, highlighting a PC refresh driven by commercial buyers ahead of the Windows 11 migration and potential tariff impacts. While IDC projects overall market growth, consumer sales remain challenged compared to strong commercial demand. The three companies show varying revenue and market share, with projections for continued PC growth in the coming quarters.

title: HP’s New Device Management Tools: ‘Better Customer Experience’ And ‘Better Revenue For Partner’
summary: HP Inc. is introducing new device management tools for PCs, print, and collaboration, aimed at improving customer experience and partner profitability. These tools allow solution providers to proactively diagnose and fix PC problems, including remote remediation for unbootable devices using Intel chips. HP's Managed Device Services for PCs and Premium+ Support for printers are designed to provide partners with more strategic customer relationships and increased revenue opportunities through enhanced service offerings.

title: India’s first Aatmanirbhar semiconductor chip is finally here!
summary: India is set to roll out its first indigenously produced semiconductor chips by mid-2025, marking a significant step towards self-reliance in technology. Union IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw confirmed that 28nm and 90nm nodes will begin production, a crucial development for India despite not being cutting-edge global technology. This initiative aims to establish India's credibility in chip manufacturing, foster a domestic supply chain for essential electronics, and reduce dependence on foreign imports and geopolitical risks.

title: Imperial College London Chooses Intel Xeon 6 for Latest HPC Supercomputer
summary: Imperial College London has selected Intel Xeon 6 6900P series processors to power its new HX2 supercomputer, built on Lenovo’s ThinkSystem SC750 V4 Neptune platform. This water-cooled system, developed in collaboration with ICICLE, is set to deploy this year to advance high-performance computing and artificial intelligence research. The HX2 supercomputer emphasizes sustainability through its liquid cooling technology, aiming to significantly reduce energy consumption and environmental impact while delivering enhanced computational performance for scientific research.

title: Qualcomm to acquire Alphawave Semi for $2.4B
summary: Qualcomm has agreed to acquire Alphawave Semi for $2.4 billion, aiming to strengthen its position in the artificial intelligence and data center markets. This acquisition is part of Qualcomm's broader strategy to enhance its product capabilities for next-generation technologies and expand into new markets. The deal is expected to close in the first quarter of 2026 and will provide Qualcomm with Alphawave's high-speed connectivity and compute silicon assets.

title: ‘Forever chemicals’ industry aims to capitalize on AI boom
summary: The "forever chemicals" industry, particularly Chemours, is increasing PFAS production to meet the soaring demand for semiconductor chips driven by the AI boom. This expansion raises environmental concerns due to the long history of pollution associated with PFAS manufacturing plants and the substances' links to serious health problems. Despite these concerns, PFAS manufacturers argue their products are essential for semiconductor production, and finding alternatives without PFAS for certain applications remains a significant challenge.

title: Mastering Inventory Finance in the Auto Supply Chain
summary: The automotive industry's shift towards software-defined vehicles and autonomous systems has increased its reliance on semiconductors, presenting new supply chain and financial challenges for OEMs and suppliers. This article highlights how strategic inventory finance solutions can help automakers manage component shortages, extended lead times, and tariff uncertainties, ensuring operational continuity and financial optimization. By reallocating working capital and implementing structured inventory management, companies can maintain competitiveness and support innovation in this evolving landscape.

title: How to weigh up companies after a big share price fall
summary: This article explores how investors should evaluate companies after a significant share price decline, drawing on research by Michael Mauboussin. It highlights the frequency of major drawdowns and the low recovery rate for many stocks, while also providing six key questions investors should ask to identify potential winners and avoid losers in such scenarios.

title: Synopsys Accelerates AI and Multi-Die Design Innovation on Advanced Samsung Foundry Processes
summary: Synopsys and Samsung Foundry are collaborating to advance AI and multi-die design on Samsung's advanced process technologies. This partnership has led to successful customer tape-outs, improved power, performance, and area (PPA) with certified AI-driven EDA flows, and a broad portfolio of Synopsys IP optimized for Samsung's nodes. Their joint efforts aim to speed up time-to-market for next-generation designs in high-performance computing, consumer electronics, and automotive markets.

title: Intel will lay off 15% to 20% of its factory workers, memo says
summary: Intel plans to lay off 15% to 20% of its factory workers, a significant reduction impacting thousands of jobs, particularly in Oregon. The layoffs are driven by affordability challenges, financial position, and a protracted decline in sales due to intense competition and a failure to develop advanced AI chips. These cuts are part of a broader effort to streamline operations and reduce complexity, though specific numbers for other business units have not been released.

title: Marvell Introduces Optimized Integrated Power Solutions to Boost Performance, Efficiency, and ROI of Accelerated Infrastructure
summary: Marvell Technology, Inc. has launched its Package Integrated Voltage Regulator (PIVR) power solutions, designed to significantly enhance the performance, efficiency, and ROI of AI and cloud infrastructure for hyperscalers. These pre-validated solutions transition from board-level power delivery to integrated silicon chips, offering up to 2X higher current density and an 85% reduction in transmission losses. This innovation supports the development of 4+ kilowatt compute platforms, providing a foundation for elevating computing operations in data centers.

title: STMicroelectronics Introduces Advanced Human Presence Detection Solution to Enhance Laptop and PC User Experience
summary: STMicroelectronics has launched a new Human Presence Detection (HPD) solution for laptops and PCs, designed to improve user experience, security, and power efficiency. This technology integrates ST's FlightSense Time-of-Flight (ToF) sensors with AI algorithms to enable features like adaptive screen dimming, automatic lock/wake, and multi-person detection, all while prioritizing user privacy by not using cameras. The solution is expected to reduce power consumption by over 20% daily and is based on extensive AI development and data collection efforts.

title: Top Health Care Execs to Watch in 2025: Intel Corp.’s Mike Namvar
summary: The article profiles Mike Namvar, Account Executive for Federal Healthcare at Intel Corp., highlighting his leadership in managing a $250 million portfolio and driving innovation in healthcare technology. It details how Intel, under his guidance, has deployed AI-powered solutions for cancer screening and mental health, and his team's fundraising efforts for veteran health and pediatric cancer research. In 2025, Namvar is focusing on developing next-generation innovation labs with alliance partners, leveraging AI and robotics to advance therapeutics and cures.

title: Here's How Much a $1000 Investment in KLA Made 10 Years Ago Would Be Worth Today
summary: This article analyzes an investment in KLA (KLAC) over the last ten years. A $1000 investment in KLAC made in June 2015 would be worth $14,967.10 by June 19, 2025, representing a 1,396.71% gain, outperforming the S&P 500 and gold. The company, a leading supplier of process diagnostics and control equipment for semiconductor manufacturing, is expected to see continued growth due to strong demand in leading-edge logic, high-bandwidth memory, and advanced packaging, despite headwinds from U.S. export controls on China.

title: Semiconductor Stocks Retreat as U.S. Plans to Revoke Export Waivers
summary: Semiconductor stocks declined after a report indicated the U.S. Commerce Department plans to revoke waivers for South Korean and Taiwanese chipmakers, impacting companies like Samsung, SK Hynix, and TSMC. This move aims to restrict China's technological advancements but risks straining U.S. relations with key allies and potentially disrupting global supply chains. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index fell 0.8%, with companies like Lam Research, Nvidia, Intel, Broadcom, and Qualcomm seeing their shares trade lower.

title: Intel alum to get $1.9M bonus as newly-minted Ciena CFO
summary: Marc Graff, formerly of Intel and Altera, is set to become the CFO of Ciena, receiving a $1.9 million cash bonus and a $10.4 million equity grant to compensate for lost incentives from his previous employer. This appointment comes as Ciena reported a nearly 24% boost in net revenue, largely driven by increased data center and cloud spending fueled by the AI boom. Graff will succeed long-time CFO James Moylan, who is retiring.

title: Malaysia Set For Semiconductor Boost As Intel, Vishay Plan Expansion
summary: Malaysia is set to enhance its position in the global semiconductor supply chain with renewed investment and expansion plans from US tech leaders Intel Corporation and Vishay Intertechnology Inc. This development follows high-level discussions with the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry (MITI). The expansions align with Malaysia's New Industrial Master Plan (NIMP) 2030 and National Semiconductor Strategy (NSS) to transform the country into a high-tech, high-value economy.

title: An Intel-HP Collaboration Delivers Next-Gen AI PCs
summary: Intel and HP have collaborated to deliver next-generation AI PCs, with Intel optimizing hardware and software for AI workloads on HP's new business laptops. This partnership led to enhanced performance for various AI-powered applications, some of which are now available on Windows-based PCs for the first time. The collaboration underscores a significant shift towards AI-first computing, aiming to provide tangible value and improved user experiences.

title: Trade Minister: Intel Eyes Further Expansion In Malaysia
summary: Malaysia's Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry (MITI) has affirmed Intel's commitment to expanding its operations in the country, highlighting Intel's significant investment of RM50 billion and its foundational role since 1972. This move aligns with Malaysia's national strategies to foster a resilient industrial ecosystem. Additionally, Vishay Intertechnology, another key US-based semiconductor company, is also planning an expansion in Malaysia, further solidifying the country's position as a hub for high-tech semiconductor facilities.

title: Intel Starts Layoffs Under Lip-Bu Tan, Some Impacted Roles Listed
summary: Intel has begun layoffs in California, impacting approximately 107 employees at its Santa Clara headquarters, following CEO Lip-Bu Tan's earlier warning about job cuts. These layoffs are part of Tan's strategy to make the company leaner, faster, and more efficient, reducing operating expenses and streamlining decision-making by cutting management roles. Specific positions affected include various engineering roles, cloud software architects, and several management positions across different departments.

title: Intel shutters its automotive business amid restructuring push
summary: Intel Corp. is closing its automotive business, Intel Automotive, and laying off most of its employees as part of a broader restructuring effort to refocus on its core client and data center portfolio. The unit developed processors like the ACU U310 for smart vehicles, a market where Intel also owns Mobileye Global Inc. This move, along with other job cuts, aims to reduce annual operating expenses by $1.5 billion by 2026 to boost the company's financial performance amidst increasing competition.

title: 3 AI Stocks That Could Be Hidden Gems
summary: This article highlights three lesser-known tech companies—Camtek, FormFactor, and Vertiv Holdings—as "hidden gems" in the AI industry. These companies provide essential "picks and shovels" support for the AI revolution, focusing on critical aspects like chip inspection, testing, and cooling infrastructure. The author argues that their foundational contributions position them for sustained growth even as AI giants capture most of the headlines.

title: Intel to shutter its automotive business, lay off department employees
summary: Intel Corp. has announced plans to shut down its automotive business, a move consistent with CEO Lip-Bu Tan's strategy to cut costs and refocus on core client and data center portfolios. The company will lay off most employees within the automotive group, with 107 workers in Santa Clara, California, already notified of impending layoffs. This follows previous warnings from Tan about upcoming workforce reductions across Intel.

title: Deals of the Year 2025: Awards reflect a buoyant year and a deepening of markets across all categories
summary: The 2025 Finance Dublin Deals of the Year Awards highlight a significant increase in deal-making activity in Ireland, with aggregate transactions approaching $1 billion annually. The awards recognize 61 deals across various categories, including M&A, Debt Capital Markets, Equity Capital Markets, Loans & Financing, and Financial Services, demonstrating the professionalism and creativity of Ireland's financial sector. Notable transactions include the Smurfit Kappa-WestRock merger, AerCap's sukuk issuance, BASF Ireland's Panda bond, and Amazon Conservation DAC's debt-for-nature swap.

title: Super Micro Computer, Inc. - Industry First -- Supermicro Systems Certified by Intel for an Immersion Cooling Solution
summary: Super Micro Computer, Inc. announced that its BigTwin Multi-Node Server with 4th and 5th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable Processors has received industry immersion cooling certification from Intel. This certification, along with compliance to OCP immersion guidelines, ensures customers that Supermicro's systems are fully functional in specified dielectric liquids, leading to significant reductions in Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) and energy costs. The collaboration with Intel and adherence to OCP standards highlight Supermicro's commitment to sustainable and efficient data center cooling solutions in the AI era.

title: Intel and Lenovo Set a New Standard for AI-Powered PC Experiences
summary: Intel and Lenovo collaborated extensively over several years to develop the new Lenovo Aura Edition line of premium AI PCs, which launched in September 2024. This partnership focused on redefining the premium laptop experience through extensive user feedback, co-engineering breakthroughs in memory performance and thermal management, and a global team effort. These AI PCs feature innovations like Smart Share, high refresh rate displays, and efficient AI software, with future evolutions planned based on Intel's Panther Lake CPUs.

title: SkyWater has big plans following $93M purchase of Infineon's Austin chipmaking facility
summary: SkyWater Technology has acquired Infineon's former semiconductor fabrication plant in Austin for $93 million. The Minnesota-based chipmaker plans to expand the facility's capacity to meet the rising demand for U.S.-made semiconductors. This acquisition will allow SkyWater to ramp up factory production and potentially manufacture millions of chips for various applications.

title: I built a brand new gaming PC from the ground up with Corsair's iCUE Link: a simplified, smart and just a little frustrating all-in-one system
summary: The author built a new gaming PC using Corsair's iCUE Link system, which aims to simplify component connectivity and control. While the system offers benefits like reduced cable clutter for some components and unified software control, the author found it somewhat frustrating due to thick proprietary cables, challenges with routing, and the need for additional standard cables for certain components. Despite its drawbacks and high cost, the system delivered excellent performance in the new all-AMD build.

title: Intel (INTC) Stock Update: Bears regain control on yesterday's surprising news
summary: Intel's (INTC) stock dropped 4.25% following news that the company might halt marketing its 18A chipmaking process due to low customer interest, shifting focus to 14A technology. This decline, amidst a rising semiconductor sector, signals relative weakness. Technical analysis shows Intel breaking below key support levels, with bears now in control, and further downside expected towards the $20.20 and $19.17 price points.

title: Report: UMC eyes shift to cutting-edge chips with Intel deal
summary: United Microelectronics Corp. (UMC) is reportedly considering a return to advanced process technology nodes by expanding its partnership with Intel, potentially including 6 nm semiconductors. This strategic shift would move UMC away from its recent focus on mature nodes and allow it to share significant capital expenditure costs with a partner. The collaboration with Intel could also extend to advanced packaging alongside their current 12 nm FinFET process technology agreement.

title: Steve Sanghi’s Microchip Redux
summary: Steve Sanghi, the former CEO of Microchip Technology, has returned as interim CEO and president after his successor, Ganesh Moorthy, faced inventory stockpiles and a sales slump. Sanghi, who previously led Microchip to significant growth and profitability over 30 years, is now permanently reinstated as CEO. His return marks an attempt to re-engineer the company's turnaround, focusing on integrating analog with MCUs and potential new acquisitions, while also facing the challenge of finding a long-term successor.

title: Tower, Tata Semiconductor in final race to upgrade India’s chip lab
summary: Israel's Tower Semiconductor Ltd. and India’s Tata Semiconductor are the two final contenders among 12 bidders for a multi-million-dollar project to upgrade the Indian government’s semiconductor lab. This news caused Tower's share price to rise 6.3% on Wall Street, reaching $45.40 and giving the company a market capitalization of $5.2 billion. The development follows Tower's withdrawal from a previous plan to build an Indian chip plant with Adani Group.

title: Intel Cuts More Than 500 Jobs in Oregon as Part of Layoff Plan
summary: Intel (INTC) is laying off over 500 employees in Oregon, specifically in Aloha and Hillsboro facilities, beginning July 15. This job reduction is part of a larger layoff plan projected to affect approximately 20% of the chipmaker's total staff. This follows a recent announcement of 107 layoffs at Intel's Santa Clara headquarters in California.

title: Intel cuts more than 500 jobs in Oregon
summary: Intel announced a plan to cut 529 jobs in Oregon, affecting engineers and technicians at its Hillsboro and Aloha campuses, as part of a broader effort to streamline operations. An update later increased the number of layoffs to 2,392. This move follows previous job cuts and a significant fiscal year loss, with the new CEO aiming to make the company leaner and more focused on innovation amidst competition in the AI-capable semiconductor market.

title: Mobileye Drops After Intel Plans To Slash Stake — But Q2 Outlook Beats Wall Street Forecasts
summary: Mobileye shares dropped after Intel announced plans to sell a significant portion of its stake in the company, including offering 45 million Class A shares and converting 50 million Class B shares. Despite this, Mobileye's second-quarter revenue outlook of $502 million to $506 million notably surpassed analyst estimates of $466.4 million. The company also secured a supply deal with TSMC to increase capacity for its components.

title: At US$72.34, Is Silicon Motion Technology Corporation (NASDAQ:SIMO) Worth Looking At Closely?
summary: Silicon Motion Technology Corporation (NASDAQ:SIMO) recently saw a significant price hike, but despite this, the stock appears fairly valued according to Simply Wall St's valuation model, trading only slightly below its intrinsic value. Investors are advised to consider other factors like financial strength and balance sheet health, as the stock is currently trading around its fair value.

title: Mobileye Global (MBLY) Dives as Intel Unloads $900-Million Stake
summary: Mobileye Global (MBLY) saw its share prices drop by 7.08% after Intel Corp. announced plans to unload a $900 million stake in the company. title: Why Chip Packaging Is the New Battleground for Semiconductor Innovation
summary: As Moore's Law slows, advanced chip packaging has become crucial for semiconductor innovation, acting as an active performance facilitator rather than a passive component. Key trends include heterogeneous integration (2.5D/3D), fan-out packaging, chiplet standardization with UCIe, and advanced substrates, all driven by demands from AI, 5G, and HPC. India is also increasing its role in this ecosystem, despite challenges like thermal management, cost, and talent gaps.

title: Intel layoffs exceed 5,000 across US
summary: Intel Corp. has announced layoffs exceeding 5,000 employees across four U.S. states, significantly increasing initial estimates, as part of a restructuring effort to cut costs and simplify operations. The majority of the job cuts are concentrated in California and Oregon, with additional reductions in Arizona and Texas. This downsizing aligns with CEO Lip-Bu Tan's strategy to make Intel a leaner, more efficient, and engineering-focused company, especially in the evolving AI-driven computing market.

title: Cadence Design Systems, Inc. (CDNS) Benefits From Its Relationship With NVIDIA, Says Jim Cramer
summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) as a crucial company in the semiconductor industry, providing essential electronic design automation (EDA) software for chip designers like NVIDIA. He noted the company's benefit from the Trump administration's rescission of export restrictions to China and emphasized its strong partnership with NVIDIA. Cramer also mentioned previous discussions about CDNS, suggesting a potential buying opportunity on weakness despite concerns about overbuilding for AI infrastructure.

title: Mobileye (MBLY) Slashes 15.5% on Intel Corp. Unloads $900-Million Stake
summary: Mobileye Global (MBLY) saw a 15.5% decline in its stock value after Intel Corp. initiated the sale of a $900 million stake in the company as part of a restructuring effort. Intel is selling 50 million Class A common shares and has the option to sell an additional 7.5 million, with Mobileye planning to buy back over 6.2 million shares. Intel also intends to convert 50 million B shares to A shares, which it will retain.

title: These 3 Undervalued Stocks Could Surge as Value Rotation Nears
summary: This article identifies three undervalued stocks to potentially benefit from a rotation back into value investments, namely Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B), and Intel Corp (INTC). These companies are presented as having significant upside potential and limited downside risk despite current market optimism focusing on growth stocks. Analyst ratings and recent institutional buying reinforce the bullish outlook for these value plays.

title: Intel Corp sells Mobileye (MBLY) shares worth $1.02 billion
summary: Intel Corporation sold 63,731,985 shares of Mobileye Global Inc. Class A Common Stock for over $1 billion. This transaction occurred amidst a recent dip in Mobileye's stock price, with analysts offering varying perspectives on the company's valuation and future performance. Intel Overseas Funding Corporation still indirectly holds 50 million Mobileye shares after the sale.

summary: Intel Corp (NASDAQ: INTC), a ten percent owner of Mobileye Global Inc (NASDAQ: MBLY), sold 63,731,985 shares of Class A Common Stock for $1.02 billion. title: A Supermicro agora oferece servidores X14 de 4 soquetes de alto desempenho para cargas de trabalho empresariais, de banco de dados e de missão crítica exigentes, com base nos processadores Intel Xeon 6
summary: A Super Micro Computer, Inc. (SMCI) lançou seus novos servidores de 4 soquetes X14, baseados nos processadores Intel Xeon 6 com P-Cores, visando cargas de trabalho de missão crítica, bancos de dados e aplicações empresariais. title: Intel cuts more than 5,000 jobs across four states as restructuring accelerates
summary: Intel Corp. is laying off over 5,000 employees across four states, including California, Oregon, Arizona, and Texas, as part of a restructuring plan to cut over 20% of its global workforce. The layoffs are part of a broader trend of workforce reductions by major tech companies, amid concerns about AI's potential impact on employment.

title: Next Generation Non Volatile Memory Market | Global Market Analysis Report - 2035
summary: The next generation non-volatile memory market is predicted to expand from USD 1,097.9 million in 2025 to USD 2,348.5 million by 2035, exhibiting a CAGR of 7.9%. summary: SRM Entertainment, Inc. has officially rebranded to Tron Inc. and will trade under the new symbol 'TRON' on the Nasdaq Capital Market starting July 17, 2025. Tron Inc. aims to enhance long-term value through its focus on blockchain-integrated treasury strategies, while also continuing its business of designing and manufacturing custom merchandise.

title: Mass layoffs at Intel will mean less revenue for Oregon, even as the semiconductor industry remains a major economic driver
summary: Intel's mass layoffs of 2,400 employees in Oregon will lead to reduced revenue for the state, despite the semiconductor industry remaining a major economic driver. While experts believe Intel will survive its current challenges as a slimmer company, the job losses signify a decline in the sector's employment, impacting Washington County's finances through decreased income and property taxes. The company aims to "right-size" itself to compete more effectively, with local officials anticipating a short-term financial hit but hoping existing agreements will mitigate the impact.

title: Investcorp BDC Appoints New CFO Amid Leadership Change
summary: Investcorp Credit Management BDC has announced a leadership change, with Walter Tsin resigning as CFO and Robert Andrew Muns appointed as his successor. summary: Mega Fortune Company Limited successfully closed its initial public offering on July 17, 2025, raising $15 million by selling 3,750,000 ordinary shares at $4.00 each. This IPO is a significant step for the Hong Kong-based IoT solutions provider, aiming to boost its market presence and expand its services in the Asia-Pacific region.

title: AMD Stock Analysis: Can AMD Stock Reach $200?
summary: This article analyzes whether AMD stock can reach $200, examining the company's overview, financial health, and key growth drivers. title: RISC-V: Advancing Towards High-Performance Computing
summary: RISC-V is increasingly advancing towards high-performance computing, building on an established ecological foundation. The instruction set architecture, known for its openness and scalability, is expanding beyond AIoT edge devices into strategically important high-performance computing scenarios, with significant growth projected in market penetration and chip shipments. While challenges remain in software ecosystem development and talent shortages, industry experts believe benchmark products and a long-term perspective will drive wider adoption and innovation.

title: Intel Stock Is in a ‘Catch-22’ Scenario. How Should You Play INTC Here?
summary: Intel (INTC) is facing a strategic dilemma, described as a "Catch-22" scenario by Loop Capital analyst Gary Mobley. The company needs to use Taiwan Semi's advanced manufacturing to compete with rivals like Nvidia and AMD, but doing so would negatively impact Intel's own foundry business. This internal conflict makes analysts cautious, with Loop Capital giving Intel a "Neutral" rating and a $25 price target, while the consensus on Wall Street is a "Hold" with an average price target of $22.68.

While analysts see it as aligning with Intel's goal to play to its core strengths, some express concern that detaching networking from an "AI-first" strategy might be shortsighted given AI's reliance on robust networking infrastructure.

title: Exclusive: Cadence to plead guilty and pay $140 million to US for China sales
summary: Cadence Design Systems has agreed to plead guilty and pay over $140 million to the U.S. government for illegally selling chip design products to China's National University of Defense Technology (NUDT), which is believed to be involved in simulating nuclear explosions. The company violated export controls by supplying NUDT, including through aliases, between 2015 and 2020. This resolution comes during new U.S.-China trade talks, signaling continued enforcement of export controls despite broader negotiations.

title: Act Fast: These 3 Undervalued Stocks Won’t Stay Low for Long
summary: This article highlights three undervalued stocks—XPeng (XPEV), Intel (INTC), and Albemarle (ALB)—that present attractive buying opportunities despite current market highs. Despite Governor Mike DeWine's optimism, Bubb highlights the local community's significant investment in infrastructure and workforce development, which now faces uncertainty with the halted construction and potential lack of future jobs.

summary: SuperX AI Technology Limited (Nasdaq: SUPX) has launched its new flagship SuperX XN9160-B200 AI Server, powered by NVIDIA's Blackwell B200 GPU. This server is designed to accelerate AI training, machine learning, and high-performance computing, offering up to a 30x performance increase compared to the H100 series for certain tasks. It features 8 NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs, fifth-generation NVLink, 1440 GB of HBM3E memory, and 6th Gen Intel Xeon processors, positioning SuperX as a key player in advanced AI infrastructure solutions.

title: Supermicro Open Storage Summit Showcases the Impact of AI Workloads on Storage: Starts August 12
summary: Super Micro Computer, Inc. announced the return of its Open Storage Summit for 2025, a free virtual conference focusing on the impact of AI workloads on modern storage infrastructure. The event, running from August 12 to August 28, will feature nine sessions, 40 speakers, and 23 companies, addressing topics like distributed inference, enterprise AI using Agentic, RAG, and GenAI, Storage-as-a-Service, and software-defined storage. Industry leaders including AMD, Intel, NVIDIA, and Nutanix will participate, offering actionable advice for data center managers.

title: Alphabet-A Options Spot-On: On July 31st, 493.02K Contracts Were Traded, With 3.59 Million Open Interest
summary: On July 31st, Alphabet-A (GOOGL.US) experienced significant options trading volume, totaling 493.02K contracts, with call options accounting for 79.55%. title: SMH ETF: Question & Answer
summary: Semiconductors are essential components for modern technology like AI and EVs. title: Teradyne Unveils Magnum 7H - The Next-Generation Memory Tester for High Bandwidth Memory Devices
summary: Teradyne, Inc. (NASDAQ: TER) has launched Magnum 7H, a next-generation memory tester designed for high bandwidth memory (HBM) devices used in high-performance AI servers. The Magnum 7H offers high-parallelism, high-speed, and high-accuracy testing for various HBM versions, including HBM2E, HBM3, HBM3E, HBM4, and HBM4E, across the entire manufacturing process. Volume shipments have already begun to major HBM manufacturers, highlighting its readiness for current and future HBM testing demands.

summary: Everspin Technologies has appointed Sean Dougherty as Vice President of Sales, bringing over two decades of semiconductor industry experience to the role. Dougherty will lead the company's sales strategy and growth, while former VP David Schrenk will now focus exclusively on Business Development. This strategic move aims to accelerate revenue monetization and strategic partnerships for Everspin.

title: China fears Nvidia chips could track, trace and shut down its AIs
summary: China has raised concerns that Nvidia's H20 AI chips could contain backdoors allowing the US to track, locate, and remotely shut down its artificial intelligence systems. The Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC) summoned Nvidia for an explanation and proof, citing allegations that US lawmakers have called for "tracking and positioning" functions in advanced chips exported abroad. While Nvidia denies these claims, the incident reflects China's growing apprehensions about potential vulnerabilities in foreign-made technology, reminiscent of previous US suspicions regarding Huawei.

summary: AMD reported strong second-quarter revenue of $7.7 billion and forecasts even higher third-quarter revenue, primarily driven by CPU market share gains against Intel and impressive growth in its AI business. Tom Cotton regarding Tan's investments in Chinese companies with links to the People's Liberation Army. summary: Rice University's Office of Technology Transfer has signed a subscription agreement with Intel Corporation, allowing Intel to explore and potentially license patented innovations developed at Rice. Under the agreement, Intel will pay an annual fee for an option to evaluate specified technologies, with additional fees for licensing selected innovations. This partnership aims to accelerate the commercialization of Rice's research and highlights the university's role in technology and industry collaborations.

The stock trades at 17x forward earnings, with long-term growth prospects appearing strong despite muted short-term forecasts.

title: U.S. takes 10% stake in Intel, Trump says
summary: The Trump administration announced it has taken a 10% stake in Intel, a move President Trump described as securing the future of the nation's semiconductor industry and a strategic assertion of federal control over private business. This unusual government intervention, valued at $11.1 billion through CHIPS Act grants and a classified program, aims to bolster U.S. leadership in AI amid competition with China, despite Intel's recent struggles against rivals like Nvidia and AMD. U.S. Senator Mark Warner (D-Virginia) acknowledged the importance of American leadership in critical technology.

title: Intel agrees to give US government $8.9B stake in its company

The deal has drawn criticism from some U.S. Senators who view it as corporate extortion.

title: US to take 10% equity stake in Intel, in Trump's latest corporate move
summary: The U.S. government, under President Donald Trump, will acquire a 9.9% equity stake in Intel for $8.9 billion, converting government grants from the CHIPS Act and the Secure Enclave program into an ownership share. This move, aiming to bolster Intel's struggling foundry business and ensure domestic chip production, reflects a growing trend of unusual government intervention in corporate America. The deal comes after previous tensions between Trump and Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan, and analysts question if the cash infusion will be enough to help Intel catch up to competitors like TSMC and Nvidia.

title: US Buys 10% Stake In Intel In US$10 Billion Rescue Deal
summary: The US government has acquired a 10% stake in Intel Corp through a US$10 billion support package, marking a significant intervention in corporate America. This deal, which converts federal grants into equity at a discounted share price, aims to provide a critical lifeline to the struggling chipmaker. The government's stake could increase to 15% if Intel's foundry business underperforms.

title: Intel: The US Government’s Stake is Good News but Not an All-Clear Sign
summary: The US government has announced an $8.9 billion investment in Intel's common stock, representing a 9.9% stake, which Morningstar views favorably for investors due to its passive nature. Despite this positive development, Morningstar maintains a $21 fair value estimate and a Very High Uncertainty Rating for Intel, citing ongoing execution risks in its turnaround efforts. The investment replaces previous Chips Act funding plans and includes a secure enclave program where Intel will provide semiconductors to the government.

title: Intel Collaborates with LG Innotek to Implement an AI-powered Smart Factory
summary: Intel is collaborating with LG Innotek to implement an AI-powered smart factory in Gumi, Korea, for precision component manufacturing. The factory will utilize Intel Core and Xeon processors along with Intel Arc GPUs and the OpenVINO software toolkit for automated inspection and defect analysis. This collaboration aims to achieve zero defects, enhance cost efficiency, and scale AI-driven inspection solutions across LG Innotek's global production lines.

title: Trump administration considers stake in defence firms like Lockheed Martin
summary: The Trump administration is considering taking stakes in domestic defence contractors such as Lockheed Martin, as confirmed by US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick. This potential move is part of a broader push by Trump to increase US investments in key industries, following recent similar actions with Intel, US Steel, MP Materials, Nvidia, and AMD. Critics argue this could prioritize financial success over strategic considerations and blur the lines between government and regulated companies, while supporters like Senator Bernie Sanders believe taxpayers deserve a return on federal investments in profitable companies.

title: Intel, a U.S. semiconductor company, said securing a stake in the Donald Trump administration "could..
summary: Intel has expressed concerns that the Donald Trump administration's decision to acquire a 10% stake in the company could pose significant risks. The company highlighted potential issues such as additional regulations abroad, restrictions on overseas subsidies, and the dilution of existing shareholders' value due to the discounted purchase price. Despite these concerns, President Trump hailed the agreement as a "great deal for the United States," and Intel's CEO welcomed the government as a shareholder.

title: BlackBerry Eyes A Turnaround With Its Software Reboot
summary: BlackBerry, once a smartphone giant, has refocused on enterprise software and services, specifically QNX embedded IoT software and Secure Communications cybersecurity. Despite recent stock gains and improved operating margins, the company faces challenges including shaky revenue growth and intense competition. Investors are watching for upcoming Q2 FY2026 earnings, an Investor Day, and milestones for its planned IoT spin-off as potential catalysts for its ambitious turnaround strategy.

title: Evolv Technologies Q2 Earnings Call Highlights Growth
summary: Evolv Technologies Holdings, Inc. (EVLV) reported strong Q2 2025 results, with notable revenue growth of 29% year-over-year to $32.5 million and an Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) of $110.5 million, up 27% year-over-year. The company achieved its third consecutive quarter of positive adjusted EBITDA, increased liquidity, and expanded its global customer base to over 1,000, while also resolving significant legal issues. Despite a slight decline in adjusted gross margin to 55%, Evolv raised its full-year revenue growth expectations to between 27% and 30%, signaling a positive outlook for fiscal year 2025.

title: US revokes TSMC's authorisation on China-bound tech
summary: The US has revoked TSMC's authorization to freely ship advanced technology from the US to China, a move that could disrupt the chip giant's manufacturing capabilities in China. This follows a similar action taken against South Korea's Samsung and SK Hynix. While TSMC is assessing the situation and engaging with the US government, experts suggest the overall impact on TSMC might be limited as its Chinese facility produces older-generation chips that constitute a small portion of its revenue.

title: These Were the 3 Top-Performing Stocks in the Nasdaq Composite in August 2025

Intel received significant investments from the U.S. government and SoftBank, while Applovin and IDEXX Laboratories reported strong second-quarter earnings and raised their full-year guidance.

title: Intel Ousted From Dow Jones By Rival Nvidia

This change reflects Intel's struggles in the AI-powered chipmaking market and its recent financial losses, while Nvidia's market value has surged due to demand for AI accelerator chips. The move by S&P Global aims to ensure the Dow Jones more accurately represents the semiconductor industry.

title: Intel Corporation (INTC): It’s “Better To Take The Government’s Money,” Says Jim Cramer
summary: Jim Cramer commented on Intel Corporation (INTC), suggesting it's beneficial for the company to accept government funding, particularly after the Trump administration converted $8.9 billion of CHIPS Act grants into equity. Intel's shares have seen a 20% year-to-date gain, with a significant jump in August. Cramer views this government support as a crucial step for Intel to raise additional funds, though he notes it's only a fraction of what the company ultimately requires.

title: Qualcomm CEO Says Intel’s Chip Production Not Good Enough Yet

However, Amon indicated that Qualcomm would consider Intel as a customer if Intel improves its manufacturing techniques to produce more efficient chips. This positions Qualcomm as critical of Intel's current capabilities while leaving the door open for future collaboration.

title: Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) Market: Current Analysis and Forecast (2025-2033)
summary: The global Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) market, valued at USD 12,788.87 million in 2024, is projected to grow at an 11.88% CAGR from 2025 to 2033, driven by expanding data centers and 5G network advancements. Low-end FPGAs currently dominate the market due to their cost-effectiveness and low power consumption, while the 20-90nm node size segment leads in adoption. The Asia-Pacific region is expected to maintain its market dominance, primarily fueled by the strong electronics manufacturing sector and increasing demand for AI and IoT integrated devices in countries like China.

title: The U.S. government is taking a stake in Intel. It's rare — and it has some risks
summary: The U.S. government is taking a rare 10% equity stake in Intel, becoming its largest single shareholder. This move, spearheaded by the Trump administration, is driven by the desire to boost domestic chip manufacturing for AI and national security amid competition with China, rather than an economic crisis. While analysts see Intel as the best U.S. option to regain leadership in high-end chip production, government involvement carries risks of political fallout and inefficiency, potentially impacting the company's business decisions.

title: The U.S. government is taking a stake in Intel. It’s rare — and it has some risks
summary: The U.S. government, under the Trump administration, has taken a rare 10% equity stake in chipmaker Intel, converting previous grants into ownership. This unprecedented move outside of a direct economic crisis is driven by competition with China and the global race for artificial intelligence, aiming to bolster domestic high-end microchip manufacturing. While analysts see Intel (INTC) as the U.S.'s best bet in this sector, concerns remain about potential political pressures and inefficiencies stemming from government involvement.

title: U.S. Retracts Tariff Waiver for Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing

President Trump also announced new tariffs on semiconductors from companies that do not produce them domestically, encouraging U.S. manufacturing.

title: Intel CEO Sells Stake to US

This warning was delivered during a Cisco Systems Inc. conference. Tan indicated that discussions with major memory players confirmed the prolonged nature of the shortage.

title: Intel retreats from $300 million manufacturing pact with Tower Semiconductor
summary: Intel has informed Tower Semiconductor of its intention to withdraw from a 2023 manufacturing agreement worth $300 million. This pact, which aimed for Intel to produce 300-millimeter wafers for Tower in New Mexico, was established shortly after Intel's $5.4 billion acquisition of Tower collapsed. Tower Semiconductor is now redirecting production to its Fab7 plant in Japan and is engaged in mediation with Intel over the withdrawal.

title: India's Antitrust Watchdog Penalizes Intel $3.3 Million Over Discriminatory Warranty Policy
summary: India's Competition Commission has fined Intel $3.3 million for a discriminatory warranty policy concerning its boxed microprocessors. The policy, effective April 2016, only honored warranties for products purchased from authorized Indian distributors, unlike its global policy. Intel has since discontinued this policy, which was a mitigating factor in the final penalty amount.

title: Chipmaker moves forward with new fabrication plant at Bowers Campus
summary: Intel is moving forward with the development of a new manufacturing fabrication plant and a central utility building at its Bowers Campus in Santa Clara. The article highlights Intel's continued investment and growth in the Silicon Valley area.

title: Supreme Court to Hear Intel 401(k) Complaint Next Term
summary: The Supreme Court has agreed to delay hearing a complaint against Intel Corp. regarding allegedly imprudent investment options in its 401(k) plans until its next term, starting in October. Both parties requested more time to submit documents. The case will review the "meaningful benchmark" rule, which requires plaintiffs to identify comparable investments when alleging mismanagement of retirement plan assets, a rule the Intel plaintiffs argue is inconsistently applied and overly strict.

title: Supreme Court Delays Intel 401(k) Hearing Until October
summary: The Supreme Court has postponed the hearing for a case against Intel Corp. regarding alleged imprudent investment options in its 401(k) plans until its next term, beginning in October. title: India's Antitrust Watchdog Penalizes Intel $3.3 Million Over Discriminatory Warranty Policy
summary: India's antitrust regulator, the Competition Commission of India (CCI), has fined Intel Corporation $3.3 million for imposing a discriminatory warranty policy on its boxed microprocessors in India. The policy, effective from April 25, 2016, limited warranty services to products purchased from authorized Indian distributors, which the CCI found to be discriminatory compared to Intel's policies in other countries and to have negatively impacted Indian consumers and parallel importers. Intel has since discontinued the policy as of April 1, 2024, and is required to publicize this withdrawal.

title: WacoWatch
summary: Judge Albright denied VLSI Technology's motion to schedule a trial in its case against Intel.
